I have several design options but have not received much interest from the U.S. companies since the money for them is in the refractive implants and that’s really what the population cares about due to lack of education about the important of sunlight to optimize health. I am giving a lecture in November to a group of 40 eye docs attempting to gain more grassroots support from the physician community and then lobby the companies to get us more options for patients who desire this type of IOL. I think it’s a long road but one worth traveling.

Hi Emma,
I have really appreciated reading your posts. My partner, Nick is dealing with a cataract in one of his eyes and is finding himself closer now to having to act. A eye exam a couple of weeks ago reported that it is now 'ripe'. We will have to work within the NHS, I guess as funds are limited, but it is great to have your IOL suggestion to work with. He has not committed to anything yet.
The pressures in his eyes were shown to be rather high in that examination, and has to be the priority right now. He has no symptoms of glaucoma at all, weirdly, and this was an utter confusion for the eye doctors at the hospital. Nick has ramped up all his JK protocols and we bought an EMR Tek red light a couple of weeks ago to add more red light into the mix. Before any mainstream intervention we are working with a UK naturopath that has studied JKs work to see what impact we can have.

# Thread: Physician burnout and quantum biology
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/physician-burnout-and-quantum-biology.18593/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2016-10-15T18:47:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/physician-burnout-and-quantum-biology.18593/post-202852>

What happpens when you have no good options for people getting sick at record rates and they begin to die at high rates from things 20 years ago would not have killed them? Doctors are retiring early because they are beginning to sense some bad is afoot and they just cannot put their finger on what it is. One of the more cool things about doctoring is that we observe and have experienced many lives situations. We get to see and learn from them. Many of those on my website, including several you here have shared with me- full rich lives from birth to death. In my day job, you just have to have these rest periods in between to reflect and keep your sanity. When you're constantly around dying man and you feel powerless to save the species you check out early. That is what this story is really about linked below. It is the story that is developing between the words and lines printed. As a modern physician you have two choices; you can check out early or do something about the current situation. Find the source of the real problem in health care and share it with the world and see what they do with it. Some people propser and some decline with these new perspectives because they just cannot fathom the power of sunlight and the power of man made light to destroy cells. Quantum biology has renewed me for a decade and make me enthusiastic about the future of change in health care. Those who get the message I will burn for. Those who piss in the wind will have their toll from Mother Natrue to deal with and they will have to pay co-pays to the burning out docs remaining doing what they are taught to do. My hope is as more physicians burnout they realize that their education and insight can still be refurbished. My job is to save the history of our our profession to help our species; if fellow docs along the way pay attention then I feel I've done my part. The more docs who are infected the more people can be helped. In my opinion, burnout is a positive. It is nature's way of telling you, you've been going through the motions your soul has departed; you're a zombie, a member of the walking dead, a sleepwalker. False optimism is like administrating stimulants to an exhausted nervous system. If you realize this, you have time to change the way you think about biology and you'll be renewed and rekindle your passion. [https://healthecareers.com/article/...tm\_medium=email&utm\_campaign=physician-101516](https://healthecareers.com/article/healthcare-news/physicians-plan-to-retire-early?respmid=101516_Physician&resprid=61472094&utm_source=jmn&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=physician-101516)

# Thread: Brandon's Journal!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brandons-journal.18596/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Supensaa — 2016-10-15T19:59:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brandons-journal.18596/post-202861>

Things I've done so far:

Wearing Uvex blue blockers around artificial light including computer usage, along with using software called F.lux to further reduce blue light exposure. Also have F.lux on my phone.

Taking only cold showers twice a day (I use minimal soap, and when I do it is just simple saponified oils as soap. So no sulfates or other unnecessary surfectants. \[using Dr. Bronners Castile Soap\])

Taking ice baths by letting my freezer store up as much ice as it can, and then transferring it to a bowl and moving it to the other side of the freezer. I can take an ice bath about once a day given the rate at which ice is made in my freezer.

Grounding outside and getting fullbody sunlight. Well not fullbody, I'm in short shorts, haha. I live across from an apartment complex in a town home duplex.

FUN FACT: Someone called the cops on me because they thought I was stripping down into my underwear outside! I guess it must have looked weird that I went out side to my usual piece of grass across the street fully dressed, then started taking my clothes off, leaving rolled up athletic shorts the only thing on my body!

I'm eating a high fat, moderate protein, little to no carb diet. Standard epi-paleo fare, although my diet is not completely adhered to the protocol at all times due to money restraints, it has definitely gotten better! I have removed brown rice and beans from my diet, and am eaty leafy greens with saturated fat usually in the form of coconut oil.

I also visit this local Asian Buffet that has some good raw oysters and raw fish. Went there last night! Had a good amount of raw oyster, crab meat, raw sliced tuna, and seaweed! I go about once every 15 days on average, I will be increasing the frequency of this.

The only DHA I might possibly be getting outside of this trip to the buffet is: sardines, tuna, oyster, BUT they're all canned. I know fresh is better, but it will have to do until I increase my earning power.

Fermented foods: I have a few Pickl-It Jars, with the gasket and airlock, and I make fermented beat juice regularly, otherwise know nas beat kvass.

I also meditate twice daily, but I find that I tend to doze off if I meditate before I get early morning sunrise rays on my eyes. So for morning meditation I like to do it after I expose my eyes to the sun! I would like to wake up earlier than the sun, but I want to be able to wake myself with its light. I know that there are full spectrum bulbs or something of that nature, but I will have to look more into it. Not sure I want to make a habit of a non-sun light source setting my circadian rhythm.

FASTING: I was eating once a day for about two weeks, and I definitely felt great, during AND after. Now I am eating twice a day sometimes, but often eat once a day for a 5 days or so periodically. I just have to make sure that it is a meal high in fat, with good proteins and sulfurous veggies, etc. etc. (epi-paleo fare) I never knew that fasting would help so much!

At around 10:30 my body definitely tells me that I need to sleep. I end up waking between 6-7 AM if I fall asleep at this time. Sometimes I will go to bed earlier just to wake up earlier and start the day that way.

THINGS I NEED TO DO:

Upgrade my water source (using carbon filtered water from the sink, occasionally I drink a bottled water or a Pellegrino)

Change my lighting environment: I want to get UV bulbs and red lights inside of my room, maybe see if I can utilize a type of light source that can wake me up like the sun does as well? Since it's winter soon, it's already starting to get cloudy! I won't be able to get as much sun or UV, I really don't want my UV and overall natural light intake to decrease. (I do realize, however, that DHA + GOOD WATER is necessary for taking advantage of the benefits of sunlight)

A Magnetico and Quantlet are also on my list

23andMe is also something I look forward to doing to further tailor what my body needs and can benefit from.

P.S. I got a job! And....I can wear my blue blockers at work, haha. I work at a Japanese restaurant in the kitchen area. Sashimi, anyone?

There's definitely more I can say, but I'll save it for the next post.

My cognitive function, and focus has improved along with energy levels. My sebborheic eczema has not reoccurred for a while now, but my extremities still get cold occasionally. Things have been getting better!

Until then, school, work, health habits, and ecommerce (been studying and attempting to import goods from different countries, etc. on different sales channels. Most of my money for the past year has gone to trying to alleviate my health symptoms and getting a car! Now I can roll all of my money into ecommerce! I want to be able to move out on my own and afford the things necessary to increase my resilience and cognitive function.)

### Supensaa — 2016-10-19T20:10:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brandons-journal.18596/post-203061>

Yesterday I CT'd and I felt a strong tingling sensation all over my body that was just a surface level tingling, but it went deeper than that. Also, when I got up at out of the tub I was a bit uncoordinated due to the shivering. I unintentionally did a whole day fast the day before, and that morning of the day I did the CT, I consumed a meal high in fat. That was the only meal I had, and I did CT around 8:00PM later that day.

OH! And I've gotten a foot cramp once while doing CT! and my foot was out of the tub. Also for the past 2 days I have gotten a foot all the way to my calf cramp. 2 days ago it was on my right leg, last night it was on my left leg.

Does anyone know anything about CT and muscle cramps/spasms?

Is it because I'm not eating enough? Or lacking in some type of food? I normally eat once a day, just one big meal, because I'm out and about at school or work or sometimes both in one day. By the time I get home, it's already too close to bedtime to eat again.

Maybe I should get an insulated lunch box to be able to eat a meal when not at home?

Meditation: it's a lot easier to keep my focus and keep my mind from wandering/day dreaming after my eyes get that morning sun. Since I meditate the first thing in the morning on an empty stomach (water only) before the sun rises, when my mind wanders it is not uncommon for me to slip into what feels like the beginning of a dream! I keep my room pitch black while I do it, with a small red light. I sit on the edge of a bunch, with my spine erect and in line with my neck (by tucking my chin in).

When I go outside to meditate after the sun has come up, plus the fresh air, I feel more alert and my mind wanders a whole lot less, and if it does wander it isn't a daydream, just a passing thought. Long story short, I feel more aware now of how much more alert I am after I receive the sun's morning rays on my eyes!

### caroline — 2016-10-20T08:03:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brandons-journal.18596/post-203087>

There is a big C/T thread - check there.....

An insulated lunch box is a great idea. We do that all the time. We go early to the beach/ocean and take breakfast with us.

We take a thermos of bullet proof coffee and water....and sit on the rocks and munch away while watching the ocean.

### Supensaa — 2016-10-25T19:34:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brandons-journal.18596/post-203304>

Yesterday during my anthropology class we were discussing forensic anthropology. During the discussion about different types of traumas: ballistic, blunt, sharp... and during the slides about how disease can leave physical markings on skeletal remains I began to feel gradually light headed. As I began to walk out of the class room I began to tingle all over my body, become uncoordinated, lost most of my hearing, and began to get tunnel vision. I was blacking out. I lied down on the floor in the hallway on my back, and I gradually regained my senses over the next few minutes.

EDIT: I also did not eat at all that day, and it was around 6:45 PM, maybe that had something to do with it?

I remember having to leave the classroom during health class when we were shown a video of liposuction. I got light headed, but I didn't black out as bad as I did this time.

A year later I would black out again, but this time I would first get hives all over my body, then I would pass out completely for a few minutes. What was bad about that one, was that I lost vision while being conscious, then I passed out. For some reason my hearing was intact that time. But prior to the hives I had ran 3 miles and I think I was dehydrated! I suspect that was a contributor to this.

Oh and once a few years ago I read an article about circumcision and I felt a little sick and had to lie down! Yeah, seems a bit funny, I know

Anyways, I came back to class, saw even more morbid photos, but I didn't feel light headed from them. Didn't really enjoy seeing them, but there was no adverse reaction....so....what gives?

I'm glad I stayed in class and didn't leave because these photos showed rotting flesh and bloating, etc. The other ones were just skeletal remains of trauma and disease markers left on skeletal remains. I was curious as to whether or not I would get light headed again, but nope, I didn't.

WHY? Why did this happen?

It always has interested me that during the healing process, when one begins to adopt healthy habits, it seems like the disease process reverses. Or at least, old problems resurface so you can once again face them because last time you failed to. I know I'm reading a lot into this, but this certainly is a curiosity.

Lately I have been getting less light exposure due to work + school + cloudy weather due to winter. I really need to get some UV lights in my bedroom, and replace my lamp's bulb with a red bulb. I just started my new job a little over a week ago, and the only things I have to pay for are gas, car insurance, and school expenses.

I really need to flesh out any emotional baggage or weird mind shit that is hiding within me! I've been looking at bioenergetics for emotional release and other methods. I've always had the tendency to suppress emotion, but lately that has been a lot better.

I have backslid a bit these past two days. I wish I could alter my environment to where I remove all bad options such as unfavorable foods. It's usually not a problem for me unless I let stress build up, when this happens I am more prone to impulsiveness and willpower failures. I need to take breaks and destress throughout the day, and also try to make the breaks themselves a reward, make going outside the reward. (I am reminded of books such as: The Willpower Instinct, by Kelly McGonigal, and The Power of Habit, by Charles Duhigg)

Of course, good health is one of the contributors to a good psyche. It's all connected. Doing good in some area will positively affect another area.

All of this sounds like bad news, but I don't feel bad at all! I don't feel sorry for myself, I am thankful to be aware of this and if anything I am fascinated by what has happened as of late, and how my awareness has allowed my to observe it.

The last thing I want to do is give up. That'd be really boring, and not fun at all!

Have a good day everyone! Kick some ass!

### Supensaa — 2017-03-01T07:30:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brandons-journal.18596/post-209464>

I ordered a Quantlet Feb 2nd, should get it in a month or so. Excited!

I'm also seriously considering getting an infrared sauna. Looking at this model: [http://infraredsauna.com/sanctuary-1-1-person-cedar-full-spectrum-sauna/](http://infraredsauna.com/sanctuary-1-1-person-cedar-full-spectrum-sauna/)

It's a big purchase, but it seems like the ClearLights are the best choice.

Does anyone have any criticisms or comments on infrared saunas or on this model or brand specifically? Thank you!

Also, I'm planning on getting a magnetico as well, looks like they have a 6 month payment plan. However, I have been trolling craigslist as well, and will ask around to see if anyone else is selling theirs.

### Supensaa — 2017-05-21T03:50:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brandons-journal.18596/post-214359>

I purchased a full spectrum clearlight sauna, and am happy with it! I haven't used it with niacin yet, but plan on doing so. Starting to feel my extremities warm up during different times of the day rather intensely, but pleasantly (only about 5% of the time or less will my extremities be cold as they were a long time ago, I feel like the sauana has helped with that a bunch).

I also got a magnetic 20gauss a few days ago, only slept on it twice. The one thing I can notice is that I have been falling asleep faster, and I don't remember my dreams as well as I did before, it's just a deep sleep, and I can only vaguely remember my dreams, though usually not at all.

Overall my ability to perform has increased, I am planning on doing a niacin protocol with the sauna. I continue to eat mixed veggies, with coconut oil 1-2 a day, with sardines every other day.

Can't wait to try out the Quantlet. I've been blue blocking for almost a year now, and am also considering looking for a pair that is more comfortable! Recently I have started to use double sided tape with foam padding (from those foam pain brushes you can get at the dollar store) in order to pad the kind of pointy corners of the UVEX blue blockers (the scientific goggle type, the other version, the skypers, never really fit me too well).

Things to do in the near future:

\-Water filter on shower head perhaps? (I take purely cold showers now)
\-Get DNA sequenced
\-Niacin protocol w/sauna
\-Heart rate variability training - been considering this
\-Industrial ice maker + feed tub to do CT in
\-Gain a better understanding of SUPPLEMENTS, what forms to of what supplements to use, the benefits of super dosing certain types of supplements, etc. I'm pretty ignorant with supplements. I take lugols iodine with food, along with pink Himalayan salt. What spurred my interest was the NIACIN protocol. Vitamin B is very low in those who have seborheic dermatitis, this is why it is commonly developed in alcholics, as alcohol depletes vitamin B. So I've been wondering about what would happen if I begin to titrate up dosages of vitamin B along with sauna usage. Similarly, what other protocols exist? I'm really just looking at the cost/benefit of it. Seems like it's worth a shot.

Can't believe that it will be a year this July since I've joined the forums and started this journey.

At one point I used to have rashes all of my face in 2015, due to intense stress, and other factors I'm sure. I haven't had those symptoms to that degree in quite some time now. There are occasional small recurrences once every 3-4 weeks as I adopt new protocols while keeping my foundations strong (not eating before bed, blocking light sources at night, healthy fats, fasting, etc.) But overall I can feel a constant improvement.

As I am healthier, I feel that the same emotional feelings from before this health downfall have resurfaced. I am reminded of how when we heal many have said that they feel like they are going backwards in time in terms of their pathology and emotions.

Because of this I would really like to start taking a Sympathetic nervous system approach, looking at neurofeedback perhaps, heart rate variability, and I've READ about someone speaking of the potential benefits of psychedelics, and of course that you can legally take these substances under supervision in certain countries of course....

I'm looking for more implementations, only now am I starting to feel some of these chronic things being lifted.

I really want to learn how to reset my nervous system and put it into a relaxed state.

Also, seeing as how the skin rashes I used to get were supposed to be fungal in nature from what I've read (seborrheic dermatitis, otherwise known as WALL STREET dermatitis due to wall streeters getting cases of this rash when the great depression occurred! Talk about stress!), I'm also looking for implementations to completely rid myself of that too. Niacin + sauna seem to be a good bet, of course all the other implementations I'm performing being synergistic, too.

Well, that's about it for now! I'll most likely post in a month or so, or perhaps a few months after I've completed the niacin protocol, and have gained more anecdote from the use of the Magnetico.

Any new implementations or things to look into are very welcome, I am super interested in the benefits of certain supplements/compounds found in food. Maybe I should start spending some of my grocery money at the local farmer's market for vegetables as well. I'm also familiar with NAC, glutathione, and Ubiquinol + PQQ.

I just want to know what I can take to get a benefit, and WHEN it is a diminishing return is all. And of course, what form to take it in. I know that Dr. Kruse has said that biologic package of nutrients are usually the best, but I still don't know if that applies to everything or only some things, etc. Another example is that of intravenous nutrients, even seeing interviews by Asprey interviewing doctors who do intravenous vitamin C. Asprey also has mentioned OZONE therapy before, and has stated that it has helped him much. That's another thing I'm wondering about as I don't know much about it.

Anyways, I'm off for now. Will post again in a few months. Thanks everyone. AND - any suggestions?

There is many goals.
1\. have plenty of cortisol to be able to maintain Circadian Cycle, have good sleep and good day
2.maintain menstruation cycle
3.have significant amount of progesterone to avoid estrogen deficiency and promote good sleep and hydration

If you have prometrium and looking for a ways to use it,
one way is to use
Cycling Progesterone Therapy
as described Here:
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/page-56#post-204266](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/page-56#post-204266)

basically it is
300mg/day prometrium taken at bed time on days 14-27 of cycle.
\---------------------
What you can expect from that:

1\. on days 14-27 you should have plenty of progesterone and cortisol
2\. on days 14-27 you should sleep good and have good cortisol and strong day

what you may be missing
1\. on day 1-14 low progesterone, low cortisol, problems with using thyroid hormones, low body temperature, problems with sleeping

Day 4 of cycle- progesterone <0.5 that is low, desired is 1.5ng/ml
You will need to address progesterone on days 1-14
prometrium is usually in 100mg or 200mg gelcaps
100mg cap may be too much for days 1-14
may interfere with ability to start blood flow

you will need a source of progesterone that will give you
progesterone=1.5ng/ml on days 1-14

progesterone or pregnenolone creams or caps

# Thread: Photoelectric life = bio-electric mechanisms in an Electric universe
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/photoelectric-life-bio-electric-mechanisms-in-an-electric-universe.18602/>
**Subforum:** Mitochondrial Rx
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2016-10-16T16:29:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/photoelectric-life-bio-electric-mechanisms-in-an-electric-universe.18602/post-202907>

We cannot live only for ourselves. A thousand fibers connect us with nature in ways we do not perceive. This leads to collateral damage and collateral benefits as this video shows from 21:00 on. Everything is quantum connected. The essence of the quantum Zeno effect is that invisible threads are the strongest ties to nature's innovation. Deep down, nature is inherently peaceful, calm, and beautiful. So are we. The universe as a whole is perfect. The chaos, we call observable life, is on the surfaces of things. What have I learned about the universe when I decided to first question the dogma I was taught in biology? All truth is quantum. It is beyond the concepts of space and time, we think we know. Why do I believe this? Relativity of all things is axiomatic, but light is not relative. It has a speed limit. Light only stamped this idea in my mind once I understood why light had to be different for all living things.

### John Schumacher — 2022-03-09T21:14:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/photoelectric-life-bio-electric-mechanisms-in-an-electric-universe.18602/post-310868>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=202907)
>
> We cannot live only for ourselves. A thousand fibers connect us with nature in ways we do not perceive. This leads to collateral damage and collateral benefits as this video shows from 21:00 on. Everything is quantum connected. The essence of the quantum zeno effect is that invisible threads are the strongest ties to nature's innovation. Deep down, nature is inherently peaceful, calm and beautiful. So are we. The universe as a whole is perfect. The chaos, we call observable life, is on the surfaces of things. What have I learned about the universe when I decided to first question the dogma I was taught in biology? All truth is quantum. It is beyond the concepts space and time, we think we know. Why do I believe this? Relativity of all things but light only stamped this idea in my mind once I understood why light had to be different for all living things.

A comment on [March 9, 2022](https://optimalklubs.com/quantum-engineering-5-what-is-redox-why-it-matters/) - [QUANTUM ENGINEERING #5: WHAT IS REDOX & WHY IT MATTERS](https://optimalklubs.com/quantum-engineering-5-what-is-redox-why-it-matters/) - Blog

Dr. [@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/),

A thousand years on the planet… what could you discover and develop?

I believe we must realize our inter-dependence with a thousand others. Like a [neuron-network](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/is-health-a-supplementation-of-multiple-interventions-or-is-it-wrapped-in-our-human-environment.25380/page-2#post-295973), we must place objectivity as a weight in our collective [neuron-judgement](https://towardsdatascience.com/whats-the-role-of-weights-and-bias-in-a-neural-network-4cf7e9888a0f) without rejecting the individual decisions along the way. It may be just a process of [reevaluating age-old ideas](https://www.scirp.org/journal/paperinformation.aspx?paperid=103636) _(like [relativity](https://beyondmainstream.org/scientist/stephen-crothers/) - [https://vixra.org/pdf/1212.0060v1.pdf](https://vixra.org/pdf/1212.0060v1.pdf))._ Placing these believes in the past may seem like [heresy](https://www.gsjournal.net/Science-Journals/Research%20Papers-Relativity%20Theory/Download/4109) while moving towards what Nature illumines. Is it the difference between: the forest from the trees; chaos from statistical identifiability, or is it our next emerging paradigm of an expanding scientific ratiocination? When examining _a whole of the data-set, providing observational behaviors to the movement through the data-set, we may identify, develop, postulate a “new” model._ My hope is we could then ask better questions like: What is outside our current data-set which influences our understanding of what we presently “can’t see”, however, [it manipulates the data-set we observe](https://aip.scitation.org/doi/10.1063/1.5144449). Speculation and prediction of theoretical outcomes could again guild our “next-steps.”

Looking Forward...
John

It is my hope that the we, who pay upfront, pay-attention, so we may "evolve" our distributed thinking towards better a model(s) for what "human health looks like."

### Jack Kruse — 2016-10-16T21:49:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-light-increases-your-bp-and-is-deadly.18603/post-202925>

TODAY'S FACTOID: The last post was on sunlight and D3. So is this one. A recent large human study was initiated in 2010 whose purpose was to assess whether targeting a reduction of systolic blood pressure below 120 mmHg was superior to the current standard of reducing it below 140 mmHg. The study is called the SPRINT study. In 2015 the NEJ published the data. the trial was stopped 3.26 years BEFORE it was completed because people with BP's below 120 mmHg were dying 26% LESS FREQUENTLY than those with BP's above 140mmHg. They quantum take home? Sunlight lowers your BP by activating NO to vasodialate your surface vessels to shunt 40-60% of your blood to be irradiated by sunlight. This means MORE SUN, not less SUN, is the best medical advice for limiting illness. Heart disease is the number one kilelr world wide of humans. This does not square with what you hear from dermatologists and ophthalmologists does it? Who do you think is correct? Nature or the "experts" advice who tell us to avoid sunlight and stay indoors under your "bulletproof man made lights your supplement experts tell you to use in the AM?" The best thing about the study was that 43% of subjects had a LOWER relative risk of cardiovscular death than those whose BP was lowered aggressively with medications. This shows the world, sunlight is the most powerful drug most humans refuse to use because of bad advice they take from their "so called experts."

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-light-increases-your-bp-and-is-deadly.18603/post-202928>

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-light-increases-your-bp-and-is-deadly.18603/post-209346>

So should we turn down the computer screen to 2700 K all the time, not just in the evening?

### Jack Kruse — 2017-02-26T22:53:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-light-increases-your-bp-and-is-deadly.18603/post-209350>

Yes.....the color temp should be below 2700K and the CRI of the light higher than 95. This improves that you have more light in the red range.

### brent-next — 2017-04-10T09:54:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-light-increases-your-bp-and-is-deadly.18603/post-212096>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=202915)
>
> Blue light increases your BP and is deadly

Respectfully, please change the theme of your web site to dramatically reduce the amount of blue on your site. You can't control the world, but you can control the amount of blue on your web site.

Please consider installing a new theme with a LOT less blue.

Yes, programs like f.lux can help, but they are difficult to use and don't work all the time.

### Mountain Man — 2017-04-10T18:17:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-light-increases-your-bp-and-is-deadly.18603/post-212111>

> [brent-next said:](/goto/post?id=212096)
>
> Respectfully, please change the theme of your web site to dramatically reduce the amount of blue on your site. You can't control the world, but you can control the amount of blue on your web site.
>
> Please consider installing a new theme with a LOT less blue.
>
> Yes, programs like f.lux can help, but they are difficult to use and don't work all the time.

If you measure the light spectrum output of an entire computer screen it does not change that much when the color blue is displayed. Most of the blue is from the LED backlighting not from the LCD colors. So changing this website to have less blue would not make any significant difference. You can purchase a special monitor that has the ability to change the LED backlighting with a blue filter. Mine is made by View Sonic and I can adjust the blue LED output to decrease it by as much as 86%. It is interesting that decreasing the blue backlighting does not make the picture look as much different as F.Lux does even though it decreases blue output a lot more. Most of the blue is hidden in the white, gray and other colors and trying to change blue output by changing all those with the LCD is less effective and more noticeable than decreasing the LED blue backlight output. Of course the other option is just wear blue blocking glasses.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eating-after-ct.18610/post-203031>

### Jack Kruse — 2016-10-18T03:24:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oct-is-always-pink-because-of-breast-cancer-awareness-is-this-wise.18613/post-203004>

Making you aware of an illness does nothing to solve it's riddle. This is a coy tact of a paradigm using it to increase utilization by over diagnosing cases. If you buy anything "pink" for breast cancer awareness month in October it just tells me you are not part of my thinking tribe and would rather follow a trend of lemmings off a cliff. If you think a mammogram is helpful, you're also not part of an "evidence based thinking" tribe. Why? Read the link. Mammography uses radiation to detect cancer and if it was sensitive and specific to do its job we should have evidence that it works? Do we have this evidence or do we just have a belief that it helps? [http://www.vox.com/science-and-heal...4/mammography-breast-cancer-research-guidance](http://www.vox.com/science-and-health/2016/10/13/13269164/mammography-breast-cancer-research-guidance) In my house all the female breasts I care about and love gets screened with manual exams and the use of thermography which is safer than using ionizing radiation to screen for cancer. Awareness does not solve diseases, it creates opportunity to wallet biopsy unripe minds. Awareness, alone doesn't enlist change. This is why I stopped joining groups for support of any diseases. All they do is institutionalize you and wallet biopsy you to death with promises of funding for research but they never do. Why? Because without their harvesting of your wallet CONSISTENTLY they would never be allowed to exist. You all have known this; you have to understand why you must change your environment. Then wellness can visit you. Just reconnect with nature and not any awareness group.

I believe your world view has to be structured in principle on some specific doctrine which shapes how you function and view humanity. Embracing QED is akin to wearing steel-toed boots in a ballet-slipper world today. This is not a disadvantage but a deep help. It stops you from being a lemming. You begin to think autonomously. Understanding that life is bigger than your set of challenges will help you overcome obstacles that will come your way from time to time. It's impossible to increase the scope of our worldview when all that we are focused on is ourselves. When 'MY GOALS' become 'OUR GOALS', success follows. Whatever a person's ultimate concern may be, it will have an enormous influence on everything else the person does or believes.

This is why I don't try communicate with folks on their level, I try my best to connect with them on my level. When someone tells me my message is "cryptic" they have alerted me to their own myopic way of thinking tied to how they were told to think by an entrenched paradigm. I pay deep attention to that kind of thinking and I destroy it. I throw them rope and see how the react and then I decide if they are part of the solution or problem and react appropriately. I want to elevate understanding and not sink to a level where a superficial connection is made on social media. I want people who are all in. Connection is empowerment. There is a very real difference between making "contact" and making a “connection” with someone else. Contact may lead to a one-time transaction in an awareness group, whereas, connection always leads to a life-time transformation. When you connect - which "investment" holds potential for the greater return with people? Modern humans make contact through social media. That is not my game. Reality is that humans contain invisible threads to nature and they bind us most strongly to our life. Today, technology allows us to contact many people together, but we are all dying of loneliness, in spite of these contacts. Reconnection to our humanness is the key to survival. Disconnection is the key to destruction. The interesting thing - the same key opens both doors.

### kyrakitty — 2016-10-19T20:48:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oct-is-always-pink-because-of-breast-cancer-awareness-is-this-wise.18613/post-203062>

Regarding this:

"This is why I don't try communicate with folks on their level, I try my best to connect with them on my level. When someone tells me my message is "cryptic" they have alerted me to their own myopic way of thinking tied to how they were told to think by an entrenched paradigm. I pay deep attention to that kind of thinking and I destroy it. I throw them rope and see how the react and then I decide if they are part of the solution or problem and react appropriately. I want to elevate understanding and not sink to a level where a superficial connection is made on social media. I want people who are all in. Connection is empowerment. There is a very real difference between making "contact" and making a “connection” with someone else. "​

I agree with this and I use a similar kind of screening process. It is difficult to be a leader in a field that is being discovered and shared in real time with so many people wanting to shoot you down and waiting for any opportunity to shoot you down. I am sure that plays a small role in the need for this screening process. Especially when people don't think that you are human and have a heart or feelings which is simply absurd.

I will add also that I am ill and it is hard for me to read things and comprehend them and then apply the information practically. It is hard for me to follow conversations sometimes. Sometimes I can't connect with you on your level because I am simply not smart enough yet to connect the dots because you are much healthier than I am.

So if I am unable to physically/mentally connect with you on your level then I would be labeled as part of the problem? or would I be given the benefit of the doubt that I am temporarily ill and doing everything I can to fix my "dopamine level" and repair my effed up mitochondria?

There are times when direct questions are asked, if a detailed context is provided, a suggestion that doesn't always require reading more blogs and posts is really a gift to help move forward faster to being at your level. Of course the links to other posts and blogs are always appreciated and I always look at them but the reason it is sometimes frustrating is that more often than not the blogs and posts end up confusing me even more than I was before I asked the question.

I am really struggling to stay alive, literally, I am alone with no one but myself to fix serious health issues. I struggle to keep up but I am giving the best that I can to contribute in a positive way. I have applied a lot of what I have learned here. When I first came here on a scale of 1-10... 1 been deathly ill and 10 being perfect health... my health was between a 1 - 2. Now two years later it is between 5-6 and occasionally it drops down to a 4 and that totally stresses me out.

Many treat people who are ill/slow and old like a useless waste of time we are not. And I am not saying that is what you are doing but I have been treated this way the last 6 years since I can't keep up with most people around me. My body looks young, very fit/lean and healthy but I am not yet it is superficial. Nearly everyone I meet dismisses me and bullies me when I can't keep up with them. They just see my physical body and base all their assumptions on one superficial thing like how I look and ignore what I tell them about the chronic issues I experience. Yes I do get it when you say:

"This is why I don't try communicate with folks on their level, I try my best to connect with them on my level. When someone tells me my message is "cryptic" they have alerted me to their own myopic way of thinking tied to how they were told to think by an entrenched paradigm. I pay deep attention to that kind of thinking and I destroy it. I throw them rope and see how the react and then I decide if they are part of the solution or problem and react appropriately. I want to elevate understanding and not sink to a level where a superficial connection is made on social media. I want people who are all in. Connection is empowerment. There is a very real difference between making "contact" and making a “connection” with someone else. "
​

I have to use this exact thinking above with people in my life who can't understand that I am trying to biohack my health so I can be well again. Connecting with people is an investment. Like you I wont go into great details about my health issues & solutions I'm exploring with people if they react in ways that I can see they are going to give me problems, waste huge amounts of my time, and not help with solutions. I don't invest in them because who has time to waste? So, when I read the above, which I have read several times now in a few other posts, I believe I understand where you are coming from and it makes sense.

The virtue of this formula is that it provides the precise amount of the long-acting MK-7 form of vitamin K2 that recent human studies have shown provides optimal K2 levels over a 24-hour period.
The MK-4 is included to provide the rapid increase in vitamin K blood levels that may account for its beneficial effects in certain studies.
Vitamin K2 have been extensively researched and the findings reveal vastly improved effects compared to K1.

### JKLearner123 — 2016-10-21T15:44:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/natto.18615/post-203144>

Natto is great for vitamin K2. I see you have listed the product's complete ingredients list. Please note that these ingredients fall in to two categories - the beans, and the sauce. The beans simply contain - Fermented Soy Beans: **Soy** Bean, Bacillus Subtilis var. The sauce contains a long list of things, some of which don't look too healthy. Please also note that \*none\* of the vitamin K2 is found in these sauce(s). It's only found in the fermented soybeans. I would discard the little sachets of sauce, and simply eat the beans only. I occasionally buy natto, and occasionally make natto at home, and I've found the store bought stuff to be of good quality. (It's nice and stringy; the Japanese call this "neba-neba".) You can freeze it, otherwise it will start to go mouldy if kept in the fridge for about 2.5..+ weeks. I like natto mixed in with homemade sauerkraut, homemade mustard (store bought should be just as good), and an avocado!

### Mikhail — 2016-10-19T16:58:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/from-russia-with-love.18618/post-203054>

Hi, everyone.

Glad I'm finally here. You know, I seriously need to get better now. For the past two years I was trying to deal with all this by myself, but it just doesn't work out. I'm 27 years old, 5' 8" and 52 kilos. Major digestive issues, low energy, weakness, bad sleep, degrading body composition, non-existent motivation, depression and on and on. Not even sure what exactly the deal is. I'm a big mess.

Hopefully I will find support and understanding here. So, how do I best start?

### Jack Kruse — 2016-10-19T19:16:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/from-russia-with-love.18618/post-203057>

Tell us about your environment, you mother and grandmother's history and what type of technology and light environment you have to endure. I'd recommend you start a journal in the Optimal Journal section.

Welcome

### JanSz — 2016-10-20T01:11:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/from-russia-with-love.18618/post-203070>

> [Mikhail said:](/goto/post?id=203054)
>
> Hi, everyone.
>
> Glad I'm finally here. You know, I seriously need to get better now. For the past two years I was trying to deal with all this by myself, but it just doesn't work out. I'm 27 years old, 5' 8" and 52 kilos. Major digestive issues, low energy, weakness, bad sleep, degrading body composition, non-existent motivation, depression and on and on. Not even sure what exactly the deal is. I'm a big mess.
>
> Hopefully I will find support and understanding here. So, how do I best start?

Hi Mikhail;

Starting with love is a very good first step.
Raise your chin when walking, look ahead not down.
Think good thoughts.

Couple items to work on.
Actually for starters only two.
Light and water.
Water is simple and short,
drink only the best spring water you can find.
Most of your daily water drink cold and not boiled.
Newer drink artificial sodas or any other concoctions.
Coffee and some teas may be exception, but newer add any type of sugar or sweetener.

Light.
We are creatures of light.
Without light we die.
Same as a grass, cover the grass with something so it will not see sunlight, and it will die.
Light is connected with everything, your sleep, weakness, depression, lack of motivation.
Light mostly falls on the ground.
You should spend lots of time being connected to ground, preferably wet.
Lots of skin should be exposed to light, that includes eyes.
Minimal clothes no glasses for maximum possible time (while exposed to sunlight).
Thinking more general, we are talking about
light and lack of light
night and day
both must come at the right time
every time, every day, at the same time day starts and latter night begins
say, if you work night shift, forget about ever getting better
if you do not see (enough) of natural sunlight, you cannot get better

when you say body composition, that sound like a talk of a gymnast or body builder
some of them look good and do great things
but they have one common problem
they spend lots of time indoor, in a gym, ballroom or office
they are missing sunlight
artificial light
is not the same as sunlight
artificial light hurts us
it comes in wrong time and have frequencies that our bodies do not recognize
it took millions of years for our bodies to develop
we cannot change in few years, it would take many many generations to change our bodies,
assuming that it can be done
you are already born, you have your body made expecting certain environment
it is up to you to deliver that environment that your body was build for

All this time it was about proper
Circadian Rhythm.
{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

\-------
There is at least one additional highly important item related to sunlight.
Many people these days have very low vit D.

Supplemental vit d is of questionable help.
May need special UV (ultraviolet) lamp to benefit.
Actually there is two types of beneficial UV and two vitamins that we need.
UVa- makes vitamin A
UVb-makes vitamin D
Assuming that we get exposed to this UVa and UVb lights,
to actually make those vitamins we need food
garlic, onions, MSM to provide sulfur so we can make vid D(sulfate)
and
carrots and peppers to make vit A.

\-----------------------------------------
Some places on earth are missing minerals.
Common missing minerals are
selenium
and
iodine and (potassium iodide)
It is good idea to supplement them
they are need to produce thyroid hormones

You may get locally something similar to this:

\-------------------------------------------------------------
1/day-->Super Selenium Complex, 200 mcg 100 capsules (lef.org)
[https://www.amazon.com/Life-Extension-Selenium-Complex-Capsules/dp/B009EA6FZG?psc=1&SubscriptionId=AKIAILSHYYTFIVPWUY6Q&tag=duckduckgo-ffcm-20&linkCode=xm2&camp=2025&creative=165953&creativeASIN=B009EA6FZG](https://www.amazon.com/Life-Extension-Selenium-Complex-Capsules/dp/B009EA6FZG%3Fpsc%3D1%26SubscriptionId%3DAKIAILSHYYTFIVPWUY6Q%26tag%3Dduckduckgo-ffcm-20%26linkCode%3Dxm2%26camp%3D2025%26creative%3D165953%26creativeASIN%3DB009EA6FZG)
\----------------------------------------------------------------
1 dropperfull/day=1mL/day=1cc/day
Lugol's Iodine Family Pack -- 6 (2 fl. oz.) bottles
[http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B00E3B49R2?psc=1&redirect=true&ref\_=oh\_aui\_detailpage\_o04\_s00](http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B00E3B49R2?psc=1&redirect=true&ref_=oh_aui_detailpage_o04_s00)

..........
do not use iodine alone
use Lugol's
it is combination of Iodine + potasium iodide

iodide is halide
it is lots about halides ( good and bad)
the good=iodide
the bad=bromide, fluoride, chloride
watch out for items that have bad halides
flour, spa water have bromide
toothpaste have fluoride
tap water, pool water have chloride

you need excess of iodide to displace undesirable halides.

[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Halide](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Halide)

less energy

### JanSz — 2016-10-20T01:43:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/from-russia-with-love.18618/post-203071>

Think of animal in the cage in zoo that you have to take care of, to keep it healthy.
More specifically, think of lizard.
They need vit D like people do.

Artificial day = 24hr
day-- 14hrs
night --10hrs
\-----
sleep time-- 8hrs
down time before bed time-- 2hrs
\------
day time you want 10hrs of very bright light and 4hrs less light
\------
Our bodies (and animal bodies)
have two hormones
melatonin--night hormone-- produced by pituitary
cortisol-- day hormone -- produced by adrenals (on top of kidneys)

when we see blue light of frequency 460-480nm
that light goes to pineal gland
and that stop production of melatonin

when melatonin level drops, cortisol raise
see chart in previous post
\--------------------

Conclusion,
if you see that blue light in wrong time, you cannot get healthy
Make sure that you do not use melatonin (or cortisol) as supplements.
Watch what your doctor prescribes, they love to give you script for both.

\-------------------
Blue light in the wrong time hurts us.
We can tolerate blue light in day time (from wakeup till about 2 better 4 hours before bed time.
TV, computer screen, cellular phone screens emit blue light.
Do not use them from 2-4 hrs before bed time till wakeup.
It is ok to use red light to see around.

\--------------------
For animal in cage in ZOO
or for you in a small room
get 2 reptile lights
and one red light fixed and one hand held red light.

Assume sleeping from 10PM - 6AM (8hrs)
wakeup 6AM look for 2-3 minutes at the sun or one of you reptile lights
for 10hrs use two reptile lamps
from 4PM till 6PM use only one reptile lamp
finish last meal 4hrs before bed time 6PM, turn off reptile lamp
6PM start red light
10PM go to bed, switch off red light
use hand held red light on the way to bathroom.

Sleep in completely dark room.
If you cannot sleep, just lay down (in that completely dark room.
Preferably without any alarm clocks, get off the bed at 6AM,
regardless if you sleept enough or still would like to sleep.

////

### Jack Kruse — 2016-10-20T15:09:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epi-paleo-religion.18623/post-203096>

People just dont get the angles of science.......it is in these shadows where the secrets of nature hide.

Society and health sciences have become so synthetic and artificial that the truth now offends people. The social construction of reality within the scientific group which determines, not only what counts as a "scientific" problem or solution, but also what is accepted by the scientific community as a "fact." In a fake world, this presents a huge problem for patients of modern medicine.

**Each branch of science works within its own particular paradigm. What I am going after assaults many beliefs across many scientific theories. No paradigm should be safe under the inquiry of someone looking for how nature might be at work below our current beliefs and precepts of truth.**

**Science** investigates the natural world, while **religion** deals with the spiritual and supernatural — hence, the two can be complementary. Many **religious** organizations have issued statements declaring that there need not be any conflict between **religious** faith and the **scientific**perspective on evolution. This is where they fit.

The irreconcilable points between the two are :

The important difference between science and religion is that religion comes with ABSOLUTE statements, that neither can be proved or disproved, and science evolves from relative truths and statements, that can be testified and proven false (which means: science has to develop, in order to replace (partly) untrue theories, and replace them with better ones). Science does not claim it has absolute knowledge on anything. Religion claims it has. All scientific theories are in principle disprovable, and in the end all theories will be disproven (at least it can be shown there is a limiiting case in which the theory does not work). Religion can in principle not be disproven. Which does not contribute either to it's proof. It is also unprovable. if something is neither provable nor disprovable, then it is useless. It can only have value to people who prefer to be ignorant, and don't want to get into complicated knowledge, and prefer to believe in something that is disprovable. Science is for people that realize that in order to aquire knowledge, some work (sometimes a LOT) has to be done! And even despite you put in a LOT oif work, someone else my disproof all (or part) of your work! That is : you have to try even harder! Religion is for people who claim to know EVERYTHING ABSOLUTELY ("God created the world", for instance ) without having done any work to get to that opinion, and for which nobody can give any disproof. So it is a very safe position. You don't have to do WORK for entitling yourself an opinion on matters that seem important, and nobovy can force you to do some work for finding a better opinion, cause there lacks the ability to disproof you.

Nothing on this site is absolute and ALL of the theories are testable and falsifiable making them scientific. Prior to Cambrian explosion there is no DHA and not enough oxygen and likely not enough UV light from the sun to act as the photocatalysis in the sea or air so the Epi-paleo Rx could not manifest. After it, it became possible. That is how science works........and not how religion works. Your Dad generalizes his beliefs to deride something he clearly lacks knowledge of because of his cognitive dissonace.

Many people when they first come here feel a bit lost because everything in life appears to be a contradiction? In QED all possibilities exist, therefore there is no contradiction. After you begin to realize the implications of QED and probability built in by Heisenberg Uncertainity principle you begin to understand why these things exist. When there is a lapse in understanding of the implication of the QED mechanisms at play, the unripe mind will default to ad hominem attacks. Understand them and contradictions vanish.

**Epistemology and philosophy is just man's way of connecting the quantum world to something he cannot quite explain well so he use words to convey this information in a way he and only he can understand. That is human reality not nature's only way of coding data. The epistemology of nature is based upon waveforms.**

### Jack Kruse — 2016-10-20T15:09:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epi-paleo-religion.18623/post-203097>

You do not prove anything to anyone. Everyone's version of the "truth" is fluid, at best a misnomer. Most people do not really want others to have freedom of speech, they just want others to be given the freedom to say want they want to hear. Your Dad seems to be telegraphing that to you. You only need concern yourself what you find by doing your sceintific due diligence. Bio-hack aspects of nature you learn and see for yourself what works in your enviornment and what does not. We forget that don't get harmony when everybody sings the same note. Only notes that are different can harmonize properly as birds and music show us. The same is true with people.

# Thread: My Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal.18631/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 165
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Michalis — 2016-10-21T17:32:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal.18631/post-203156>

Hi All,

I have been a lurker in the forums and listening to Jack's podcasts over and over for more than a year now... Some things really sunk in like the 3 legged stool for example, but others need a lot of work to be understood.

My current environment:

I work in an emf hell hole next to London Bridge station. Recently i moved to a flat and i am surrounded by wifi and there is an antenna mast around 300 meters away from my flat. No visual contact though. I don't use a smartphone and wifi is permanently disabled on my router and laptop. No smart meters in the flat (I refused the "free" upgrade).

Really difficult to move away at the moment , i need to stay to London and keep working so i am exploring options to mitigate the effects of my environment/work until i get a gig somewhere with less population density.

Family:

Mother severely sick : 73 y.o now, Chronic pain, elevated blood sugar , heart disease, attrial fibrilation - tricuspid valve replaced. Really low weight - long story. Nobody seems to want to dig deep to find out why this is happening. Doctors happy with managing the situation only.

My mothers mum was healthy until she developed goiter? - enlarged thyroid. Was operated on and died after the op from bleeding during that night.

Grandma's mum died from old age but severely arthritic.

I had my second knee arthroscopy 2 months ago and i am still recovering from it. The first time was due to a torn meniscus. Arthritis has been developing since so i had a stem cell and micro fracture operation. Hopefully that will work but i am thinking that my environment might be sabotaging the whole thing. If someone has any insight on this please comment.

I cannot work out at the moment due to my operation so i need o go back to the gym gradually. No squat or deadlift for at least 9 moths to a year. The fact that my gym is a blue light "alien" world makes me having second thoughts about going back now. I am thinking of attempting a bodyweight training in the park.

So last week i came back from holidays. I spent 2 weeks in Crete, where i originally come from and i was at the beach every day for a few hours swimming and absorbing the sun. I found out that there are couple of beaches with river estuaries which are really cold so i was going there very often and combining my swimming and sunbathing in the same time introducing myself to proper CT that way. On the CT front I have been only taking cold showers since last March. I have also been eating mostly ketogenic or with low carb spells in general. When i was in Crete i was consuming more carbs due to the sun exposure i had and that it is on a lower latitude.

Before i finish my holiday i did a blood test with the results below.

rT3 : 32.1% \[24.0-39.0\]

Testosterone : 509.00 ng/dL \[249.00-836.00\]
Testosterone Free: 8.95 ng/dL \[ 95.80-18.00\]

Blood Sugar : 92 mg/dL \[60-115\]

Urea: 23mg/dL \[10-50\]
Uric Acid : 5.70 mg/dL \[3.40 - 7.00\]
Creatinine : 0.79 mg/dL \[.5 - 1.40\]

HDL : 62 mg/dL \[35-80\]
LDL : 180 mg/dL \[50-150\]
Triglycerides : 65 mg/dL \[36-165\]

Serum glutamic pyruvic transaminase (SGPT) 40 U/L \[5-40\]
aspartate aminotransferase (AST) (SGOT) 30 U/L \[5-45\]
Alkaline phosphatase (ALP) 152 U/L \[60 -279\]
γ-glutamyltransferase : 11 U/L \[10-45\]

I have used a website to calculate my Urea to Creatinine ratio and comes up as 29.1 which is really high.
Can someone comment on my lab values? Is there anything i need to be looking into?

I was feeling great when i arrived back to London. It has been one week here now, and i feel severely demotivated. Might be all this blue light ?? the only thing that creates a bright light in the tunnel is the idea of a new holiday.. The sun and the sea have a massive impact on me.

### Michalis — 2017-02-19T13:47:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal.18631/post-208890>

So i have a family health emergency right now and i have to care for a family member. For the last 3 weeks i haven't had a proper nights sleep. I have been spending the last week in the hospital and taking naps while sitting on the chair. My circadian rhythms are completely screwed up. I took my laptop with me and i use it in the hospital and i can literally feel the wifi as a knife cutting through me. It is an EMF hellhole in the middle of Athens but i dont have a choice, i have to be here. I feel terrible and my knees are killing me. My left knee was operated 8 months (microfracture and stem cells) ago and i feel that the whole ordeal is damaging it.
I am going to be be flying back to another nnEMF hellhole , back home at London in couple of days as my work has been almost done here.
What can i do to mitigate all the damage i have been doing to myself? Will CT be enough ?My laptop is going back to ethernet and my cell phone will be definitely reduced though. I have found and open air pool close to where i live open all year round. Any hacks anyone can suggest? I am a bit lost and the sleep deprivation doesn't help.

### Michalis — 2017-11-27T00:33:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal.18631/post-228911>

Weather report: I haven't written for a while , things are not great. Lately I keep thinking i have to move however i got 2 bad knees now which complicates things

The past year has been extremely stressful mentally and physically for me.My knee situation and my mum's sickness has caused an awful lot of stress. Lately i get chest pain often. The doctor thinks it is my high LDL cholesterol. I think it is the stress and keto.
I have also developed ribcage pain, just on the junction of my ribcage, i looked it up and might be costochondritis.. Possibly i overdid it in the gym or bad posture slouching in front of the computer+bad light. So inflammation of the cartilage... Something is way out of order with the cartilage and connective tissue in my body. My mum has extremely painful degenerative arthritis on her spine so obviously i got her mtDNA. My GP thinks i have indigestion, so go figure.

My left knee arthritis is a lot worst now after my arthroscopy.. I think i chose the wrong doctor. My understanding from our meeting back in early 2016 was that he would put stem cells from my hip into my knee joint. The result was that i woke up post-op with a micro-fractured knee condyle and stem cell augmentation. I only found out after the op, nothing was mentioned beforehand...Initially i though it will just heal quickly but that is not the case. That has caused me extreme distress so far. I keep beating myself for my ignorance and not asking the right questions.. Dopamine issue?

I could at least squat and dead-lift before the arthroscopy , i just had discomfort on my knee. Now i cant do anything. Sometimes i feel i have lost everything.My confidence has tanked..I gave up my musical instrument lessons. I have stopped all exercise now , i have far too many injuries of the connective tissue everywhere, my knee arthroscopy created a domino effect, a massive downward spiral.

But i admit, if i had digested Dr Kruse's work and understood things a little bit more in detail earlier on i should have dismissed my orthopedic doctor on the spot. So people take note, if something doesn't go down well trust your gut feeling. Especially if what they say are too good to be true. When i asked him about arthritis and epigenetics he categorically said it is a genetic issue. He dismissed the environmental factor. It is tough to learn like that. Dopamine again?

The bones on the lateral side of the knee must be quite inflamed even though there is good growth of cartilage 16 months post op. I now realize that even if the cartilage regrows arthritis which is the underlying condition is still there..That needs to be addressed first not the cartilage damage.
Is it the heteroplasmy on the affected areas raising and causing all that mess? Is heteroplasmy working in the body as a whole or it could be topical too? Is it the master clock that went cuckoo because EMF and blue light that is throwing out the rest of the clocks causing the issue? How can i tell? I read somewhere that Dr Kruse knee issue has disappeared from the MRI scan after consistent CT. Need to find that blogpost. I might be able to reverse it if i understand more.

The icing on the cake :

Last month i fell of my bike and i partially ruptured my right knee ACL too. The last thing i really want now is another arthroscopy.

I have been thinking to do a Regenexx stem cell protocol for both knees (please comment if you got any feedback on them) , however it seems extremely expensive. I spoke to them already to explore what options i have and they have explained everything to me in detail. I was told am an average candidate for the procedure. If i decide to commit to this financially i need to make sure that i must maximize my stem cell production and change my environment for at least sometime before and after the procedure. It is a lot of money and i have to work a lot for it.
I am scouring the site for information constantly, it is just takes so long to piece this puzzle together. I am sure i have seen a blog post why we deplete out stem cells for various reasons including exercise.

I have been trying CT on and off lately and the temperatures are currently dropping so that is a good thing. I get into the tube and i can stay for 30-45 mins on a temperature ranging 11 to 16 degrees Celsius . I am low carb/keto too. I have introduced a lot of seafood and seaweed. I rarely eat fowl now.
I dont know if i am leptin sensitive/resistant as i cant say from the mirror. I have lost a lot of weight so there is just lose skin with some fat.

I take lugols 5% in the morning without any issues 6-8 drops. (Loaded with Selenium for sometime, now i eat brzil nuts) I supplement Mg, Vit C, occasionally K+ (remnant from my introduction to keto, i think i dont really need it any more) and i think i will get into curcumin and resveratrol for arthritis however i dont know if resveratrol is a good idea without sunshine. I rarely drink alcohol nowdays and only socially. I used to be a party animal , not any more. I try to drink a lot of water and herbal teas however coffee is my weak spot. I know it is dehydrating but i find it difficult to kick the habit. I have tried twice to go cold turkey but the withdrawal was brutal. I just could not believe it. Need to work on this.

I am thinking to incorporate long term fasting for a while to promote autophagy and mitophagy and then at some point after a few months take a trip to the tropics get a lot of sunshine , swim ,ground and eat local grown carbs to give the spark to my mitochondria to regenerate. My understanding is that this will promote redox, healing and will reduce inflammation. Any fallacies in my thinking?

I have tried fasting and usually i give up on day 3 or 4. It is really hard and people dont help when they dangle donuts in front of my face at work. (which i don't touch)

I ordered a 23andme test to get some more info on my haplotype. I come from the Mediterranean island of Crete and living in London must be a huge mismatch. For years i was living in Edinburgh , Scotland not exactly the definition of optimal but i will find out when the test will be done. I could easily go back to Crete but not on a city on the island. The south of the island is pretty good i believe but needs testing before. Lots of shipping routes, tourism in the summer and military activity. Also given the economic climate it is pretty tough now.

I will get some labs done based on what i read on the blog on the next few days to get an idea how things are going and will post here as well
In the meantime feel free to comment. The road is steep.

### Michalis — 2017-11-30T23:19:49+0200

So today i have been thinking that i have got 2 small patches of Eczema exactly on the lateral sides between the calf and the sin and below my joints, they seem symmetric to each other!! I think this is not by chance and it might have to do the the epigenetic expression of my arthritis/degeneration on both knees.. It has been there for at least before 2012 when my left knee started going south... One is slightly larger on the left side where i got a massive issue and one smaller on the right that i have a lesser issue.. I think this could be an indication of degree of the problem. I might be spotting a pattern for my n=1 or i am just going mad.

### Michalis — 2018-01-03T12:48:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal.18631/post-231468>

At the airport, off to Thailand for a month. Finally booked it as i am feeling i am breaking down in London. 1 month of sunshine. My previous trip there was 3 years ago... I felt really alive the time i spent over there. I hope to get that feeling back under the strong sunshine.
Lets see. Unfortunately i have to go through Bangkok and it is not exciting at all but it will only be 2 days there. Then heading south. Anyone around Phuket reading this ping me a message.

### Michalis — 2018-01-15T06:51:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal.18631/post-232581>

In Thailand absorbing the sun. I got plenty of time so I read Jacks' book couple of times. Reading the blogs and watching the podcasts with him on. Small steps to try understand what is going on with me. In parallel i understand how the medical establismesment is deeply flawed.Having relieved of my savings to pay for my mums spinal osteoporotic fracture and my knee arthritis ordeal is only validating Jack's point along with the others who talk about it like Becker and Marino.I try to follow his advise to embrace my discomfort as an agent of change. This is the lesson I am trying to internalize and believe me it is a really a hard pill to swallow as I feel I am breaking down completely.I wake up I the morning and feel 100 years old with a mind like a flat line.
The sun feel good for me over here but a month won't cut it. I will need a hell lot of sun and CT to see some change. Everything that I read points to that direction so yes the data is there. The most radical change I need to do is move from London and that means I should also find a way to make ends meet doing manual work which I am not afraid of, what I need to make sure is see if I am able to do it with all the aches I got. Jack posted a something the other day on a thread about nihilism that was really inspiring and I keep going back to it for inspiration. It is really great to understand that Einsteins' equation works both ways and that actually everything is possible but it needs consistency and discipline to make like a sphinx and moreover stop listening the others who haven't got the data. Especially those who keep telling me to put my tinfoil hat on. It is a very lonely journey and tough going against the grain however I am grateful about this forum as it us a beacon of knowledge on this journey.

### Michalis — 2018-01-22T05:36:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal.18631/post-233104>

Observations from Thailand.
The European elderly tourists are quite fat and run down if you compare them with the Thai of the same age group. The difference? Thai people live an outdoor life and they constantly are under uv light and grounded.
However: The amount of WiFi and nnemf is vast here too. It is easier to spot the celltowers and the power and telecom cables are really wild over the streets.
I had a conversation with a Canadian teacher who was hosting me and living here for 15 years. She has noticed that obesity has started appearing on the sly.. Not like the west obviously. I have noticed a few obese people but not many. One common theme like everywhere is seeing people clutching their mobile phones constantly.

Personally I am under the sun a lot here. I go to the beach , ground, sleep a lot and early and eat carbs as obviously they grow naturally here. I haven't put any weight on , I might have actually lost weight as I have noticed the fluctuation.

But again it is really striking to see the state of all these elder European in comparison with the Thai. What the indoor blue lit world and lack of natural exposure is doing to us. Sometimes one has to see or experience to believe.

I haven't done any CT here but coupled with strong sunlight must be a match made in heaven.

I did a basic blood test and all came up great except HDL which was slightly low at 53. So more sunlight for me. Even my Bun/creatinine ratio dropped to 13. In London it had reached 29. So something good us happening. My blood glucose was relatively low too at 87.

I am curious to retest but in depth. Possibly before I leave.

my 23 and me is currently getting analysed so I am curious to see what that will show in terms for snp and such.

Generally the sun is doing a lot of good to me. My aches are still there obviously so work in progress.

### Michalis — 2018-02-02T22:35:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal.18631/post-234012>

Back to London. Holiday in the sun-mission accomplished. I loved the sunshine and i got dark really fast. I was seriously jet lagged and i slept a lot the last 2 nights... like 12 hours each. I feel super weird being back and not motivated at all with the Great British weather.

It is really cold here and i feel my cold adaptation has gone down after Thailand... i had a cold shower without problem as soon as i arrived but i feel cold inside the house now! That was not happening before.. I guess i got unused to the cold very fast..

It took me a while to wind down in Thailand but eventually it happened.. I had a lot to think and at some point i had a breakthrough through the clutter in my head.So for at least a week my brain stopped and let go and just absorbed the sun. I don't know if it was my mental state or eventually my dopamine levels rose or both... I guess these two are dancing together.. It was a Jack Kruse holiday really.. Lots sun, grounding reading blogs and podcast watching..

I don't really think that Thailand is optimal though unless you go somewhere with really really low population density.. I think they have got a massive nnemf issue from my observation of cell towers, Wifi Repeaters on the street everywhere, power lines and this crazy mess of telecom lines hanging. And 4G Everywhere! those who have visited SE Asia probably know the kind of cable mess i am talking about. I think it is possible to find places to live over there at least temporary , but it has to be very rural , or in remote islands.

For the first time in my life i got a smartphone in Thailand and used it over there..I wasn't very confident going about with 2 bad knees so i wanted to be able to have a map and info for my surroundings always. All i can say is i find it extremely disruptive as wanted to keep looking at it.. Eventually i was going to the beach and leaving it at the hotel.. Now i am back i have already DE-commissioned it. Enough!

Food-wise there is a lot of amazing seafood there... and really cheap oysters. When spotted i was always having them.. super cheap and already cleaned ,put into a container and it was 50 to 100 Baht for at least 20.

The ugly side of the food is MSG... it is ubiquitous there... I visited supermarkets and it seems like a staple... not good.. Also sugar.. The concept of not using sugar fleets them, which i guess makes sense because of the high quantum yield environment.
Fruit is great though.. Since the sunshine and UV was quite strong i seized the opportunity to sample the fruit and it was just great..And the greatest thing of all, i didn't put any weight on... Which for an x-obese person is great

The craziest thing of all is seeing all these products that they use over there to whiten their skin , for cultural reasons obviously. I made a Thai friend there and jokingly i was called Burmese and Khmer too, as i got really dark (They got a lot of Burmese and Cambodians working outside in construction, so they are exposed to the sun all the time and get really dark) . It is a class issue.. How people twist things...

Bottom line, it was a good holiday, i got a lot of sunshine but i need a hell of a lot more to make a difference.

### Michalis — 2018-02-28T00:46:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal.18631/post-235899>

I have completely fallen off the EpiPaleo wagon the last couple of weeks, negating my last post... I am still wearing my blue blockers in the evening though.
Not much of London was needed to reverse the optimism and the chemical balance in my head.Got a new IT contract and i am back to work. I am not feeling great.

On another note I got my 23andme test results and i think it confirms i have to go south, not worst, just south

My maternal Haplotype is **U1a1** (Paternal _J_\-_L26_)

The U1a1 haplogroup (Found in Caucasus , Middle East, Greece, South Italy, Balkans, West India)

**Methylation Profile**

VDR Bsm rs1544410 CC -/-
**VDR Taq rs731236 AA +/+
MTHFR A1298C rs1801131 GG +/+
MTR A2756G** rs1805087 AG **+/-
MTRR A66G** rs1801394 AG **+/-
MTRR K350A** rs162036 AG **+/-**

**My Detox Profile:**

**CYP1A1 C2453A** rs1799814 GG -/-
**CYP1B1 L432V** rs1056836 GG +/+
**CYP2C19\*17** rs12248560 CT +/-
**GSTP1 I105V** rs1695 AG +/-
**GSTP1 A114V** rs1138272 CT +/-
**NAT2 R197Q** rs1799930 AG +/-

I did a little research on the VDR Taq and it seems i might need a hell of lot more sunshine that from what i was even expecting to get Vit D into my system.. But again i am not sure of the interplay of all these things along with my environment as well.. I will keep researching. If i have to take into account what i have been reading in my Promethease report i will just go mad.. There is so many conflicting genes and information in the genome... It is amazing how the environment can influence this blueprint and tip the scales one way r another..

Anyone with more knowledge on SNPs , please feel free to comment. I would be greatful for any insights.
