Also keta?

### Jack Kruse — 2016-11-05T23:48:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brie.18698/post-203647>

Gouda has the highest content of vitamin K2 than any other cheese with approximately 20 mcg per ounce

### Penny — 2016-11-06T00:45:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brie.18698/post-203650>

And I take it still a nyet on kefir for k2?

### Inger — 2016-11-07T09:41:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brie.18698/post-203680>

I think K2 is heat sensitive, so it is better when the cheese or butter or dairy is raw? I eat raw cheeses and butter once in a while. I get very nice raw cheeses here pretty cheap.... I love Roquefort.. and Comte´is a favorite too.... I have not yet seen a raw Gouda here

### Inger — 2016-11-08T16:14:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brie.18698/post-203716>

I am sorry, I got it wrong
K2 is not much sensible to heat, but it is to light. So keep your cheese in darkness
Swiss Cheeses like Gruyere are good sources too.. as is liver

### Inger — 2016-11-08T16:19:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brie.18698/post-203718>

I found this too, looks like Brie is great too

> In my interview with Dr. Rheamue-Bleue, she identified the cheeses highest in K2 are Gouda and Brie, which contain about 75 mcg per ounce. Hard cheeses are about 30 percent higher in vitamin K2 than soft cheeses. In perusing the nutritional tables myself, I found it interesting that the cheeses highest in vitamin K2 also tend to be the highest in protein and calcium — so the most nutritious overall. Just realize that the values listed for "vitamin K" in common nutritional tables are of limited value because they don't specify what TYPE of vitamin K they're measuring.
>
> As it turns out, scientists have found high levels of MK-7 in one type of cheese: Edam.[11](http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2013/06/17/heart-healthy-cheese.aspx#_edn11) This is wonderful news for those of you who would much rather sit down to a slice of Edam than a bowl of natto! (Natto, a strongly fermented Japanese soybean product, has the highest MK-7 level of any food.)
>
> Earlier, I made my case for selecting raw cheeses from grass-pastured, grass-fed animals. However, cheese contains a bacterially-derived form of K2, so it doesn't matter if the cheese was made from grass-fed milk or not — the bacteria used to culture the cheese is the same. Grass-fed dairy is important for the other reasons I've already discussed — just not specifically for the K2.
>
> To summarize then, if you're going to select cheese with your primary goal being a good source of vitamin K2, the best ones are:
>
>
> -   Gouda
> -   Brie
> -   Edam
> -   Other cheeses with lesser, but significant, levels of K2: Cheddar, Colby, hard [goat cheese](http://recipes.mercola.com/goat-cheese-recipe.aspx), Swiss, and Gruyere.[12](http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2013/06/17/heart-healthy-cheese.aspx#_edn12)

[http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2013/06/17/heart-healthy-cheese.aspx](http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2013/06/17/heart-healthy-cheese.aspx)

### Jenny S — 2016-11-14T11:00:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brie.18698/post-203945>

I believe Natto is good source of k2

### Alper — 2016-11-14T16:47:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brie.18698/post-203956>

> [Jenny S said:](/goto/post?id=203945)

Has a procedure similar to what Dr. Becker describes in the Body Electric involving silver nylon that can regrow cartilage been developed yet?
I'm eating more seafood stock - in order to get more chitosan which should help with cartilage nutrition.
Have purchased a Magentico - I'm assuming this may help with healing of cartilage as well.

Anything else I can do, beyond adhering to the quantum principles discussed here, to hopefully return my defect to optimal?

New Uncle Jack podcast out this AM. Let's just say the tech industry centralized system got punched in the mouth. A lot more details of the POMC story is given out.

You don’t need people’s expert opinions on a fact of nature, says Mother Nature. Understanding how light from the sun is tied energy and information transfers to hormones and cholesterol in our membranes, skin, and eyes to support this idea. The modern paradigm has carefully crafted a meme that the sun is toxic for us and this is ludicrous when you consider we have massive data that the sun reduces all cause mortality.

The skin is a cholesterol laden insulator of fat with lipid raft antenna's deisgned to connect us to the sun. Melanin embedded in the skin is the semiconductive protein that creates electrons and light to work with cholesterol. These melaninated sheets act as a solar panel for the brain to give it more energy from the sun to run the Ferrari engine in our head. This has to be optimized for neurological function. Most modern human disease is linked to a break in this quantum biologic connection.

Eating cholesterol is of zero consequence to mammals. Creating it in the liver is critical in understanding the biophysics of cholesterol non-visual photoreception. The lipid raft's ability to change in mammals occurs by seasonal light variation and collection via the non-visual photoreceptors via perception on the skin, eyes, and gut. That external light determines the reality the mammal faces. When the solar cycles change so do the lipid rafts. This photoelectric change alters biochemistry in mTOR, PPP, glycolysis, the TCA cycle, and POMC cleavage. When the lipid content changes they induce changes in the semiconductive proteins embedded in them. This changes the physiologic ability. This is why the clock mechanism in mammals is linked to light and temperature. Both signals change to the surfaces of mammals.

This change in the skin has massive implications for the circulatory system, arteries, blood, and especially the liver. Most people do not know the deuterium content of blood and the lumen in the gut is also plastic via light and temperature signaling for two reasons. Deuterium has an extra neutron so this heavier atomic mass means more energy is needed to move it. Deuterium has a different magnetic moment than H+ so this means it reacts differently when the electromagnetic signal from the sun varies seasonally. This is what causes mammalian membranes changes on their surfaces and interiors.

Because semiconductive proteins like melanin are embedded in the skin, what type of cholesterol and fatty acids matter to the functioning of VGCCs. Why? Because the lipid rafts are like Morse code for the creation of Vitamin D from the cholesterol system in the skin. This Vitamin D system has to share information with the Vitamin A signal in the opsin system. this is the non visual photoreceptor system. Most people have no idea that cholesterol is a non visual photoereceptor. The lipid rafts alter the functioning of voltage-gated channels that control the photoisomerization step of the conversion of cholesterol to 25 D (OH). This chemical has to go to the kidney and/or liver for final conversion to act at target receptors in this system of the mitochondria. This only happens when cholesterol is sulfated and in its HDL formati because this form of cholesterol has more electrons allowing it to capture the UVB light to complete the first conversion in the skin. At the same time UV-A sunlight increases NO and oxygen deliver to mitochondria to alter their function because sunlight controls the oxidation state of Fe and keeps it in the +3 state. This increases the sulfation of all things in the system and it makes them MORE WATER SOLUABLE. When cholesterol is sulfated it does not need LDL particles to transport it. It uses HDL to do it. ApoB and LpA drops as a result when the system is properly attuned to the solar cycle and they cease to be an issue for the mammal. This mechanism simultaneously thins the blood while lowering calcium flows in the mitochondria. This lowers our BP along with NO actions in the RAS. Lowering calcium and raising NO both act to reduce mitochondrial power. What takes over when all this happens to create H+ and oxygen and electrons to run the system? POMC creates melanin and melanin makes all three things to fill the system with massive energy. This is why NO slows mitochondrial metabolism and lowers BP. Centralized medicine does not understand this wiring diagram in 2023. Their longevity experts are still advocating the use of statins which completely ruin the fidelity of this system. Sulfate platelets and GAGs in the vessel wall are less sticky and there is better laminar flow. This is why we have an epidemic of patients on blood thinners. No one is going outside enough. As a result, clots cause both heart and brain damage. This is why PAD is linked to both diseases. The cholesterol made in this way links to hormone creation in us.

Hormones effectively are light ferries boats and most human hormones are made from choesterol. Understanding how cholesterol operates in sunlight is critical. If your hormone panels is trashed and your LDL is up and blood glucose high you are under a chronic BLUE LIGHT STRESS. That is your problem. Few centralized MD understand why statins make absolutely no sense in this modern world. Hormone panels are a reflection of the cells reaction to their light environment.

Another new podcast out on water. This was part 3 of a series but I think I may have to take a break from doing anymore on this platform. I will say if you listen you will notice the interviewer is seriously married to his biochemical level understanding of food in this podcast and listening through it again really hurt the podcast in my view.

When you view your world exclusively through the lens of science, the scale you see "your world" through is altered and never parallels reality.......leaving 'your prescription' too weak to explain what is really at the source of the paradox.

I dropped some bombs about water in this podcast but I think the interviewer did not ask follow-ups on these things. I hope some of you do.

The modern mind can't fathom the primacy 'of scale' because their scale for understanding biology is not at a nano/QED level yet. This is where the microcosm meets the macrocosm.

Life is a kind of prison for us. We are connected to the universe, but we experience this world disconnected from the whole. It is an optical delusion of our conscious perception allowing us to separate space from time. This prison is unique. We can constrict our life or we can broaden its walls via our perspective. Our perspective is our conscious optical delusion of how we control the flow of light in our brain and emit those frequencies back to others to sense. As you shine your light you become your own social media company for others to share or reject broadening or tightening the walls of your prison.

What is decentralized medicine, and why do you need it?

The extra mile is a vast, decentralized, underpopulated wasteland where the Black Swan mitochondriac goes.

People say they go the extra mile, but almost no one does. Most people think, "Wait, no one else is here. Why am I doing this?"
The Black Swan is rare because their mindset is unique.

That's why the extra mile is such a lonely place for the common man. That's also why the extra mile is a place filled with opportunities. Be early with the sun. Stay late, but don't use manufactured light. Make the extra phone call to Nature. Send the extra email. Do the extra research. Help a person solve a complex problem.

Help a customer unload or unpack a shipment. Don't wait to be asked for help; offer help. Be uncommon. Whenever you do something, think of one extra thing you can do--especially if other people aren't doing that one thing. In this unpopulated wasteland, many will ask you why you persist. Tell them you are using nature's light to sculpt your life by thinking better than before.

Sure, it's hard to think anew and adapt. Being solo in the modern world is not something to fear........it is something to conquer.
But that's what will make you different and unique--and over time, it will make you incredibly successful when you begin to write your next chapter. It will be a book all will want to read when they see the words of your story spill all over the pages...........

### Jack Kruse — 2023-10-14T18:23:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/podcasts-dropbox-collection.18701/post-327256>

I've done hundreds of podcast interviews by this point. I've seen their process and the product with a big sample.

The true decentralized scientific podcasters are ones that want to explore multiple perspectives and challenge themselves, rather than be tied to always reinforcing one theme.

But there's more to this radical idea. True decentralized clinician scientists keep track of their industry, specifically seek out guests that differ from each other, and importantly, they research enough to understand the nuances of how they disagree and want to explore those questions and bring the answers to their audience. But... But there are two false paths here.

The first false path is the path of the middle ground. It's the idea that between two ideas, the answer is necessarily in the middle.

No, sometimes the answer is one or the other. Explore perspectives but don't assume the middle is right. The second false path is of audience capture. They just tell their audience what they know they want to hear. Get the clicks! This is the Rogan, Attia, and Huberman path.

This is the short time preference method; it works in the near term but lacks long-term decentralization respectability. You eventually get written off by the public when your luster fades. The most impressive decentralized interviewers/scientists to me nail all of this?

\-They know their industry well, and purposely seek out competing views.

\-They honestly want to learn and build truth, not just assume the answer is in the middle. They explore and press each idea individually. They go down the rabbit holes and seek the chaos. This is where the truth in nature lies. Dr. Ted did that.

Why was I worried that Cutter 1.0 from polio would show up in mRNA jabs in 2021 when I had the member event at my clinic when I invited Judith Vary Baker to speak to my members? The nuclear envelope is a major barrier for nuclear uptake of plasmids and represents one of the most significant unsolved problems of nonviral gene delivery in centralized science. The literature has previously shown that the nuclear entry of plasmid DNA is sequence-specific, requiring a 366-bp fragment containing the SV40 origin of replication and early promoter. Data from 1999 was reported to show show although fragments throughout this region can support varying degrees of nuclear import in humans, the 72-bp repeats of the SV40 enhancer facilitate maximal transport. Big Pharma read this work just like I did. I knew they had to get this virus in to make this technology functional. This is why in the COVID Revealed documentary movie I went to great lengths to discuss the legal wording of the EUA and why it seemed they would use two jabs to get passed the FDA safety meeting. One would be submitted to the FDA sans the SV-40 and the other would be given to the public that had SV 40 in it. When those people who attended and look back on that July 4th meeting they should realize how much value I delivered to them that day.

A new podcast is out, PART 2, with my VIKING BUDS!

One of the most overlooked health risks today is chronic exposure to blue light. It is the dominant form of nnEMF but not the only one. Abuse of technology screens can lead to diabetes, obesity, depression, suicide, and many other modern-day diseases. Blue light actually looks white to the naked eye. It is emitted from screens on smartphones, tablets, TVs, computer screens, eBook readers, and energy-efficient fluorescent and LED lights.

So, if you work indoors, you are chronically exposed to high levels of blue light. This issue is getting more attention. In May 2016, Apple released Night Shift with its new iOS 9.3 update in an effort to reduce the amount of blue light emitted from iPhones and iPads. In June 2015, the AMA adopted guidance for communities on selecting LED lighting options to minimize potential harmful human and environmental effects. But the effect still needs to be mitigated by most modern humans.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-01-06T06:48:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/podcasts-dropbox-collection.18701/post-329885>

A new podcast with Dr. @alexisjcowan is out tonight.

We talked about decentralized science and medicine. We covered a ton.

The transition to decentralized medicine represents a shift towards a more evidence-based, patient-centric approach. This system values the proof of work concept, rewarding clinicians for their efforts in generating positive patient outcomes rather than their adherence to pharmaceutical company directives in the education curriculum.

This leads to more personalized care and encourages innovation and experimentation in the medical field.

Dr. Alexis Jazmyn did her doctoral research in Josh Rabinowitz's lab. I have brought up Josh's NAD+/NAD precursor paper during many podcasts (which, in many ways, debunked a lot of the claims made around NAD and aging). So, she cut her teeth in metabolism while specifically looking at changes in tissue metabolism that occur in response to different dietary inputs and in response to fasting. She said she honestly felt grateful that her research was in this lab as it's one of the more open-minded and least reductionist groups she had ever encountered in academia.

Josh Rabinowitz slayed much of the marketing nonsense pushed by PhDs like David Sinclair and Lenny Guarente around NAD supplementation. Josh was also Dr. Attia's classmate in med school. Alexis was a pleasure to talk with.

What is the 2024 centralized medicine trend I am to reverse now? When we look at how centralized doctors or PhDs perceive disease-screening tests, we find that they tend to underestimate the potential harms of screening and overestimate the potential benefits. Decentralized clinicians note that a “striking feature” of recent research is how much of it contradicts traditional centralized medical opinion. Decentralized medicine is bringing healthcare back into balance with Nature by elevating the importance of the mtDNA genome and pushing the nuclear genome to the rear.

Consider gorillas/chimps body plans compared to ours. Our cousins have way more strength and power, lack frontal lobes and have 3.8 billion base pairs in their DNA to our 3.2 billion base pairs. Humans also have 23 pairs of chromosomes. Apes have 24. They have more musle hypertrophy and their guts are 30 ft longer than ours, but their DNA is not more complex than us. They do not live as long as we do either. Their longevity is stunted compared to humans.

We took those excess mitochondria from the larger Neanderthal brain and moved them. As neurons degraded so did melanin and dopamine surged on a raltive basis and we got language and art in one fell swoop. As the genome shrunk the integumentary melanin over dermatomes internalized to our CNS/body plan.

Most of the mtDNA were shared in the brain and immune system in humans to explain human abilities, speech and creation. It is also why our gut shortened 30 feet compared to theirs. Our GALT is the result of this quantum engineering of the combination of melanin and water as the body of Interlandi showed up to sculpt the gut via the splanchnic nerves.

This was done at the same time sonic hedgehog, melanin renovation, and mitochondrial redeployment went into other cell lines. This explains the condensation of the Great Apes' 24th chromosome to the human number two chromosome should make colossal sense. This is where the POMC gene lives in humans. Strength is based on different metrics than just hypertrophy. It is based on dopamine and melatonin levels selecting fiber type to make smaller muscles stronger. This defines the muscle changes in our eyes and guts compared to Great Apes. Sonic hedgehog is a member of the hedgehog (Hh) family and was initially recognized as a morphogen protein possessing critical characteristics for neural development during embryogenesis.

Recently, however, Sonic hedge hog has emerged as an essential modulator in adult human neural tissues through different mechanisms such as neurogenesis, anti-oxidation, anti-inflammation, and autophagy. This is a major player in the transition to human. The changes in the immune system from the muscles and gut were transferred to our immune cells, resulting in longevity. Inside each immune cell is a stockpile of POMC. Mitochondria create the light to make melanin do its magic. This new podcast gets into a lot of it in the first ten minutes.

How did this story in the Danny Jones podcast below link JFK, Israel, MOSSAD, and MKULTRA to mRNA jab of 2021? It began with an intelligence failure of the CIA and John Kerry's father. Documents published in Wilson Center from the National Archives now have shed light on a particularly notable intelligence failure: how Washington missed warning signs that the Israelis had a nuclear project underway, but also how the U.S. belatedly realized what the Israelis were doing, and how Eisenhower and his senior advisers reacted to this discovery. Among the new documents are: The June 1959 Israel-Norway secret agreement providing for the sale of Norwegian heavy water to Israel (through the United Kingdom), transmitted by Oslo Embassy political officer Richard Kerry (father of former Secretary of State John Kerry). Reports about information from a then-covert source — University of Michigan nuclear engineering professor Henry Gomberg — who learned that the Israelis had a secret nuclear reactor project that involved experiments with plutonium. A telegram from the U.S. embassy in Tel Aviv reporting on Finance Ministry official Addy Cohen's statement that "we've been misbehaving," and one by an unidentified official close to Prime Minister David Ben-Gurion that the secrecy surrounding Dimona was unjustifiable and that it was "a stupid mistake on the part of Israel." Reports by U.S. Ambassador Ogden Reid on conversations with Ben-Gurion. A State Department message to the embassy in Tel Aviv conveying irritation that the responses of the Israeli government showed a "lack of candor." Messages about a role for the International Atomic Energy Agency in inspecting and safeguarding Dimona. When Eisenhower left office Kennedy assumed this mess but he knew Ben Gurion for over a decade as the picture above shows. Now President Kennedy pressured the government of Prime Minister David Ben-Gurion to prevent a military nuclear program, particularly after stage-managed tours of the Dimona facility for U.S. government scientists in 1961 and 1962 raised suspicions within U.S. intelligence that Israel might be concealing its underlying nuclear aims. This went on during the Cuban Missle crisis and Kennedy was worried about nuclear proliferation post WW2. Kennedy’s long-run objective, documents now show, was to broaden and institutionalize inspections of Dimona by the International Atomic Energy Agency. Of all U.S. leaders in the nuclear age, John F. Kennedy was the nonproliferation president. Nuclear proliferation was his “private nightmare,” as Glenn Seaborg, his Atomic Energy Commission chairman, noted many times in interviews. Seaborg discovered Plutonium for the Industrial military complex leaders. On 30 May 1961, Kennedy met Ben-Gurion in Manhattan to discuss the bilateral relationship and Middle East issues. However, a central (and indeed the first) issue in their meeting was the Israeli nuclear program, about which President Kennedy was most concerned. According to a draft record of their discussion in the National Archives, which has never been cited, but has now been released for the first time by the Wilson Center, Ben-Gurion spoke “rapidly and in a low voice” and “some words were missed.” He emphasized the peaceful, economic development-oriented nature of the Israeli nuclear project. Nevertheless the note taker, Assistant Secretary of State Philips Talbot, believed that he heard Ben-Gurion mention a “pilot” plant to process plutonium for “atomic power” and also say that “there is no intention to develop weapons capacity now.” This information stunned JFK. Ben-Gurion tacitly acknowledged that the Dimona reactor had a military potential. The final U.S. version of the memcon retained the sentence about plutonium but did not include the language about a “pilot” plant and “weapons capacity.” Anyone who understands basic nuclear physics (see my Patreon Thorium blog on BTC) knows the only reason to use plutonium as a fuel is because you can make military grade uranium for nuclear weapons. The differences between the two versions suggest the difficulty of preparing accurate records of meetings. But whatever Ben-Gurion actually said, President Kennedy was never wholly satisfied with the insistence that Dimona was strictly a peaceful project. This really was the main precursor to his death in my estimation. Neither were U.S. intelligence professionals. A recently declassified National Intelligence Estimate on Israel prepared several months after the meeting concluded that “Israel may have decided to undertake a nuclear weapons program. That CIA information was leaked to the mob in 1963 after the Bay of Pigs invasion went south. This information was critical to Santo Trafacante because it allowed him ot gain leverage over Castro and the attempts of the Industrial military complex to kill him versus JFK. Trafacante ensured it was JFK that would pay the ultimate price. He viewed his appointment of RFK Sr as Attorney General as the ultimate act of betrayal to the mob bosses in Chicago, New York, Florida, and New Orleans. In fact, Carlos Marcello was on trial as JFK was shot in Dallas on November 22, 1963 because of an RFK Sr prosecution that Joe Kennedy swore would never happen if the mob swung the swing state of Illinois to JFK in 1960. These details matter big time to understand how things really unfolded. I'll have an explosive blog out soon with even more details on this story. Are you too much for some people in your dopamine or social media network?

Let me put some Windex on your glass eyes today with some words.

The truth of what one says lies in what one does. Sometimes, the people you care about are walking through life dead. How you react says a lot about you. Redemption requires action, not empathy.

When someone you love dies, you, the living, are given the gift of "second chances." Their eulogy reminds us that the living can turn their lives around at any point. You’re not bound by the past; that is who you used to be. Your decisions and choices help bury your old past. You’re reminded that your feelings are not who you are but how you felt at that moment.

Your bad choices defined you yesterday, but they are not who you are today. Your future doesn’t have to travel the same path with the same people. You can start over when you embrace life. You don’t have to apologize to people who won’t listen. You don’t have to justify your feelings or actions during a difficult time.

You don’t have to put up with people who are insecure and want you to fail. All you have to do is walk forward with a positive outlook and trust that there is a natural plan more incredible than the sorrow you left behind. The people of quality that were meant to be in your life won’t need you to explain the beauty of your heart. They already understand what being human is----a roller coaster ride of emotions during rainstorms and sunshine, sprinkled with moments when you can almost reach the stars........This is why the truth of what one says lies in what one does.

Here is a new podcast that might be the best podcast I have done in 2024. What do I long for right now? Freedom, liberty, and allodial title. All I want is to lessen the pain I feel every night when I see violent protests tear the fabric of both away.

People have only as much liberty as they have the intelligence to want and the courage to take. Liberty allows for disobedience, but that disobedience needs to be peaceful.

Liberty gives us the ability and the right to tell people what they do not want to hear. This is the basis of leadership.

Freedom is not something that anybody in government can give. "We The People" take freedom, and people are only as free as they choose to be.

But every morning I wake up is another day, hopeless and miserable, with nothing but a deafening silence, a wave of tears, memories, and your absence in my life. That will change in 2025.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-03-04T19:14:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/podcasts-dropbox-collection.18701/post-347870>

This is the best podcast I've done to date. I've been deeply influenced by a group of people I speak about in this podcast. Over the last 35 years, I have been entangled with many powerful people who have inspired me to write about my philosophy for life.

How do you touch others in your own life? Do you use your hands? Your words? Your ideas? Is touch physical for you, or can we do it wirelessly? This podcast reflects this.

Must it be used in close proximity, or can your ability to reach people expand? Can you connect well to anyone anywhere and at any time?

Reading deep thoughts of ideas you get is like undressing before a bath. You don't undress out of fear that your clothes will become wet. You undress because you want the water to touch your body. You want to immerse yourself entirely in the feeling of the water and to emerge anew from the experience.

The most substantial ties we touch others with are the invisible threads nature uses to connect to us.
Touch is a very complex thing that is intoxicating and intangible for most.

Friends might lose touch, but they never seem to lose feelings........Friends tend to misplace their power to touch us. Wounded animals touch each other, and surgeons must touch their patients to heal them. The sun has to touch us to regenerate ourselves to live. Touch is incredibly complex, and how we apply it or misuse it defines our lives.

THE WISE IN LIFE DO NOT FEEL THE TOUCH OF PRESSURE.......THEY APPLY PRESSURE TO TOUCH THINGS THAT MATTER TO THEM.

### MonteD — 2016-11-09T16:52:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/goodbye-tinnitus-montes-optimal-journal.18712/post-203748>

There's always those things that you know you should be doing but until something makes it real enough you never do it. Well tinnitus was it for me. I've been following Jack's work since he was on Mark's forum years ago. Until now I was just a casual observer happy with repeating the knowledge to everyone I see but not following it myself. Then tinnitus happened. I woke up one morning with a bad headache to the back of my head and a really achy neck. It didn't go away all day, by the next day I got a horrible ringing in my ears that hasn't stopped since. It's affected sleep and given me anxiety. Doctors have been predictably of little help.

Reading through the literature and Jack's work I know this is a byproduct of a sub optimal environment and inflammation. I just purchased the Cornet ED88T Electrosmog Meter so I can remove any sources of nnEMF from my house.

Background:
32yo old male
5'7" @ 230lbs
living in Fort Worth, TX
No other health conditions that I know of...

Protocol: (In progress)

1\. Blue blocking lenses (done)
2\. Removal of nnEMF (ongoing)
3\. Epi-Paleo Rx
4\. Magnesium L-threonate
5\. Epsom Salt Soaks 3x weekly
6\. Ginger and Turmeric daily
7\. Vitamin K2
8\. LLLT therapy (hopefully)

I'll update back here when I know more and especially after playing around with my new meter!  If anyone has any suggestions or sees anything I'm missing let me know and thank you!

### MonteD — 2016-11-20T18:49:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/goodbye-tinnitus-montes-optimal-journal.18712/post-204120>

Thanks for all the responses!!! I believe it is time for an update!

First some history: My tinnitus, I believe, started two weeks ago after I woke up with a bad headache at the back of my head and neck. I went outside and stretched really hard at the back of the neck and then bam I got this ringing in the ears. At first it was rather severe but with the advice of a very good doctor who is part of the Quantum Biology group told me to take high doses of turmeric and systemic enzymes. By day 3 I was had a crazy pop sound in my left ear and the tinnitus got much quieter. Ever since then it has been a very high pitched background noise that rarely goes away. My ears still pop and I did feel a bit sick for a while. The sick feeling is fading in the last 3 or 4 days and finally yesterday I felt major breakthrough in my eustachian tube not popping all the damn time. This morning however, the high pitched tone is still their. If I move my head I can change the sound of the tinnitus. This, to me, is a clear indication of something physical whether that be some type of joint issue or just inflammation I don't know. The tinnitus seems to get better during the late afternoon and then get worse in the early AM or second part of sleep. I don't understand this pattern.

Now the good stuff:
In the last week I've started taking therapeutic doses of

1\. Turmeric (3x daily),
2\. Ginger
3\. Grape seed extract
4\. Systemic enzymes
5\. DHA (1 grams per day) from fish
6\. At least a half gallon of RO water daily.

The Cornett has shown me that:

1\. My noise canceling headphones were cooking my brain. No longer.
2\. My microwave was effectively cooking my entire house. No longer.
3\. The smart meter is now wrapped in foil.
4\. Cellphone is in airplane at all times I am not using it.
5\. The router is unplugged after 10pm.

A non coil spring mattress is on the way as the coils may be interfering with the tinnitus as well by acting as antenna for magnetism or nnEMF.

Reading the latest blog it appears like adopting a ketogenic seafood based diet Epi-Paleo Rx may help me a bit during this time. It also seems like the quantlet may help.

### PaulG — 2016-11-21T01:17:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/goodbye-tinnitus-montes-optimal-journal.18712/post-204127>

With regards to wrapping smart meter in foil, if it is on an external house wall, that may be a good strategy for your neighbour but not for you as it reflects back the radiation into your house.

What I did is put up a large book case against the wall opposite the meter, covered the back in multiple layers of foil and then grounded it by attaching a crocodile clip and running a wire out the window into the earth.

Also worth a try ringing your electricity provider (more than once!) and asking for it to be swapped out for an analogue one as it is giving you medical issues.

If the tinnitus is coming back in the middle of the night or in the morning, you may want to check the back of your walls in your room for magnetic EMF sources like a gas or electrical meter or even a cable cord running on the outside of the wall. I'm assuming you're shutting down power to your room from the circuit breaker. If you're not, consider unplugging all lamps, clocks or any devices near your bed when you are sleeping, and just to play it safe, move the bed itself about 2 inches away from the wall into the room. If you have a game console, also unplug from the wall because of the RF emission. If these things don't fix the problem, then at least it helps to eliminate variables.

### Sue-UK — 2016-11-21T15:34:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/goodbye-tinnitus-montes-optimal-journal.18712/post-204143>

I started getting intermittent mild tinnitus in September, usually when I woke up, or sometimes after visiting my Mum in the nnemf hell nursing home. I did a search of the word tinnitus on the forum, and cut and paste any comments Jack had made on the subject. My Dad had chronic tinnitus, my mother didn't but started losing her hearing in her early fifties, by her 70s she was extremely deaf, and developed AD in her 80s, (dots connected from things Jack had said) so with the maternal mitochondria link I was interested in why I was suddenly getting tinnitus. I notice it more in the mornings after I have had a good nights sleep with vivid dreams .... So its got me wondering if, if tinnitus is the brain's way of trying to heal itself by creating phonons to raise the DC electric current in the brain to increase the local melatonin levels to shrink the respiratory proteins, whether - in my maternal mitochondrial context- in some cases this could be a good sign? At least my brain is able to try after a good night's sleep......? My main "treatment" is to drink a lot of water, and go outside and CT (30 minutes of 5C in the pouring rain this morning), with grounding, and photon scavenging. ### Scompy — 2016-11-21T20:48:11+0200

Since I have a tinnitus super-power that comes and goes very quickly, I've observed some longer periods of tinnitus CLEARING after eating 85% or higher dark chocolate. The nnEMF gets the tinnitus to kick in within a minute, but if I had just eaten like a half bar of dark chocolate about an hour previously, the tinnitus is louder and takes a little longer for it to go away (like about a half-hour instead of 10 mins).

SPECIAL NOTE: I have a massive control over my eardrums and opening/closing them. I had over 100 inner ear infections as a child, and I can literally get fresh air to be pulled in and out of my eardrum whenever I want and somehow open/close my inner ear drums. Secondly, part of this airflow can even go into my sinuses and up into my eyes. Case in point... one time eating salsa and doing this breathing technique, I got hotsauce into my eye itself from the sinuses.

Here is what I suspect as a possibility: due to the caffeine and dehydration influence, it's somehow influencing the EZ within my inner ear fluid. Possibly because of my breathing, I'm able to cool down the area quickly and pull in water/moisture from the air to shut down the tinnitus as another thought.

I think the probiotic under the tongue is an interesting idea. I can try that one to see if anything happens, but this past year, I've also been opening my mouth to the sun mid-day to see if that helped my tinnitus, and it seemed to...but not enough to say conclusively yet.

### Valuation Man — 2016-11-22T02:14:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/goodbye-tinnitus-montes-optimal-journal.18712/post-204188>

I have struggled with Tinnitus for well over two decades. My tinnitus is much louder on my right side and it could be related to an injury I had as a teen when I knocked out teeth playing football. It changes tone and loudness with movement of jaw and neck.

There is someone on the following forum that has said he has cured his tinnitus by using very similar protocols as we learn here - except 1.4 . There are some good conversations there that may assist you in coming up with hacks for this.

[https://www.tinnitustalk.com/thread...faster-from-tinnitus.16236/page-2#post-200859](https://www.tinnitustalk.com/threads/health-tools-to-recover-faster-from-tinnitus.16236/page-2#post-200859)

My failed hacks include:
1) TMJ Splint therapy
2) Regular and Cervical Chiro
3) Massage
4) Various diets and supplements
5) Seeing ENT

I have seen significant improvement in all my other health issues and energy since I became aware of this site and switched my focus to increasing my Quantum Yield (Sun, Grounding, Water, CT, Magnetico, limiting Blue light). But the Tinnitus remains the same. I do accept that I live in nnEMF area (UHF TV Tower very near) and will be moving soon to more remote area away from all towers and wifi.

This year I was accepted at Ohio State University in their study of Tinnitus and they are providing free services and advice on this. Right now, they are testing me with hearing aids that provide a zen like tone. This is both masking and habituation. Only wear them when I am home because they are ugly and unsure about if there is emf issues with wearing them.

Read Jack's recent blog and made an appointment with craniosacral massage therapist and there are some positive comments about this on the Tinnitus talk forum.

A couple of questions about some of the post on this thread and perhaps future hacks for me:
1) LLLT - How long do you put the light on the impacted area
2) Oxygen - Could increasing oxygen to brain through breathing exercises or EWOT reduce tinnitus?

Well here's the update:

Doctor checked and said there was no obvious infection right now and said that it could have been a previous bacterial or viral infection. Right now they're saying it is eustachian tube dysfunction and they've given me some nasal steroids to lower that inflammation. He says that with people with no hearing loss that develop tinnitus and popping in the ears this regimen is typically really effective although it takes around 3 to 4 weeks for full effect. At the same time that I'm doing this I'm definitely going to be on a ketogenic Seafood diet and hopefully do around 15 minutes of Sub-Zero temperature per day.

### MonteD — 2016-12-02T07:11:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/goodbye-tinnitus-montes-optimal-journal.18712/post-204616>

Well finally a good breakthrough. I was sitting down after eating my morning DHA and the volume of the tinnitus did a sudden drop to half of the volume it was before! It has been getting better slowly but surely every day but today was a dramatic drop.

I've been doing the turmeric and system enzymes every day. I added monolaurin for an immunity boost in case this was due to some sort of viral infection. At the same time the Cornet Electrosmog meter was very very very helpful. It showed my headphones were cooking my brain and this was likely what led to inflammation and infection because of what we know about nnEMF and bacterial growth. I'm sure turning off the wifi at night, going to bed earlier, keeping the phone in airplane mode, eating DHA every morning and drinking lots of alkaline water has been a big help.

Also, I stopped obsessing and sending negative energy to the ear (psychosomatically making the noise louder if nothing else) and I started getting better almost immediately. Now I can only here in a very quiet room. I suspect that will improve in the next few weeks as well as I can still pressure and tingling in the ear and my tubes are still popping and crackling at times but no where near what it used to be.

p.s. ....I dropped 15 pounds in 3 weeks. wow....

### MonteD — 2017-01-12T20:04:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/goodbye-tinnitus-montes-optimal-journal.18712/post-206699>

Thought it would be good for another update.

I'm hearing the tinnitus in less and less situations now. Now I only hear it in very quiet environments or if I wake up in the middle of night. It is so much more faint than before that it never actually bothers me, I just sometimes notice it is their. This is a colossal improvement from before. My ETD which started the same day as the tinnitus has consistently gotten better and better. In fact, every time it improves the tinnitus improves right along with it. It seems like every two weeks I get a lot better. I have no doubt that adhereing to quantum principles saved me from more long-term/permanent damage. At this rate I would be very surprised if I'm dealing with this in 6 weeks.

My protocol is still daily DHA, epsom salt baths, grounding, and nnEMF mitigation.

Monte there are not sad to say.........and one more thing.......your tinnitus in Texas should be worse at night than at day.

Why?

protons.

How can you hack that? Pull out your RF, trimeter, and gausmeter and check day and night. Texas has a massive swing from day to night for several reasons above and below ground..........

So why will your EMF meters varying during the day in the same environment when you test? Sunlight makes protons rise and darkness makes them condense on things on Earth. Is Vitamin A the brain's EMF meter the same way your devices are? Yep. This means tinnitus is too.

Vitamin A and your cochlea are akin to your brain RF/trimeter/gaussmeter device. These meters should give different readings at different times of the day or night. It should give different readings at different times because electric and magnetic fields change as night and daylight changes. In fact as the January 2017 webinar tells you Vitamin A is a bio-molecule that is how the brain measures electric and magnetic fields. It is not as big a deal for hearing as vision but it matters.............This is why all opsin are bound to Vitamin. It tells the opsin something powerful about light.

The sun’s light is a cathode ray. When it hits the Earth in daytime, the Earth acts as an anode in this cosmologic circuit and this allows for more evaporation of water on the planet's surface to create more protons to dissipate in the atmosphere. Simultaneously the evaporaation effect will liberate more electrons from Earth that our tensegrity system connected to the Earth via our limbs is designed to collect. We collect and harvest this energy to store or use to do physiologic work to lower the resistance of our inner mitochondrial membrane. This reduces the electrical resistance of mitochondria and this helps stimulate autophagy and not apoptosis. During daytime the electric field of Earth is higher than it is at night when light is absent. Magnetic fields, however, are higher at night, and this is likely why sleep is linked to a loss of the DC electric current in diurnal animals. Protons at night are more plentiful around you and this makes the tinnitus worse at night.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-02-04T03:48:06+0200

TINNITUS FACTOID: Cranial Nerve 8 (acoustic/vestibular nerve) is a bigger canary........hence why tinnitus is a big clue to a poor environemnt around your brain case. There is a growing body of research showing radiation from cellphones, wireless devices, and wifi is harmful. Minimize your exposure by placing it in airplane mode while carrying against your body, use an airtube headset or speaker phone while talking, and turn off your wifi at night.
"Trigeminal neurons were capable of detecting temperature changes far below skin temperature increases caused by cellphone radiation. Simulated cellphone radiation affected brain electrical activity associated with nonlinear cognitive processing of radiation-induced thermal afferent signals. Radiation standards for cellphones based on a thermal/nonthermal binary distinction do not prevent neurophysiological consequences of cellphone radiation." [http://www.tandfonline.com/doi/abs/10.1080/15368378.2016.1194294?src=recsys&journalCode=iebm20](http://www.tandfonline.com/doi/abs/10.1080/15368378.2016.1194294?src=recsys&journalCode=iebm20)

Time for an update.

The tinnitus has subsided almost completely with a dramatic decrease in the last few days. My 6 weeks prediction seems to be on track. I don't notice it in the day and at night it is so faint that it doesn't bother me at all. If I get better, at all, I'll consider myself completely healed. I hope for the best but I'm cautious because I've gotten better and then much worse before......

Here is what I'm doing/have done:

1\. New mattress without coils. Wooden bed frame as well.
2\. sleeping with the windows open.
3\. Daily seafood intake. Aiming for at least 1/2 pound.
4\. At least 8 cups of RO water daily
5\. LOTS MORE SUN AND GROUNDING. Sun has recently gotten much stronger in Texas. I have been in the backyard for at least 1 hour every morning.
6\. I got rid of an old electric fan in the room. I think this was big. I tested it with the Cornet and thought it was far enough away from me....it was not apparently because within days of removing it...I got better. Coincidence? Maybe. Maybe not.
7\. I wear the blue blockers more...but still not nearly enough
8\. Phone charges in another room. Phone is in airplane mode most of the day.

Supplements:
Nature's Plus Krill Oil (high astaxanthin) Only on days I don't eat fish.
New Chapter MindForce. Contains Lion's Mane Mushroom which is excellent for stimulating BDNF, NGF, remyelination, anti-inflammatory, immune boosting, anti-anxiety, anti-stress.
New Chapter Turmeric Force
New Chapter Omega 7. Excellent for inflammation and hydration.
Systemic Enzymes. Various brands, Wobenzyme, American Biologics, Helion.

Rx:
Nasal spray: Azelastine (Astepro)

I'm excited. Let's see where I am in two weeks with even stronger sun.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-02-15T23:57:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/goodbye-tinnitus-montes-optimal-journal.18712/post-208669>

Poor mitochondrial function strikes again in another sense under attack in this modern world we've created. The small mitochondrial protein, Fus1 acts like a small carbon nanotubes in the inner mitochondrial membrane. Membrane fusion allows the exchange of cellular contents including cytoplasmic proteins, nuclei, mitochondria, secretory organelles, and vacuoles. Fus 1 is such a fusion protein. This protein acts like a carbon nanotube connecting the cytosol to the outer and inner mitochondrial membrane. When this nanotube like structure is defective structurally, electronically, or atomically it acts like an aberrent carbon nanotube that disrupts the inner mitochondrial membrane to increase ROS and lead to hearing damage. It is now well known in the literature that carbon nanotubes (CNTs) are capable of instirgating damage in cells. CNT's are novel materials with unique electronic and mechanical properties. There are many published reports that multi-walled carbon nanotubes (MWCNT) have potent, dose-dependent toxicity on cultured human cells. Molecular characterization studies have revealed that MWCNT induced substantial ROS production and mitochondrial inner membrane depolarization at sub-toxic doses that lead to end organ damage leading to tissue failure from energy deficits. MWCNT have been shown to stimulate the secretion of a panel of inflammatory cytokines and chemokines (TNFalpha, IL-1beta, IL-6, IL-10 and MCP1) from macrophages (Raw264.7) by activating the canonical NF-kappaB signaling pathway linked to leptin control. Activation of NF-kappaB signaling involves rapid degradation of NkappaB alpha, nuclear accumulation of NF-kappaBp65, binding of NF-kappaB to specific DNA-binding sequences, and transactivation of target gene promoters leading to cellular and tissue wide damage. Sadly this basic research has not reached the clinical world as this article from Yale clearly demonstrates. Quantum clinicians do not miss this kind of information. Tinnitus is a warning shot from your cochlea that your redox level in Ammon's horn is not good at all. Sound waves (phonons) are the pulse of the ocean in our cells, in our cochlea, or endolymphic sac and gives the spark of life that nature is capable of giving to life. Hearing is a sense built around air and the atmosphere. The atmopshere is a sphere within a sphere and it can create and electrical tension within the magnetosphere and the surface of earth where the gases we breathe and exhale is located. The Earth is a sphere surrounded by the sphere of ionosphere. That tension becomes an "acoustic pulse wave" for things that live within both spheres. When the tension changes in the environment the pattern of signaling in the hearing mechanisms changes things in these sensory cells. Blue light and nnEMF change the tension in that sphere to increase ubiquitination rates on all things. Tinnitus is the result you get when this occurs. These waves in our sensory cells connect to the tension present in tides of the sea in our cochlea and our endolymphic sac and they connect to us in ways few observe. Tides bring food to creatures and tides take boats out to sea. A sailor has the impression that the ocean is made of waves more than it is made of water because of how they sense them. The same is true with the sensory system. Heredity is about waves too but few realize it. Most humans believe life is about genetic waves in the nuclear genome but guys like me think it is about the environmental waves the crash and collide into our mitochondria and make mitochondrial waves that impact the inner mitochondrial membrane and the respiratory proteins to effect electron and proteon tunneling are they in turn make free radical signals and they quantize to the light a mitochondria liberates. It is general expression of the periodicity of organic life that is buried in our mitochondria. All generations belong to a continuous succession of waves of some sort, in which every single one resembles its predecessors and its followers yet changes because the world around forces that change.[http://news.yale.edu/2017/02/07/antioxidants-and-hearing-loss](http://news.yale.edu/2017/02/07/antioxidants-and-hearing-loss)

# Thread: April's Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aprils-journal.18724/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ab70bandit — 2016-11-11T18:29:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aprils-journal.18724/post-203838>

I thought it's time I write my journal.
I my self have health issues, but I desperately search for others in my family, if I can help them it will take a burden off me.
I came across jack after searching for help , years of clean eating and supplementing just wasn't showing a difference.
I don't come from a family with longevity, **on my Mom's side** - grandmother died at 60 of massive coronary, my grandfather died at 69 after 2 bouts of cancer years apart(bowel & Throat). My Mom's siblings(2) - Aunt Jes died at 42 of Pancreatic cancer and my uncle Mick had a major stroke at 50 and after 9 years in a nursing home dies at 59. My grand mother suffered for 30 years before she died with severe depression(shock treatments) and terrible digestive issues before finally dying of the heart attack. I was 11 when she died...I remember her to be short tempered & sad. My grandfather died when I was 18, I remember him as a happy, easy going man, my mom said she had never heard him raise his voice.
My mom is a mixture of both parents: Kind, caring, short tempered(that's the red head). She has suffered from depression for the past 20 years. It was mainly seasonal for many years but it all changed 2 years ago when she was diagnosed with an asymptomatic UTI found on a routine blood test, six months later & 5 courses of antibiotics, she became quite ill & her depression became 10 times worse & it just never went away.
My mom is a pale skinned, blue eyed red head.
**On my father's side** \- I did not know my father, my parents split when I was a toddler, my mom was left a single mother of 2 toddlers. My father started a new family & never looked back(he tried to contact me when I was 20 but I wasn't interested).
All I know about my father's side of the family is that my grandfather was a bad diabetic, who died early from diabetic complications in his early sixties and my father was a bad diabetic that died from complications at 56.
I am also fair skinned, although not as fair as my mom & I have dark hair & dark eyes. None of my family members are or were obese or even over weight. I've been a little over weight ever since having my daughter(prior to that I was always under weight).
**My only brother** \- was diagnosed at 30 with an autoimmune disease - **Ankylosing spondylitis(**I hope I spelled that right) and his condition is progressing.
**My daughter Meg** - is a high functioning Autistic young lady of 20. She also takes tonic-clonic seizures. Skin issues(eczema) She is my only child and the light of my life. Meg is high functioning and very smart. She loves online crossword puzzles - she can finish a puzzle in 30 seconds - she has the highest score posted online. Her brain can work at lightning speed visually but verbally she is like a small child. She has difficulty with expressive/receptive speech...you may have to ask her a question twice or give her time to think about a simple question that is asked of her. Her expressive speech can be somewhat incomplete or broken sentences. Visually you can teach her almost anything. Since applying the 'Six Healers, as well as, using natural calcium blockers daily, I have seem a little progress with her speech and socialization, not as much as I would like. One thing I have noticed, she use to have a strong smell of ammonia to her(I thought it was her diet but even changing her diet didn\`t take that away) not I don\`t smell it now. Meg is on a Ketogenic diet for the seizures, she had her first one at 13 then she didn\`t have another until a year later, 2 in one week, I think it was stress because I was getting gallbladder surgery and she liked to read up on these things & in the write up she read that in rare cases people have died from gallbladder surgery, she had 2 seizures that week 2 days apart. We decided not to put her on medication...it was scary because they were tonic-clonic seizures, I just kept up the Keto diet. She went 11 months after that before her next seizure, then 23 months after that. Then she went 13 months, then 4 months, then 3 weeks. I started her on the Six Healers in june with myself, a couple of weeks after a seizure. Things were going good, & I was really hopeful, but she had another seizure 3 weeks ago, I had her following everything pretty much to the letter. That got me down.
I joined early the site in early summer of this year. I found everything I was reading fascinating and although most of it over my head, the material I understood resonated with me. I had tried many things nutritionally to help family members with some success but it was limited, I was searching for more when I came across Jack's site.
When I told my mother about the things I was reading on the site(at this point she was in really bad shape with suicidal depression), she was very skeptical, but because of low dopamine, easy to lead.
It took months of reminding my mother to take off her sun glasses(which she wore 90% of the time - saying that she was sensitive to the light & it covered up her sad eyes) eventually it became a habit. We ground every morning between 8 & 8:30 before I leave for work(she won't ground at her house because of nosy neighbours so close on either side) my home is in a more wooded area.
It has taken many months, but my mom is now having more good days that bad(which is amazing because they were pretty much all bad days for almost 2 years) I not saying everything is great, she still has some depression and difficulty with socializing, but slowly that is changing but a long way to go. Mom was on many medications for depression over the years, most didn't work or barely worked. Mom has a very high tolerance for medication(I think I read somewhere that those with less melanin have a different tolerance for medication. My mom was under the knife a few times(C-sections/ hysterectomy/ Cholecystectomy) and each time she can recall feeling/hearing everything that was done/said but couldn't move or talk - the Doctors didn't believe her. Any way, mom's spirits(without meds) following Jacks RX's, are better than any med that she has tried. The only problem is having the runs for well over a month now.
**My story:** I have had digestive issues since as long as I can remember - a doctor diagnosed me with a nervous stomach at age 7(I was a C-section premie with many issues and was not breast fed). I had reflux from a young age but was not tested for GERD until my late teens. I was treated with many PPI's for over 20 years. I came off them about 6 years ago, they were not working anymore. I also have seasonal allergies most of my life. When I was 23, I had a severe anaphylactic reaction that landed me in the hospital for 2 weeks on deaths door(I looked like the character Martin short played in that old movie when he was stung by a bee, remember that one)I was unrecognizable. When the PPI\`s stopped being effective, I was getting a feeling of having a global object in my throat, my breathing wasn\`t affected, but that constant feeling was there and I was getting heartburn 4-5 times a week on the meds.
I had a severe gallbladder attack about 7 years ago, the duct was completely blocked and they ended up removing my gallbladder(hindsight), since than I have had worse digestive issues, with distension & constant discomfort after eating, especially high fat foods, but sometimes it could be any food...if I don\`t eat there is no issue. It was a constant condition for 2 years, I had my doctor do upper and lower ultrasounds with no results, I was basically told to watch my diet.
I tried many elimination diets, but nothing has helped as much as the 'Six Healers', I am feeling a lot better - been doing this for 5 months, it\`s not totally gone but most days I feel normal.
I worried a lot years ago knowing that my aunt Jes died younger that me from pancreatic cancer and she suffered from many of the same digestive issues that I did. She was also like my mom...Pale skinned, green eyed red head, who spent most time indoors. She was an extremely smart lady, she wanted to make a difference...she had her masters in Special Education, which makes me so sad because she never got a chance to meet my daughter who I believe she would have been her biggest advocate had she lived.
That pretty much sums it up...I know, its a lot of info, but I had to get it out. Thanks

### Jack Kruse — 2016-11-12T15:41:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aprils-journal.18724/post-203872>

What about the environment of your maternal side.......and your specific one. That is the key

### ab70bandit — 2016-11-12T17:52:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aprils-journal.18724/post-203891>

My environment is not optimal, I work in an office on a computer...I work during the day and have been leaving the lights off(I have a wall thats all window. When I do have to put the lights on I use full spectrum lighting. My daughter also works in the same office.
My mom is retired. She hates computers, but she does spend alot of time indoors, much less the past couple of months.

### Liam Taylor — 2016-11-15T20:47:21+0200

Lots of detail there, lots of useful info. Keep posting what you're learning from the blogs, posts etc.

# Thread: Lucien's Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/luciens-journal.18725/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Lucien Burke — 2016-11-11T20:07:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/luciens-journal.18725/post-203841>

Hey Guys!

I'm new... seems like a journal is a good way to begin, I'll try to keep it short and sweet...

I'm 20, live in Burlington VT, and have "Chronic Fatigue Syndrome."

I felt healthy most of my life until about a year and a half ago when after a slew of infectious problems including Lyme, mono, and strep all within the span of a few months, I became crippled by severe fatigue and neurological problems that have manifested as chronic depersonalization/brain fog.

Since then I've been on a journey for answers... And have entertained everything from candidiasis to chronic Lyme and Biotoxin illness. Then last summer I heard Jack and Ruben speak at Shelburne Farms in VT and my mind was blown... Puzzle pieces slowly started moving together.

After spending a little time sifting through Jack's work, my intuition tells me that the answers to my problems don't lie in antimicrobial and detox treatments, but in mitochondrial revitalization (Recharging a dead mammalian battery).

I'm sick of the bullshit that alternative health leaders spout about killing every last pathogen in your body and detoxing until the grave. I've come to the belief that we're sick because we're weak at the cellular level and pathogens and biotoxins have become opportunistic because of this deficit in human resilience.

So I came here for support in my quantum journey.

I'm curious what steps I should take and in what order, and how they should be executed...

I'm open to any and all suggestions...

Thanks!

Lucien

### Jack Kruse — 2016-11-12T15:38:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/luciens-journal.18725/post-203870>

welcome.....I think Dr. Wallace's data show that CFS is really a function of a broken mitochondrial flux system. Redox before detox. I have a huge problem with alternative pratctioners who do not understand this basic issue. They just make huge $$$$ going detox over redox.

### Lucien Burke — 2016-11-21T18:47:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/luciens-journal.18725/post-204153>

_**ROS Signaling**_

I've been dissecting the quilt document to begin with, and the ROS levee caught my eye. It talks about the importance of ROS signaling among our mitochondria in terms of cell health and maintenance. For someone like myself with weakened immunity due to a broken mitochondrial flux, optimization of ROS signaling seems critical. I know ROS signaling is closely tied to circadian biology and light exposure to the full spectrum including Blue (at the right times), UV, and Infrared. In addition to this, I'm curious about oxidative therapies such as Ozone and UV Irradiation of the blood. I'm excited about researching ROS signaling and oxidative therapies in greater depth...A potent immune system and resilient cell infrastructure rely on healthy ROS rhythms from Mitochondria.

Factors that interrupt proper ROS Signaling:
Mismatched Circadian Rhythm ~ Circadian Biology dictates waves of ROS production in cells
nn EMF exposure ~ facilitates chronic oxidative stress, loss of proper ROS signaling

Again, I wonder if adding oxidative therapies like Ozone can work synergistically with minding light and nn EMF....

I'll be investing in Ozone soon and keep an update on that.

### Lucien Burke — 2016-12-11T19:30:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/luciens-journal.18725/post-205162>

_**Lake Champlain CT
**_
It's early winter in VT and that means that although the atmospheric temps are dipping into the low 20s and teens up here near the Canadian border, the Lake hasn't frozen yet. Perfect for early morning polar dips down at the beach.

The solitude is centering and my relationship to water has become a deeply spiritual experience. It's amazing how the cold water can pierce through all the layers of prejudice, false security, and ego that I have become accustomed to insulating myself with. Each session seems to take me deeper and deeper into new frontiers of my conscious mind and the cold seems to have an amazing ability to enhance my personal strengths and dispel my weaknesses. Who new a freezing lake could be such as incredible mentor and healer... I'm still physically very weak so the CT is intense, but the mental and spiritual benefits I've realized keep me going back down to the beach in the AM...

### Penny — 2016-12-23T21:41:39+0200

>
> I felt healthy most of my life until about a year and a half ago when after a slew of infectious problems including Lyme, mono, and strep all within the span of a few months, I became crippled by severe fatigue and neurological problems that have manifested as chronic depersonalization/brain fog.
>
> Since then I've been on a journey for answers... And have entertained everything from candidiasis to chronic Lyme and Biotoxin illness. Then last summer I heard Jack and Ruben speak at Shelburne Farms in VT and my mind was blown... Puzzle pieces slowly started moving together.
>
> After spending a little time sifting through Jack's work, my intuition tells me that the answers to my problems don't lie in antimicrobial and detox treatments, but in mitochondrial revitalization (Recharging a dead mammalian battery).
>
> I'm sick of the bullshit that alternative health leaders spout about killing every last pathogen in your body and detoxing until the grave. I've come to the belief that we're sick because we're weak at the cellular level and pathogens and biotoxins have become opportunistic because of this deficit in human resilience.
>
> So I came here for support in my quantum journey.
>
> I'm curious what steps I should take and in what order, and how they should be executed...
>
> I'm open to any and all suggestions...
>
> Thanks!
>
> Lucien

### Lucien Burke — 2017-01-31T19:02:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/luciens-journal.18725/post-207665>

CFS/ME has become severe, mostly bedridden now. All reading/research points to mitochondrial dysfunction, as JK wrote above ^. The question is how to improve with such broken mitochondria. DHA and water are obviously key, but cells can't become hydrophilic with extremely poor electron flow from busted mitochondria like mine...

Lots of info on this site about how when heteroplasmy rates are high and the mitochondrial flux system is so broken, ketosis can be a dead end or even dangerous because of senescent mitochondria being unable to produce adequate electron density. I'm trying to decide whether or not to utilize ketosis, as I don't know whether it will help me or hurt me based on the status of my mitochondria.

Currently focusing on loads of DHA, water, and electrolytes, mitigating artificial light and nnEMF, but unsure about ketosis due to broken mitochondria.

Does anyone know of hacks to stimulate autophagy, mitophagy, and mitochondrial biogenesis in an affective and safe way for someone with severe cfs/me?

So I can restore electron density in mitochondria, assimilate light, and restore DC electric current to tissues.

like I said...

SEVERE cfs/me.

### Lucien Burke — 2017-03-01T01:24:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/luciens-journal.18725/post-209453>

Interesting new study from the Naviaux Mitochondrial Medicine lab at UC San Diego... "Metabolic Features of chronic fatigue syndrome"

[http://www.openmedicinefoundation.o...016/08/Naviaux-PNAS-CFS-Metabolomics-2016.pdf](http://www.openmedicinefoundation.org/wp-content/uploads/2016/08/Naviaux-PNAS-CFS-Metabolomics-2016.pdf)

Shows many metabolites extremely low in cfs, including intermediates of the citric acid cycle. This confuses me because from all the research I've done, including on this site, the etiology of cfs seems to be a mitochondrial defect from a poor environment. However, if there were a mitochondrial defect, shouldn't there be a buildup of TCA intermediates? Because glycolysis should still be intact and producing TCA intermediates, they just wouldn't be burned by faulty mitochondria. Since TCA intermediates are low, this would suggest that glycolysis is blocked. This hypothesis is supported by another recent study that concluded a dysfunction of the pyruvate dehydrogenase enzyme:

[https://insight.jci.org/articles/view/89376](https://insight.jci.org/articles/view/89376)

Im just confused why these findings show an interruption in glycolysis when CFS appears to be the result of disturbed mitochondrial function. Glycolysis produces the products that are burned by mitochondria, and as far as I know isn't under control of the mitochondria. Does glycolysis and the PDH enzyme somehow have to do with redox?

Hoping someone can clear the air on this...

### Lucien Burke — 2018-01-18T01:37:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/luciens-journal.18725/post-232796>

I can't believe it's already 2018... 2017 flew.

I can't think of a better place to document my dreams and focuses for this year so I'll do it right here.

**1\. Continue to prioritize and improve my health.
**
If you don't have your health you don't have anything. You can't be of service to anyone or add energy to the system. For too long I've told myself stories about why I can't put my health first, and this is why I've lost my light and lost my path. It's time to find my way once again. The energy of my spacesuit is #1, the rest follows.

**2\. End my technology addiction.
**
It drains my energy and gives nothing of value in return. Instead of being used as a tool, my technology has been using me. Not anymore. I've already deleted many apps, deactivated many accounts, and gotten rid of many devices that weren't of service to me in these first few weeks of the year. I have a good head start on this and it feels amazing.

**3\. Follow the Sun.**

I've made good on this one already as well. My families house on Marco Island, FL was damaged in the hurricane, and I volunteered to live here for a few months and oversee the repairs. I managed to escape a big chunk of vit D winter in the north this time around. I'm not gonna miss a single sunrise this year.

**4\. Reconsider my relationships
**
If I'm not resonating someone in my life, I'll let the distance grow naturally. My inner circle needs to ignite my spirit.

**5\. Simplify**

Drop extra baggage. Let go of extraneous emotions. Live in the moment. Accept the things I can't change. Reconnect with nature. This includes following my inner compass. If I disconnect from the radio static and pay deep attention to my inner signals, I find they point me in the right direction. I'm focused on finding the courage to follow my instincts.

...

**_Focused on bringing light back into my life this year._**

Dear all,

a couple of days ago I followed a podcast with Jack (Ironman Executive - Cold Thermogenesis) where he mentioned that at some degree before water freezes it contains the highest amount of electrons and thats why there is much more life in the polar sees compared to the equatorial sees or something like that.
Let us suppose that I could stand water in a lake (5°C water temperature) without having used the ct-protocol lately. Is there any chance of getting cold urticaria above 0°C??? I got cold urticaria once when I was using the protocol in the past (I know about the omega 6:3-ratio issue) at the point where I took off the compressing shirt and put ice on my naked thoracial skin. Cold water though doesn´t seem to cause any problems for me. The skin gets cherry-red but doesn´t turn white at 5°C. I stay in the water for 1-1:30min at the moment daily. It seems to me that water above 0°C (with most electrons in it refering to Jacks podcast) instead of ice seems to be more "healthy". It that correct? Maybe healthy is a confusing term.
If might be relevant that I´m at about 18-20% bodyfat at the moment.

# Thread: Everyone needs to watch and share this on social media: Devra Davis
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/everyone-needs-to-watch-and-share-this-on-social-media-devra-davis.18731/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2016-11-12T17:07:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/everyone-needs-to-watch-and-share-this-on-social-media-devra-davis.18731/post-203882>

Electromagnetism can be discussed in two ways: #1 in terms of the field and #2 in terms of the radiation. The field is something that exists in space around an object that produces it. An electric field extends outward from electrically charged objects. Both magnetic and electric fields are static and unvarying. When time is introduced to them, by varying the intensity of the field, like a radio antenna, an electromagnetic field is the result. This kind of field fluctuates often and emits waves of energy that behave as massless and chargeless (photons) energies. Each energy wave consists of an electric and magnetic field at right angles to one another. The energy wave generated is traveling at right angles to both the magnetic and electric field. This gives it a 3 dimensional look when you reconstruct the view in your minds eye. This is precisely how the brain reconstructs time. The number of waves formed in one second is called the frequency of the EMF wave. The distance the energy travels during one oscialltion is the wavelength. The higher the frequency, the shorter the wavelength is, and vice versa.

EMF’s span large frequencies. Gamma rays are very small. They are a tenth of a billionth millimeter long and vibrate at sextillions of times a second. They are related to Xrays, short wave UV radiation are called ionizing radiation. There photons are so powerful that they can knock out electrons from atoms and cause highly reactive ions to be formed where they should not be. The next band of EMF’s are the longer wave UV radiation and then we hit EMF we see in visible light. Infrared radiation comes next and we feel it as heat. Below the infrared spectrum is the area of EMF’s is where modern communications frequencies are found. Here, microwaves (giga or mega Hz), and radio frequencies exist. The RF (radio frequency) spectrum is broken up into many smaller groups with names you know (UHF, VHF and ELF). Extremely low frequency (ELF) waves are where the Schumann resonance is below 10 Hz. At 10 Hz, one wave is 18,600 miles long. The key point is, except for visible light and infrared EMF, we can not perceive any of these energies without instruments designed to find them. The problem is our brain senses them all without sensation being perceived consciously. WifI frequencies vary by country law: 2.4 GHz (802.11b/g/n) Most countries. United States. Interference concerns.
3.65 GHz (802.11y)
4.9 GHz (802.11j) public safety WLAN.
5 GHz (802.11a/h/j/n/ac)
5.9 GHz (802.11p)
60 GHz (802.11ad)
900 MHz (802.11ah)

Grounding every single day (before I go sleep 30-40min), I do not live in city.

Cold baths with diving

### Jack Kruse — 2017-02-15T02:50:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mito-connections-to-heart-disease.18737/post-208612>

With heart disease uou must pay attention to your Potassium (K+) levels and caclium levels in cardiac physiology of what makes up the electrical deflections on an ECG. The reason potassium is critical inside a cell, and supersedes magnesium and sodium ion concentration is because it is naturally tied to beta and gamma carboxyl side chain groups found in proteins, where potassium specifically binds because of “quantum advantages.” These beta and gamma carboxyl bonds allows K+ to ADSORB to this protein site and donate electrons (this is why K+ has the second highest redox potential to Lithium in life; to the polarized water gel crystal.

Each molecule of ATP in a cell controls 8,800 water molecule binding sites and 20 potassium ions to make this liquid crystalline semiconductor inside every cell of your body. Potassium acts like “the glue” to keep your protein back bone and water in a gel state inside your cell to maintain the semiconducting plates together in a cohesive form. This is why K+ is critical in setting the redox potential of water in a cell. In this way, you are building a special type of semiconductor that forms “the fourth phase of matter” and can act like a “topologic insulator.”(TI) A ‘TI’ allows quantum effects to happen in warm wet environments.

[https://arxiv.org/abs/1508.06135](https://arxiv.org/abs/1508.06135)

### Jack Kruse — 2017-02-15T02:50:35+0200
