### Sean Waters — 2018-07-17T16:07:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-progress-with-eczema.18880/post-246055>

Looking back on this my issue was actually the Vitamin A cycle... it's the only thing that has made a difference to my skin is going to bed at 8pm to be asleep for before 9pm (best).

If i do get to sleep before 9 my skin doesn't itch during sleep, i don't wake up intermittently, and the next day it is not inflamed and just peels off. It is actually healing and smooth for first time in years - It's all to do with the Cortisol/ DHEA/ Melatonin axis and the Vit A cycle. I thought 9.30/10pm bedtimes were sufficient for Vit A cycle but I was wrong (for me that is - others may be different).

My problem is i've found it hard to be consistent at getting to sleep before 9pm.

But on the days i do, going to bed EARLIER and even before sunset in the summer has made such a difference to me. Just 1 hour.

Matthew Walker (Why We Sleep author) said on a podcast that every hour asleep before midnight is "worth" 2 hours to our cells. Double points basically. I found that really interesting. So sleep by 9pm with a 5am wake up = a "feels like" 11 hours sleep, not 8 hours. Usually i wake up at 4am, so i get a "feels like" of 10 hours.

At first i woke at 2.30am and so did my best friend who has also tried it. That would count as 8.5 hours according to Walker. I said to my friend that the Growth Hormone phase 3-5am shouldn't be missed, but he said he felt good so he rolls with it. After doing it for a while, i noticed if i use black out blinds on the windows i don't wake at 2.30am, and it extends me an hour or 2 to a closer to sunrise wake up time.

His adrenal fatigue has disappeared though, and he gets up at 2.30/3am everyday now and has a Bulletproof Green Tea and Salmon/Seaweed epi paleo breakfast. He feels amazing. so maybe that growth hormone phase is brought forward?! I don't know. Maybe noone does. But the early bedtime + black out blinds and a cave-like dark room that is cooled with a fan in summer seems to be best practice for me...

In indonesia i was eating past 8pm on the beach (prolactin secretion @ midnight/GH destroyed by leptin), and i didn't use blue blockers back then. I woke at 10am most days absolutely drained. It was early adrenal fatigue. I'm finding a lot of my posts from 2016/17 are wrong or misinformed - i've learned a LOT more since then.

This post could be wrong too. So as always.... try it, observe, and report back with your n=1

### drezy — 2018-07-17T16:14:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-progress-with-eczema.18880/post-246057>

Sean Waters -- Circadian zero to Circadian Hero?

> But the early bedtime + black out blinds and a cave-like dark room that is cooled with a fan in summer seems to be best practice for me..

I'm completely with you here. I don't write about it and probably should, but doing sun and cold as much as I try to I naturally get tired earlier and sleep before most residents at senior homes. If the sun is down then whatever it is isn't important anymore.

If you're "wrong" then your wrong with skin that's not itchy...

### Sean Waters — 2018-07-17T16:32:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-progress-with-eczema.18880/post-246060>

Hahaha yeah i've just bought 4 books: Matthew Walker's one i just mentioned, and then: Rhythms of Life (Frietzman and **Foster - discovered melanopsin**), Seasons of Life (same authors but 2010 updated research on seasonality) and the Circadian Code (Satchin Panda - june 18).

I just read a little free e-book today on amazon kindle too called: Circadian Rhythms: Below Our Awareness (C.D. Shelton)

I'm fascinated. I **assumed** my circadian rhythms were in tune until recently. But little details matter so much. 1 hour earlier - that is it!! to stop my lifelong eczema!! 1 hour man. Incredible.

Yes the daytime Sunshine has a huge affect on my sleep - i'm way more drowsy if Grounded in Sunlight all day. Me and Shah would be so tired at 7pm sunset in mexico... i dubbed the term "Mela-stoned" to describe the melatonin drowsiness we got everyday. We were out at 5am at the beach and then all day in the water/sun until 7pm. Blue blockers on at 7pm made it even more serious.

Don't get it as much now i'm back at work in London of course.... but a big difference is waking up early, forcing it a little bit with an alarm clock for a few days, and that will tire me earlier in the evening.

Cold shower too or CT helps so much (as long as bathroom isnt mouldy then you get a stress response). Any kind of stress fucks this up.. arguments, cooking dinner, watching tv, literally anything... I have zero activity at night now. Sit in my garden and relax with some water before i go to bed.

Another factor is dim light. Any amount of light can fuck this up. My GF checked me on this last night. She loves the black out blind now, before it was "too hot" for it, but the fan sorted that out.

Just got to make this consistent.. see what results i get. **full skin clear up would be immense**

### Hunger4More — 2018-07-18T02:42:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-progress-with-eczema.18880/post-246099>

To add my update to this:

It's been said before on this site that eczema is an "allergy" to nnEMF. I believe this to be 100% true.

Where did I have my worst eczema? On my left groin area. What did I often carry there? My car keys - which use RF to start my car (I haven't carried my cell in my pocket for a long time). Moving the keys made a huge difference in my eczema.

It still flares up from time to time with excessive cell phone use. I've used the app "Moment" to track my cell usage and it correlates with any flare ups.

I live in a big city - the eczema only fully goes away when I can get sunshine in more remote areas (ie Vermont June 2018).

But I'm going to give Sean's hack above a try as well and report back.

Hope this can help others.

# Thread: Sunlight & Immune System
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sunlight-immune-system.18881/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### prime1 — 2016-12-21T05:24:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sunlight-immune-system.18881/post-205577>

Georgetown University Medical Center researchers have found that sunlight, through a mechanism separate than vitamin D production, energizes T cells that play a central role in human immunity.

### Penny — 2016-12-23T21:32:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fcc-chairman-tom-wheeler-steps-down.18884/post-205711>

**Or we could all start bombarding the potential replacements... :**

**Trump May Appoint Pence Ally And Telecom Deregulator To FCC**
[Aaron Pressman](http://fortune.com/author/aaron-pressman/)
Updated: Dec 02, 2016 8:26 PM Pacific | Originally published: Nov 30, 2016
A former ally of Vice President-elect Mike Pence with a strong focus on deregulation could be in line for an appointment to the Federal Communications Commission, possibly as the next chairman.

Brandt Hershman, a longtime Indiana state senator, is rumored to be the leading candidate for an opening on the agency, which oversees the telecommunications, broadcasting, and cable industries, [Politico reported on Wednesday](https://www.politicopro.com/technology/story/2016/11/early-chatter-starts-around-potential-trump-fcc-picks-139172) citing unnamed sources. Hershman worked closely with the vice president-elect after Pence was elected Indiana's governor in 2012.

Hershman declined to comment.

The current senate majority floor leader, Hershman authored a massive law to deregulate telecommunications in Indiana a decade ago. The bill ended government regulation of phone rates, freed up cable companies from needing to get dozens of local licenses to offer service, and stopped cities and towns from setting up their own municipal broadband services.

_[Get Data Sheet](http://fortune.com/getdatasheet/), Fortune's technology newsletter._

The FCC under chairman Tom Wheeler, appointed by President Obama, has pressed several major regulatory initiatives, including net neutrality rules to prevent Internet providers from blocking or charging more for access to any web site or online service. Wheeler also pushed online privacy rules that apply to Internet service providers. The chairman dropped his efforts to break the cable industry's set top box monopoly, however.

Analysts expect an FCC run by a Trump appointee would roll back those rules, which have been opposed by Republicans in Congress and major communications companies. The president's party typically occupies three of the commission's five seats, and the president designates one member as chairman. Trump could name a new appointee such as Hershman chairman or possibly designate current Republican FCC member Ajit Pai as chairman.

Trump has already named three prominent opponents of net neutrality to his transition team overseeing the FCC. [Two weeks ago](http://fortune.com/2016/11/21/trump-net-neutrality-fcc/), he picked Jeff Eisenach, an economist who has been on Verizon’s [(vz, -0.24%)](http://fortune.com/company/vz) payroll, and Mark Jamison, who formerly worked on Sprint’s [(s, +0.18%)](http://fortune.com/company/s) lobbying team and now heads the University of Florida’s Public Utility Research Center. This week, trump added Roslyn Layton, a telecom industry consultant and visiting fellow at the American Enterprise Institute, who has opposed both the net neutrality and privacy rules.

### Matty_M — 2016-12-24T21:19:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fcc-chairman-tom-wheeler-steps-down.18884/post-205756>

**[FIRST DRAFT OF LETTER TO TRUMP (and/or Surgeon General/FCC/FDA/EPA); Please suggest additions, deletions, and modifications.](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1UZ6BT0auVbxj_03rjALjvMMRQySb4Elqf5e0Trn20sQ/edit?usp=sharing)**

\- [It is long, however I do not see any redundancies. Please point any out.
\- Anyone, including Jack, please optimize the "health effects" section for the greatest effect and clarity, and provide the best evidence you have](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1UZ6BT0auVbxj_03rjALjvMMRQySb4Elqf5e0Trn20sQ/edit?usp=sharing)

\---------------------------
****[LINK TO GOOGLE DRIVE DOC WITH HYPERLINKS, ability to comment (first draft)](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1UZ6BT0auVbxj_03rjALjvMMRQySb4Elqf5e0Trn20sQ/edit?usp=sharing)

[https://docs.google.com/document/d/1UZ6BT0auVbxj\_03rjALjvMMRQySb4Elqf5e0Trn20sQ/edit?usp=sharing](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1UZ6BT0auVbxj_03rjALjvMMRQySb4Elqf5e0Trn20sQ/edit?usp=sharing)****

\-----------

[Dear Mr. President,

I believe that fierce protection of the Health, Welfare, and Safety of American citizens from any threat, external _and_ internal, ought to come before all other considerations of a government of the People, by the People, and for the People. Not only is such my belief, but it is guaranteed in the preamble of the Constitution: “…to insure domestic Tranquility, provide for the common defence, promote the general Welfare, and secure the Blessings of Liberty to ourselves and our Posterity…”

Not only is the government of the United States of America now actively attacking the Health, Welfare, and Safety of American citizens, who created it to protect themselves; it is allowing a specific regulatory body to commit a sick violation of human rights upon its own citizens.

On July 14th, the _Federal Communications Commission_ (_FCC_) voted to allow the unregulated use of a new band of radio/microwave radiation nationwide with “5G Spectrum Frontiers”. This is due to the push of Chairman Tom Wheeler since his appointment and unanimous confirmation in 2013, despite the fact that he was previously president of _The Wireless Industry_ (_CTIA_\- formerly the _Cellular Telephone Industries Association_), the world’s largest Telecommunications Lobbyist Organization. This represents a conflict of interests that fundamentally undermines the rights of American citizens and the democracy of the United States of America as a whole.

The ongoing debate about the safety of cell phones and wireless radiation in general was suppressed by claims of major media outlets and the American Cancer Association that the evidence does not support a link between radio/microwave radiation and cancer. These claims are based on industry-conducted research. For research to be considered scientific, it must be falsifiable. However, the Telecommunications Industry will never publish evidence that proves the harms of its technology; in fact, on numerous occasions the Industry has not released findings of this type. Because the Industry is only able to publish data that does not contradict the use of Wireless Radiation, it is fundamentally unable to falsify its hypotheses; its research, therefore, does not constitute science. Using it to make public policy is akin to letting a King pass whichever laws best suit his endeavors. Our nation was born to prevent this, and you must not be the president that allows it to continue.

The FCC’s decision to allow the use of 5G technology was made despite scientific consensus that **5G is not safe, it threatens the safety of, and is actively harming the health of Americans**, **especially of children, who are astronomically more susceptible to the toxic effects of wireless radiation**. Likewise, there is scientific consensus that the FCC’s safety limits, which only consider _thermal_ effects of wireless radiation (such as literal microwaving of tissue), are more than 30 years out-of-date. They do not protect the population from, rather they **completely ignore all non-thermal effects**, including:

· Brain Tumor Formation and DNA Breakages, confirmed by the US National Toxicology Program’s $25 million dollar study in 2016

(http://www.saferemr.com/2016/05/national-toxicology-progam-finds-cell.html , http://microwavenews.com/news-center/ntp-cancer-results )

· DNA Breakages, Chromosomal Aberrations, Increased Oncogenic Activity, Altered Blood Pressure, Increased Brain Cancer, Altered Melatonin Levels: study

· Anxiety, Depression, and Suicide:

· Do You Have Microwave/EMR Sickness? , Study, Study, Study

· Lowered Sperm Count, Sperm Damage, and Infertility across Society:

study , study , The Disappearing Male

· Alzheimer’s: study

· Asthma: study , study

· Meta-Analyses:
Bioinitiative: Inadequacy of Standards, Gene Expression, DNA Damage, Stress Response, Immune Alteration, Brain Tumora, Childhood Cancers, Alzheimer’s Disease, Fertility
Powerwatch: Peer-Reviewed Scientific Studies on EMF Related Subjects

EMF-Portal: Thousands of Studies on Effects of EMFs
Prove-It: over 5000 Studies on Effects of EMFs

At a press conference on June 20th, the FCC confiscated the Credentials of Bloomberg Reporter Todd Shields because he was speaking with a former Congressional candidate, Kevin Mottus, about health concerns of 5G. Mottus asked Wheeler “The NTP study shows wireless causes cancer sub-thermally. How can you proceed with more wireless expansion with FCC standards only recognizing thermal effects, ignoring thousands of studies showing cancerous effects, neurological effects, and reproductive harm?” and was ignored. He was then prevented from displaying a sign and taking a t-shirt out of his back to inform the press of this information, and was repeatedly threatened by security. This suppression of truth is reminiscent of Fascist regimes and is not just unconstitutional, but sick and evil.

](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1UZ6BT0auVbxj_03rjALjvMMRQySb4Elqf5e0Trn20sQ/edit?usp=sharing)
_[“5G will use much higher frequency bands \[24 to 100+ GHz\]… antennas that can aim and amplify signals… massive deployment of small cells… tens of billions of dollars in economic activity… hundreds of billions of microchips… if something can be connected, it will be connected…”](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1UZ6BT0auVbxj_03rjALjvMMRQySb4Elqf5e0Trn20sQ/edit?usp=sharing)_
[
](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1UZ6BT0auVbxj_03rjALjvMMRQySb4Elqf5e0Trn20sQ/edit?usp=sharing)
_[“Unlike some countries, we do not believe we should spend the next couple of years studying what 5G should be or how it should operate. The future has a way of inventing itself. Turning innovators loose is far preferable to expecting committees and regulators to define the future. We won’t wait for the standards.” – Tom Wheeler, FCC Chairman (LINK)](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1UZ6BT0auVbxj_03rjALjvMMRQySb4Elqf5e0Trn20sQ/edit?usp=sharing)_
[

The 5G buildout is infrastructure intensive, and requires towers on top of lamp posts and telephone poles every twelve homes. This puts every American, especially children, at high risk for the carcinogenic and other effects of this technology. To ignore this massive risk is the highest form of negligence. Those responsible will pay, **but the true damage will be unjustifiable and irreparable harm to the lives of hundreds of millions of American citizens.**

The longer we ignore this reality, the higher the price American citizens will pay in the form of disease and skyrocketing healthcare costs. **Insurance giants are aware of these facts, as are Cell Companies**. Lloyd’s of London has now excluded any liability coverage for injuries, “directly or indirectly arising out of, resulting from or contributed to by electromagnetic fields, electromagnetic radiation, electromagnetism, radio waves or noise.” In its 2013 Emerging Risks report, Swiss Re moved the risk of EMFs affecting the insurance industry in the future from “medium” to “high”, due to courts ruling in favor of EMF claimants and “unforeseen consequences of electromagnetic fields”. In its 2014 annual report, AT&T states “as we deploy newer technologies, we also face current and potential litigation relating to alleged adverse health effects of customers or employees who use such technologies including, for example, wireless handsets”, as does Verizon “… our wireless business also faces personal injury and consumer class action lawsuits relating to alleged health effects of wireless phones or radio frequency transmitters, and class action lawsuits that challenge marketing practices and disclosures relating to alleged adverse health effects of handheld wireless phones. We may incur significant expenses in defending these lawsuits. In addition, we may be required to pay significant awards or settlements.”

People want faster connections because they trust that if there were any significant evidence of harm, the responsible authority (the FCC) would make it known through stringent regulations, warning labels, and media attention. Ironically, the FCC is creating massive consumer demand for this technology by ignoring hundreds of researchers and thousands of studies contradicting industry interests, and suppressing scientific discussion at press conferences. The FCC has employed, and continues to employ its power to assist the wireless industry in constructing as many Cell Towers and Cell Antennas as they can, as fast as they can, wherever they want, including atop public schools. The FCC is acting unconstitutionally and is not working for the People, but for the Industry. This gross Violation of Democracy and Human rights must end immediately to preserve the Health, Welfare, and Safety of American citizens and all Posterity.

**America will never be “Great Again” as long as our government allows this Orwellian suppression and ignorance of scientific evidence and the propagation of carcinogenic technologies to continue without any consideration of the Health, Welfare, and Safety of American citizens.** We will continue to pay in the form of America’s massive epidemic of chronic diseases until the FCC is rebuilt with non-industry experts to put the People first, before all considerations, including industry profit, as the government of the United States of America ought and was ordained to do by the Constitution of the People.

To end this unconstitutional violation of the rights of American citizens, four things must occur:

1) Repeal or Amendment of the 1996 Telecommunications Act in alignment with the precautionary principle, i.e. that antennae should not be erected in residential areas or in the vicinity of childcare centers.

a. This act has stripped all States and local governments of their power to consider the potential adverse health impacts of RF radiation from Cell Towers and Cell Antennas, when a wireless company files any type of zoning application seeking to install them.

b. In addition to imposing this new time constraint, the FCC further ruled that wireless companies can sue local governments in court if they fail to render a decision within the deadlines which the FCC has imposed upon them.

c. Furthermore, this act prevents anyone from suing a Telecommunications company for damages incurred due to the use of their technology.

2) Appointment of an FCC Chairman who will stop the 5G rollout and take a cautionary stance on the further development and current use of wireless technology or,

3) The dissolution of the FCC and replacement with a balance body of non-industry researchers who are qualified to protect Public Welfare and not industry interests alone

4) A cautionary stance on the development of technologies in general where there is significant dissent from qualified scientific researchers, in order to protect the safety of unknowing citizens

To echo Martin Luther King Jr.: “Nothing in the world is more dangerous than sincere ignorance and conscientious stupidity”. We have not forgotten our rights as Americans, and we will hold the industry and responsible governmental authorities responsible for any and all damages occurred due to this negligence. Please do not be a president who irreparably insults the Health, Welfare, and Safety of future generations by allowing the FCC to disregard scientific evidence and suppress discussion on the biological effects of wireless technology, particularly 5G.

\- Matthew Maruca

Narberth, Pennsylvania](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1UZ6BT0auVbxj_03rjALjvMMRQySb4Elqf5e0Trn20sQ/edit?usp=sharing)

### ShannyM — 2016-12-22T20:34:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sex-and-acute-head-pain.18891/post-205679>

Oh my, such a personal question but here it goes:

A month ago, seconds before the point of climax I had an intense rush of pain in my head. It felt like the lining of my brain or skull was being pulled from the back of my neck and traveled around my head over the top to my forehead and behind my eyes. So freaking intense and painful it brought tears to my eyes and afterward I bawled like a little girl. Wtf? It happened twice more after but no where as painful and sustained.

My body is lacking something...???

Has anyone else experienced this or am I odd? I don't believe it's only a female thing, I'll assume men have experienced this as well.

### Lahelada — 2016-12-24T01:23:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sex-and-acute-head-pain.18891/post-205727>

From what I could figure it migraines at point of orgasm are due to insufficient Prolactin being available at the time .I think in my case I was a bit happy go lucky with the MB dosis. Mine took a bit of tweaking only and it disappeared in a week at the most never to return.

Don't stress,test..

### ShannyM — 2016-12-24T05:30:02+0200

Ok got it. The excruciating episode happened after I started the epi-paleo rx which is why I posted about it and was very puzzled by it. After the mentioned episode I began the Mitochondria Rx and the pain didn't happen but I could feel it try to roar it's ugly head and I "willed it away" so to speak - I meditate and can control tensions and discomforts - but that puppy snuck up on me when I least expected it. I haven't done any of the reading on methylation so I'm in the dark as of yet. I'll study up, I assume it has to do with how your body processes hormones which explains partly why my sex hormones are out of balance.

I think it is related to breathing during the excitement leading up to the orgasm.

# Thread: Spectral comparisons of growth lights
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/spectral-comparisons-of-growth-lights.18898/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### findinghealth — 2016-12-27T17:13:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/spectral-comparisons-of-growth-lights.18898/post-205823>

Chameleon Plasma achieves pretty complete spectrum according to their measurement.
[http://chameleongrowsystems.com/grow-light-grow-lights-grow-lighting-spectral-specific/](http://chameleongrowsystems.com/grow-light-grow-lights-grow-lighting-spectral-specific/)

They seem to compare their measurement against "Action Spectrum" for plants. File below gives som explanation of what "Action Spectrum" is:
[https://www.heliospectra.com/sites/default/files/general/What light do plants need\_5.pdf](https://www.heliospectra.com/sites/default/files/general/What%20light%20do%20plants%20need_5.pdf)

"An absorption spectrum defines the wavelengths that are absorbed. An action spectrum defines the wavelengths that are most effective for photosynthesis. In other words, it is the portion of the spectrum that does the work. This is what is most important in plant growth and metabolism. It is important to note that light absorption and light utilization are two different phenomena."

\-------------------------
Newbie Question: Is there any work done on "Absorbtion spectrum" vs "Action Spectrum" curves for humans?

### Jack Kruse — 2016-12-27T17:27:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/spectral-comparisons-of-growth-lights.18898/post-205824>

# Thread: Electricity at night
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/electricity-at-night.18901/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Lisbeth — 2016-12-28T11:08:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/electricity-at-night.18901/post-205851>

hi

Does anyone here turn off their electricity supply at night (at fuse box or elsewhere?)

Have you noticed any difference in self or cohabitants?

How does that work with fridge/freezer? Any other problems?

Thank you

### Dimitri — 2017-01-12T03:13:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/electricity-at-night.18901/post-206647>

I keep the electricity permanently off in my bedroom which is not a problem since I don't do anything else in there. Luckily my fuse box allows me to turn off the electricity just to my bedroom. It's convenient keeping the electricity off so I don't have to go up and down to the basement everyday to turn it on and off. Using a body voltage meter I got a reading of around 3 volts when lying on my bed with the power on. With it off I get a reading of .175 volts which is a big difference. EMF help center recommends getting your living spaces to .100 volts or lower, but I'm not sure if there's anything else I can do to get the reading even lower.

I've only been doing this for a couple weeks so haven't noticed any different in the quality of my sleep yet. I still fall asleep in about 20 minutes and wake once in the middle of the night and sleep for about 9 hours. I don't wake up feeling very refreshed many days but maybe with time this will change.

One caveat according to EMF help center is that when you turn off the power to one part of the house, the voltage in another part of the house could potentially increase I guess because there might be some stray voltage in your house's circuitry. I tested multiple rooms of my house before and after turning off the breaker and found that there was no difference. I would definitely test the voltage before and after of your bedrooms at the very least to make sure you're not accidentally frying someone else.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-01-12T03:58:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/electricity-at-night.18901/post-206657>

Kill switches at night are wise.

### Just Breathe — 2017-01-26T23:11:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/electricity-at-night.18901/post-207407>

Yes. Cut it in the bedroom and nearby rooms. Keep your kitchen one on, of course!

# Thread: Tens Unit Usage
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tens-unit-usage.18902/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Diana Ugoretz — 2016-12-28T17:27:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tens-unit-usage.18902/post-205860>

In November I listened to a Ben Greenfield podcast entitled "How To Explore Your Body & Your Body’s Electrical Charge In Unprecedented Detail: The Future Of Body & Brain Imaging" which featured Jack Kruse.

I have used a tens unit, but after listening to that podcast I had a question about where it would be best to place the patches.

If I have degeneration in my L4 and L5 and also have SI joint pain especially the left SI Joint due to Ankolysing spondilitis, where would be the best place to put the patches.

If anyone has insight on that, I would appreciate it.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-12-29T03:45:45+0200

The key to getting it to work is not just where to put it........if your BUN/creat ratio is high and your D low, which is likely with that autoimmune condition a TENS wont work unless both are fixed at the same time.

### Allin — 2017-01-10T20:21:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tens-unit-usage.18902/post-206537>

In the morning session, I would drink a 16 oz of cold water, do CT on my lower body by sitting in tub from belly button on down for 30 minutes, then attach the four leads of the tens unit to my lower back bony area (1 on right and 1 on left) and to the back of my left and right mid-thighs. Set the TENs unit to 100 Hz and time for 15 minutes. Repeat a session in the evening but add a candle and meditation, Pandora has a Yoga channel which is free.

After 2-3 days, I would add to the morning session 20-30 minutes of sun to my back side as naked as possible, even when it is cloudy. I would do sun after my CT session and would add eating a can of sardines everyday. After doing this for 2-3 weeks, I would add 2-3 drops of MB to my morning cold water, 4-5 days a week.

If your back has been going downhill of 10 years, I would target doing this protocol for 10 months. In my experience you do not get better a little each and everyday. You will improve in steps and not in a linear fashion.

This template will get you started and after doing it for a couple of weeks, you will start picking up changes or modifications from your body to this basic template. Somedays you might need to do 40 minutes of CT, TENs unit for 2 - 15 minute sessions or 60 minutes in sun split into 30 minutes a side. What is this going to cost you, very very little money and mostly for the sardines to get the DHA you need. Get it done!!

### Michalis — 2017-01-11T18:10:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tens-unit-usage.18902/post-206619>

Allin, thank you very much indeed!

Ankylosing spondylitis is a type of arthritis in which there is long term inflammation of the joints of the spine. Typically the joints where the spine joins the pelvis are also affected.

No real need to read what they say here:
[https://www.spectracell.com/clinici...center/online-library-mnt-arthritis-abstract/](https://www.spectracell.com/clinicians/clinical-education-center/online-library-mnt-arthritis-abstract/)

[https://www.spectracell.com/order/](https://www.spectracell.com/order/)

$390

### Martha Ray — 2021-01-21T03:47:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/l-tryptophan.18909/post-294799>

Thanks Cpt.Tired, I am improving my environment ....
**RE: L-Tryptophan as a supplement** For about 6 months I was taking [_Lidtke_ brand L-Tryptophan](http://store.lidtke.com/products/l-tryptophan-30capsules) every other night- because a _Cardio Ion Test_ indicated I was very Low.
I stopped about Nov 15, 2015 as I was not sure about the information I read in this article:

[Lesser Known Facts About Tryptophan Side Effects, The Case Against Taking L-Tryptophan Supplements](http://www.supplements-and-health.com/tryptophan-side-effects.html)
[**https://www.supplements-and-health.com/tryptophan-side-effects.html**](https://www.supplements-and-health.com/tryptophan-side-effects.html)

I was not sure of the motivation behind this article and I thought that it might be unfair bias but **I did stop taking** it because I was also having some nightmares on the nights I took it.

Flash forward to about a year ago I found a 100% Food Supplement, Vegetarian Tryptophan
_Item No. 797 - By Food Research and_ I've been taking it occasionally and feel it does helps my sleep and causing pleasant dreaming.

I really need to retest and study L-Tryptophan more to reevaluate. Because I only want to supplement what I really need and or only take supplements short term or as needed.
**I'd like to know what some others think of that article** **and what they might know about the pros and cons of supplementing with L-Tryptophan.**

**RE: Sleep**
Keeping room very cold, (IBT) Incline Bed Therapy and Mouth Taping at bedtime have all greatly helped our sleep in the past few years.

Sweet Dreams.

### Penny — 2021-01-22T22:47:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/l-tryptophan.18909/post-294848>

I just use the reptile light from Petco - but it's kind of bright - the UV light that JK recommends is really difficult to find - but my standard routine for sleep these days is: one can of oysters with garlic and siracha and turmeric/black pepper - chase it with 6 Kalamata olives (=salt) - one glass of Kirkland Malbec ($6.99/bottle and it's good) - then do not go to bed early - if you do the oysters too late (short of 3 hours of sleep) take a digestive enzyme so it doesn't screw up sleep... sometimes I add natto powder to a glass of water and drink it - but then before bed I do l-serine/magnesium citrate/inositol - all of these, I buy by the sack... just the straight powder... I was doing passion flower which is also great to knock you out - and now I have passion flower leaves (by the sack) and it appears to be too much of an effort to make the tea... but passion flower is great to take the edge off... when I get to bed, I read with a lamp that is red incandescent and I may or may not have the overhead light on - usually before bed, I play the piano and guitar - no screen time... lots of times I wake up with my hand still holding the book open... also, have a cool room and I use black out shades - the following is all micro ingredients brand...

**Micro Ingredients Pure Inositol Powder, Inositol B8 Powder, 1KG (2.2 Pounds), Strongly Supports Liver Health & Antioxidant, Super Inositol for Hair and Inositol for**

**US Origin Pure Magnesium Citrate Powder, 2 Pounds (32 Ounce), Powerfully Supports Cardiovascular Function, Relaxation and Nutrient Utilization, No GMOs and Vegan Friendly**

**Pure L Serine Powder, 500 Grams, Supports Production of L-Cystine, L-Tryptophan and Serotonin for Brain, No GMOs, No Gluten**
[Brand: MICROINGREDIENTS](https://www.amazon.com/s/ref=bl_dp_s_web_0?ie=UTF8&search-alias=aps&field-keywords=MICROINGREDIENTS)

Here is the natto powder - you can actually ferment yogurt with this too - great for K2 and gut flora...
[https://www.amazon.com/Soybean-Natu...c&sr=1-1-526ea17f-3f73-4b50-8cd8-6acff948fa5a](https://www.amazon.com/Soybean-Natural-Nattokinase-Freeze-Dried-Fermented/dp/B077T6HDZF/ref=sxts_sxwds-bia-wc-rsf1_0?cv_ct_cx=natto+powder&dchild=1&keywords=natto+powder&pd_rd_i=B077T6HDZF&pd_rd_r=426f1526-04bd-4662-b698-931bd330af77&pd_rd_w=snVYX&pd_rd_wg=SvLlE&pf_rd_p=e0f994a8-a359-40a9-8917-dadca71c7184&pf_rd_r=49B3TP5G3FGMK5E5AP1D&psc=1&qid=1611348153&s=hpc&sr=1-1-526ea17f-3f73-4b50-8cd8-6acff948fa5a)

These sacks will last you a very long time...

I also swim in the ocean in the afternoon a few times per week - which is very helpful for inflammation - oh, and when I take a shower, I rub epsom salts and baking soda all over my body - you can do a foot soak if you like -

Sometimes I use lavender essential oil and progesterone cream when I remember...
**NOW Solutions, Natural Progesterone, Balancing Skin Cream, 20 mg of Natural Progesterone Per Pump, Unscented, 3-Ounce**

**It is important to get out into the sun during the day and use blue light blockers at night - no led lights anywhere - plus see the sunrise/sunset - lots of times I will actually fall asleep watching the sun set...**

I know this is an old thread but I was researching how to halt and heal the progression of cataracts. I searched my own data and came up with a quote from an article **Lesser Known Facts about Tryptophan Side Effects.**

“The biologist Raymond Peat, PhD, pointed out how an animal study on **L-tryptophan demonstrated that the amino acid may increase the risk for cataracts by disrupting the energy metabolism of the eye lens, and thus Peat cautioned against the practice of using tryptophan supplements** (Peat, Spring 2006).”

Not sure if that is true but I looking for more information.

# Thread: Justine's Optimal Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/justines-optimal-journal.18913/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 48
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### SFBaySwimmer — 2017-01-01T04:16:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/justines-optimal-journal.18913/post-205979>

I wasn't sure if I was supposed to start a journal in the forum or on a separate blog.

[Here it is on blogger right now.](http://justineoptimaljournal.blogspot.com/) Posted below:

Hooray! One Month of CT & Ending 2016 with a Cold Swim 50 degrees!!

I started being more regimented about my cold thermogenesis protocol starting in December. I was already swimming in the bay, but hadn't really done much during the month of November (with not as much sunlight and really cold weather/rains).

But I read up more on CT & weight loss and decided to give that a try instead of water fasting this month. Then I ended up reading or hearing Jack Kruse on a podcast and became more intrigued by the other health benefits of CT.

So began my journey down another rabbit hole w/ regards to blue light, EMF's, RF's, and getting enough UV every day. It's now like an obsession.

My weight hasn't really changed much, I think it was around 119lbs yesterday morning, but it can range between 118-121lbs. It's also hard for me to judge changes to my body composition. it's minute if anything.

The biggest change is probably getting really restful sleep. There are still some nights where it's hard to sleep, but I can usually pinpoint it to alcohol consumption.

I've been wearing my blue blocking safety glasses everywhere, work, at parties/restaurants, etc. I get a lot of stares but mostly people are friendly and interested in why I'm wearing them. Some or most compliment me on them, thinking they are trend forward/fashionable.  Who knew that being a dork about blue light would make me hip?

I got my trimeter a few days ago and started testing EMFs around my home and then my parents' home. It's been pretty fascinating to see what has a lot of MF. We took a BART ride for 30 min, and we were pretty much being microwaved for that entire time... yikes! 100 milligause + for 30 min. That's crazy. I can't believe it.

My car only gives off 4-7 milligause, not great but a lot better than BART.

I found electrical plugs, lights that were bad at my parents' home. Letting them know what to change. I also got all of them blue blocking glasses to wear while on their devices or at night. My mom seems to like her, the others aren't as good about wearing them.

I really push myself out of bed each morning into the cold weather to find some sunlight. It doesn't help when it's a cloudy or rainy day. I'm curious what other folks do on those day. Is that a day when I should just give up on UV light or just go to the sauna instead?

Other than that, I do feel like it's hard to fit in real work at a computer while I'm doing CT, getting sunlight (tried nude bathing at beaches 2 times already!), or grounding/hiking. I feel like this could be a full-time job being as connected to the earth.

Not sure how to balance this with needing to focus and take the time to learn Ruby on Rails and also finish up work projects. :-(

But overall, I'm grateful for finding out about Jack Kruse and for all his riddles and tidbits that got me interested in reading more about mitochondria. It really pushed me to do a lot more swimming in the bay in December. I've gotten used to the 50 degree temps now and it doesn't hurt as much in the beginning anymore. I can swim about 25-30 minutes each time before my right hand starts cramping up. I don't want to be too far from the shore when that happens in case I need to get back to safety. But I'm also trying to absorb UV light outside after the swim, but man is it FREEZING after being in the water. I may need to bring a towel outside to help me dry off and a t-shirt to help me warm up a bit. I've done pushups, squats to try to warm myself up but I still end up shivering a bunch and feeling light headed.

Thanks for coming to my Optimal Journal and I'll update it around 1 X a week. Let me know if you have any questions about my protocols or systems.

### SFBaySwimmer — 2017-01-02T20:47:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/justines-optimal-journal.18913/post-206059>

**My maternal family history:**

\- Grandmother was born in Shanghai (PuDong district) she was adopted so don't know anything else about our maternal history.

She never really had any health issues, very independent, maybe a bit angry/anxious.

She immigrated to HK then migrated to the US when my mom got married to my dad. She died in 2004 in her late 80s from abdominal cancer. It came very suddenly and she didn't let anyone know. She did drink CocaCola every day (cans of it) and walked all over SF to do her errands.

\- Mom was born in Hong Kong. Came from an abused childhood background since daughters weren't valued. So she was focused on success in business world at a young age hence moving to the US. She worked in banking (VP) and then CFO at various companies. Now she's retired with my dad, she does a lot of gardening work. But she also spends many hours in front of a computer screen and in front of the TV.

She is very very critical of my sister, me and my dad. We try to avoid spending too much time with her since she is such a drain on our energies. She constantly criticizes the little things and makes a scowling face. She can turn on her charm with strangers or not immediate family so most people don't understand what we have to deal with.

She had pre-diabetes before, obese/fat (not sure percentage yet), white hair since her 30s, depression/bipolar (we never know what mood she'll be in and she takes it out on everyone in the immediate family), angry/aggressive/needy are her traits. Inside, I know she can be kind but she's like a porcupine.

She had one surgery back in the late 80s for a huge uterine fibroid. She ended up getting some other fat removed as well. She's far-sighted now and uses reading glasses and can't read things up close.

\- One older sister by 1.5 years. She lives in Bali, likes being away from everything, not much internet out where she is, she goes barefoot most of the time over there. I think it's been good for her health, but she still seems to have some frenetic energy -- but that could just be leftover from her 20 years in NYC in the fashion world. She has PCOS and has had multiple fibroids removed from her uterine area. Myopia and astigmatism but she got Lasik decade ago and can see fine now. Melasma on her face.

\- Myself -- I haven't had any surgeries. But I have had ankle sprains on one ankle from a basketball accident in college, persistent neck pain from a ski accident 10 years ago, and last year injuring my knee while backpacking around Patagonia --nothing is hurting at the moment though.

Three years ago I had my eggs frozen and found my FSH and AMH levels weren't at an optimal level. I became very depressed during that time period since being a mom was very important to me at the time. I worked on improving it with acupuncture and supplementation and seemed to be able to improve it after traveling around SE Asia for 3 months (probably all that UV light I was getting and being away from internet and wifi a lot in Myanmar).

I was pre-diabetes over a year ago but was able to reverse that with diet changes (water fasting and potato only diets). Got my labs done last week and I'm waiting for the results to post them.

Brought my Trimeter to work yesterday and last night when at dinner/lecture. It was fun to show my coworkers the levels of MF's at their laptops and with different lights.

Levels weren't too bad at work, I also try to work right underneath a roof window so I get some UV, although it's mixed with the fluorescents in the building.

At dinner, I took out my trimeter at a fancy restaurant with a date. It was surprising to see the trimeter show extremely high MF's in the restaurant pretty much all over. At 100 or more, I used the trimeter to find a place with slightly less -- 10-30 milligausse. I didn't want to be a pain and find another place at the last minute. I had chosen this place specifically for the liver so that I could up my iron intake, too.

Food was delicious, but afterwards I really felt dehydrated and all night I needed lots and lots of RO water. Still feeling it today and I had pretty anxiety-filled dreams even though there isn't much going on in my life that should cause them. Career is going well, dating too. Working on relationships and wellness though this site and other books.

I think I need to take a swim in the bay tonight to try to counteract all the MFs. :-/ Anyone else have MF's affect them so much?

Also my date was curious about the type of MF my Trimeter is measuring and if that is indeed what's causing body harm. I assumed it was the right type of MF but I'll have to research this more.

SF has a lot of nasty nnEMF.........and good geo-magnetic opportunity

A large number of studies have identified significant physical, biological and health effects associated with changes in Solar and Geomagnetic Activity (S-GMA). Variations in solar activity, geomagnetic activity and ionospheric ion/electron concentrations are all mutually highly correlated and strongly linked by geophysical processes. A key scientific question is, what factor is it in the natural environment that causes the observed biological and physical effects? The effects include altered blood pressure and melatonin, increased cancer, reproductive, cardiac and neurological disease and death. Many occupational studies have found that exposure to ELF fields between 16.7 Hz and 50/60 Hz significantly reduces melatonin. They are also associated with the same and very similar health effects as the S-GMA effects. The cell membrane has an electric field of the order of 105 V/cm. The ELF brain waves, the EEG, operates at about 10-1 V/cm. Fish, birds, animals and people have been shown to respond to ELF signals that produce tissue electric gradients of ULF/ELF oscillating signals at a threshold of 10-7 to 10-8 V/cm. This involves non-linear resonant absorption of ULF/ELF oscillating signals into systems that use natural ion oscillation signals in the same frequency range. A long-lived, globally available natural ULF/ELF signal, the Schumann Resonance signal, was investigated as the possible plausible biophysical mechanism for the observed SGMA effects. It is found that the Schumann Resonance signal is extremely highly correlated with S-GMA indices of sunspot number and the Kp index. The physical mechanism is the ionospheric D-region ion/electron density that varies with S-GMA and forms the upper boundary of the resonant cavity in which the Schumann Resonance signal is formed. This provides strong support for identifying the Schumann Resonance signals as the S-GMA biophysical mechanism, primarily through a melatonin mechanism. It strongly supports the classification of S-GMA as a natural hazard. [https://researcharchive.lincoln.ac....EMR\_Schumann\_Resonance\_paper\_1.pdf?sequence=1](https://researcharchive.lincoln.ac.nz/bitstream/handle/10182/3935/90_n1_EMR_Schumann_Resonance_paper_1.pdf?sequence=1)

### SFBaySwimmer — 2017-02-14T18:57:46+0200

EMF Consultant & Snow Camping Updates...

\- I went on a snow camping trip 2 weekends ago up near Lake Tahoe. It was a trial run, my fave part was building my snow trench and cave for sleeping in -- a little faraday cage.

\- Last weekend I had an EMF consultant over for 4 hours or more. Good news, my place is actually really good! No bad wiring or electrical fields. My bedroom has measurements of 0 in electrical and magnetic. I do have a neighbor's wifi router by my bed that I need to mitigate. It's not super high but it's definitely a concern. I'll look into fabric or a canopy to help with that.

\- Turns out no cell towers can get into my apartment since there are buildings blocking the way and I'm on a lower level. Phew! I know that may not last with 5G but so far it's good news.

\- The other area I need to mitigate is my wifi router (which I turn off at night mostly). It was interesting to see how much higher the radio frequency was all over my home with the wifi router on. Seeing the numbers with my own eyes made me more vigilante about turning off my wifi and getting all my devices wired in.

\- Just got my Jins night vision prescription glasses - even though it's less orange than my cheaper glasses it seems to make me sleepier at night.

\- Also got my blood drawn yesterday for DHEA and other tests to see how my supplementation is working out.

I think you should talk to him. I was not very up on DHEA until reading your post so I read what Dr Kruse wrote way back then about it. Physician involvement may be good the way I read it anyway. Other than the labs "out of range" do you actually feel worse better ? How is your sleep ? One lab value on its own does not tell you anything and being out of range on one lab value can actually mean that you are more optimal than what is currently considered normal.

Read here [https://www.jackkruse.com/hormone-cpc-1-dhea/](https://www.jackkruse.com/hormone-cpc-1-dhea/)
An extract here:
"
Dehydroepiandrosterone (DHEA)
15 mg to 200mgs, depending upon your physician’s advice. Do not use a podcast as a dosing guide. Recently, I had to treat someone who got real bad advice from an ‘untrained guru’ about this very issue."

This next post comes close to your situation but as in all case s N=1

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/dhea-s-and-teststerone-results.5794/page-1](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/dhea-s-and-teststerone-results.5794/page-1)

Read the whole post but this post on page 3 is key. [https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/dhea-s-and-teststerone-results.5794/page-3#post-78671](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/dhea-s-and-teststerone-results.5794/page-3#post-78671)

### Lahelada — 2017-03-02T18:49:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/justines-optimal-journal.18913/post-209528>

I am neither an expert nor a doctor but looking at your labs I am wondering about the sun exposure/quantum yield of your location. When do you take sun , how much of you is exposed etc ? Where do you take sun? City centre,country side. I know you are a bay swimmer but do you get pm sun exposure after 3 pm let say ?

### SFBaySwimmer — 2017-03-03T08:21:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/justines-optimal-journal.18913/post-209545>

> [Lahelada said:](/goto/post?id=209528)

Where do you take sun? City centre,country side. I know you are a bay swimmer but do you get pm sun exposure after 3 pm let say ?

Thanks for responding Lahelada -- I'm not sure what you mean by quantum yield (I'm guessing you mean nnEMF exposure?).

I'm hearing from others that these protocols don't work that well for women compared to men... so I think I may stop and go back to what worked for me last year.

With regards to sun exposure:
I've been wearing my Kiniki swimsuit as a top to work -- and I found a rooftop near my work where I can do sunbathing at around noon for my lunch break. I sometimes stay out there for a good hour in just the swimsuit.

I wear shorts + sportsbra or my Kiniki and shorts/ just swimsuit in the morning, not as much sun exposure after 3pm.

Mostly in the city (but by the waterfront) since I live in the city. I'm going snow camping for 3 days starting tomorrow up in the mountains and will try to wear short sleeves and get as much sun as I can without getting hypothermia.

### Lahelada — 2017-03-03T18:24:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/justines-optimal-journal.18913/post-209566>

My recommendation would be to be less guided by the opinion of others but do your own diligence. I noted that you quote "on the advice of" . Any advice you take from a third party is in the end your advice you give to yourself. Without knowledge you cannot evaluate.
I have heard the same voices as you that it does not work for women as well as..etc. It is as basic as AA though. It works if you work it. Once you stop bargaining and take action it does. Careful,it has not worked gpr me at 100% yet either but I am coming to new conclusions about that.
Quantum yield is a the amount of sunlight that actually reaches you. In a lot of places you have smog,cloud cover EMF domes that impede a varying percentage of sunlight from reaching you. The second part of the equation is whether you can use all the light you are getting yet. Hydration and correct lipid levels play a role here.

### Inger — 2017-03-13T18:54:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/justines-optimal-journal.18913/post-210262>

22 % body fat for a woman is pretty ideal I think. To be skinny might not be healthy after all....

That said, Jack has said somewhere, that if one eats lots of fatty seafood (like, really much) and CT a lot, body might make some "blubber"
To isolation from the cold. Like the arctic whales and animals do.
This is not unhealthy at all, but maybe not what is fashionable today

What kind of seafood are you eating?
If it is most fatty fish like salmon etc. maybe switch to more mussels, oysters, cod and other not so fatty animals. That way you still get all the minerals and benefits from the sea but a little less omega 3.

I eat lots of mussels BTW. They are really good for you!

Thanks Inger. Yeah 22% was pretty ideal but then I gained 5 lbs with Epi Paleo Rx. So I'm working to get back to my ideal body composition again. As Jansz says, I need to work on having kids soon (I am) but the search for a partner requires not being a big blubbery girl, ya know?

Still eating my fish (mostly cod, oysters, shrimp and sardines), getting sunlight, getting my place protected from nnEMF, grounding etc. I don't worry as much about following these "protocols" as rigidly now since I've seen some of the negative effects it has on me.

### MSH — 2017-01-03T23:29:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alcohol-and-depression.18922/post-206100>

I don't drink much, maybe 3-5 times a year on special occasions and usually only a couple of glasses of wine. But, for the next 3-5 days afterwards my mood is very low and I feel very irritable. Why would such a small amount of alcohol cause such a strong reaction? It seems to me that this would be evidence of something being way out of whack! But what is out of whack or what to do about it ( other than not drinking) eludes me. I'm more concerned about its profound effect on me and what that says about my health that I need to correct than I am about drinking alcohol as I can do without that. Thanks to anyone who has any ideas.

### nonchalant — 2017-01-03T23:50:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alcohol-and-depression.18922/post-206101>

I think Jack said that alcohol affects the mitochondria adversely. If you are not able to spin your ATPase well with light, you would get a health hit. I avoid alcohol also, though in the past I've found a single glass of wine helps if I'm frustrated or angry.

Perhaps CT would help. It teaches the mitochondria to uncouple, and uses the heat generated to spin the ATPase separate from the ETC.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-01-17T18:08:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alcohol-and-depression.18922/post-207004>

Some of my friends ask me: if Glyphosate is in say.... your wine.... is it safe to consume?
I sold all my Cali wine because every bottle I tested including the Organic stuff had it present.
What is the problem fundamentally with glyphosate? It replaces glycine in proteins and collagen is the number one protein in your body (71% by volume) that uses glycine. Glycine is unique among amino acids in that it is achiral. Chiral amino acids offer the ability of cells to polarize sunlight. Glycine is used in places in cells where we do not want to use the Faraday effect of polarization so this changes optical signaling. Glycine can fit into hydrophilic or hydrophobic environments, due to its minimal side chain that contains only one hydrogen atom. Glyphosate is chemically unusual and very different than glycine because of its "achiral ability" because glyphosate's nitrogen atoms chelate to surrounding metal atoms in proteins ruining their stereochemistry and tertiary and quaternary structure. When this pestacide's nitrogen binds to the copper ions in collagen then all versions of collagen's sterochemistry becomes fixed atomically even when sunlight hits it and the protein loses its ability to become piezoelectric and release light or a DC electric signal to water shells around collagen. This stimulates ubiquitin marking and lead to many problems in tissues. This bonding also affects the protein side chains electric fields and this ruins the Faraday effect in tissues and tissues loose their optic-magnetic coupling of growth and metabolism pathways. The amino acid glycine does not chelate metal atoms and this allows sunlight to be trapped in cells and have that light polarized in a specific way that cells use for signaling. Any food with glyphosate residue cause the colllagen in those cells not to transfer energy to the EZ and it loses its ability to remain piezoelectric. For those who do not know collagen is the major N-type semiconductor in humans, so when glyphosate replaces glycine all proteins where this replacement occurs under go dielectric collapse. This means energy leaves drop in these tissues because of the effect on the exclusion zone of water which is dielectric crystal. This causes you to have a lower redox evne if you were naked on the equator with no clothing on. This is something most food guru's whiff on. Too much physics for them to explain.

# Thread: What does CT make me more tied?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-does-ct-make-me-more-tied.18925/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 14
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Lydz — 2017-01-05T08:10:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-does-ct-make-me-more-tied.18925/post-206150>

I have CFS. I started CT in Sept swiming at a cold lake 1x per week. Overall I feel better but as it's gotten colder I notice I'm exhausted after my 2o min in the water. I did the polar bear swim here in Vancouver Jan 1st in the ocean. With the frigid air temp it's the coldest I've been. Water was 7 degrees C. I slept for 2 days after. It felt like post workout exhaustion. Why is this? It's hard to get in the cold water knowing I'm going to be out of it for the next 2 days even if the next 5 after are great.

### caroline — 2017-01-05T09:59:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-does-ct-make-me-more-tied.18925/post-206152>

Did you follow the C/T protocol?

### Lydz — 2017-01-05T16:55:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-does-ct-make-me-more-tied.18925/post-206160>

No because I started months before discovering this blog (which was about a month ago). There is a lot of information here to process! I have red this blog every day since I found it and still feel like I don't know much!
I used the wim hof method. I started with cold showers every day and worked up to swimming in the lake. I combined that with his breathing techniques. I've notice I've got more neg effects as the outside temp dropped.

### DLO — 2017-01-13T21:52:50+0200

# Thread: How concerned should I be of lightning striking my earthing rod?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-concerned-should-i-be-of-lightning-striking-my-earthing-rod.18929/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Nicolaj Sølvsten — 2017-01-07T08:43:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-concerned-should-i-be-of-lightning-striking-my-earthing-rod.18929/post-206259>

Will my earthing system attract lightning to an extent that I should be worried?
The grounding rod has a 100kOhm resistance built into it and possibly also the earthing sheet aswell.

I am sleeping in a tent in a forest lying on an Earthing bed sheet connected to a grounding rod that is connected to the ground. I am concerned that the grounding rod will attract the lightning significantly. { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

Once, I was caught in a thunderstorm and I immediately disconnected the earthing product from the grounding rod and ran to my home 10 minutes away. This was an insanely unpleasant experience and I am now very terrified of getting struck by lightning. I know the odds of getting struck are very small but that assumption is based on a sample of people mostly not connected to the earth.

Location: Denmark, northern Europe, and yes it is cold, -13 degrees Celsius the other day

### Jack Kruse — 2017-01-09T23:02:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-concerned-should-i-be-of-lightning-striking-my-earthing-rod.18929/post-206421>

I would not worry about it that much. It could be an issue but I would imagine in your location I seriously doubt it is a huge risk

### Inger — 2017-01-10T17:53:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-concerned-should-i-be-of-lightning-striking-my-earthing-rod.18929/post-206517>

I would just watch the weather news and it is very seldom lightnings anyways and when there are some on the way the weather forecast will always tell
then those thunderstormnights you could sleep inside in your home

You are one lucky guy to sleep like that in the woods  How does it feel?

### Nicolaj Sølvsten — 2017-01-11T06:47:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-concerned-should-i-be-of-lightning-striking-my-earthing-rod.18929/post-206600>

I feel extremely well rested the following day, that is however, if my cortisol levels have not been spiked due to the fear of the weather conditions such as lightning and heavy storms.
I can truly recommend it!, I feel so in spirit the following day

### Jan Christer — 2018-03-02T20:24:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-concerned-should-i-be-of-lightning-striking-my-earthing-rod.18929/post-236158>

> [Nicolaj Sølvsten said:](/goto/post?id=206259)
>
> Will my earthing system attract lightning to an extent that I should be worried?
> The grounding rod has a 100kOhm resistance built into it and possibly also the earthing sheet aswell.
>
> I am sleeping in a tent in a forest lying on an Earthing bed sheet connected to a grounding rod that is connected to the ground. I am concerned that the grounding rod will attract the lightning significantly.
>
>
>
> Once, I was caught in a thunderstorm and I immediately disconnected the earthing product from the grounding rod and ran to my home 10 minutes away. This was an insanely unpleasant experience and I am now very terrified of getting struck by lightning. I know the odds of getting struck are very small but that assumption is based on a sample of people mostly not connected to the earth.
>
> Location: Denmark, northern Europe, and yes it is cold, -13 degrees Celsius the other day

If you don't sleep in an area that's particularly exposed to lightning, and where the rod is the highest object, then the rod itself will not attract lightning more than any other object, like a tent, bushes or trees. Lightning is attracted to the highest objects, whatever they are.
Rather than getting a direct hit, there is much greater concern of being injured or killed by ground currents from lightning striking nearby. That's how most livestock and people are killed by lightning. The only protection from this is good insulation from the ground, however that also negates the purpose of grounding.
It would also be important to have a 1 mA fuse in the connection to the rod, but this does of course not protect from currents from lightning that strikes close.

[http://www.aztrail.org/lightning\_safety.pdf](http://www.aztrail.org/lightning_safety.pdf)

Thesis: Tonsils are called "open wounds for the immune system" (German). They are not open wounds but sensors of information from our food. # Thread: Hello from Ontario, Canada
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-ontario-canada.18932/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### FNJORD — 2017-01-07T15:01:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-ontario-canada.18932/post-206267>

Hi there, I'm Freja from Ontario, F 35years old

I came across Dr. Ruse's site after going to a Naturopath and signing up for a doctor this December for what I thought may be an extreme adrenal fatigue. I had not had a doctor in over 20 years.

What I have is as of yet undiagnosed but I showed signs of excessive cortisol when I was a child (bad environment at home) and grew up mostly binging on wheat products, I was robust until puberty and went downhill. Found Atkins and lost weight and "got fit" in my early 20s however I also unfortunately discovered stimulants as I was trying to get the figure of a fitness model. After one cycle of a stimulant which went off the market my muscles were shot. However, I continued to take ephedrine/caffeine in order to be able to move.

What happened is that I progressively gained weight and became less "able" to tolerate things, I've always had excessive soreness from exercise even when I was at my most capable. In the last years it's been a steady gain of weight with exercise binges then crashes as I attempted to get my shit together and start working out. I was at the point where just to get out of bed (no exaggeration) I had to pop 400mg caffeine and 32mg ephedrine, then I would repeat the dose later on in the day one or two times to get through the day. Every day was excessive fatigue and muscle pain, and I began to be unable to tolerate the stress in my life. There was an excessive amount of alcohol in order to deal with the stress, and I did not go to bed until 1-3am. Get up between 5-7 depending on whether I was going to go to yoga. I tried to intermittent fast because it was the thing to do and failed many times, though admittedly I would be energetic --relatively speaking -- if I managed to get to the evening without eating, especially with all those stimulants.

This December it felt like I just couldn't take it and I scoured the internet. Decided I had adrenal fatigue, went to the naturopath and doctor but they were busy so went it on my own. Weaning from stimulants felt like it was going to kill me, thankfully I work for myself. Have been doing low/red light in evening and early to bed for several weeks. Got rid of ephedrine in Dec, am on 5th day caffeine free and 4th day of the Leptin Rx with very low carb. Am out fish shopping and getting a stock tank today for Cold immersion since I find cold showers useless and want a full body dip. There is a beach nearby but it's too polluted and the nearest is a 20 min drive, I'm not ready for full ice immersion to be honest, but I want to be.

All my life I felt like my body was not what it should be, functionally and "trying" to do the things I was supposed to never worked. It saddened me that I was dealt a shit card with genetics. However, I now have hope I can change that.

### caroline — 2017-01-08T00:01:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-ontario-canada.18932/post-206291>

Welcome Freja ....

where are you in Ontario? I was born in Toronto.

Please start a journal in the optimal journal section and give Dr. Kruse some health history ...also your Mom's and Gran's and siblings.

Have you read the C/T protocol? important to follow JK'sprotocols.....

Do you listen to JK's podcasts? Do you have JK's book? these are a quick way to get lots and lots of info.

Have you read the book - Health and Light by John Ott? this is a must read ....a fast, easy read.

Do you have blue blockers?

### FNJORD — 2017-01-08T15:23:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-ontario-canada.18932/post-206305>

Hi Caroline, thanks for the welcome!
I'll answer your questions and then start a journal
1\. In Hamilton, moving to Toronto this spring
2\. Yes, I read the protocol: my intro to CT was actually via Wim Hof, a lot of my friends follow his protocol and last summer I had the pleasure of a few weekends and 4 day trips up to Tobermory where I could dip into 50 degree water many times a day, it was amazing. Sometimes the beach nearby (in summer) is really cold, 50ish and other times it's in the 70s. I got a 110gallon stock tank and put it in my vestibule yesterday which is below freezing, basically just a concrete pad beside the house with a frame, some wood panels and a roof and filled it up with cold tap water and had a 25 minute soak. Felt fine, but afterward shivered for 3 hours! My forays into it will be more careful as the temperature in the tub drops and I'll have to find a way to keep it from freezing. Cold water getting into has been limited to me by the excruciating unbearable pain in my feet and hands/wrists when getting into cold water. I'm very very interested to see how this changes as I make the effort to reduce omega 6s and increase 3s. I always thought it was funny that my whole body could be submerged in the water but I had to keep my hands and wrists out to bear it, though I managed to get in the water by wearing neoprene 5-finger shoes to help my feet feel .. safer?
3\. I JUST discovered Jack's site and have, in my lack of stimulant induced lethargy been scouring it and reading everything I can. Haven't had time for the books and other things but that will come.
4\. On the must read list
5\. The glasses? I have them on my laptop and phone now (since Christmas) and have switched out lights in the house so that come evening (after 5 here) things darken to red lights.

Working on being able to expose skin outside. I do work for myself and currently am in a re-structuring of my business that I want to do mainly for stress and health reasons, as well as personal satisfaction and learning. Before Jack's recommendations of exercise in the afternoon I was attempting to structure things so I'd be intermittent fasting -- no food until evening and all exercise done in the morning, and morning sun if there is any. However this fall/winter I've been too exhausted for anything so it was not working well

### Jack Kruse — 2017-01-08T16:33:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-ontario-canada.18932/post-206311>

[https://www.jackkruse.com/time-12-adrenal-fatigue/](https://www.jackkruse.com/time-12-adrenal-fatigue/)

### FNJORD — 2017-01-08T21:19:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-ontario-canada.18932/post-206328>

### caroline — 2017-01-09T05:34:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-ontario-canada.18932/post-206369>

Do you have to move to Toronto?????? You need a log cabin in the bush! Can you move somewhere that has lots more sunshine?

# Thread: Claiming Health
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/claiming-health.18934/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 23
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### FNJORD — 2017-01-09T18:05:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/claiming-health.18934/post-206389>

Starting a journal here to log progress! I'm going to copy and paste a bit from my introduction:

I came across Dr. Ruse's site after going to a Naturopath and signing up for a doctor this December for what I thought may be an extreme adrenal fatigue. I had not had a doctor in over 20 years, not including a few checkups when pregnant.

What I have is as of yet undiagnosed but I showed signs of excessive cortisol when I was a child (bad environment at home) and grew up mostly binging on wheat products, I was robust until puberty and went downhill. Found Atkins and lost weight and "got fit" in my early 20s however I also unfortunately discovered stimulants as I was trying to get the figure of a fitness model. After one cycle of a stimulant which went off the market my muscles were shot. However, I continued to take ephedrine/caffeine in order to be able to move.

What happened is that I progressively gained weight and became less "able" to tolerate things, I've always had excessive soreness from exercise even when I was at my most capable. In the last years it's been a steady gain of weight with exercise binges then crashes as I attempted to get my shit together and start working out. I was at the point where just to get out of bed (no exaggeration) I had to pop 400mg caffeine and 32mg ephedrine, then I would repeat the dose later on in the day one or two times to get through the day. Every day was excessive fatigue and muscle pain, and I began to be unable to tolerate the stress in my life. There was an excessive amount of alcohol in order to deal with the stress, and I did not go to bed until 1-3am. Get up between 5-7 depending on whether I was going to go to yoga. I tried to intermittent fast because it was the thing to do and failed many times, though admittedly I would be energetic --relatively speaking -- if I managed to get to the evening without eating, especially with all those stimulants.

This December it felt like I just couldn't take it so scoured the internet. Decided I had adrenal fatigue, went to the naturopath and doctor but they were busy and wanted me to wait for any kind of testing and treatment for 3+ weeks over Christmas holidays so went it on my own. Weaning from stimulants felt like it was going to kill me, thankfully I work for myself and could rest. Have been doing low/red light in evening and early to bed for several weeks. Got rid of ephedrine in Dec, am on 5th day caffeine free and 4th day of the Leptin Rx with very low carb.

All my life I felt like my body was not what it should be, functionally and "trying" to do the things I was supposed to never worked. It saddened me that I was dealt a shit card with genetics. However, I now have hope I can change that.
\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_

Day 7 of being free of caffeine/ephedrine and doing the BAB
SERIOUSLY impressed with how normal ie: not totally trashed I feel. I wake up in the morning without an alarm clock between 545 and 630 and WANT to get out of bed. I don't remember when that last happened, it must have been in my early 20s.. before I seriously trashed things with that stimulant. I used to wake at 530/6am naturally.

I eat, and then don't really think about food which is opposite of how I was, it used to be always on my mind. Also, eating does not exhaust me, before starting the BAB if I ate I would have to nap afterward, which was another reason I tried to avoid eating/do IF until I was ready for bed or had free time to lie down.

Partner has commented on me looking fresher and happier. I have a happier disposition over all, don't hate him in the morning, I liked to secretly deep down think of myself as positive, happy and so on but to be honest I was miserable as fuck and could not manage to change my thought processes and emotions no matter how much I believe in being happy just because it's good to be so!

Due to recent increasing exhaustion plus the enhanced exhaustion from quitting stimulants I haven't been exercising beyond hot yoga 2x a week in the morning, but did two 3km walks yesterday and am starting to feel the urge to move, thank GODS.

Seriously need to lose fat. Like, SERIOUSLY. I feel like I'm wearing a fat suit. Nothing fits. I have so many nice clothes that I've gradually grown out of in the last years.. and that was starting at my "fat weight" which happened post pregnancies. Last summer I was cycling hundreds of kms a week, swimming 3x week, doing yoga as well as walking (all fueled by ephedrine) and IF and my weight went up. That was a major blow since I always thought if I could IF and exercise enough the weight would HAVE to go down. No, it didn't. Crashed in September and have not been able to do anything since, except for the 2 morning yogas. I need them!

Put a stock tank in the vestibule Saturday and have been dipping in it, added some warm water today because 1degree celsius was too much for my feet to handle, I'll get there.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-01-09T23:55:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/claiming-health.18934/post-206438>

In the past coffee consumption was protective. Coffee consumption going forward is going to be a bigger problem than solution for humans but the scene will be difficult for many to see short term. And the higher the network strength increases, as cell towers are taken down the more you will need to avoid coffee to maintain sanity and wellness. So if you play the market longer term, consider using instruments to short the coffee markets or stocks. The trend to follow: In the future people will crave it, to initially drive demand, but the more humans use it, the worse they will feel and the sicker they will get and this will confuse clinicians and coffee drinkers…….just watch
