### FNJORD — 2017-01-10T01:24:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/claiming-health.18934/post-206468>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=206438)
>
> In the past coffee consumption was protective. Coffee consumption going forward is going to be a bigger problem than solution for humans but the scene will be difficult for many to see short term. And the higher the network strength increases, as cell towers are taken down the more you will need to avoid coffee to maintain sanity and wellness. So if you play the market longer term, consider using instruments to short the coffee markets or stocks. The trend to follow: In the future people will crave it, to initially drive demand, but the more humans use it, the worse they will feel and the sicker they will get and this will confuse clinicians and coffee drinkers…….just watch

I wasn't even on coffee.. not strong enough, was taking caffeine and ephedrine. Not for fat loss, just to move in the am and make it through the day.
Today was my first day with consistent energy -- probably in my life, because I remember massive afternoon crashes every day after elementary school and throughout my life. I wasn't extremely energetic or wired, just there. Even, and consistent all day long. It's dark now and I had a big bowl of bone broth, so am getting into the quiet zone. Normally at this time I'd start trying to "get moving." I did sun my toes/calves today for about 25 minutes, I was surprised that I did not get cold out there.

I work with clients one on one which has been wonderful, but since starting this recovery journey I'm wanting to restructure my job to do some kind of distance work. My partner has a property up north on the lake (3 hours away) that is undeveloped and as soon as the snow melts we'll pop up there to get a look at the lay of the land and get to work on it. It was originally going to be a "getaway" spot for us near our favourite local vacation spot, but I'm just DREAAAAMMING of being there more often and making it a perfect little retreat for sunning and cold water dipping.

Tuna salad for lunch and a big bone broth and coconut bar (coconut mana, coconut oil, cocoa butter, stevia, maca, shredded unsweetened coconut) for an early dinner after 25 minutes in 3 degrees tub.

### FNJORD — 2017-01-11T19:24:20+0200

Went to a reputable fish guy today and bought cod, haddock and salmon. Soooo so so delicious, never thought I'd be a fish person. It's expensive, but then so is eating out, and not feeling good.

Made my own mayo with eggs, fresh squeezed lemon, spices and mct oil - I have gallons of it for my practice. My partner and I adore mayonnaise but I want it to be healthy.

One thing I noticed is that fish doesn't make me feel full, instead there is a sort of "pinched" feeling in my stomach. I'll have to get used to that.
I think I'm getting a bit warmer, the house is more than 10 degrees lower than I used to keep it before this CT. Granted I'm wearing a cozy fleece and socks but there is no wanting to turn it up.

Will do my walk in the sun (cloudy sun) and CT this afternoon. It's 4 degrees today so maybe I'll last 30 minutes, we'll see!

### FNJORD — 2017-01-13T15:50:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/claiming-health.18934/post-206774>

Is it possible to make changes quickly?

-   Change in your sweating pattern
-   Energy levels will rise
-   Hunger and cravings will become less and eventually disappear
-   When you awaken you will feel refreshed and well rested
-   **Men:** quicker weight loss
-   **Women:** Mood changes (calmer/sleepy), then sleep will improve. Clothes will fit differently and weight loss will occur if you continue the program

\-it's pretty cold around here since I've lowered my house temp, not many chances to sweat, though I had my sweatiest day ever at hot yoga the other day compared to barely breaking a sweat before. AND even in the cold house temperatures I'm feeling warmer, warmer in bed warmer in my body even if skin is cold etc.

\-energy levels are incomparable!! As I said I used to pop 400mg caffeine and 32mg ephedrine just to be able to move when I had to get out of bed in the morning. I hated getting up. Now I wake up, and am immediately alert, there is no grog. There is no grog in the day WTF?!? I have no afternoon slump and don't pass out after meals.

\-not hungry

\-mood has changed completely. I'm wearing stretchy clothes and it's only been like a week so no perceived changes in shape. However, last night I ate lunch at 230 and then did not eat after that and this morning I felt a bit of belly shrinkage.

\-"fibromyalgia" symptoms, I say symptoms because I never identified with the disease are mostly gone

\-red painful bumps on toes diminished after the UV exposure

Am I ready to make some shifts to start losing weight .. hopefully quickly? Can I skip dinner and add in some exercise in the afternoon? Due to the stimulant issue I don't feel like anything I would do would be high intensity, I know what it's like to do explosive exercises (were my fav. back in the fitness days) and mine would be more of a dribble than an explosion now. I want to start, just do not want to screw myself by acting too quickly.

Also, this early in the reset can I start skipping dinner and eating less? I would not skimp on the BAB.. I love it, it totally regulates me in a way I never believed possible.

Also, if I work out in the afternoon say after lunch -- do I need post workout recovery proteins?

Thanks so much for reading!!

### FNJORD — 2017-01-16T16:59:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/claiming-health.18934/post-206908>

I'm going to get my period soon and this weekend fell off the wagon a bit. Had some rice, ate too late. Had honey in my decaf coffee  Actually have been expecting it for the last 10 days but it hasn't come yet.

Doing some form of CT every day, it's freezing here so that makes it easy. I'm sitting 30 minutes in the tub most days, started to go for walks, yesterday I went for a short bike ride which was nice though my butt is as tender as a ripe peach..I need to break that in. Due to the info in the forums I'm going to start cycling outside instead of on the trainer inside. It's seriously too dangerous to do that when it's snowing here, because cars are fucking assholes and hate cyclists. Even nice and polite ones like me. Last year I signed up for a charity 200km bike ride, so am on the hook for the $2500 and the ride personally and eliminating cycling is not an option. The issue is going to be time in the saddle, I trained for a few months last year and did 100km no problem, my muscles were fine it was my butt that was sore as heck. I LOVE cycling and last summer the best times were the ones spent out in the country for hours on the road happily soaking up the nature on the bike. Except that one horsefly from hell that chased me for 8kms. I am ridiculously attractive to bugs that leave my partner alone, this is the only reason why I would not sleep outside!

Have decreased the avg temps in the house from 68-72, down to 55-60. I turn them up when doing a cold soak though, yesterday's soak had me shivering a lot. It's time to significantly reduce food now. I was afraid to because of the adrenal fatigue thing but feel like it should be ok if I'm getting cold soaks and stick to very strict keto.

### shah78 — 2017-02-20T21:40:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/claiming-health.18934/post-208966>

> [FNJORD said:](/goto/post?id=206389)
>
> Starting a journal here to log progress! I'm going to copy and paste a bit from my introduction:
>
> I came across Dr. Ruse's site after going to a Naturopath and signing up for a doctor this December for what I thought may be an extreme adrenal fatigue. I had not had a doctor in over 20 years, not including a few checkups when pregnant.
>
> What I have is as of yet undiagnosed but I showed signs of excessive cortisol when I was a child (bad environment at home) and grew up mostly binging on wheat products, I was robust until puberty and went downhill. Found Atkins and lost weight and "got fit" in my early 20s however I also unfortunately discovered stimulants as I was trying to get the figure of a fitness model. After one cycle of a stimulant which went off the market my muscles were shot. However, I continued to take ephedrine/caffeine in order to be able to move.
>
> What happened is that I progressively gained weight and became less "able" to tolerate things, I've always had excessive soreness from exercise even when I was at my most capable. In the last years it's been a steady gain of weight with exercise binges then crashes as I attempted to get my shit together and start working out. I was at the point where just to get out of bed (no exaggeration) I had to pop 400mg caffeine and 32mg ephedrine, then I would repeat the dose later on in the day one or two times to get through the day. Every day was excessive fatigue and muscle pain, and I began to be unable to tolerate the stress in my life. There was an excessive amount of alcohol in order to deal with the stress, and I did not go to bed until 1-3am. Get up between 5-7 depending on whether I was going to go to yoga. I tried to intermittent fast because it was the thing to do and failed many times, though admittedly I would be energetic --relatively speaking -- if I managed to get to the evening without eating, especially with all those stimulants.
>
> This December it felt like I just couldn't take it so scoured the internet. Decided I had adrenal fatigue, went to the naturopath and doctor but they were busy and wanted me to wait for any kind of testing and treatment for 3+ weeks over Christmas holidays so went it on my own. Weaning from stimulants felt like it was going to kill me, thankfully I work for myself and could rest. Have been doing low/red light in evening and early to bed for several weeks. Got rid of ephedrine in Dec, am on 5th day caffeine free and 4th day of the Leptin Rx with very low carb.
>
> All my life I felt like my body was not what it should be, functionally and "trying" to do the things I was supposed to never worked. It saddened me that I was dealt a shit card with genetics. However, I now have hope I can change that.
> \_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_
>
>
> Day 7 of being free of caffeine/ephedrine and doing the BAB
> SERIOUSLY impressed with how normal ie: not totally trashed I feel. I wake up in the morning without an alarm clock between 545 and 630 and WANT to get out of bed. I don't remember when that last happened, it must have been in my early 20s.. before I seriously trashed things with that stimulant. I used to wake at 530/6am naturally.
>
> I eat, and then don't really think about food which is opposite of how I was, it used to be always on my mind. Also, eating does not exhaust me, before starting the BAB if I ate I would have to nap afterward, which was another reason I tried to avoid eating/do IF until I was ready for bed or had free time to lie down.
>
> Partner has commented on me looking fresher and happier. I have a happier disposition over all, don't hate him in the morning, I liked to secretly deep down think of myself as positive, happy and so on but to be honest I was miserable as fuck and could not manage to change my thought processes and emotions no matter how much I believe in being happy just because it's good to be so!
>
> Due to recent increasing exhaustion plus the enhanced exhaustion from quitting stimulants I haven't been exercising beyond hot yoga 2x a week in the morning, but did two 3km walks yesterday and am starting to feel the urge to move, thank GODS.
>
> Seriously need to lose fat. Like, SERIOUSLY. I feel like I'm wearing a fat suit. Nothing fits. I have so many nice clothes that I've gradually grown out of in the last years.. and that was starting at my "fat weight" which happened post pregnancies. Last summer I was cycling hundreds of kms a week, swimming 3x week, doing yoga as well as walking (all fueled by ephedrine) and IF and my weight went up. That was a major blow since I always thought if I could IF and exercise enough the weight would HAVE to go down. No, it didn't. Crashed in September and have not been able to do anything since, except for the 2 morning yogas. I need them!
>
> Put a stock tank in the vestibule Saturday and have been dipping in it, added some warm water today because 1degree celsius was too much for my feet to handle, I'll get there.

socks (wool)or better yet diving boots/sox for the cold feet.

### FNJORD — 2017-03-11T17:08:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/claiming-health.18934/post-210122>

three things

1\. I tried raw oysters for the first time last night. OMG. **OMG** how could I have lived this long without them? Now I need to find a supply, eating them at a restaurant is pricey.
2\. Bloodwork was perfect, according to doctor. He tested a ton of things, but not vitamin levels. I am amazed to be honest, after the decade and a half of caffeine and ephedrine and alcohol in excess and atrocious sleep habits. I wonder if high healthy fats -- though I gained weight excessively -- was protective in some way. I did atkins as a teenager and learned that high fat low carb was the way I needed to be, so whilst there has been deviations and unhealthy additions my primary WOL was always based on meat, fat, and vegetables. Now it has shifted into a fishier way.
3\. Now that there is no ephedrine asthma is _really_ acting up, my gut feeling is that as we get into summer sun and more outdoors this is going to decrease, but I've got some meds for now.

# Thread: Grounded CT and a few questions
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounded-ct-and-a-few-questions.18935/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Hunger4More — 2017-01-09T21:38:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounded-ct-and-a-few-questions.18935/post-206406>

So we have a "spa" about a 30 min drive outside the city here. A nice cold "river" at 10 degrees C along with other pools at varying temperatures. Plenty of saunas. The best? All outdoors, cell phones are banned, and lots of trees around.

Jack, you introduced me to grounding while doing CT. Went last week for the day. In and out of the cold and sauna all day... I estimate I lost nearly 1% bodyfat from the day... and I don't have a ton of fat to lose! Thank you for introducing the combo of CT + grounding!!

My questions:

1\. I know that CT + grounding cause fat cell apoptosis? Can you expand on the mechanism that grounding helps with the apoptosis? Would love to learn more. Perhaps you can point to a blog, don't remember reading about it in the CT series.

2\. Am I losing some benefit by alternating the CT with some sauna? Or is there some benefit to adding sauna?

3\. I've noticed that CT doesn't have the same fat loss effect on me in the summer (its currently winter here). Is there any way to keep the fat loss going through the summer season?

4\. I've noticed that as I continue with the CT throughout the day it becomes much more difficult. First dip in the pool, I easily last 20 mins. Hours and several dips later, by the evening time, within minutes I start shivering uncontrollably. Do you think this is due to an accumulation of heat loss throughout the day, or perhaps my body temp dropping due to natural circadian rhythms? Just curious.

Once again - thanks for all that you do!!

### Jack Kruse — 2017-01-09T22:55:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounded-ct-and-a-few-questions.18935/post-206415>

your welcome. Science is never static. It is a moving target.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounded-ct-and-a-few-questions.18935/post-206416>

grounding increases the DC electric current which increase melatonin which increase your mtDNA control to control apoptosis and mitophagy. That is the basis of the Time and Ubi series. Read them now.......with this understanding.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-01-09T22:57:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounded-ct-and-a-few-questions.18935/post-206417>

2\. no
3\. it should not.
4\. redox is why

# Thread: Greetings from Argentina/Mendoza

### BrunoB — 2017-01-10T04:06:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-argentina-mendoza.18937/post-206478>

Hello . I am looking for input regarding of what I can do to help my situation.
I may have refractory celiac disease or perhaps other pathologies ? My lymph ganglia are inflamed to the touch and the tomography also said that the lymph nodes were inflamed. I have read that rcd is closely related to t cell lymphoma. I have many symptoms and I have permanent statorrhea confirmed by van kremer and because I see that it floats every day. Bloodtest and genetic tests confirm celiac disease. The final test for refractory celiac disease has not been done yet. However the tomography and MRI seem to exclude everything but.
I sleep about 7 hours per day,foloow low fructose paleo diet. I turn off the wifi every night. I use the pc several hours a day, about 4. . I use lenses to block blue light blue( dewalt.). I have difficulty keeping weight on. I am 5 ft 7 and currently weigh 140 lbs . I have a celltower 7 block from my house.
My mother had lymphoma, is bipolar, her mother (my grandmother) had depression, obsessive and tuberculosis
More issues: Paresthesia in head and extremities Buzzing in ears,my left foot has been inflamed since May and has never returned to its original size and artificial light bothering me in the night,
Would CT be a good idea in my case ,what else should I look at ,please?
Many thanks for any light that you may shed on this.

### caroline — 2017-01-10T04:27:30+0200

look at the C/T protocol.

Do you live in the city? what do you do? nnEMF???????

### Jack Kruse — 2017-01-10T04:32:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-argentina-mendoza.18937/post-206482>

Welcome. Celiac has many causes........right now most want to blame it on foods with grains and a "genetic predisposition" but prior to 1990 the incidence of this disease was less than 1% according to Robbins Pathology text. Today the incidence and prevalence is over 5% in some populations. My belief is that most of the causes of modern celiac like conditions is due to hypersenstivity to nnEMF. I think celiac is a manifestation of an allergy to nnEMF.

artificial light in the night is a no go! As is to be bombarded with nnEMF

# Thread: Loosely vs Tightly Coupled Haplotype
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/loosely-vs-tightly-coupled-haplotype.18938/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 34
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### kris90 — 2017-01-10T16:27:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/loosely-vs-tightly-coupled-haplotype.18938/post-206506>

I have a few questions regarding loosely vs tightly coupled.

My understanding is that (correct me if I'm wrong) the Northern European haplotype have more loosely coupled mitochondria, so less ATP and more heat is generated. Equatorial African haplotype have more tightly coupled mitochondria, so more ATP and less heat generated.

For those of us living in the Northern Hemisphere (I'm in Ontario, Canada), our mitochondria will be more tightly coupled in summer, and more loosely coupled in winter? How does this impact energy requirements (I would assume we burn more calories in winter than summer)?

What confuses me is that using CT seems to burn a lot of calories to maintain core temperature, while simultaneously increasing Leptin sensitivity which lowers appetite. So if we live in winter, and CT daily, should we be eating more, or less calories? Or, does that depend on how much fat mass we have (i.e. if we are storing excess fat, we can eat less, because CT helps burn that fat to feed the body, and less food is required, and on the contrary, if we are lean, then we should eat a high calorie/high fat diet in winter)?

In my personal experience, I am finding that I am eating less in the winter (maybe 2000 cals) and that seems plenty for me (i.e. not hungry), but in the summer, I was probably eating closer to 2500-3000 cals. I am actually leaner in the summer than I am in the winter, mind you I CT consistently year round.

One thing I find is that I often feel cold in winter. I do feel warmer after a high fat meal, but between meals (fasting) I find I have goosebumps a lot and feel cold (I do drink a lot of ice water). Is this because I am relatively lean (i.e. 10% BF)? Do I need to eat more calories to stay warm? Sometimes I might eat a little more than usual, but my weight seems to be very stable whether I eat 2500 cals vs 2000 cals or less. I wouldn't mind dropping to 8% BF as I am in summer, but I don't want to eat less food, because I think that will be counterproductive to staying warm.

### kris90 — 2017-01-10T19:38:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/loosely-vs-tightly-coupled-haplotype.18938/post-206533>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=206507)

15:57 - Now everybody in this room has a different efficiency of pumping protons out, and converting them to ATP. And we call that the coupling efficiency. So people that are very efficient at pumping protons out and converting them into ATP, they will require the minimum amount of calories for the maximal amount of ATP, and everytime you burn a calorie, you generate heat, so they will generate the minimum amount of heat for the maximum amount of work. On the other hand, an inefficient coupling system will require more calories to pump the same amount of protons and convert them to ATP. So now you will need to eat more calories for the same amount of ATP, but you've also burned more calories, so you generate more heat.

44:09 - Here in Africa if you want to reproduce and be a woman, you need to run away from lions. That means you have to be tightly coupled to have maximum ATP for calorie use. But up here the lions froze to death, so that's not a problem. Problem is you're gonna freeze to death. So how does that get solved? By accumulating mutations that decrease the coupling efficiency. Now that's making less efficient mitochondria so you'll eat more calories for the same amount of ATP, but generate more heat.

So it is true then, loosely coupled systems need more calories to support chemical energy (ATP) and thermal energy (heat). So technically, I should have a higher caloric requirement in winter than in summer.

Does a loosely coupled system correspond with living a more disconnected life (i.e. low quantum yield) and hence we need to consume more food to get electrons, whereas in summer time, high quantum yield allows us to harvest the sun's energy more easily, thus requiring less electrons from food? My mindset is validating this thought, however my experience seems counterintuitive: I eat more in summer, but am leaner than I am in winter.

### Inger — 2017-01-10T20:12:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/loosely-vs-tightly-coupled-haplotype.18938/post-206535>

I am loosely coupled too for sure. If I eat too little in winter I feel cold too...and lots of fat and protein warms me up. I wonder if the sun makes us lose weight in summer? In winter a little fat layer might help stay warm. The eskimos was not skinny either. Just my thoughts..... so we add fat in fall and slowly lose it so in late winter-spring when sun shines stronger we start getting slimmer? This would be practical, at least

### Inger — 2017-01-10T20:14:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/loosely-vs-tightly-coupled-haplotype.18938/post-206536>

Maybe grounding more in winter will help not get too fat
I do not get fat in winter really. Only if I eat crap drink too much wine late at night etc and get swollen but it goes away fast

### kris90 — 2017-01-10T20:48:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/loosely-vs-tightly-coupled-haplotype.18938/post-206542>

Yes, the sun must have something to do with it for sure. But ya, you're right, maybe the cold can help an "obese" person lose excess fat by burning it for heat, but for someone going into winter already quite lean (i.e. single digit BF %), maybe we accumulate a small layer of fat? That seemed to happen with me. Since mid November, I went strict keto, and since then it almost seems I have a slightly thicker layer of fat now than I did even in mid fall.

I feel like if I overeat on keto right now (winter), I won't gain any weight. But if I decide to do a long, extended fast, and eat only 1 meal (1/2 my normal caloric intake), I also won't lose any weight. Maybe my body is right where it wants to be and I have metabolic flexibility due to leptin sensitivity? Who knows.

### JanSz — 2019-09-02T20:51:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/loosely-vs-tightly-coupled-haplotype.18938/post-277007>

[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mitochondrion](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mitochondrion)

**Heat production**
Under certain conditions, protons can re-enter the mitochondrial matrix without contributing to ATP synthesis. This process is known as _proton leak_ or [mitochondrial uncoupling](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mitochondrial_uncoupling) and is due to the [facilitated diffusion](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Facilitated_diffusion) of protons into the matrix. The process results in the unharnessed potential energy of the proton electrochemical gradient being released as heat.[\[15\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mitochondrion#cite_note-Voet-15) The process is mediated by a proton channel called [thermogenin](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Thermogenin), or [UCP1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/UCP1).[\[82\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mitochondrion#cite_note-Mozo-82) Thermogenin is a 33 k[Da](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Atomic_mass_units) protein first discovered in 1973.[\[83\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mitochondrion#cite_note-Nicholls-83) Thermogenin is primarily found in [brown adipose tissue](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Brown_adipose_tissue), or brown fat, and is responsible for non-shivering thermogenesis. Brown adipose tissue is found in mammals, and is at its highest levels in early life and in hibernating animals. In humans, brown adipose tissue is present at birth and decreases with age.[\[82\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mitochondrion#cite_note-Mozo-82)

[http://krisdomi.blogspot.com/2017/01/mitochondrial-coupling-haplotypes.html](http://krisdomi.blogspot.com/2017/01/mitochondrial-coupling-haplotypes.html)

### JanSz — 2019-09-02T23:14:06+0300

//////////////////////////////
Mitochondria are the power houses of our cells and used as backup systems to generate energy by inputting electrons from food, and pumping out protons to produce ATP.
//////////////////////////////

> [kris90 said:](/goto/post?id=206506)
>
> For those of us living in the Northern Hemisphere (I'm in Ontario, Canada), our mitochondria will be more tightly coupled in summer, and more loosely coupled in winter? How does this impact energy requirements (I would assume we burn more calories in winter than summer)?
> \-----------
> In my personal experience, I am finding that I am eating less in the winter (maybe 2000 cals) and that seems plenty for me (i.e. not hungry), but in the summer, I was probably eating closer to **2500-3000 cals.** I am actually leaner in the summer than I am in the winter, mind you I CT consistently year round.

**2500-3000 cals.**
860 kcal=1Kw >>--->> food
2.500/860=0.0029075 kWh

Hi Kris;
Wonder if you can help in understanding how our bodies work.

For years I have always thought that we are living on energy that
comes from food (2500-3000)calories
and
sunlight.
I think that you think similarly.

We knew that our bodies are producing a lot of ATP daily (equal to about body's weight),
but not much of further thought (at least on my part).

I got awakening when I heard that
who really cares about all that ATP
thinking about that just makes the brain busy
It is much better to think that all that ATP will result in water production at one of many metabolic steps.
And that it is really lots of water being made daily.
7437 liters/day when resting doing nothing, 3x that much when working hard.
Even that, as big as it is, is not that impressive.
Better to look at is as, when resting our bodies burn 7437/9= kilograms of hydrogen.
That is a tremendous amount of energy. It can provide for 24 hrs daily power to lit 1358 sodium 1000W lamps to provide light to medium-size cities.

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/granpa-johns-optimal-journal.23952/page-2#post-275364](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/granpa-johns-optimal-journal.23952/page-2#post-275364)
Above and posts after that one.
\------
The intermediate question, how many street lamps there are in the biggest city in Canada?

So that is 1358 kWh daily at rest, 3x that at full throttle.
Now
860 kcal=1Kw >>--->> food
more interesting but not much
sunlight
Average over the entire earth = 164 Watts per square meter over a 24 hour day
Make it 10x more on the equator, and that is still nothing, make it 100x more, not much.
The energy coming in real-time from the sun can be put aside.
Sunlight is about information (that keeps it all together)(on edge)(of statistics laws of physics).
There is likely more sunlight information on the equator than on poles,
but I have my doubts that we must limit habitability to plus/minus 20-degree latitude.
I am voting for at least 60 (sixty).

We still need to discuss energy.
In my dreams, I have not thought of my self as such a heavy energy guzzler.
How many trees must work hard to support just one person, me.

\==================================
At this point it is not about apropriedness of calling:
**Mitochondria** are the power houses of our cells and used as **backup systems** to generate energy**.
**
**I would love to understand this topic.**
**Doubt if I ever get it entirely, but hopefully, it would be better than I understand it today.**
**
[@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/)
[@kris90](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19157/)

that energy is millions of times less than energy from (matrix, Cytochrome IV, CCO) water synthesis (that is not mentioned at all).
\-----------------------------------------------

Energy can't just disappear or appear.

### kris90 — 2019-09-04T22:41:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/loosely-vs-tightly-coupled-haplotype.18938/post-277164>

Water is the key. I try to look at it simply as you quoted above: Water stores those energies. When energy is stored in water, we need more external energy to "free" or release that energy. Cold water can store more energy, and our mitochondria release heat which releases that energy. So I think in cold environments when we uncouple, we burn more calories to split water because the sun is weaker, but we also produce more DDW in our mitochondria which helps us recapture that energy. I think that's why autophagy works best in cold/darknesses because of water density. So we can eat less even though we burn more, but we can also eat more calories to burn more. Most people think that the more calories you burn, the more you need to consume, but we know this is not true with the mitochondriac perspective. And on the flipside, consuming more calories (high fat/keto) when cold adapted will not cause weight gain/obesity if we can uncouple our mitochondria. We simply produce more heat and more water.

because
that synthesis happens regardless of latitude.
It happens at the equator and the poles.

CCO (cytochrome c oxidase) has 4 red light chromophores. We use red/IR light to drive ATP synthesis. This is done via sunlight in summer months or at the Equator in tightly coupled haplotypes, and via mitochondrial uncoupling in winter months or at the Poles in loosely coupled haplotypes.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-01-11T00:49:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/promagnet-sleep-pads.18940/post-206576>

Mitohack it and give us feedback.

### Haley_Pendragon — 2017-01-11T05:56:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/promagnet-sleep-pads.18940/post-206598>

Would be interesting too see how they go - they are a lot better priced than the magneticos. Let us know if you get one!

### MSH — 2017-01-11T16:27:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/promagnet-sleep-pads.18940/post-206611>

I may go ahead and order one. I'm hoping it will work out as a poor mans Magnetico. I'm thinking I will try out the travel sized one. It is 2X 3 feet and only runs $74 with shipping.

### MSH — 2017-01-11T18:56:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/promagnet-sleep-pads.18940/post-206621>

I ordered it and it should be here by the end of the month. I will give an update after I've used it for a few days.

### MSH — 2017-01-25T05:04:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/promagnet-sleep-pads.18940/post-207311>

Ok, so I actually got the pad in earlier than expected and have been able to sleep on it for a while. I must say that I am pleasantly surprised by what I've experienced thus far. The two biggest things that I've noticed is that I fall asleep faster and I am having more intense dreams. I do feel more refreshed when I wake up. I am impressed enough that I am going to by one of the larger pads now.

I called the company and the lady I talked was very knowledgeable and nice. I mentioned to her that I was going to review the product and then post my experience on a forum where there would be some interest. She asked me to give out her contact info if anyone had any questions. Her name is Janice and can be reached after 5 EST at 480-773-8710. She had mentioned that anyone wanting it shipped overseas should contact Donna at 1-877-858-9082.

### caroline — 2017-01-25T08:05:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/promagnet-sleep-pads.18940/post-207316>

I was reading on their website ..... so this pad goes on top of the mattress and under the top sheet - unlike the magnetico which goes under the mattress.

You got a smaller pad? where do you place it - body wise?

interesting - Please Let us know when you get the bigger pad and how it goes......

### MSH — 2017-01-25T16:14:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/promagnet-sleep-pads.18940/post-207333>

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/from-sunny-south-africa.18944/post-206617>

Greetings and loads of exciting and interesting experiences for 2017. Promises to be a year loaded with the Supernatural!
My name is Chabad de la Fontaine and I live in beautiful Western Cape in a very windy coastal village about 5okm from Cape Town, Gordon's Bay. I am a naturopath and quantum bio-physicist researcher of blood, nutrition, water, salt and environmental effects on the human body based on your blood type. For more than 60 years I have been a vegan and for almost 40 years I have been eating to my blood type. My general health is good, but dry skin and hair, even though I drink at least 2.2 litres of water daily and use Omega 3 and other supplements.

# Thread: Career path for pharmacy student
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/career-path-for-pharmacy-student.18945/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### John Smith — 2017-01-12T05:23:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/career-path-for-pharmacy-student.18945/post-206661>

I am going into 4th year pharmacy student in Sydney, Australia and this year will be my final. After two years of learning Kruse's work I have grown dissatisfied with the profession, job opportunities as well as the pay. It is one of the lowest paying healthcare jobs in Australia. There is more and more lack of communication between pharmacists and patients as pharmacies become more commercialized. I want to interact with patients to a greater extent. I hate thinking of working in a pharmacy where half the shelves are filled with sugar and weight loss shakes and bars. Dispensing on a blue lit computer under strong artificial lights like a machine for medicines such as statins, cipro etc... which you know all screws the mitochondria is not what I wish to do. Is studying conventional medicine or alternative medicine a bad idea after finishing pharmacy. They both have it wrong with their science and I will continue to learn about half truths. What would be a good career path after completing pharmacy school so that I can apply the principles of quantum medicine to my patients and help change health and medicine like Jack?

### Jack Kruse — 2017-01-12T05:27:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/career-path-for-pharmacy-student.18945/post-206662>

We had a pharmacist with us from Oz in Mexico.........this came up between her and myself. I would open a compound pharmacy that integrates patients and doctors to solve their problems........and I would avoid the box pharmacy most chose. When you link the docs to your serivces and patients you can win big.

### John Smith — 2017-01-13T08:37:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/career-path-for-pharmacy-student.18945/post-206754>

Do you mean opening a compound pharmacy by using a multidisciplinary model and patient centered service? Basically the compound pharmacy is within one clinic that is made up of a team of medical doctors, pharmacists, psychologists, diabetes educators, podiatrists, physiotherapist etc... So healthcare professionals are collaborating together to ensure best health outcomes for patients.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-01-14T04:12:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/career-path-for-pharmacy-student.18945/post-206799>

This is one idea. I think when you read an coming guest blog from one of our student members from Mexico you might get others.

### John Smith — 2017-03-23T03:27:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/career-path-for-pharmacy-student.18945/post-210912>

I Will most likely try to have my business in far northern Australia

# Thread: Optimal Physique vs Optimal Health
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-physique-vs-optimal-health.18948/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Fitness
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### kris90 — 2017-01-12T21:08:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-physique-vs-optimal-health.18948/post-206706>

Looking for some guidance on exercise for optimal health. It seems the consensus is that, brief, high intense exercise should be incorporated a few times a week (or as can be handled depending on redox) once leptin sensitive. This would be a heavy weight training session, or HIIT based sprint training. Additionally, one should try to get as much low-intense exercise (i.e. walking and general moving around) as possible to increase NEAT.
It also seems (with some debate and controversy) that long duration, endurance-style training is a no-no, for those looking for optimal health, because chronically, it can lead to overtraining, lowered redox, trashed hormone panels, and issues with temperature change if the athlete is warm adapted (cardiac arrest).

So with all that being said what is the best bang for your buck in terms of weight lifting? Would a simple 3x5 Rippetoe’s SS be optimal in terms of progression, recovery and body composition? Is Bodybuilding split/pump training detrimental to health? I typically have the best results in terms of my body composition and how my physique looks on the outside when I follow bodybuilding training that involves less focus on the amount of weight lifted, but more emphasis on volume: basically squeeze in as many sets as possible within a 45 min period, minimal rests, reps anywhere from 8-20 per set, dropsets, and focusing on engorging the muscles with blood. This seems to be more metabolically taxing, but less neurologically taxing, making training more enjoyable and keeping me motivated.

Lately I’ve been doing lower volume workouts less frequently, but focusing on brief, intense lifts like Squats, Deadlifts, Presses, Rows and Chinups. I find my motivation is lower for this type of training over bodybuilding/pump training. What I’d like to know, is bodybuilding training worse for you in the long run (i.e. am I going to deplete my stem cells quicker)? Should we draw a line between optimal physique and optimal health, as they seem to only go hand-in-hand up until a certain point? Bodybuilding training does wonders for me on the outside, but want to explore maybe what might be going on on the inside.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-01-12T23:06:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-physique-vs-optimal-health.18948/post-206722>

Training can be pushed to the max until it ruins the hormone panel......when that happens you've stepped into the bad world of heteroplasmy

### kris90 — 2017-01-12T23:24:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-physique-vs-optimal-health.18948/post-206731>

Got it. So as long as recovery is taken into consideration and workouts are brief but intense, the style of training is irrelevant?

I believe training with weights 3x per week is my sweet spot, since after all the years of lifting 5-6x per week trashed my T levels, and I'm still recovering.

### JanSz — 2017-01-13T00:10:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-physique-vs-optimal-health.18948/post-206732>

Training indoors is done under fake lights, bad air, etc.
Do it outdoors.
.

### JanSz — 2017-01-13T00:11:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-physique-vs-optimal-health.18948/post-206733>

Pizza's toppings may be ok.
Just skip bread.

# Thread: Chabad Shalom
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chabad-shalom.18950/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Chabad de le Fontaine — 2017-01-12T23:11:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chabad-shalom.18950/post-206730>

Thoroughly enjoying the Optimal Health Forum. This is a much needed way to communicate than FB. So much to learn and also some to share. Way back in the late 1950's I started focusing on my personal health and disagreeing with medical people about my health. Listened to my own intuition and this caused stress galore, for me and my family. My label was "difficult child when it came to food and medicine".

As to my maternal hereditary info, that is something I will never fully know. My grandmother from my mother's side was Jewish from France and England, she died when I was 12 years and I lived with her sometimes and sometimes with my mother who I hardly knew as I left home at age 14 years.

I was a sick child, burst appendix between 18 - 24 months old, did not speak willingly until about 15 years old, had diphtheria and tonsil removed between 8 - 9 years of age, always had throat problems - stress related as well as wrong diet in childhood is my assessment - I became vegan at about 7 years - I always struggled with meat and diary- so refused to eat these produce - and there was always stressful meal time because of my stubbornness - very happy I stopped eating meat and dairy as by 10 -12 years I learnt about vegan cooking from Indian families and I started on my road to optimal health and still learning.

During my childhood I had chicken pox, measles and German measles, migraine headaches and heavy menstrual for years until I regulated my salt with very high quality mineral salt and added tissue salts and extra calcium from Ayurveda supplements - the mineral salt I still use today as it is estimated to be 280 Million years fossil salt - my miracle supplement and structured natural water. Plus about 4-6 gram omega 3, but also Omega 6 and 9 , eat loads of raw foods, limited lentils and beans, and rice...potatoes occasionally in winter. I eat limited quantities of coconut oil, about 1 litre a month of top quality olive oil and in winter add fish oils to my pasta, lentils, beans and rice. Eat limited carbohydrates and snack on macadamia, almonds, pecan and walnuts, raisins and berries. Fruit in season and 2 - 3 litres of pH9 water daily.

My mothers health was stress and she died of heart failure. As I hardly spoke to my family I do not have much info. My mother was blood type A and my father O as both were blood donors and I discovered their info while working at a SA blood transfusion lab as a blood technician.

I am aware my dopamine levels fluctuate as I only need about 4-6 hours sleep in winter and about 4- 5 hours in summer. Fortunately I lived many years on the Tropic of Capricorn area of Botswana and enjoyed loads of good water, sunlight and moon light. Slept well on the ground often as a younger woman until about 40 - 50 years and discovered that I am an East feet sleeper. Very important to me this position to sleep as well as the type of mattress...using high density foam with cotton covering and percale sheets and feather duvet. Must have windows open even when gale force winds blow...when ever possible I make sure I have a large glass door in the bedroom facing East. Limited electrical devices and place all EMF devices in the passage or sitting room when I do go to sleep. Switch off the electricity at the mains often at night...especially in winter.
Being Hebrew my grandmother insisted I sleep early and rise early and communicate with GOD and enjoy all of Nature , including the sunlight, moonlight and star gazing. We only had feather duvets, cotton and wool, for bedding, and other household linen etc., and clothing.

In my research in the frequency fields of the human body, I find that natural earth organic fibers and foods (not the commercial organic system) have a significant effect on humans. Quantum physics is my passion and studies of ancient spiritual and lifestyle rituals has given me more information about vibration and intelligence than medicine and science. Modern science is proving ancient spiritual rituals and laws are foundation blocks to human development.

My aura is quite strong as many people tell me I vibrate too much energy. Three marriages ended because of my higher energy fields than my partners. So this is something I am investigating deeper as I do find people move away from me in crowds and queues. I mediate daily and sun gaze daily.

Sharing all this in the hope that there are other people who resonate with some of my habits and will share.
Love and Light to all.

### JanSz — 2017-01-13T00:44:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chabad-shalom.18950/post-206737>

When sleeping my feet are real close to East, head West
Have another room where I could have feet South.
How to figure what is healthier?
\==========================================
tissue-salts
\----------------------------
[http://tissue-salts.com/](http://tissue-salts.com/)

These mineral salts are:

1.  Calcium Fluoride ([Calc. Fluor.](http://tissue-salts.com/calc_fluor.html))
2.  Calcium Phosphate ([Calc. Phos.](http://tissue-salts.com/calc_phos.html))
3.  Calcium Sulphate ([Calc. Sulph.](http://tissue-salts.com/calc_sulph.html))
4.  Iron Phosphate ([Ferr. Phos.](http://tissue-salts.com/ferr_phos.html))
5.  Potassium Chloride ([Kali. Mur.](http://tissue-salts.com/kali_mur.html))
6.  Potassium Phosphate ([Kali. Phos.](http://tissue-salts.com/kali_phos.html))
7.  Potassium Sulphate ([Kali. Sulph.](http://tissue-salts.com/kali_sulph.html))
8.  Magnesium Phosphate ([Mag. Phos.](http://tissue-salts.com/mag_phos.html))
9.  Sodium Chloride ([Nat. Mur.](http://tissue-salts.com/nat_mur.html))
10.  Sodium Phosphate ([Nat. Phos.](http://tissue-salts.com/nat_phos.html))
11.  Sodium Sulphate ([Nat. Sulph.](http://tissue-salts.com/nat_sulph.html))
12.  Silica ([Silica](http://tissue-salts.com/silica.html))

When one needs the Calcium Fluoride?
How do you figure which one you need and how much? Using taste buds?

Sort of similar idea to this:
[http://www.bodybio.com/storecategory112.aspx](http://www.bodybio.com/storecategory112.aspx)

• Potassium #1
• Zinc #2
• Magnesium #3
• Copper #4
• Chromium #5

### FNJORD — 2017-01-13T15:10:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/uva-vs-uvb-the-tanning-salon.18951/post-206767>

Because of the weather (an hour of sunlight in 2 weeks) I've decided to go to the tanning beds to get some UV. They have different grades of beds. Am I looking for higher UVA or UVB? I thought I remember reading something ages ago that said the UVB was the one for stimulating vit D and other good things. When I asked the people there about it they were like NO, you want rays that make a sunburn? What you want is the higher UVA beds which give you a tan. I said I really don't care one way or another whether I'm tanning, I just want the UV rays.

Anyway, if I can pick a bed that has higher one or the other... I think the older ones might have more UVB.. which kind is the better one? I'm extremely white with a lot of freckles and moles.

### Inger — 2017-01-13T15:32:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/uva-vs-uvb-the-tanning-salon.18951/post-206772>

well, if you want to boost vit D then you want to go for the higher UVB, I personally like to use the one that have both. The tanning salon here has one with only UVB too. But it feels natural to get both, the UVB only, might be more effective for boosting vit D tho. But then, it also not really natural to get UVB in winter in the north.... I remember Jack wrote somewhere that in winter UVA is the more interesting one...

This winter I have used no tanning salon because I am so poor..lol But I have my reptile light that I use once in a while, it has UVA UVB and IR. It is not very strong tho. Not like a tanning bed. But I do like how it makes me feel.

Do you know your vitD status? Jack has advice not to use tanning beds if the vitD is low.

Complicated, no?

Maybe just biohack and let us know how it makes you feel

### FNJORD — 2017-01-13T16:23:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/uva-vs-uvb-the-tanning-salon.18951/post-206776>

I'm just going to do whatever. Anything is going to be better than nothing so I'll switch around, all of them have both types, but some boast higher UVA for the instant tan aspect since the brown effect is what all of the salon goers are looking for. It is really difficult to find positive material on UV exposure, even the WHO has a negative write up on their website, citing that low vitamin D is very rare. I did notice, when I lived in Germany, that the Germans who appear to be much much healthier than my area of Canada (Hamilton/Toronto) and where I lived in NY (Cold Spring) were big on eating earlier in the day and light supper, crazy about their sun exposure and also very active.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-01-13T17:31:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/uva-vs-uvb-the-tanning-salon.18951/post-206777>

I wish they made a full spectrum bed........but you can do this through the winter to help yourself. The key is understanding how to use it for your particular context with your doctors.

### kris90 — 2017-01-14T14:40:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/uva-vs-uvb-the-tanning-salon.18951/post-206810>

Not to hijack thread, but if we choose to tan 2-3x per week for the benefits of UVA/UVB in winter, could this create a mismatch with our high fat/keto winter diets? I remember Jack mentioned that keto in summer is not a great idea, I think because on keto, electrons enter ETC at complex 2, so no superoxide pulse is generated, thus the body is running off bad mitochondria, and cannot use mitophagy to replace them.

If we tan frequently in winter, should we remain strict keto, or should we include a small amount of carbs for the superoxide pulse? Or, instead of carbs, could we use nicotine w/ methylene blue along with to the tanning? I know the nicotine biohack is to get a superoxide pulse, but my understanding is you need UV and O2 to make this work.

I find since I began tanning a few weeks ago, my winter keto diet seems like a mismatch (and I've gained a bit of weight, and feel a little off).

### brandie — 2017-02-10T07:19:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/uva-vs-uvb-the-tanning-salon.18951/post-208353>

As far as tanning beds go the salons don't seem clear on what the percentages are for each bed. I believe all beds have UVA and UVB. I am almost certain that the beds they say are "all UVB" are actually about 7-11% UVB, the rest being UVA and maybe a little UVC, maybe. The US regulated tanning salons in the last few years. Started charging tax and limiting allowable minutes spent in each type of bed. I own a tanning canopy for home use, I couldn't find a bulb stronger than 9% UVB. During the process I learned a tanning bulb needs the ambient temperature to be near 80° F to be optimal. See even light needs optimal. So tanning in a bed after someone else just used the bed will give stronger results than if the bed was "cold".

### Just Breathe — 2017-02-13T19:15:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/uva-vs-uvb-the-tanning-salon.18951/post-208522>

Hi Brandie, I agree that salons are not clear at all on percentages! Their staff is usually missing this information. I learned from one educated staffer at a tanning place and take that knowledge with me. An "Open Sun" tanning bed is 100% UVA. Anytime they tell you its "got the light that bronzes you and you don't burn from it" that's code for UVA. If they tell you this has more of the light that "burns" you, it has UVB. I also totally agree that using a bed after someone else will give stronger results! I went to a place over the weekend and they had had back to back users in the bed, ten people in a row. It was too hot for me to even touch with my skin! It felt like a microwave in there and I declined to actually tan in that bed. Peak tanning time in the year seems to be February-March...spring breakers getting a "base tan."

### Kathy1967 — 2017-04-19T07:38:19+0300

I'm wondering if I am doing more damage than good even though it raises my vitamin D (used to be 16 now it is in the 40's) I think my skin is starting to look crepey (mostly on my legs) - I'm 49 so I don't know if it's from aging or from the tanning bed.
any thoughts would be greatly appreciated!

# Thread: Will all worlds/ planets support Earth like  life?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/will-all-worlds-planets-support-earth-like-life.18952/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2017-01-14T05:24:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/will-all-worlds-planets-support-earth-like-life.18952/post-206800>

Life is built on Earth to be built far from equilibrium.

Might life have an equilibrium bias on other worlds?

Might life still be asleep in a palce like Jupiter waiting to awaken when the planet stops spiinign so fast?

Before there is life, there must be structure. Our universe synthesized atomic nuclei early in its history from exploding stars. Those nuclei ensnared electrons to form atoms. Those atoms agglomerated into galaxies, stars, and planets. At last, living things had places to call home. We take it for granted that the laws of physics allow for the formation of such structures, but that needn’t have been the case.

Over the past several decades, many scientists have argued that, had the laws of physics been even slightly different, the cosmos would have been devoid of complex structures.

This tells us that the charge differential present on stars and planets alter the scale of the forces. As the scale of things are changed the physical laws of nature also become dynamic. Most of us fail to relaize this because on Earth scale is perceived by our five senses as relatively constant.

Is the same true in our cells at the mitochondrial level? How about at a subatomic scale on the inner mitochondrial membrane? My April 2016 webinar explored this in some detail for members.

Astrophysicists have discussed the fine-tuning of worlds as scale changes so much that many people take it as a given that our "observable universe" is preternaturally fit for complex structures. Even skeptics of the multiverse concepts (me included) accept fine-tuning at some level; On Earth I think fine tuning is changed by scale. What happens at the cosmologic scale in nature is not equivalent that what we observe at the subatomic scale. This implies the laws of nature are dynamic and plastic. These scientist simply think it must have some other "physical explanation". Is this true?

But in fact the fine-tuning has never been rigorously demonstrated by experiment, it has just been assumed by cosmology.

We do not really know what laws of physics are necessary for the development of astrophysical structures, which are in turn necessary for the development of life. Recent work on stellar evolution (what I call speculation), nuclear astrophysics, and structure formation suggest that the case for fine-tuning is less compelling than previously thought. A wide variety of possible universes could support life. Our universe is not as special as it might seem.

The first type of fine-tuning involves the strengths of the fundamental forces of nature in working stars. If the electromagnetic force had been too strong, the electrical repulsion of protons would shut down nuclear fusion in stellar cores (assuming you believe this), and stars would fail to shine because no light would be created. Without light no life could evolve.

If electromagnetism had been too weak, nuclear reactions would run out of control, and stars would blow up in spectacular explosions. Could this situation be how matter was originally created as the scale of the universe changed? If gravity had been too strong, stars would either collapse into black holes or never ignite. the point is the forces of nature determine thermodynamics of matter by determining size and shapes of things. All size and shape tie to frequencies because all matter vibrates at a certain frequencies. It turns out light also vibrates too. Light and matter that form resonances tend to interact. In this way light binds things made up of charged particles to make things made of complex structure.

Light is critical to life and biology:

The allowed strengths for the gravitational and electromagnetic forces depend on the nuclear reaction rate, which in turn depends on the strengths of the nuclear forces. If the reaction rate were faster, stars could function over an even wider range of strengths for gravitation and electromagnetism. Slower nuclear reactions would narrow the range. What do all these variables mean for life? The April webinar of 2016 examines these implications.

In addition to these minimal operational requirements, stars must meet a number of other constraints that further restrict the allowed strength of the forces. They must be hot. The surface temperature of a star must be high enough to drive the chemical reactions necessary for life. In our universe, there are ample regions around most stars where planets are warm enough, about 300 kelvins, to support biology. In universes where the electromagnetic force is stronger, stars are cooler, making them less hospitable. Stars liberate many frequencies of light.

In nature, UV light builds pressure photoelectrically by storing energy in a cell structure; pressure builds during this storage and heat (IR) is emitted when electric and magnetic forces are confined to a tight space. Cells have things that are "tight spaces" like a mitochondria. When mitochondria are energy efficient in a cell, they become more capable of confining light using the electric and magnetic charges in mitochondrial membranes and spaces. This is why healthy cells release less light and why overly stressed cells release more ELF-UV. We know that light slows down in a dense media and density links to buoyancy in a cell. **Snell’s law says and proves these relationships exist in nature. That law is important evidence of light being used as a wave and not as a particle. Usually when light is used as a wave its frequency must be lower than we see in the ultraviolet range. This means light is more "wave like" as it is red shifted and more particle like when it is ultraviolet and purple shifted. In the visible spectrum, the lowest frequency of light is red and infrared. 42 % of sunlight is IR-A light. Most of the critical proteins of the inner mitochondrial membrane are red light chromophores. So is water.
_**
It is interesting that fat oxidation by mitochondria, creates protons, infrared light, CO2 and water. Those products react on the surface of mitochondria inside of cells to change each other's phsyical characteristics by changing the scales of the atoms that make them up. It appears that respiratory proteins shrink most when our surfaces are absorbing ultraviolet light and out mitochondrial matrix is emitting infrared light. Infrared light changes water’s physic characteristics and makes it more dense. Liquid water has more density than ice, hence why ice floats in liquid water. As a result, ice also has less light energy sotred in its lattice. This is also why ice reflects sunlight to create the albedo effect at the poles. This physical change is how Snell’s law works in our cells because water density is linked to its refractive abilities to light. This simple small alteration, changes how light can interact with it at our surfaces. A long term stressor overwhelms the regenerative side of the coupled feedback system and this results in energy loss and because energy cannot be stored. **When energy cannot be stored in a cell, the energy must be thermalized (heat).** This is why all mammals are warm blooded. This happens routinely in mammals mitochondria. This heat provides the scale and complexity that life on Earth can operates at.

_**Life is clever.**_ It has figured out how to transform electrons out of everything we eat and keep them under control in cells until we need their energy. Electrons must flow in order for energy to be gained elsewhere. Electrons can be stored in tissues in semiconductors, but when they are stored they cannot act like a wave. They only have particle like behavior. When the electron leaves the tissue its energy has to be thermalized to affect energy flows elsewhere in a cell to be used to power physiologic work of living.**_**

# Thread: Improving In Home Grounding
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/improving-in-home-grounding.18955/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### bionaut — 2017-01-14T18:47:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/improving-in-home-grounding.18955/post-206825>

Does anyone know what labs are useful for determining a positive/negative effect from grounding using a pad?
I only have anecdotal evidence from sleep.

I own a grounding mat that I don't use. The wire that connects this to a stake out my window is pretty dinky.

I was thinking about using shielded cat6 cable to connect from the mat to the stake outside

Cat 6 is used in high emr environments to prevent signal degradation in datacenters.

The individual wires are insulated then there is a second jacket. I was thinking about connecting the interior wiring to a grounding rod and earthing mat. Then taking the exterior either braided or foil jacket and grounding that to a separate stake. In theory this should reduce the ambient emr running through the cable by having a grounded shield enveloping the wiring. Foil jackets provide heavier shielding that braided forms but also have a higher resistance which could be a problem for the ground.

> I own a grounding mat that I don't use. The wire that connects this to a stake out my window is pretty dinky.
>
> I was thinking about using shielded cat6 cable to connect from the mat to the stake outside
>
> Cat 6 is used in high emr environments to prevent signal degradation in datacenters.
>
> The individual wires are insulated then there is a second jacket. I was thinking about connecting the interior wiring to a grounding rod and earthing mat. Then taking the exterior either braided or foil jacket and grounding that to a separate stake. In theory this should reduce the ambient emr running through the cable by having a grounded shield enveloping the wiring. Foil jackets provide heavier shielding that braided forms but also have a higher resistance which could be a problem for the ground.
>
>
> Any of you electrically inclined folk out there, your thoughts are welcome.

My electrical experience with grounding.

My house is about 60' long.
On one end comes electrical power.
There is a ground there.
Third wire everywhere in the house is grounded thru that ground.
When I was planning to have a spa I need 100A outlet with GFCI on the other side,
straight from main box, up to the attic above 2nd floor, across the attic, then down, then about 20' out.
I got about 100 feet
4/3 With Ground Electrical Wire

Hooked it up to main box on one end
hooked it up to sub-box on the other end
hooked in to GFCFI

The GFCI was tripping.
So I stuck ground wire similar to what I had at the main side.
The GFCI was tripping.
I was tired at that time, and did not want to make to many trials any more.
Got 10 feet long 1"dia copper bar (plus my son with the sledge hammer to get it in).
It worked.

My city electrical inspector was wondering about my installation.
Way over of what he sees every day.
I told him that I did it all my self with advice from my electrical friends.
The problem was, I was working at the time tending to Nuclear Power Plants and so did they.

What is the lesson here?

One of them is that good ground counts in a major way.
And I had lousy ground at the main box.
Few years latter I got a house generator and a deal to replace my main box.
I agreed under condition that contractor will give me serious grounding bar (while I was watching him doing it).
Now I am good.

//


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

I am Tammy and just found this info a few days ago.

I am hoping it will help me regain my health and start to live again.

I've been telling the Doctors that I don't have any "Life Force Energy" left and they all look at me like
I am stupid AND crazy.
I am going to give this a shot as I have no other means to get well so please be patient with me as I learn and experiment with all of this.
I am also looking forward to reading about all of the success stories.
I don't expect it to be easy but right now, I have an energy level of 0 so any and all help you can offer
I am willing to explore and implement.

Here's to us, may we be awake and fully alive now!
Tammy

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-st-petersburg-florida.18960/post-206938>

Welcome Tammy!

You need nature more than anything.... are you living on the countryside or in a city? Are you spending time otside in the wild nature? How is your sun exposure, do you get lots of sun on your whole body regularely? I think this is what really will help your energy.... are you walking barefoot oustide too?

How is you technology use/non native EMF exposure (do you use wifi, lots on the computer, smart phone..etc)
Are you living alone or with family? If you have family with you are they using technology?

How is your lights situation at home? Do you use lights after dark, do you use enrgy saving light bulbs? They are awful for health! Candles are the light to use after sunset.. if you use no other lights it is very possible that you will sleep way better  especially if you got more than an hour of outside time in daylight/sun

Are you eating seafood often, and drinking spring water withouth chloride and fluoride in large amounts?

The more you tell us about your every day life the better we can help/guide you to help you make better choices to bring you back to health

every minute that I am not using them.

need to keep my place to live.

I am going to get candles later today as I just read about the lighting last night.

I really don't sleep and I used to get up around 6 am all of my life and miss it so much!
To be able to sleep and have a normal schedule would be awesome!

I am in exhaustion mode 24/7 and I ache in my neck and back so much that doing anything physical is nearly impossible for me right now.

I am going to get enough food for 3 days to start which will include fish, eggs, sweet potatoes, salad fixins and a few veggies
and then read more and go from there.

as much as possible.

I've been praying for a remedy and this seems so right. I am so happy to have found you!

### Inger — 2017-01-18T18:36:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-st-petersburg-florida.18960/post-207048>

When the body heals the excess weight will come off withouth effort. I think it is wrong to concentrate on losing weight. Becoming healthy and increasing energy is what we need to concentrate on!
The lucky ones become fat, do not forget this. It is for a reason the body accumulates fat. The thin ones that are sick might do worse! I would just connect to nature, do all the changes possible to gain energy back, and the body will not need the fat anymore.

I would not advice a obese person to diet or fast......
Instead, to ground, avoid technology, tan (as much as possible and preferrably nude!), drink plenty of good water, and eat according to seasons and what is available around. Wild foods are magic! To me they are the most energizing ever
Like wildcaught fish, herbs, berries, whatever grow and moves around you
and... relax
and.. love.. and kiss.. hug.. laugh, cry.. and skin contact to a beloved human.

### Inger — 2017-01-18T18:52:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-st-petersburg-florida.18960/post-207051>

> [Tammy Rogers said:](/goto/post?id=206952)
>
> Hi Inger,
> Thank you for wanting to help me, I have never done this before and am a little scared of it all.

Do not be scared Tammy  It will be a beatiful journey, I promise!
To help the mental part you copuld buy and read the monk who sold his Ferrari, it is a great read to come back to when life feels tough  It is super cheap on amazon  It was the book that helped Jack change...

> I have worked from home on the computer and phone for the past 17 years and have just recently figured out that
> this si why I am sick. I am in the first stages of cleaning up my environment and turning off the computer and phone
> every minute that I am not using them.
> I have wanted to stop working on the computer altogether but being so sick, I cannot go get a regular job and really
> need to keep my place to live.

You can use corded internet and switch off wifi, and use f.lux to avoid bluelight (it is a free download) and use blueblockers in front of the screen ( they are very cheap for less than 10 bucks on amazon)
and you can buy a long cord (also cheap) for internet and use the computer in the garden as you tan and ground!

> It will happen eventually!
> I was outside in the sun for 30 minutes when you responded to my post!
> I started yesterday and will do it every morning along with incorporating the rebounder and qi gong outside on the grass barefoot.
>
> I have been craving the sun and being outside so this is perfect timing.

Nice   great start

> I am going to get enough food for 3 days to start which will include fish, eggs, sweet potatoes, salad fixins and a few veggies
> and then read more and go from there.
>
> I am a little concerned about eating meat with the Diverticulitis being so bad right now but willing to eat the fish
> as much as possible.
>
> I've been praying for a remedy and this seems so right. I am so happy to have found you!

I do not know what to eat for diverticulitis but I suppose when you eat natural foods and use sun and avoid nnEMF it sure will heal eventually  If you are scared of meat just use seafood, it is fine! But I do not think meat is any dangerous.... just try to get a good source, grassfed or game like moose, or lamb/sheep is mostly grassfed too.

I often eat pollock when I am out of money. It is very cheap and I love it! I buy it frozen. That is the cheapest fish here, and it is wildcaught. I eat it raw and cooked. Raw I marinate it in white balsamic vinegar, onions, grated fresh ginger and add some curcumin, black pepper and olive oil. Delicious!  Or cook it in a little water with onions, and add lots of butter and curcumin and black pepper and salt.

Herring and mackerel are so great too if you can get them! Fresh is best but frozen is fine if you cant get any other or budget is too tight. Remember light is the most important thing to get right

I recently thought about using the Pedialyte and got 4 bottles of it yesterday as I want to use it for the electrolytes.
I am going to drink it in between meals until I feel I can not eat and be calm because when I don't eat, I get really dizzy and shaky.

I drink enormous amounts of water with sea salt in it and it's doing me no good.

Got a grounding cord yesterday and am going to get some seafood today or tomorrow and start getting r done. lol

I work on the computer all day and night for the past 17 years so I am turning my modem and phone off as much as possible
and everything off when I sleep.
I am wearing the grounding cord when I sleep because I live in a garage apt with a window air conditioner and the owner's electrical
EVERYTHING JUST ON THE OTHER SIDE OF THE WALL.

I am sitting in the sun every morning for 30 minutes with my feet in the ground but don't think that is going to be enough.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-01-18T22:39:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-st-petersburg-florida.18960/post-207061>

What makes us fat faster than anything? Fake blue light day and night. You can use blue light flicker to see if it changes your stress response and your BUN and creatine ratio and makes you fatter. See what effects it has on your eyes in your mito-hacks. You might be shocked at what you find. You’ll see less dermal pooling of blood, massive effects on melanopsin regeneration, and centralization of blood plasma and a rise in Blood Pressure and a drop in vitaminD3 levels even when you are naked in Florida sun. You can use your computer screens dimming mechanism to bio-hack the effect on yourself for very little money. [https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/artificial-blue-light-dehydrates-your-cells-jack-kruse?published=t](https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/artificial-blue-light-dehydrates-your-cells-jack-kruse?published=t)

### JanSz — 2017-01-18T22:48:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-st-petersburg-florida.18960/post-207063>

\[qu ote="Tammy Rogers, post: 207054, member: 19702"\]I recently thought about using the Pedialyte and got 4 bottles of it yesterday as I want to use it for the electrolytes.
I am going to drink it in **between meals** **until I feel I can not eat** and be calm because when I don't eat, I get really dizzy and shaky.

We are talking electrolytes here. That is:
sodium
potassium
magnesium

This is not a food, you do not need a lots of it.
Definitely you do not want drink tons of water, even if some of it contain electrolytes.
Be ready for a fight that theoretically last 2 days, but 4 days is not out of the question.
It should get better latter.
All mostly psychological.
If you are religious, that will buy you a lot.
You fight with your self.
Do not feel guilty if you "fail", just pick up pieces and continue.
Tell us about it, we will root for you.
**If you are using insulin or blood pressure medications.**
**You must monitor dosing.**
Required dosing will change, sometime drastically.
Eventually, there is a good chance that you will wean out of those.
Unless you are type 1 (diabetic).

I drink enormous amounts of water with sea salt in it and it's doing me no good.

**Got a grounding cord yesterday** and am going to get some seafood today or tomorrow and start getting r done. lol

In short.
**Please do not use grounding cord.**
I posted recently my experience with grounding I had in my house.
Nothing pretty.
But, bare feet walking, laying on a ground, specially wet ground, grass or sand next to skin, far away from electric power lines, is ok.

I work on the computer all day and night for the past 17 years so I am turning my modem and phone off as much as possible
and everything off when I sleep.
**I am wearing the grounding cord when I sleep** because I live in a garage apt with a window air conditioner and the owner's electrical
EVERYTHING JUST ON THE OTHER SIDE OF THE WALL.
(She now thinks I am COMPLETELY crazy fyi lol)
I am sitting in the sun every morning for 30 minutes with my feet in the ground but don't think that is going to be enough.\[/quote\]

\----------------------
**Do your best with grounding, nnEMF etc,**
**but remember sequence of importance of your goals.**
**You want to burn (your own) fat is a priority #1.**
/

Thank you Jack.
I installed F.LUX and it has made a great difference so far. Dimmed my phone and do not use any lights at all unless absolutely necessary.

I am so overwhelmed by all of this. I used to be what I call "highly intelligent" but now, I get no sleep and can barely function.
I was just reading this from your Michael jackson post:
When we lose the fundamentals of neural chemistry we begin to de-evolve and see more primitive neurologic state of function.
I honestly feel I am at the point he was at-no joke. It is scaring me big time!

It all seems like a maze to me and trying to find what I need to do from first thing in the am until I go to sleep is deluding me.

I DON'T SLEEP AT ALL yet, all I want to do is sleep!
My head rings constantly and I feel like I am on the verge of an anxiety attack every time I try to concentrate (I have never had anxiety problems before) so the panic is real now.

I got the eggs, good meat and some veggies and started today when I woke up but I don't get up until 11 or 12 BECAUSE I CANNOT SLEEP so
I am not sure how to fix that.
I ordered the NOW Thyroid Energy to take in the am for energy.
I have the Vitamin C, apple cider vinegar and sea salt and am taking it every morning.
I got the grounding cord and am turning off all wifi at night and even wearing the grounding cord to bed.
Only candle light and a salt lamp at night.
Grounding in the sun as soon as I eat in the am for 30 minutes.

What else can I do to
1\. GET SOME SLEEP
2\. STOP THE RINGING
2\. HAVE MORE ENERGY?

so truly looking for some new intelligent friends who want to have fun getting healthier.

I've actually sat and cried all day trying to figure this site out!

I keep saying tomorrow I will get it but 3 days in, I am still confused. My brain doesn't work the way it used to for sure!

I want to feel better right now and just need to accept that it's going to take some time and focus.

The bottom line is:
do not hook up grounding pads to a house circuit.

Should not do harm if you hook your wire to a copper rod that is fully in the ground.
That copper rod should be substantial. One piece of wire.

### Sun Disciple — 2017-01-17T00:22:36+0200
