Just listened to Jason Pralls's new podcast with DR Cowan. They get into Gerald pollocks work and how it shatters the standard heart as a pump model. Im 3 qaurters through the fourth phase so It was deffinetly good timing for me. Emjoy!

### Jack Kruse — 2017-01-17T03:27:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dr-thomas-cowan-secrets-of-the-human-heart.18961/post-206974>

The problem with these "discoveries" is that they become systematized and programmed by paradigms.

The public needs to realize that "systems and programs" are for computers.... and they are not designed for living things like us. We are designed to be wirelessly connected to the sun and fully connected to Earth to work properly in nature to remain well.

We simply need to connect with nature and feel the zeitgebers..........and Mother Nature will take care of us. The Rx is simple but our brain's wants desirtes and nedds of society and culture obscure that perspective. [http://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1111/joim.12251/full](http://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1111/joim.12251/full)

### Jack Kruse — 2017-01-17T03:28:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dr-thomas-cowan-secrets-of-the-human-heart.18961/post-206975>

### Lahelada — 2017-01-17T14:43:46+0200

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dr-thomas-cowan-secrets-of-the-human-heart.18961/post-206998>

### Lahelada — 2017-01-17T14:56:34+0200

### Alper — 2017-01-17T17:10:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dr-thomas-cowan-secrets-of-the-human-heart.18961/post-207002>

I haven´t head this recent podcast with Thomas Cowan but another one he did with Ben Greenfield. I´m also familiar with the literature (Rudolf Steiner) that he is referring to mainly I think.
Jack, on the one hand you´re absolutely right - he (T.C.) is not putting human beings in contact with nature so his conclusions seem incomplete/incorrect. On the other hand he is referring to the other site of the coin - Rudolf Steiners unterstanding of medicine or rather of mankind in complex with the universe regards this from the arts of humanitis (Geisteswissenschaft in german - I think its more clear than the englisch term) and does not regard it from your view, the area of natural science (Naturwissenschaft).
Mankind evolved the same way minerals, plants and animals did. There are certain aspects that differ men from animal although we are mammals. But mankind is the first beeing to be able to think and reflect its own nature - with is nature itself. Animals can´t do that. They experience the state of consciousness (instincts/impulses) but the do not experience the state of self-consciousness (seperating the individual as "I" from the surrounding or the group they are living in).
I´m often confused when you are saying "mother nature will take care, if we couple with nature" or something like that (don´t be too picky on my quote - I think you know what I´am talking about). We are the embodyment of mother nature. Mother nature is a spiritual term (not meaning religious). Without mankind there will not be a term mother nature. This is a human realisation of its own. Nature holds the principles, not laws (laws only exist in disciplines like physics), that build our surrounding as well as us. So their is no real difference between nature and us (check the term "emergence").
It was kind of hard to couch these mental ideas in terms. I hope you get a feeling.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-01-17T17:38:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dr-thomas-cowan-secrets-of-the-human-heart.18961/post-207003>

You are correct.......When I listened to this podcast it was clear to me the Dr. and the interviewer where passing through life completely blind to what really matters and focused in on what does not. This will confuse the person who is learning. It is also why I try to limit my exposure to people with half vision who develop and propagate half truths.

### Sun Disciple — 2017-01-19T01:18:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dr-thomas-cowan-secrets-of-the-human-heart.18961/post-207073>

Arnt half truths just dirty puzzle peices removed from the jig saw? only a single component but when cleaned and dusted off they fit nicely into the big picture.
I enjoy getting different perspectives and Im still learning and finding my way around. You half to test the boundries to know where the middle lies I suppose.
Im not really up to date with where J Pralll stands on everything. I started listening resently after I heard his interview With Jack. There seems to be more guests on his show that share commonalities with this community than not. Jack has connected important dots and synthesized his discoveries with the work of other brilliant minds who all brought to the table their own puzzle piece.

### John G — 2017-01-18T18:27:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-circadian-rhythm-question.18963/post-207047>

I'm clarifying a comment I found on this forum post:
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/so-you-think-ct-and-fake-light-is-bunk-huh.9780/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/so-you-think-ct-and-fake-light-is-bunk-huh.9780/)

\---------------------
Christos writes: "Jack from CT 6 and I quote '...activation of eNOS by cold actually blocks the SCN from reacting to photo stimuli to entrain circadian cycles'. Does this mean that artificial light would no longer affect melatonin signaling if one is "cold adapted'?"

Jack replies: it would...
\----------------------
I just want a clarification on this process of entraining my circadian cycle to the cold. If I do two CT sessions a week at 45 minutes...will my SCN still react to photo stimuli? Or, do I need to CT every morning to boost my eNOS in lieu of the morning sunlight stimulus? Ultimately, how would I know my SCN no longer reacts to photo stimuli and eNOS is dominating?

Thanks,

John

### Jack Kruse — 2017-01-18T19:25:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-circadian-rhythm-question.18963/post-207052>

eNOS is thermoplastic.

# Thread: DHA in Fillets
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dha-in-fillets.18964/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### cap — 2017-01-19T03:42:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dha-in-fillets.18964/post-207084>

I Realize that Ideally, we want to each the whole fish, sea animal etc....

If one eats a lot of Salmon, Tuna, Sardines etc...but only has access to the Fillets (most stores and canned products are just the Fillets), are they still getting a significant amount of DHA from those sources?

### JoeT — 2017-01-19T16:30:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dha-in-fillets.18964/post-207095>

The only way to know is if the manufacturer discloses it, and only the higher end ones do For instance, I eat about 6 cans of Wild Planet Sardines per week. They disclose that each can contains 313mg EPA and 688mg DHA. You'll pay more for these, but you can also be much more sure as to purity.

There may be studies out there on the cheaper brands; I don't recall seeing one though.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-01-19T18:51:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dha-in-fillets.18964/post-207109>

Fresh fish is the key........

### cap — 2017-01-24T07:16:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dha-in-fillets.18964/post-207267>

Of course Fresh Fish is Key, along with "whole fish" is key. I was referring to less than ideal scenarios. Some Brands I use list that there are Omega 3's but don't give a breakdown at all. Others (supposed higher quality brands) from Spain may come in glass or canned, and don't say anything other than that they are fillets.

My question is more regarding is there enough DHA in fillets without skin? whether canned or not is irrelevant... Stores usually sell Steaks/Fillets and remove the skin, as if its a good thing . But can you still reap the benefits of plentiful DHA so long as you consume alot of these sources?

# Thread: Optimal Journal for Tammy Rogers
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-journal-for-tammy-rogers.18965/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 15
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Tammy Rogers — 2017-01-19T03:47:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-journal-for-tammy-rogers.18965/post-207086>

**I copied and pasted my info from my signature as I will delete it from my signature soon.

Age:** 53 **Sex:** F **Joined JackKruse.com:** Jan 2017

**Location: St. Petersburg Fl,** USA

**Dx:** Diverticulitis | Morbidly Obese **|** Sleep Apnea | Thyroid Disease | Chronic Fatigue | Fibromyalgia | High Blood Pressure | Extensive Scar Tissue from back injury in 1989 (4 herniated discs-they have been healed but the scar tissue pain is awful!)

**Surgeries:** 1982, Appendix Removed | 1987 C-Section | 1992 Gall Bladder Removed| 1995 C-Section |2006 Hysterectomy for Endometriosis

**Current Rx: Metoprolol |**

**Current Therapies / Protocols:
**Vitamin C, sea salt and ACV every morning**
****Soon-the NOW Thyroid Energy that is recommended on this site**
**Epi-Paleo Diet****
Sun every morning so far 30 minutes a day and increasing daily
[Leptin Rx](http://www.jackkruse.com/easy-start-guide/) Having a hard time with this since I DO NOT SLEEP MUCH AT ALL.
[Cold Thermogenesis Rx](http://www.jackkruse.com/cold-thermogenesis-easy-start-guide/) starting tomorrow
**

I don't sleep much at all and do not fall asleep until I am completely exhausted or between 4-7 am (whichever comes first) so the brain fog is horrendous.
I have -0- energy level and am at the point of not being able to even walk around the grocery store.
I went to a Holistic Dentist who finally looked at me and told me it was my Thyroid so I have been using a Rebounder for 5 minutes at a time and doing Yoga every other day and they help but the soreness and swelling in my body isn't budging. I just ordered the NOW Thyroid Energy.
I only take the blood pressure medicine because I feel so sick sometimes that it's the only thing that calms my nerves. I am a calm person and do not want to take it.
I do not have insurance and am at the point of no return now.
I really need help and am praying this is what will help me.

I truly want to be able to wake up and have energy again.
I want to eat to live and change my work so that I am not on the computer all day and get all of this excess weight off of me.
I do not drink, smoke or do any drugs and want more than anything to have my clear thinking mind back again!
I am in constant pain all the time.
I have headaches all day every day. My mind is a foggy mess and for someone who likes to be clear-minded this is torture!
I hurt all over my body and feel the beginnings of carpel tunnel syndrome creeping up.

I am reading as much as I can daily and the success stories are giving me hope of feeling good again.

I hope I am not too far gone to fix this as I feel like I m slipping away mentally and physically.
My Dad, dog and brother all died in the last year so the grief is too much when the pain gets bad.

I can honestly say that I believe this is my last hope.

I have spent thousands upon thousands of dollars trying to get well and now that I have no insurance and am broke living in a garage apartment, this protocol is all I have left.

I have so much respect for Dr. Kruse.
He is so knowledgeable and I am so grateful for this platform.

Any help you can offer will be explored as I am willing to do anything at this point.

I have no insurance and no support system so thinking this is going to be so much work sends me into a panic
so I am just talking myself into it being easy and that I will be feeling better in no time.

I just got the Thyroid Energy today and took one at noon.

### caroline — 2017-01-20T02:00:09+0200

slowly you will shift into a correct circadian rhythm.

### Dimitri — 2017-01-20T05:12:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-journal-for-tammy-rogers.18965/post-207133>

Hi Tammy, I was someone who had my fair share of sleeping issues with nights where I would not be able to fall asleep until 4AM. Those restless nights would only happen on an occasion for me but it could still take me an hour or more to fall asleep on many nights. I started to fall asleep faster when I began blocking the blue night at night with blue blockers but this did not fully solve my issue. What gave me consistently good sleep is when I heard Dr. K mention how you need UV light to build up your ocular melatonin, and I started making an effort to really spend much more time outside. That was when my sleep dramatically improved, especially when I would take vacations to the beach where I would fall asleep like a baby on those nights.

I see you spend 30 mins outside in the morning and increasing it which is great. I would suggest to really push the amount of time your outside and try to spend most of the day out there if you can. All in all from my experience the blue blocking only solved maybe 1/3 of my sleep problem, but the sun ultimately was the real deal.

I'm not sure what kind of thyroid disease you have but I had hyperthyroidism for a while which eventually I fixed with an increased selenium and iodine intake. Problem with me was that I supplemented with these instead of increasing my seafood intake because I was following food guru advice, but if you keep eating epi paleo you will do great.

And finally I will say that with time the weight will start melting off if you stick to the leptin rx. I lost fifteen pounds in 3 weeks and I wasn't even overweight to begin with. This was following a standard paleo diet back while knowing nothing about circadian biology before I heard about Dr. K, and I'm sure you will see great success because the leptin rx takes things to a new level.

### Jack Kruse — 2019-11-20T18:02:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/restore-gut-junections-with-zach-bush.18973/post-282563>

Another study shows why docs like Zach Bush have zero understanding of how sunlight sculpts the microbiome. If he did he would never try to sell any human restore.......he's sell them on vacations to the sun. This is how Vitamin D is created in the body by UVB LIGHT exposure on the skin and NOT by oral supplementation. This ultimately leads to big improvements in gut bacteria populations. Looks like another win for Kruse Longevity Center in stomping out marketers for the public.
Skin Exposure to Narrow Band Ultraviolet (UVB) Light Modulates the Human Intestinal Microbiome
Microbiol., 24 October 2019
Participants who took vitaminD supplements and use the narrowband had no improvement in microbiome diversity.
Participants who used narrowband uvb light only had significant improvements.
Bacteria from several families were enriched in the group that did not take prior vitamin D supplements.
This is the first study to show changes in intestinal microbiome in response to Narrowband-UVB skin exposure.
This evidence suggests the existence of a novel skin-gut axis that could be used to promote intestinal homeostasis and health.
[https://www.frontiersin.org/articles/10.3389/fmicb.2019.02410/full](https://www.frontiersin.org/articles/10.3389/fmicb.2019.02410/full)

### DLO — 2017-01-23T00:33:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/adding-wheat-back-into-diet.18974/post-207229>

Good interview with the guy who wrote Perlmutter of grainbrain fame

In the original article I also like
**The Importance of Seasonal Eating**
In his book "The 3-Season Diet: Eat the Way Nature Intended," Douillard delves into other fascinating research suggesting there may be biological imperatives to eating foods in accordance to season. Different microbes will be present in soil and plants during different seasons, and by eating certain foods at certain times of the year, you may be able to radically optimize your [gut microbiome](http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2015/07/13/importance-gut-microbiome.aspx).

For example, in the fall and winter, enzymes like amylase are increased in grains. During summer and spring, amylase is decreased, and this enzyme specifically helps your body digest foods such as grains. So it may not be a fluke that grains are harvested in the fall and winter, when amylase levels are at their highest.

_"When you think about \[it\], maybe we are supposed to eat these grains at the right time of the year, as opposed to eating everything all day long three times a day in a processed form, which we can't digest,"_ Douillard suggests. _"We're part of the circadian rhythm of nature. We completely lost that … Birds fly south, whales migrate. Our survival also depends on us being connected to those rhythms of nature. Part of that is what we eat._

Yet they still say that winter is protein/fats.... doesn't the higher amylase mean we can tolerate some in the winter too?

Assume that you are dealing with 100% acceptable grain, say the kind that was grown 2018 years ago..

To be harvested the plant cannot be green, have to be bone dry.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-01-24T15:35:56+0200

### JanSz — 2017-01-24T15:42:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cdc-study-shows-city-living-is-healthier.18978/post-207278>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=207276)
>
> CDC makes 5 billion dollars per year on vaccines. Most are done on city dwellers. Of course they have a vested interest in saying being in the city is safer.........that means more people will come there and get vaccinated by rules and laws they set up.

**Not everybody who helps you is your friend.**

Not everyone who shits on you is your enemy

A little bird was flying south for the Winter. It was so cold the bird froze and fell to the ground into a large field.

While he was lying there, a cow came by and dropped some dung on him.
As the frozen bird lay there in the pile of cow dung, he began to realize how warm he was.

The dung was actually thawing him out!
He lay there all warm and happy, and soon began to sing for joy.

A passing cat heard the bird singing and came to investigate. Following the sound, the cat discovered the bird under the pile of cow dung, and promptly dug him out and ate him.

Morals of the story:
(1) Not everyone who shits on you is your enemy.
(2) Not everyone who gets you out of shit is your friend.
(3) And when you're in deep shit, it's best to keep your mouth shut!

When your body knows it cannot produce heat well, or that it is in a poor energy state, those fears are justified.
When food is the only tool (crutch) you know, you'll find that most problems can be remedied or at least soothed by it.

### JanSz — 2017-01-24T19:56:47+0200

> [nonchalant said:](/goto/post?id=207283)

Walking barefoot when we step on a small stone, there is a reflex where we can lessen the load on that area to lessen possible injury.
I could use a help.
I have a problem getting used to walking barefoot over frozen grass (sharp rigid pointy blades) or snow with ice on top.
Basically I am not able to stop worrying that I may get cut.
Specially after a while on cold snow or ice, I feel my feet less, any reflexes are delayed, and I am afraid of injury.

### nonchalant — 2017-01-24T21:22:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cdc-study-shows-city-living-is-healthier.18978/post-207294>

> [JanSz said:](/goto/post?id=207287)

I don't worry about frozen grass (probably because the sunlight is already softening it, this far south), but I just can't get used to the cold of snow under my feet, though I've tried it during our infrequent snow days for several years. I've been wondering if the problem is that snow insulates my feet from grounding. If I scrape the snow away, I can stand in that spot ok. Since I'm away from the city now, I haven't seen much ice on the ground. Perhaps you could put a tarp on the ground to protect an area? Or would it just be buried in deep snow and be impossible to remove for grounding?

# Thread: Look back and face forward
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/look-back-and-face-forward.18983/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 21
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Just Breathe — 2017-01-25T00:00:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/look-back-and-face-forward.18983/post-207300>

I deleted my old journal because it needed to be rewritten.

37 yr old Female, 250 lbs. I currently live at 44° 56' 39" N / 93° 5' 35" W. All my life I have been very heavy, losing 100 lbs about ten years ago. That's all come back now. Mom grew up on a farm, after engaging in SAD diet and modern life, thyroid issues and recent gallbladder removal.
When I moved to my current location, the health challenges really began.

After about 5 years at this location, I moved into a really pretty old Victorian house. It was gorgeous inside, perfect location! One friend mentioned that there might be mold in the basement, and that the bedroom and living room wiring was ungrounded. I didn't listen to that concern, but I should have. The year before I moved in, a smartmeter had been installed on the bedroom wall.

Within a year of living in the house, things that had been on the edge, crumbled. Major GI upset after every meal, vomiting, diarrhea, at least 3x a day. Extreme fatigue. Stress. With a stressful job and difficult group dynamics in my hobby, I was stressed all the time. I went paleo with the Whole 30. It didn't fix anything. Diagnosis with a smart practitioner of adrenal fatigue. 2 years of following the protocols, and I still had brain fog and fatigue.

Summer of 2015 I began following JK lifestyle modifications, including hacking the environment and CT April of 2015 I began the Leptin Reset. It all worked incredibly well, giving me better results faster than I thought possible! I was cured! I was shrinking and my brain fog was gone!
Fall of 2015 something was turned on in the neighborhood. The wires in my home in the ungrounded areas actually hummed. Tinnitus, headaches, eye twitching, chestpains, crazy scary stuff!
I moved out of the area..when I return to that area I noticed that there was a cell phone tower within a half mile of the home. Whenever I return to the neighborhood, I get the same symptoms.

I moved out of that neighborhood into a new area. This has much more population density and is a worse location. But you do what you have to do. When I moved in, I knew something wasn't right, but it was an improvement over my last location so I tried to bear with it. Over time, my ears started ringing when I was in the home only. Symptoms increased in intensity such that they now ring all the time. I got a crazy case of food poisoning and potentially cardiac arrest when something was turned on in my area, likely the smart grid or 5G. The food poisoning took 5 months to cure. I was left with symptoms of EHS.

I invested in an environmental consultant, who tested body voltage, dirty electricity, RF, geopathic stress, everything. As a result of their testing, we discovered 2 cell phone towers beaming directly in the windows. I invested in RF film for the windows, a Naturell bed canopy, and greenwave filters for the outlets. We moved my bed and they put in a few copper things to "break up" the geopathic stress lines.

Thanks to Jansz comments in another journal, I realized that I was really losing out on potassium, with the struggles to take a full breath and rapidly declining body composition. Regular consumption of prunes and coconut water really helped with that. That year I probably gained another 40 lbs! (With all that crazy, I had abandoned paleo eating, except I still was doing the BAB.)

After the RF film was up, blocking the two towers, my relief and dramatic loss of symptoms is palpable!
Whew!!! Constant sugar and chocolate cravings are gone! After a year which felt like a significant setback, I feel like I'm back on track again. Interestingly, while all that insanity was going on, there was still some healing from the AF that went on inside. I feel like my bones, muscles and organs still did some great healing last year and I am in a better spot today. I am frustrated at my huge size, of course. Once the films were up, (2 weeks ago) I stopped the almost constant weight gain. I feel like I've lost the leptin potential sensitivity I had, when I was losing lots and changing shape over night. I now have drippy sweaty head when I workout, which had disappeared with the leptin reset.

I know I'm still in a bad location and have plans to move. The market is a bit hesitant to hire right now, so I hope for some movement on that this year. I'm concerned about bias in hiring based on the way I look, but I just have to do my best and remind myself that there is a company out there that is just waiting for me!

Bad location equals vigilance:
Vitamin D & K
Turmeric
Probiotic
Vitamin C & 50 mg zinc.
Biocidin drops once a week to stave off anything and help my poor gut win again!

20 G magnetico within a naturell bed canopy.
Planning to dump the cell phone and smart TV.

Grounding and being outside for the sun are hard in this location, its a concrete jungle and the parks are not very safe. I do look out the window in the mornings. The windows have the RF film, which drops a lot of the UV coming, but its better than nothing.

I dance once a week and have been attending barre classes twice a week. Rest is key for muscle building for me. After three years no exercise allowed from AF, it is SO wonderful to workout again! I take it slow, do CT, take magnesium, rest. A few days later when I try again, my muscles feel strong and improved, no soreness at all!!

Yes to all spring water, all blue light sources in the home and workplace have been blocked. Nothing in the bedroom except a white noise machine. I turn the bedroom breaker off at night. The food poisoning was from seafood, so it took me about 6 months to returning to adding seafood to my diet again. I did do a bit of fiddling with iodine and selenium last fall and this winter. What I learned is GO WAY Slow. Once or twice a week dosing is all I needed with one drop to feel that things were on the move. I had the weird acne on my face and scalp so I was like, yeah let's be cautious with this.

Healing is a non-linear path, alright. While I thought all last year was a huge setback, there were some gains forward too! I learned a lot and figured out I can't always be in charge and its ok to just be.

Snoring is still an issue, likely because of my size. I had a sleep study done and I had mild sleep apnea during a very short time in the evening. Sleep tech was not concerned and thought a CPAP wasn't necessary. The sleep doc gave the choice to CPAP or sleep on my side and lose weight. After the same doc was talking to me about calories to lose weight, I elected to focus on weight loss.

Going forward: Leptin reset principles. I was exhausted with all that and struggled to cook, so a lot of grains and sugar were in my diet. Back to cooking and thinking clearly again! yay!
Issues to work on: weight loss, tinnitus, EHS, snoring.
Cheers to my fellow biohackers!

### Just Breathe — 2017-01-26T22:36:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/look-back-and-face-forward.18983/post-207402>

I thought of a few more updates & clarifications that may be helpful.

**Plantar fasciitis
**
My case of PF was multi-factorial. 30 years of being on releve made for shortened achilles, tendons and calf muscles. Physical therapy, acupuncture and boots that stretch the heels out were helpful, but did not do the trick at all. I believe this condition was excessively aggravated by a poor nnEMF environment. Often, PF is electron loss. Following the approaches to regain electrons, seafood, water, grounding, made a huge difference! The last factor is still in progress. I had a massage therapist ask if I ever used a treadmill, stair stepper or elliptical. I said that yes I had. She said that regular use of these non-native movement patterns can put your spinal cord into a twist (stair steppers/ellipticals) or a jam (running on a treadmill) that can be difficult to undo. She turned me on to the Egoscue method. It consists of a series of exercises that take a half hour. They put your spine into realignment. To maintain and keep this realignment you need to be walking (outside is best of course!) for 30 min, 3x a week. A poor nnEMF environment slowed the success of this, tightening and stressing my muscles. I highly recommend this method for anyone who has tried everything...It also helped with a knee ache.

**Iodine/Seafood**
It's often recommended that iodine protocol be matched with selenium in the form of brazil nuts. I'm not a fan of the brazil nut approach, as you can't tell what amount of selenium is in them. I did 200 mg of selenium, b complex and one drop every few days. Vitamin C comes in powder form and can be dumped into an emergen-c shot to have a heavy dose that's not in those huge pills. After researching Vitamin C, I tested in a separate hack what my level was. I read that you can go up as high as you need to, until loose stools.. that is your clue for maximum dose. vitamin c worked better when taken at a different time than the iodine. Wild-caught seafood is best, as is adding sea vegetables to a meal, but hard and expensive for some people to do. I only used the iodine protocol when my tummy was so upset from seafood food poisoning. I think the food poisoning took 5 months to cure because of a not so good environment.

**Candida**
Candida is a real thing and can be incredibly difficult to fight back. I now know that a poor nnEMF environment contributes to candida...candida is a mold and light destroys mold...nnEMF encourages it. I used Candex and a strict no sugar, no fruit, no alcohol, no grain diet. I added drops of Biocidin to an Emergen-C shot to mask taste. Biocidin plus Candex plus the diet is a significant knockout for candida. I supplemented maybe once a week with a half teaspoon of baking soda in water, whenever I felt I was losing the battle. I've got to re-do this protocol, based on last year.

**MSG poisoning**
I got a huge overdose of presumably MSG from a restaurant a few weeks ago. Everyone who ate at that restaurant had searing headaches immediately after and for a week after!!
I tried the cream of tartar hack, putting 1/2 teaspoon in water. Not super helpful. The Vielight in the evening did a much better job.

(might you notice that I've received subpar quality food from restaurants in the past year? I sure have. Blue-lit workers don't care about food quality or service here. I've been served cold food, & when I complained, they remade it, but did not apologize nor comp my meal. I've now been poisoned twice, vibrio and MSG. I'm now cooking at home much more often & carefully vetting a restaurant. One more nail in the coffin towards this so-called 'great' city life.)

**Tanning**
I get a spot of seborrheic dermatitis that arises every year now after the fall solstice and disappears at the spring solstice. Literally within a day or two of the solstice! I go tanning and use red light therapy to treat it. Winter can be polar vortex type weather for months here, so anytime I can get outside and walk, I will. If I can't, I'll consider tanning. I use the expensive beds, based on their percentage of light. Cheap beds just burn you and take your money. If I go in the morning, I'll do 12 min of red light therapy and a 100% UVA session. If I go at noon, a UVA/UVB 50/50 mix bed. If I go in the evening, UVA session and then red light. I try to only go in the day time, when it would be natural for me to receive this light. This, plus turmeric, has been really helpful in the winter. Once the spring solstice returns, or any temperature above 20F, I'm outside again!

### Just Breathe — 2017-03-27T21:02:06+0300

I went to see a seasoned acupuncturist for sinusitis and tinnitus. I have been going twice a week for close to 8 weeks and have made HUGE improvements!

She noted that I had a very sensitive system and nothing was talking to anything else. Each system was angry and fighting for dominance! Ha, no surprise there.

After a few sessions focusing on all systems talking to each other, we started adding in points for any and all points of the digestive system. Due to hitting those points, something crazy was flushed out of me. The detox/flu from that took about a month to recover, with joint aches, severe headaches, major EMF sensitivity and exhaustion. I was taking activated charcoal just to help me get through it all. I'm curious what others think what might have been flushed out? I had a gallbladder attack during that time and things have since calmed down. I added in MSM and DMSO to help flush things out. Having such a sensitive gut system, I can literally "feel" in the evenings when bad guys are being taken from one spot to be flushed out of my system. It's been a wild ride with this acupuncture thing, but it has been SOO WORTH IT. Sinuses are opening up and now more receptive to the vielight. Skin is more receptive to the sun and redlight/tanning treatments. We finished outfitting the house with RF Film on all windows, so now with the acupuncture, tinnitus is GREATLY reduced. EMF sensitivity is incredibly reduced as well.

During this time, I've also re-focused on Leptin Rx principles. I've been imperfect with food selections but perfect with timing. I'm now having the incredible hot flashes after I eat, or right when I am supposed to eat. I started my re-focusing in early February. This is GREAT NEWS! The last time I tried the Leptin RX, it took 4 months to get the heat after eating. (All last year I made sure to have a BAB within an hour of waking, so still chipping away, even though that was all I could do.)

I specifically selected a community acupuncture business that offers sliding scale and community style. You can go more often, inexpensively and get the treatment you really need. This one also keeps good records and everything is hard-wired in her practice space. I previously went to one that had a ton of wifi and TV's with aquarium scenes and it felt like a wasted electron exchange where I was the loser. I've also tried it in the past, but had no success because my home environment was terrible.

This new place is A+++

It's been almost 1.5 years since I've had real forward progress on anything. I'm so happy to finally report it!

Based on what you've written, I'm going to write what I'm up to in regards to that.

Morning light: I'm out there 3-7 days a week catching at least ten minutes. Ideally before sunrise, but always getting at least ten minutes before 9 am. Weekends its much more. RF films on the windows block a lot, but not all, of the UV.
Weekdays are spent with a few walks during the workday to get light outside, grounding and ideally the full lunch hour outside. With a book, of course.
Cell phone has a low blue light film on it and usually stays at home on airplane mode. Work computer has a privacy screen and f.lux. A full spectrum bulb on a lamp next to me. Fluorescent bulbs everywhere and a TON of wireless. Laptop is on a charging station by my feet with two defender pads between me and it. Mouse and keyboard are hardwired.
Weeknights have a total of 2.5 hours under flourescent for my dance classes. The rest of the evenings are at home, going on walks outside or using natural lighting in the home if I can. I'm in a condo in a city center, so it takes effort to get to greenery. After dance classes I'll be in a cold bath.
Salt lamps are on timers and are on from 7 am to 10pm in the home. Blacklights in the bathroom and a purple and red light in the living room. I'll get a red light treatment once or twice a week.
If I watch TV, its on a hardwired screen wearing Carbonshades.
Weekends are camping or heading to a park for a walk or a book.
Bedroom is a full RF canopy/faraday cage over & around the bed, on a mattress on 20G magnetico. Breakers are off for sleeping, no wireless devices in the bedroom.

Job search and relocation plans are still in the works. They are just taking a while.

I had a great time shrinking and I miss it! New to me: I have 3 BM's in the morning, processing junk out and take charcoal every once in a while to suck up the junk. ### Just Breathe — 2018-02-14T00:14:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/look-back-and-face-forward.18983/post-234699>

The past 8 months have been something else !

Amazingly, I lost about 1 inch a month each month last year, starting in about April.
I still had a lot of bloating, tummy pains, tummy pain 2 hours after eating, loose technicolor BMs (3x week), Gall bladder pains, exhaustion and stress.

What didn't work:
DMSO +NAC +MSM (it solved the GB pains, but then nothing else.)
colostrum
Garden of Life probiotics
Biocidin
coconut oil

After further testing and review, in December I started a protocol of:
MegaSpore Biotic
Zinc Carnosine 30mg
Garden of Life Ultra (with Saccharomyces Boulardii in it)

This is one heck of a detox! I even had nightmares about bugs and snakes!

I had the detox type flu/cold, bizarre acne on scalp and face, loud tummy, sweating, no appetite, dizzy. After a while eating food felt like a waste of time...so I cut back to one kind of BAB a day and some vegetable juices. That helped lighten the load of processing on my system. I started feeling like a million bucks when I shifted to juices.
I also added lots of fresh-squeezed lemon water in the morning. This helped with the removal/processing somehow.
I'm still doing acupuncture once a week. They are treating "dampness" and I noted I had a lot of need for warm epsom salt baths during this time.
Over this time, I needed coffee for the energy, not for the 10 am cortisol pick-up. I'm slowly getting to a point where that's not a requirement.
The stress, of course, had me not sleeping well and clenching my jaw at night. Now that the sun rises before I need to get up, I don't need an alarm and I wake up with the sun. I still get a morning light dose, red light in the morning, red light at night.
I do CT after a workout, and I can work out up to 4 times a week if desired.

Inches being dropped, I'm not needing a lot of food but needing a ton of water, 10 lbs lost in a month. Loose technicolor BMs down to 1x every two weeks.
Things are moving in the right direction!
My old workout clothes are stretched out and are falling off, so I need new ones!

The best part of last year and continuing into this year:
I faced down some darkness/low-dopamine people in a way I never expected to. I used every skill, every savvy, every tool available to me.
My entire life experience brought me to these moments.
I kept shining the light. I led the way. I spoke up when it was hard and scary to do so.
Every softball thrown at me, **I knocked it out of the park**. **Every. One.**
I don't know what God's plan is for all this, but I embraced my role, and **I NAILED IT**!

All that complaining I've done on here about all the stressful & bizarre environments I've been in?
I know now that they were preparing me for this and I am grateful for them.

I was being prepared and schooled in order to do what needed to be done. Also to be ready for the awesomeness coming my way.

P.S. I'm close to being able to fit in the clothing in my avatar picture again.

### Just Breathe — 2018-07-12T01:58:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/look-back-and-face-forward.18983/post-245689>

Hey, long time since I posted...I've been a busy gal.

That big scary project I'm working on? It's still very stressful, but I am shining the light, leading the way and repeating facts and truths for others to observe. I am doing GREAT and SO PROUD of what I have done! This scary project brought me to a place where I had to believe in myself and take new scary steps every day. I'm still doing it but each new scary step is less scary.

Stress slowed my weight loss progress, as did an interruption in the ability to get the spore pro-biotics. I had to take a few months off and let things chill.

Things that have been on my mind:

Acupuncture has been very successful for me for over 1.5 yrs now. So if acupuncture is effective and it manages qi, manages bad energy and balances chakras, what else could I be doing to balance my chakras?

Randomly, I ended up going to a music festival. It was not advertised as such, but it was a sacred music festival (not Gospel or religious sacred music). Listening to some of the sacred musicians and bands? Same feeling as the completion of an acupuncture treatment. It turns out, the sacred music folks and hippies have much in common with this group.
They believe in:
1\. reconnecting with the Earth to charge up
2\. the Sun has millions of light codes that we need to download
3\. healing yourself first helps heal the Earth
4\. vibrational frequencies matter
5\. Quantum entanglement is a big deal
6\. Crystals help us manage and modify our frequencies
7\. Thoughts have matter/energy and can create reality
8\. When you fill up with light and love, you are unstoppable at creating more
9\. Gratitude is key
10\. Sound healing at specific frequencies can heal
11\. Your electromagnetic body operates at certain frequencies to move or create energy

Sound familiar?

While I was there, someone gave me a few trinkets of something containing crystals called "orgone energy." I didn't really get what it was, but I certainly felt "clearer" after wearing it for a bit. This stuff is claimed to have EMF protection, but in my experience you must properly manage your environment and reduce nnEMFs, not rely on a trinket. Having said that, what if this stuff has something to it for managing qi?

There was also a "gong tent" at this place where you could get a "gong wash": sound vibrational healing?
Drum circle: sound vibrational healing?

Anyways its been a lot of fun reading about all this stuff. I picked up a book called "Hermetic Code in DNA" which discusses light and DNA and its divine design. After viewing the solar eclipse last year, I'm keeping an eye on the skies as well...daytime and night time.

I've been managing my qi, trying to manage stress, blasting light, love and truth where I"m called to. I consume very little blue light, perhaps only when I enter a store with fluorescent lighting or something. REd and purple lights in the house on during the day and into the evening briefly. Blublockers on at all times on computer.

Physical things that still bother me/undesirable:

I now "need" coffee to wake up when I feel foggy in the morning. It's not every day, maybe 3x week. It does not seem to tie in with the days that I did or did not get morning sun. I'm not proud of this bad habit, but I am where I am called to be right now and doing my finest work.
Stress: clenching the jaw at night and waking up with headaches. Caffeine helps the headaches too.
Still very overweight
Low energy
tummy aches and the loose technicolor BM once a month.
I can have burning or sore feet in the morning or during the day. It's not clear what causes this. It's not plantar fasciitis, that's gone. This is something different. Acupuncture folks say I'm not moving all the right fluids around so feet and calves are missing out on their nourishment.
I know I'm still estrogen dominant because of the chin hairs thing.

### caroline — 2018-07-12T02:40:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/look-back-and-face-forward.18983/post-245694>

nice update. I love the sounds of that music festival! I keep wanting to get a singing bowl....

### Just Breathe — 2018-07-12T05:10:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/look-back-and-face-forward.18983/post-245697>

Thanks, Caroline! It was an amazing music festival! Someone I met there said the festival finds you at just the right time.

A few random other thoughts:

They have a concept that we all have light bodies that are in alignment with the chakras. I'm not a master on the subject at all, but this was a beginner link for me:
[http://www.karenneverland.com/chakra-system-and-light-body-basics/](http://www.karenneverland.com/chakra-system-and-light-body-basics/)

There is also a concept out there that any trauma impacts DNA and stays with it. I think the theory is also in the book I mentioned above. Theory as I've read it: heal the chakras, heal the DNA, impact light body and physical body. I could definitely see trauma impacting DNA.

Say "Om" heal thyself and step into the light!  Not a bad message.

Two years after food poisoning, I'm ready to eat some fish and seafood again.

My phrase of the week: "Boom Shaka Laka!"

Deploy as needed.

### Just Breathe — 2019-01-06T04:08:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/look-back-and-face-forward.18983/post-259539>

Great news! An update and request for advice here.

In July and August, I did the Leptin Reset AND IT WORKED. FINALLY!

Since August, I have lost what feels like 50 lbs I don't weigh myself but I've dropped from a size 24 to a size 16. I've donated SO MANY bags of clothing!
I had all the symptoms of leptin sensitivity. I noticed that for me it mattered much more WHEN I ate, rather than WHAT I ate. It has been absolutely effortless and a wonderfully fun ride!

I picked up weightlifting and it is easy and enjoyable. I'm doing the Body For Life 20 min work outs to complement my aerobic sprint-type dancing classes.

I had great recovery from exercise, didn't need CT, just wanted warm epsom salt baths to pick up lots of magnesium. I just get a little sweaty at the end of the work out.
Slept well, amazing energy.

So I've run into a bit of a stall. Holidays and solstice seem to have halted my progress. At 44th Latitude it was difficult to eat before sunset for the past month. I don't seem to have lost any weight since mid-December and energy levels are up and down. After a five day visit to someone else's house for the holidays, I definitely feel off. I picked back up where I left off, on the first; solid high fat moderate protein breakfast, paleo lunch, paleo dinner. I had the large protein meal tonight at 4:30 and by seven I have hunger pangs. Sometimes I'm super cravy and hungry for meals, sometimes no interest at all. When I do eat, I get full pretty quick and pretty uncomfortable (for lunch and dinner. Breakfast seems to be normal response.)

Can you get off-track for a bit and just get back on track and keep going with this? I'm inclined to just keep going, get back to a good routine and see if it all mellows out.

Or would I have to do another reset? I feel like it's only been a month of being "off," so I don't know how much harm that does. Perhaps I should change tactics somehow now that I'm smaller? I welcome any suggestions.

in life news, I've embraced the purpose God has for me in this time. I battled it for some time, being very afraid of what I was being asked to do. I am not afraid and I am guided by God. I have been doing lots of emotional healing (perhaps trauma has an impact on your DNA..).

These days I am working my tail off, but I am WINNING. I am WINNING SO HARD!

As for surviving winter in the frozen tundra, I think I am holding light and holding a charge better than I have in my lifetime. Tanning seems like a waste of time, but I still have a craving for red light here and there. It's been a pleasant and painless winter so far. I've only needed those warm epsom salt baths to warm up from time to time.

I haven't visited this site much in the past six months. That is in great thanks to the work Dr. K put out there and all of you. I was able to turn SO much of my life around thanks to everyone here and I wanted to say thanks!!!

Here's to an AMAZING 2019 for all of us!

Thread closed for avatar infraction.

# Thread: Breakthrough
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/breakthrough.18988/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 39
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Sean L. — 2017-01-26T02:48:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/breakthrough.18988/post-207360>

I've been a passive commenter and active practitioner of the quantum protocols on this forum for about 2 years now. I feel like I have to share my story and breakthrough as inspiration. I may also need to pack up and move south...

I'm 27 years old and live in New Hampshire. For context, I was an elite athlete in high school and graduated from one of the top business schools in the country at age 20. I was fit and smart and quickly became sick and stupid. A car accident spiraled my life out of control and I've been very sick since then but with the help from Jack and the members of this group, I've healed about 99% of my ailments (listed below) and today felt a since of baseline and relaxation I thought would never return.

I'm 5 days into a winter escape to Florida and sat down to dinner on the beach tonight with my wife and out of nowhere, tears start welling up in my eyes and I started crying like a little girl. I'm very quiet, reserved, almost stoic-like when it comes to my emotions, but I couldn't control the powerful response to what felt like a complete return to equilibrium, a sudden wave of normalcy and tranquility after years of chaos and wreckage. It was an eerie sense of weightlessness and a complete devoid of any tension in my nervous system. I've felt really good lately after years of progress following these principles, but my mind was vividly clear and almost sedatively relaxed. I've been splurging on sun and grounding this week after a winter loaded with Seafood, CT, grounding, nnEMF reduction, etc.

With the help from Jack and everyone's participation in this group, i've successfully overcome: a 7 year drug addiction (opiates, benzos, amphetamines, marijuana, you name it), chronic migraines and brain fog from a traumatic head injury, severe chronic fatigue syndrome, chronic pain from degenerative discs, joint pain where I couldn't walk down stairs, dropped 45 pounds, eliminated the annual confrontation with seasonal affective disorder, reversing nervous system damage from drug withdrawals and several encounters with life threatening serotonin syndrome, chronic bruxism, insomnia, PTSD, massive depression and panic attacks, cognitive impairment, memory loss, seizures, leaky gut (still in progress but about 90% better. Would love additional tips on getting over the hump here), and last but not least the wasted time, hope and money on countless doctors, cardiologists, neurologists, therapists, psychologists, nutritionists, pharmaceuticals, etc.

I used to fear discomfort, hide from the sun, betray my circadian rhythms and seek pleasure to find happiness. Adherence to natures rules and patching up the holes of disintegration have proven a worthy venture to this point. I'm grateful for everyone's continued investment in this forum and hope this can inspire those who share in this daily grind, in a perpetual climb towards the light at the end of the tunnel.

Sean

### caroline — 2017-01-26T03:16:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/breakthrough.18988/post-207363>

I love how you have written of your successes Sean - you sound so capable and genuine and dialled in. I had that same uncontrollable crying response one day at the ocean and weightlessness etc. You described it beautifully.

Never forget ..... When you know better - you do so much better!

You are a great communicator Sean and I can't wait to hear more!

tips??? - just keep reading the blogs and listening to the podcasts .... and make sure you get to the next quantum meet up. Some how you must make that happen....

Is your partner on board?

Welcome!!

BTW - please give Jack some Health history and your Mom's and Gran's.

Where do you live? work?

### Sean L. — 2017-01-26T03:45:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/breakthrough.18988/post-207364>

Thanks Caroline - really appreciate your warm comments

I've spent my entire life in New England. My grandmother is from Ireland and is a very bright woman now at 79. She is medicated like crazy for heart failure but maintains high energy and is super with it cognitively. Mom is 57 and struggles with depression but seems in good health otherwise. My moms dad died of a heart attack in his 30s. Genetics aside, I've certainly been operating with shit mitochondria from the dirty fishbowl I've been swimming in.

I've worked from home the past 5 years which has helped advanced the healing process with the extra time to focus on getting things right. When I started following Jacks protocols, I moved out of an apartment in the city and bought a house on 8 acres of land up away in the woods of NH.

My poor wife was tormented for years dealing with my chaos. I owe her a lifetime of repayment and part of my motivation for healing is to give back the support she gave me. She's an APRN and bought into the conventional dogma she learned in school. That said, she is amazed at my transformation and is buying in slowly. She wears her Uvex nightly now

In terms of lingering struggles, I've recently embarked on the leaky gut protocol about 3 months ago and have made progress via fasting, probiotics, bone broths, glutamine -- but I think I'm in for a battle here. I've been sick a few times this winter between adapting into ketosis late fall and jacking up doses of probiotics.

I've overcome the worst of the storm and now, as I'm starting to reaching baseline, I'm really seeking consistency in energy and mood. I've been hiding in a shell of pain for almost a decade now and just want to get back out with a sense of confidence and vitality. I feel like I'm at the doorstep.

### Sean L. — 2017-03-28T23:59:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/breakthrough.18988/post-211312>

Suffered my 5th concussion last weekend during some MovNat training. Decided to go to Costa Rica and spend the week eating seafood on the beach to expedite the healing process.

I can deal with all the physical symptoms, however am nervous over what I expect will be lingering cognitive impairment.

My last concussion was 2 years ago and I don't feel I ever regained my full cognitive abilities. Word capture, working memory, vocabulary and even my personality seem blunted/reduced. Compounding that with a new injury has me fearful of further decline.

I understand I am still in the eye of the storm, but want to get ahead of this cognitive issue in particular. I work in tech sales and need to be quick minded.

I've employed the protocols from the concussion blogs, using my FIR sauna, DHA, ketosis, sun, grounding, PQQ, CoQ10.

What else can I employ here? Wondering if CT can help or is too much stress on the nervous system at this point?

Thanks for your input.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-04-02T02:43:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/breakthrough.18988/post-211566>

Humans live their life based on the deep seated beliefs they hold and this fools them because they do not understand Bayesian math of probabilities. QED is all about them and nature is based upon them.

Beliefs are a funny thing. You should listen to my April 2017 webinar..........what do I discuss in it?

I discuss my beliefs.

I can believe in Santa Claus and the Easter Bunny and the Beatles and Marilyn Monroe and Elvis and Mister Ed. Listen - I believe that people are perfectable, that knowledge is infinite, that the world is run by secret banking cartels and is visited by aliens on a regular basis, nice ones that look like wrinkled lemurs and bad ones who mutilate cattle and want our water and our women.

I believe that the future sucks and I believe that the future rocks and I believe that one day White Buffalo Woman is going to come back and kick everyone's ass. I believe that all men are just overgrown boys with deep problems communicating and that the decline in good sex in America is coincident with the decline in drive-in movie theaters from state to state.

I believe that all politicians are unprincipled crooks and I still believe that they are better than the alternative. I believe that California is going to sink into the sea when the big one comes, while Florida is going to dissolve into madness and alligators and toxic waste.

I believe that antibacterial soap is destroying our resistance to dirt and disease so that one day we'll all be wiped out by the common cold like martians in War of the Worlds.

I believe that the greatest poets of the last century were Edith Sitwell and Don Marquis, that jade is dried dragon sperm, and that thousands of years ago in a former life I was a one-armed Siberian shaman.

I believe that mankind's destiny lies in the stars. I believe that candy really did taste better when I was a kid, that it's aerodynamically impossible for a bumble bee to fly, that light is a wave and a particle, that there's a cat in a box somewhere who's alive and dead at the same time (although if they don't ever open the box to feed it it'll eventually just be two different kinds of dead), and that there are stars in the universe billions of years older than the universe itself.

I believe in a personal god who cares about me and worries and oversees everything I do. I believe in an impersonal god who set the universe in motion and went off to hang with her girlfriends and doesn't even know that I'm alive. I believe in an empty and godless universe of causal chaos, background noise, and sheer blind luck.

I believe that anyone who says sex is overrated just hasn't done it properly. I believe that anyone who claims to know what's going on will lie about the little things too.

I believe in absolute honesty and sensible social lies. I believe in a woman's right to choose, a baby's right to live, that while all human life is sacred there's nothing wrong with the death penalty if you can trust the legal system implicitly, and that no one but a moron would ever trust the legal system.

I believe that life is a game, that life is a cruel joke, and that life is what happens when you're alive and that you might as well lie back and enjoy it.

I believe God is light and light is a God...............for all humans.

I believe in an impersonal god who set the universe in motion and went off to hang with her girlfriends and doesn't even know that I'm alive. I believe in an empty and godless universe of causal chaos, background noise, and sheer blind luck.

# Thread: Blood Sugar and UV/Red light
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-sugar-and-uv-red-light.18992/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Dain Sorensen — 2017-01-26T15:26:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-sugar-and-uv-red-light.18992/post-207386>

Can anyone explain this to me?

I am not diabetic but I track my blood sugar. When I take my morning (fasting) blood sugar it is say anywhere from 4.9 to 5.7 typically. When I go down to my basement and expose myself to a red/near infrared sauna I have and then spend say 7 minutes in front of my Sperti (red) UV lamp, my blood sugar shoots up into the 6s every time. Sometimes as high as 6.9. What's going on here? I did not eat or exercise.

Thanks
Dain

### JanSz — 2017-01-26T15:41:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-sugar-and-uv-red-light.18992/post-207388>

I suggest that you de-emphasize blood sugar
and
emphasize fasting insulin.
Aim at 2-3.

..

### Jack Kruse — 2017-01-26T15:41:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-sugar-and-uv-red-light.18992/post-207389>

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-sugar-and-uv-red-light.18992/post-207392>

### Scompy — 2017-01-26T22:25:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-sugar-and-uv-red-light.18992/post-207400>

For the obese (my former self 20 years ago), this makes me believe in part there a possible inverse relationship with fat buildup under the skin pitted against charging up the veins and arteries with sunlight penetration into them. This is where the Quantlet could really 'shine'...

### kris90 — 2017-01-27T16:31:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-sugar-and-uv-red-light.18992/post-207435>

An acute rise in blood glucose is nothing to be concerned about. It could be that glucose uptake is enhanced with your UV/IR light, similar to exercise.

I normally have a fasting glucose of about 4.0 to 4.5 mmol/l, and I remember completing a leg workout in the fasted state, and my glucose shot up to 6.4 mmol/l. I tested again about an hour after, and was at 3.5 mmol/l and felt great.

Exercise suppresses insulin secretion to favor gluconeogenesis, but once exercise is over, any excess glucose remaining in the blood is then shuttled back to muscle via insulin bringing glucose back down to normal. I'm sure UV/IR does the same by enhancing glucose uptake via gluconeogenesis.

# Thread: Thinking of Relocating
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thinking-of-relocating.18995/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Alex V — 2017-01-27T06:23:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thinking-of-relocating.18995/post-207423>

****Hi everyone,

My family has been thinking of relocating, and I would really appreciate hearing feedback from others on the forum.

I live in a Los Angeles suburb, which is a terrible environment. I myself am electrohypersensitive (EHS), and, as a result, I am rarely able to work. Since I don’t really have an income, I am currently still living with my parents. They are fine with moving somewhere else, but it would have to be near a city to which my father can transfer to.

We have been looking through the list of cities to which he can transfer, and one of the cities which we are strongly considering is Portland, Oregon. We realize such a move would entail a number of serious drawbacks: it’s located at a much higher latitude, among major U.S. cities it’s among the cities with the most rainy days (so it’s cloudy very often there; I hear it’s cloudy about 8 months of the year). On the other hand, it also has desirable qualities: unlike most other options, there are houses in very isolated rural areas some 30-45 minutes from the Portland International Airport; they are surrounded by mountains/hills; and they are far from sources of air pollution. Since I’m EHS, I really like the fact that it would be surrounded by hills/mountains, as they would serve as an excellent natural EMF barrier against RF/MW radiation. In fact, of the cities I researched on Portland was the only city for which we were able to find houses in rural areas which were surrounded by mountains or hills, and which were far from airports, cities, or other sources of environmental pollution.

Other options we are considering that my father could transfer to include New Orleans, Fort Lauderdale, Orlando, St. Louis, most of the major Hawaiian Islands, Atlanta and Houston. I know Dr. Kruse lives in the New Orleans area, and that most people are probably wondering why we don’t just go there. But as I mentioned before I suffer from EHS, and am sensitive to radar. But the New Orleans area and all the cities I listed above (except Denver) is flat and would not have mountains or hills to block EMF. But perhaps if one finds a place far from cities, the radar or other EMF would be too insignificant and wouldn’t affect me? I’m not sure? I don’t know enough about the nature of radar. So I appreciate hearing what others think. Thank you very much.
**
**

### JanSz — 2017-01-31T01:29:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thinking-of-relocating.18995/post-207600>

Whatever place you pic, you should rent house or apartment there and live there for a year.

.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-01-31T01:50:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thinking-of-relocating.18995/post-207601>

I think considering this is wise based upon what we already know.
It's impossible for a doctor to practice preventative medicine in today's environment, only the patient can practice preventative medicine.
Moving is preventative medicine that a person has to consider when mitigation is deemed untenable.

Thanks for the suggestion Jan. I don't think I could rent a place for a year, but a couple months is feasible I think.

### JanSz — 2017-02-01T23:19:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thinking-of-relocating.18995/post-207743>

> [Alex V said:](/goto/post?id=207742)
>
> Thanks for the suggestion Jan. I don't think I could rent a place for a year, but a couple months is feasible I think.

I live in NJ 07054, since 1971. Previously 31years in Poland, 10deg higher.
In 1979 I got a one year contract to work in Angra dos Reis Brazil.
After more than 6 months I realized discomfort.
It was the way the beautiful hot sunny days ended, abruptly.
There, relatively close to equator, days and nights are 12 hr long.
Transition from day to night is quick.
I, specially my body expects high sunny days to last till almost 9PM.
There all is over at 6PM when 15 min earlier there were no hints that day is about to end.
I had to wait over half a year to find out that it was a mismatch for me.

# Thread: Doctor Fail
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/doctor-fail.18996/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### John G — 2017-01-27T06:48:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/doctor-fail.18996/post-207424>

So I tried to get my primary care doctor today to get the Optimal Labs testing for me. She said no to 90% of them. I quote, "Why do you need homocysteine checked?", "You can get these tests, but your insurance won't cover them...I need a reason".

Any thoughts? Do any doctors do these? I thought I had great insurance (Carefirst/Blue Choice).

Thanks,

John G

### John G — 2017-01-27T06:51:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/doctor-fail.18996/post-207425>

Oh...I forgot. It was suggested I eat whole grains and she re-instated my old Vyvanse prescription for some fatigue I've been experiencing. XD Love it. Oh quote, "I'm not into that homeopathetic stuff", "I think your diet is terrible". (I follow Epi-Paleo).

### caroline — 2017-01-27T07:02:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/doctor-fail.18996/post-207426>

I would and have made up a scenario of why I need certain labs.....like something in your Mom's or Gran's health history that you are worried about.

### Huck — 2017-01-27T12:42:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/doctor-fail.18996/post-207429>

The doctor needs a diagnosis code to justify the labs to insurance company. Some doctors are quite creative at this - yours isn't.

You might check for online labs. There are several where you just select the labs you want and their doctor writes the prescription. Most are associated with LabCorp. You just print the prescription and take it to your local labcorp or whatever lab they are associated with

You have to pay for the labs yourself, but they are reasonably priced.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-01-27T16:09:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/doctor-fail.18996/post-207433>

Why do you need the homocysteine checked? Tell her it is a way to measure your redox potential because it is critcal in glutathione recycling which helps modulate the NRF2 pathway.

Google and paper on it and hand it to her and you can put my name on the note to her. Upgrade your knowledge to help your patients.

### prime1 — 2017-01-27T18:02:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/doctor-fail.18996/post-207439>

.
maybe this pdf file from Dr Kendal Stewart MD would help? graphs, citations, and more....

[http://www.drkendalstewart.com/wp-c...9/Significance-of-Low-Plasma-Homocysteine.pdf](http://www.drkendalstewart.com/wp-content/uploads/2011/09/Significance-of-Low-Plasma-Homocysteine.pdf)

Any condition that increases oxidative stress tends to increase the demand for hepatic glutathione production. Thus oxidative stress draws homocysteine
into glutathione synthesis, potentially causing a drop of plasma homocysteine to levels where total body glutathione status is critical

.

.

Hi!

Can anyone recommend a blue blocker screen for TV's? I'm growing tired of watching the news with 2 pairs of glasses on! (My RX and then my Uvex).

Lowbluelights.com used to sell one but they don't anymore....any ideas?

Thank you!

Christine

### River — 2017-01-29T07:00:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-blocker-screens-for-tvs.18998/post-207512>

Cool product. Is it a half-truth though, like f.lux?

### PaulG — 2017-01-29T08:48:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-blocker-screens-for-tvs.18998/post-207513>

I think so, they target narrow range of blue in the 460-480nm range so green remains. According to this paper [**Effect of light wavelength on suppression and phase delay of the melatonin rhythm**](https://www.researchgate.net/publication/11593795_Effect_of_light_wavelength_on_suppression_and_delay_of_the_melatonin_rhythm) The shorter wavelengths of 470 (blue), 497 (blue/green), and 525 (green) nm showed the greatest melatonin suppression, 65% to 81%..so this implies that even with blue blockers you are getting some level of melatonin suppression with artificial light.

### JanSz — 2017-01-29T15:08:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-blocker-screens-for-tvs.18998/post-207518>

> [PaulG said:](/goto/post?id=207497)
>
> [http://seesaffron.com/drift-tv-box](http://seesaffron.com/drift-tv-box)

Nice find.
Sort of like flux.com but for TV.
I noted there a sentence:
"Removes blue signal from the red-green-blue signal according to desired intensity."

That is there is still blue remaining, just of lower intensity.

I assume that we do not need any blue at night.
Actually green is also undesirable.

\---

### Rubicon — 2017-01-29T21:32:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-blocker-screens-for-tvs.18998/post-207536>

I've tried a number of things for filtering blue light on displays. To test how well blue/green are being blocked, I open this image and visually check how much blue/green I can see: [RGB.png](http://i.imgur.com/rwemyMX.png)

**Mobile phone screens**
I was telling Jack in Mexico about how good the blue blocking ability of Android phones are compared to iPhone because of apps like Twilight that make the screen really red. However, after recently testing with the RGB image I'm not sure if Twilight removes much blue after all.
Screenshots: [Before](http://i.imgur.com/jBYNn7G.png?1) / [After](http://i.imgur.com/rUgUHOu.png?1) / [Twilight Settings Used](http://i.imgur.com/gzqOp7j.png?1)
I would like to see exactly how the RGB image would appear on a Nightshifted iPhone.
I may try a plastic blue blocking phone screen filter in the future to see how that compares.

**Rubylith Film**
I bought some custom sheets of Rubylith on Ebay that fit my computer monitors. They block all blue, and nearly all green.
[Before](http://i.imgur.com/k2fpCdf.jpg?2) / [After](http://i.imgur.com/aiBTtx2.jpg?2)
I think this would work on the TV if you could get a big sheet of rubylith, but I did not end up using this solution since I found a way to make f.lux do the same/better
Another similar option I've seen but not tried is [this amber UV filter film](http://www.uvps.com/product.asp?code=FILTER+++L)

**F.lux with customized setting for 900K color temp**
Normally the lowest color temperature you can select with f.lux is 1200K. At 1200K there's still a lot of green. @[Rerun](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/9579/) mentions [here](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/flux-optimal-settings.18319/#post-200138) how to go into the registry on a windows computer and set f.lux at a color temp of 900K. At 900K, all blue and green disappears from the RGB image
[F.lux at 1200K](http://i.imgur.com/9pocr9u.jpg?2)
[F.lux at 900K](http://i.imgur.com/K4xFUKJ.jpg?2)
I set my computer monitor to 900K if I use it in the evening, and on a laptop that connects to the TV in the living room for when I'm occasionally watching video.

**Iris**
Getting f.lux adjusted to display at 900K as mentioned above is tricky. Recently a new app has was developed for blocking blue light called Iris ([https://iristech.co/](https://iristech.co/)).
They sell a "mini version" for $2 that has the ability to set the screen to zero blue with much less effort. If you don't mind paying two bucks per computer, I would recommend that at this time.

Some images of my TV connected to a laptop set at 900K in F.lux / zero blue on Iris:
[RGB.png](http://i.imgur.com/n7TBzr0.jpg?2)
[Netflix](http://i.imgur.com/UXh3jwS.jpg?1)
[Forum](http://i.imgur.com/eTKTT9e.jpg?1)

### Rubicon — 2017-01-29T21:41:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-blocker-screens-for-tvs.18998/post-207538>

Since returning home from the Optimal Resolution 2017 event in Mexico we've seen some big gains in our household that we attribute to aggressively blocking blue light at night with F.lux, minimizing screen time, and wearing blue-blocking glasses during the day while indoors under fluorescent/LED lights. Our n=2 experience would confirm that blue light really is perhaps the #1 most dangerous nnEMF

### Ted — 2017-01-30T02:18:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-blocker-screens-for-tvs.18998/post-207554>

Wow, thank you for this amazing information. I need to give these things a try

### LisaLearning — 2017-02-06T21:18:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-blocker-screens-for-tvs.18998/post-208071>

lowbluelights told me that they stopped selling the larger filters that cover televisions because the optical quality of them was not very good. I think they said they still had some left, so you could try getting in touch with them directly to discuss.

I went looking for a large screen protector bc, like you, I was uncomfortable with my rx plus uvex over but I never found anything that really blocked any significant blue. I expect most people will only tolerate a very mild tint on a filter (like blutech) and anything else is not going to sell to the mainstream market, so there isn't anyone doing those as far as I can tell.

### JCDC — 2017-02-28T15:58:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-blocker-screens-for-tvs.18998/post-209429>

Night shift is worthless on iPhones but for some rI haven't seen anybody mentioning the new-er capability the iPhone has with iOS 10. Its called display accommodations and its better than twilight on my friends android and puts the jailbroke F.lux I used to have on my iPhone to shame.
Settings--general--accessibility--display accommodations--color filters--turn color filters on--select color tint--drag intensity & hue bars all the way to the right.
Using rubicons pics above I cannot see the red or the green. Occasionally this makes it really hard to see some things on the phone that are colored primarily blue/green but that can be fixed by turning on the gray scale which just adjust the relative colors of everything so its like looking at a black and white screen but dark red and light res instead.

There is also a way to "reduce white point" and set a shortcut to activates the on command simply by pressing the home button 3 times
[https://www.howtogeek.com/242626/use-a-triple-click-on-your-iphone-for-these-useful-shortcuts/](https://www.howtogeek.com/242626/use-a-triple-click-on-your-iphone-for-these-useful-shortcuts/)

### Scompy — 2018-04-12T04:41:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-blocker-screens-for-tvs.18998/post-239261>

This video shows us how we are being visually tricked by "Oz Behind the Curtain" and why we need blue-blockers and mitigation strategies against artificial lighting.

**Seizure Warning!** I only watched a few sections of this video with my blue-blockers, shrunk down my web-browser to a very small view and listened (audio) to most of the video rather than viewing it. But it's a great resource to show what goes on in front of you!

### Katie Durham — 2019-01-02T22:24:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-blocker-screens-for-tvs.18998/post-259353>

> [Rubicon said:](/goto/post?id=207536)
>
> I've tried a number of things for filtering blue light on displays. To test how well blue/green are being blocked, I open this image and visually check how much blue/green I can see: [RGB.png](http://i.imgur.com/rwemyMX.png)
>
> **Iris**
> Getting f.lux adjusted to display at 900K as mentioned above is tricky. Recently a new app has was developed for blocking blue light called Iris ([https://iristech.co/](https://iristech.co/)).
> They sell a "mini version" for $2 that has the ability to set the screen to zero blue with much less effort. If you don't mind paying two bucks per computer, I would recommend that at this time.
>
> Some images of my TV connected to a laptop set at 900K in F.lux / zero blue on Iris:
> [RGB.png](http://i.imgur.com/n7TBzr0.jpg?2)
> [Netflix](http://i.imgur.com/UXh3jwS.jpg?1)
> [Forum](http://i.imgur.com/eTKTT9e.jpg?1)

What I believe Rubicon was referring to, and what I discovered this weekend and confirmed with the nice folks at Iristech, is that if your tv is hooked up to your computer via HDMI, then your tv screen will reflect conditions on your computer screen. I often hook up my laptop to my tv so I can turn up the sound on Dr. K's podcasts. Iris lets you turn blue light way down this way:

[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=90OD-hD-0Us&list=PLYgsC6pUhQFpDMsoxDQQgZYaikOFzevw6&index=10](https://protect-us.mimecast.com/s/cG18C2kqpycVzzXnfnlxBG?domain=youtube.com)

Either way I wear blue blocking glasses, and I have a Reticare screen on my laptop, but this gives me more flexibility with my tv.
