# Thread: Consequences of Any Light After Dark (even Red)
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/consequences-of-any-light-after-dark-even-red.19002/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 28
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Matty_M — 2017-01-29T03:53:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/consequences-of-any-light-after-dark-even-red.19002/post-207507>

Through what mechanisms can non-blue light (red/orange/etc.) alter sleep and circadian rhythm?

Jack suggested me against using a computer after dark, even with red or "darkroom mode" (inverted colors), even if wearing blue blockers. He also mentioned on FB that no light after dark is truly ideal.

How detrimental to regeneration is using light like a candle after dark, if wearing 600nm(all red) blue blockers?

**Using non-blue light after dark may appear to _extend_ the waking day, but is it actually _taking_ "time" in the form of health/focus from a later date, by lowering our regeneration?**

Does anyone use no fake light after dark? Have you noticed a signicificant difference in your n=1 between complete darkness after dark vs. candles/red light?

Is dim red light the least potentially disruptive light for after dark? Again, how, and to what extent can even this hinder regeneration and health?

Your thoughts are appreciated

### caroline — 2017-01-29T04:22:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/consequences-of-any-light-after-dark-even-red.19002/post-207511>

We normally don't have any lights on at all - we have our breakers off ...... but the last week we have been watching the Australian open \[tennis\] on TV - with blue blockers on and only a hour or two. Our sleep is totally screwed.

When we were camping for 5 weeks there was no TV and only very limited computer use occasionally and almost zero iPhone use. We were outside from daybreak to sunset.
we both slept like babies and often didn't even need the toilet.

and - our new blue blockers block way more blue lite than our cheapie ones used to......

we, generally, don't even use candles - we find them too bright.

love your new pic Matty!

### Rubicon — 2017-01-29T22:10:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/consequences-of-any-light-after-dark-even-red.19002/post-207540>

I haven't been able to discipline myself to eliminate all screen time & fake light 2-4 hours before bed. However, [blocking all blue/green using f.lux](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/blue-blocker-screens-for-tvs.18998/#post-207536) for me has had a noticeable subjective effect.

_Through what mechanisms can non-blue light (red/orange/etc.) alter sleep and circadian rhythm?_
I would think the mechanism is photoreceptors in the eye/skin that are sensitive to any light brighter than moon/starlight.
You might be able to biohack how much of an effect red has by monitoring your sleep quality over time comparing when you're exposed to red light vs complete darkness. I recall Ruben mentioning somewhere that of all the sleep monitors he tried the only one that really worked was a non-contact radar-based device. Perhaps something like this: [https://sleep.mysplus.com/](https://sleep.mysplus.com/)

_Using non-blue light after dark may appear to extend the waking day, but is it actually taking "time" in the form of health/focus from a later date, by lowering our regeneration?_
Dr Kruse mentioned in the biohacking mitochondria webinar using a crisper CAS9 kit to biohack your bacteria/yeast. Perhaps there is an application here. This may be a total bs idea...I've got a crisper kit on order, but have not learned much about exactly how to use it yet.

### Dimitri — 2017-01-30T02:08:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/consequences-of-any-light-after-dark-even-red.19002/post-207553>

One thing I started to pay attention to recently was the lux emitted from my light sources, as this study ([http://journals.sagepub.com/doi/abs/10.1177/0748730413493862](http://journals.sagepub.com/doi/abs/10.1177/0748730413493862)) showed that even 5 lux's of light was enough to suppress melatonin which is pretty dim light. I guess the brightness of lights can have an additive effect in combination with the blue frequencies.

I found a handy app for the iPhone called LightMeter which measures the lux of your environment using the phone's camera. Granted this is not professional equipment and can't be very accurate but it was good in giving me a general idea of how my lighting was.

I found that my Rubylux red small bulb went over 5 lux at a little over a foot away from me. My computer monitor went over at about 2 and a half feet. If you have a mac I would recommend RedScreen as it is more effective than f.lux as it makes the screen completely red and lets you dim the screen further than the laptop's built in controls.

Keep in mind that the lux of a full moon is 1, so yes ideally you would want to use no tech at all.

### Penny — 2017-01-30T19:43:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/consequences-of-any-light-after-dark-even-red.19002/post-207585>

I run a red party bulb (incandescent light) in a lamp which I put next to my books when I read at night - I then pass out - in fact, I sometimes think I sleep better with the bulb on than off - even more weirdly, I have a couple of UV lights on my ceiling fan and sometimes I'll read with that and the red party bulb and I'll be out of the covers and pass out with even the UV bulbs on... it seems to me this is a total circadean mismatch, so I am attempting to just use the red light and turn it off before I fall asleep - I also use black out shades - IMHO, even with blue blockers, your skin is still absorbing fake light from a screen, so media at night is a complete fail -

One other huge thing - a giant hit of oxytocin seems to work regardless of what the hell light you were exposed to...

### Matty_M — 2017-01-31T16:11:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/consequences-of-any-light-after-dark-even-red.19002/post-207637>

> [Ted said:](/goto/post?id=207599)
>
> How late do people use the Uvalights? I have trouble understanding why we want those on when it is night and there isn't any uv outside.

It is not "natural" per se, but if you have strictly UVA light (no blue), it does not work on melanopsin. It provides extra UV for the rods and cones to regenerate, which can also be used to regenerate melanopsin during the daytime, and for dopamine & melatonin creation in the eye. If someone lives in a natural environment (outdoors 24/7) this is definitely not necessary, but for someone who is indoors all day at work, it may be better than no UV at all.

### Sue-UK — 2017-02-06T12:23:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/consequences-of-any-light-after-dark-even-red.19002/post-208044>

> [Matty\_M said:](/goto/post?id=207534)
>
> Thanks. I've had the suspicion that, although blue-blockers protect the eye clock, sleep is still altered by any light after dark.
>
> I'm curious if anyone knows the mechanism. It seems intuitive that any light will damage sleep quality, but I can't find how. I do remember Jack writing about certain photoreceptors that respond to light intensity, regardless of whether it's blue or something else; maybe this is it.

From Niels Finsen's book Phototherapy (1901 translation of an 1894 paper):

"Engelmann says that the _Pelomyxa palustris_ (a kind of amoeba) contracts strongly when exposed suddenly to light, but that it quickly expands under the influence of a sudden darkness. The same author has pointed out that the rods and cones of the retina shorten in light and lengthen in darkness."
(Engelmann, _Pfluger's Archiv_., xxxv., p498)

### Sue-UK — 2017-02-06T14:46:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/consequences-of-any-light-after-dark-even-red.19002/post-208047>

> [Matty\_M said:](/goto/post?id=207507)
>
> Through what mechanisms can non-blue light (red/orange/etc.) alter sleep and circadian rhythm?
>
> Jack suggested me against using a computer after dark, even with red or "darkroom mode" (inverted colors), even if wearing blue blockers. He also mentioned on FB that no light after dark is truly ideal.

I've been thinking about it in terms of photosynthesis (and human photosynthesis). I've seen pictures (light Medicine of the Future by Jacob Liberman) of how chloroplasts stream under natural daylight, another pic done under a light source that lacked UV (many chloroplasts dropped out of their normal streaming pattern and formed a clump at one end of the cell), and a picture of chloroplasts at rest. _"Illuminating the chloroplasts through a red filter, which allowed only the longer wavelengths of light through, resulted in some of the chloroplasts remaining in their normal streaming pattern, some totally dropping out of the pattern, and some beginning to shortcut the pattern. If a blue filter was used, allowing only the shorter wavelengths of light through, some of the chloroplasts again remained in their normal pattern, whilst those that were previously short-cutting moved to a different position prior to beginning their short cut. Dr Ott also found that when he added some long-wavelength ultraviolet light to the microscope light source, so that it more closely simulated sunlight, all the chloroplasts returned to their normal patterns. By the end of a normal day, chloroplasts, like people, gradually slow down, come to a stop and then remain motionless during the night time period. This required period of rest allows them to once again respond to light energy and resume their normal streaming pattern the following day. It appears, therefore, that altering plant cellular function by altering the light source affects the normal process of photosynthesis and the resulting cell chemistry."_

The same book also says: _"In further studies utilizing pigment epithelial cells from the retina of a rabbit's eye, Dr Ott found that filtering out normal sunlight also caused abnormal cell function. When he used a blue filter, the cells went through all sorts of contortions, while a red filter caused apparent cell-wall weakening, followed by rupturing and the eventual death of the cell."_

So my feeling is that any light after dark could be interfering with the "dual control" switch from "day mode" to "night mode" (perhaps 2 modes trying to cycle at the same time?) and (although I don't know about the intensity or duration of the rabbit experiments) I'm cautious about having my eyes (already have damage to one) too close to an unopposed red light source.

### Sun Disciple — 2017-02-13T19:36:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/consequences-of-any-light-after-dark-even-red.19002/post-208523>

summer time Its alot easier to get into darkness after sunset. But January, febuary here the sun is rising just before 8 am and setting just after 5 pm. If I were to blackout everything after sunset I would be left with 13 hours of darkness. I can only sleep for 8 hours I have a hard time sleeping in. When im up im up. That leaves 5 hours of darkness. I could meditate for an hour no problem but what to do for the rest? That is assuming I could fit my entire day in winter light hours, tough to do. So Ive been restricting screen time to light hours no problem. Using blue blockers,red rubylux or beeswax candle after sunset. Killing breaker and complete blackout by 730 or 8 and asleep by 9 or 930. Up at 630 rubylux till blue light starts creeping in at 715 and natural light all day. I dont think I could make a complete black out work realistically at the moment.

I've been trying it and I end up feeling like I'm getting nothing done, because I go through all the motions during the day with school (thankfully I have all morning free periods) and after sunset was generally a time when I would read by candlelight.

I feel like the health benefit of using darkness after dark consistently could not possibly outweigh the benefit from reading an extra 2-3 hours/day, especially if it's Jack's blog and giving me a better understanding of what I need to do for my health, and more so how I will make a career with this stuff.

Yet, everything else Quantum, including CT being outside a lot, seemed like a big inconvenience at first to the low dopamine mind, yet the benefit is so tremendous, I couldn't not do it.

I have a feeling that most people on the forum do not use darkness after dark, especially at northern latitudes, but do use the lowest light possible.

> (2) I read somewhere that Dr Kruse mentioned certain mobile phones were the worst, certain types of cell towers were more devastating, and certain carriers also .. were the worst .. I'd like to know which, given we are the canaries in the coal mine, I think we more than anyone need to know .. Has he put this info out anywhere yet? .. specifics I mean ..
>
> thanks

generally we use UVEX blue light blockers and we also have carbon shades. They are quite a big darker. I will be interested to hear about you RA optics. I would like to get a prescription pair but not sure how to get all that right. My partner bought some prescription glasses from an optometrist that are supposed to block blue light but they aren't very good and cost a lot.

The best cell phones are the really old ones and all the rest are all bad. I don't recall that Jack has said one carrier is worse than another

Jack has just mentioned on FB that we should throw our phones in the nearest river ....or ocean in our case!

Are you following EMF warriors? Jason Baden Powel is in Sydney.

The best things we can do for ourselves is live in very low population areas and as far away from towers as possible, and always keep our cell phones etc on airplane.

drink good, imported water

### drezy — 2017-04-07T01:03:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-the-ct-tub.19003/post-211939>

Here is what I used for my tub:
[Grounding Rod](http://www.homedepot.com/p/ERICO-5-8-in-x-8-ft-Copper-Ground-Rod-615880UPC/202195738) (I got over 5' of it into the earth which in Central Texas ain't too bad)
14 gauge wire I ripped out of some [Romex](http://www.homedepot.com/p/Southwire-250-ft-14-2-Gauge-White-Solid-NM-B-W-G-Romex-Cable-28827455/100010980) I had sitting around in the garage
One [butt splice](http://www.homedepot.com/p/Gardner-Bender-12-10-AWG-Yellow-Butt-Splice-Wire-Connectors-50-Pack-75-126/202522634)
Lastly some [braided copper](https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B003HGHQVU/ref=oh_aui_detailpage_o00_s01?ie=UTF8&psc=1) for in the tub itself

I made absolutely sure:
that my wire was as short as possible (you don't want it to be an antenna)
that wire wire didn't run anywhere near any electrical runs/conduit inside the wall (you don't want it to be an inductor)

All that and my ice source at $1.50 per 20lbs plus a couple 2.5gal frozen ice containers in my chest freezer makes the regularly recurring CT expense for my family (3 of us) about $0.50 per head. I suspect maybe it's raise to $1.00 per head in the summer.

That's what I love about Jack. I spend $0.50 per person on my family's health and notice an across the boards health boost for all of us and this is information that Jack puts out there for free.

Lastly, don't forget to get some cheap epsom salt to throw in there too.

For one reason or another, these don't seem to meet the requirements.

Here's a great thread on infrared light therapy - plus, on Dr. K's facebook page he chats about - I think it was 850nm - IR therapy for hypothyroid -
[https://selfhacked.com/2013/11/14/my-review-of-lllt/](https://selfhacked.com/2013/11/14/my-review-of-lllt/)

Here it is:
Most people know that sunlight has 42% IR-A light present in it. Most people do not know that red light at 613.5 and 623.5 nm (in one spectrum, at 606 nm), in the red maximum have some interesting effects on human tissue. Are there other key frequencies buried in the sun for healing? The far-red maximum has exact peak positions between 667.5 and 683.7 nm in different spectra. Two near infrared maxima have peak positions in the range 750.7-772.3 nm and 812.5-846.0 nm. Can these be used to treat things like auto-immune thyroid disease which is now explosive in the USA with no good therapy? Yep.

[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/22718472](https://l.facebook.com/l.php?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov%2Fpubmed%2F22718472&h=ATMJTwvNb0ZagnvRMzwtUxGoANgdL0H8dMFnWpGxyC7y37rI10fIfHxypLy7jnlvVcr2JzkmZpRywc7K4ExV1xhj9x3Nl70YxHkxshE9hmWSW39EcBuVZtiqCt4&enc=AZPEO_lsTe_nu4x-GUPUeJjdlagVoG-dhl8r8pVHLwt6vsY2EIMfAi5-VOwvG3sZdHEWTIcz62ZYVBrwEPRHO2V_rC92ysA9LU2eQIsnlRJZvKj3hRdRhYvyQ3FCbtZ--nZs2nRpP_Lmv7wNK6kCQQ4keyrQOZKQcIgANhoOnPYQDG-jv4J_O2ZzIHT3KsLmhwBhs2v7-ebuEXANJqsTWWTJ&s=1)

[Lasers Med Sci.](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/22718472#) 2013 May;28(3):743-53. doi: 10.1007/s10103-012-1129-9. Epub 2012 Jun 21.
**Low-level laser in the treatment of patients with hypothyroidism induced by chronic autoimmune thyroiditis: a randomized, placebo-controlled clinical trial.**
[Höfling DB](https://forum.jackkruse.com/javascript:void\(0\))1, [Chavantes MC](https://forum.jackkruse.com/javascript:void\(0\)), [Juliano AG](https://forum.jackkruse.com/javascript:void\(0\)), [Cerri GG](https://forum.jackkruse.com/javascript:void\(0\)), [Knobel M](https://forum.jackkruse.com/javascript:void\(0\)), [Yoshimura EM](https://forum.jackkruse.com/javascript:void\(0\)), [Chammas MC](https://forum.jackkruse.com/javascript:void\(0\)).
**[Author information](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/22718472#)**
**Abstract**
Chronic autoimmune thyroiditis (CAT) is the most common cause of acquired hypothyroidism, which requires lifelong levothyroxine replacement therapy. Currently, no effective therapy is available for CAT. Thus, the objective of this study was to evaluate the efficacy of low-level laser therapy (LLLT) in patients with CAT-induced hypothyroidism by testing thyroid function, thyroid peroxidase antibodies (TPOAb), thyroglobulin antibodies (TgAb), and ultrasonographic echogenicity. A randomized, placebo-controlled trial with a 9-month follow-up was conducted from 2006 to 2009. Forty-three patients with a history of levothyroxine therapy for CAT-induced hypothyroidism were randomly assigned to receive either 10 sessions of LLLT (830 nm, output power of 50 mW, and fluence of 707 J/cm(2); L group, n=23) or 10 sessions of a placebo treatment (P group, n=20). The levothyroxine was suspended 30 days after the LLLT or placebo procedures. Thyroid function was estimated by the levothyroxine dose required to achieve normal concentrations of T3, T4, free-T4 (fT4), and thyrotropin after 9 months of postlevothyroxine withdrawal. Autoimmunity was assessed by measuring the TPOAb and TgAb levels. A quantitative computerized echogenicity analysis was performed pre- and 30 days postintervention. The results showed a significant difference in the mean levothyroxine dose required to treat the hypothyroidism between the L group (38.59 ± 20.22 μg/day) and the P group (106.88 ± 22.90 μg/day, P<0.001). Lower TPOAb (P=0.043) and greater echogenicity (P<0.001) were also noted in the L group. No TgAb difference was observed. These findings suggest that LLLT was effective at improving thyroid function, promoting reduced TPOAb-mediated autoimmunity and increasing thyroid echogenicity in patients with CAT hypothyroidism.

### caroline — 2017-01-31T07:03:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bio-hacks-mito-hacks-anyone.19009/post-207612>

Is anyone doing a mito/bio hack right now? ....... or thinking of starting one?

It would be great if we could get a few people who are starting a mito hack and share information such as -

What do you hope to achieve?

What parameters will you observe?

time frame?

Hopefully Dr. K. can give us some help and guidelines for a successful biohack.

If we intend to be mitochonriacs we might as well walk the walk and talk the talk!

How are you living the "three legged stool"????

what has been working for you? what hasn't?

Have you done a biohack that was crazy successful? or a dismal failure?

What did you learn? how was your n=1 different from other's n=1?

What is working for you? what isn't?

Please share successes ....and failures too - so we can al learn from each other's experiences.

tell me .......

### caroline — 2017-01-31T07:49:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bio-hacks-mito-hacks-anyone.19009/post-207614>

mine ....

I am staying away from coffee full stop. I have tried decaf a couple of times but I think I must be very sensitive to caffeine lately- so no decaf either.
Coffee has been very, very detrimental to my sleep.

I am assuming that I am dehydrated from too much computer time/ nnEMF and caffeine is heightening the problem.

I have also stopped using stevia - I think it increases my appetite. I am guessing that stevia has been adulterated lately by mass production methods.

My caffeine issue also puts chocolate on the no go list...... and - I can't stop with just a little.

I am going to stay off coffee and chocolate and stevia until we go away camping in three weeks.

I slept incredibly well on our last 5 week camping trip and I was drinking some coffee - probably one mug a day - although we used freeze dried/instant coffee. I don't know if that makes a difference?

So being outside everyday and all day and no TV, very little computer or iphone made a huge difference to my quality of sleep and not needing the toilet so much in the middle of the night.

Is anyone finding the same issues with coffee? stevia?

I wish I liked tea - but I just don't ..... I suppose I will have to resort to coconut rum ..........kidding!

a glass of red wine with breakfast?????

This is probably just a no brainer ...... coffee and too much nnEMF = dehydration = lousy sleep = way less than optimal

BTW - this coffee issue is in spite of getting tons of sunshine and grounding and outdoor time - it is summer in Oz.

thoughts anyone?

### Lahelada — 2017-01-31T13:45:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bio-hacks-mito-hacks-anyone.19009/post-207626>

Great idea for a thread. I am betting on sun at the moment. My PC is always outside , I am barefoot i.e grounded. CT in a metal bath tub but with temps in their 90s the water is not really cold enough without bags and bags of ice. But I will keep doing it . The second part of the biohacck I am failing at miserably. Blue light avoidance! So many interesting things to see and so little time. The idea is only to use a short morning window for that.

The next biohack will be more interesting,a search for what to do with myself next or more precisely where to do it . To that end I will have my body experience a real winter at altitude with CT every day . I actually found a place with no cellphone reception and electricity supply. But that is three months away.

Edit : I forgot to mention. I bought a Biobeam Biostimulation Light treatment system . Fancy name for handheld device with 660nm and 940nm Leds. I have not delved into it too deeply yet. It does mention the magic 100HZ number when used in pulsed mode. Hmm.

The biohack on the island showed me that it was not far enough from civilisation. But even there I noticed an improvement in brain function. Information retention was better. I had all my unread blogs with me and Life on the edge . The upshot of that biohack was better but not anywhere near optimal. Sleep was disturbed unlike in my own house where that is rare and directly tied to some circadian transgression. Oh remember that I was wondering how a woman who lives there full time outside all the time ends up with T2D ? Deet ... the mosquitoes appear in clouds there. I think that is the secret behind that.
Going back to "gone fishing"

### Inger — 2017-01-31T16:21:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bio-hacks-mito-hacks-anyone.19009/post-207639>

I tolerate coffee pretty well but I do like Walter... I drink water and have breakfast (mostly) before coffee.. and use coffee in between 9 AM and 3 PM. Then it works fine... but I feel not too good if I drink too much. 1-2 cups are fine. I still think about quitting it for a while tho just to see how I feel.

I have started turning off the breaker to my bedroom and living room at 7-8 PM. My partner helped me figure it out because the electricity breakers in this house are terribly old and I have no idea...lol (I am wondering if the grounding is not properly done either...)

I had quite some time with disturbed sleep and I could not really figure out what it was. Could be so many things... new landlords moving in on my left....too much stress.... not eating as optimal, and working late... etc. Well after work stress ended, sleep was still not too good, I was waking up every night once at least and had to pee too.
I plastered the both walls in my bedroom with double aluminium foil and could reduce the wifis from neighbor quite a bit. but issues were still there. Well right after I started to turn the electricity off -
.....that is, from that day on, when I did not woke up in the night anymore, and had not to pee either! This is so cool
My meter does not show much of a difference, which is weird, well, I had pretty good readings anyways, between 2 and 5 volt/m2 (well not perfect.. )

Must be some dirty electricity or something! I am so happy now that I did what I did   feels really good

Hi Dia I'm kind of a freak - I used to drink camomile tea in the morning and all day at work and run circles around people I had \*so\* much energy - the coffee more than makes up for it - for me, it is calming - in fact, I ate 2 biscotti yesterday as a hack after breakfast and dunked them in coffee where I increased the glycine and zippo reaction to it - caveat: they were theoretically non GMO from Trader Joes and mostly chocolate but they only had 16 grams of carbs - I'm trying to see if it's the carbs/sugar or the wheat - today will tell as my son - damn him to hell - made these unholy pretzel fat bombs -

But, if I take magnesium at night, it is bound to glycine as I've heard it helps with sleep - probably because it reduces inflammation - you need proline/glycine/glutamine for your gut wall - this is why a bone broth at night also will help you sleep and in fact, one of my latest - actually good for you hacks - is to increase collagen with bone broths and lime juice - Ben Greenfield sent out a hack that had 15 grams of gelatin plus 200mg of vitamin c to rebuild collagen so I figured the bone broth/lime juice hack would be similar - I think a broth at night would be great for anyone with dehydration/sleep issues - of which I am their queen

### Penny — 2017-02-04T02:30:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bio-hacks-mito-hacks-anyone.19009/post-207906>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=207903)
>
> I wouldn't say she is in a lot of blue light at all. She is a very active woman - plays tennis etc.

Well, the 4G would spike histamine and maybe the addition of the sun would set off a mast cell reaction? Also, remember insulin is made of zinc and if she doesn't sleep, the pancreas is still pumping out insulin - which also depletes zinc... we call that insulin resistance... don't you just love this blog? It's funny, but it's not...

Maybe she's deficient in zinc? My son had histamine issues - the dermatologist prescribed a whopping amount of zinc (it's poorly absorbed) - my son's girlfriend (she was a vegetarian at the time) had histamine issues - they were both low in zinc -
From this article:

[https://draxe.com/zinc-deficiency/](https://draxe.com/zinc-deficiency/)

Zinc is a key factor in how your body stores histamine. So since it is required to store histamine, **zinc deficiency** allows _more_ histamine to be released into the surrounding tissue fluids. This is important for two reasons:

-   **Excess histamine in your body will produce many of the common symptoms associated with allergies (running nose, sneezing, hives, etc.).**
-   **High histamine levels increase one’s sensitivity to all allergic reactions.**

**Will she eat oysters? They have \*loads\* of zinc in them -**

### PaulG — 2017-01-31T11:28:04+0200

We don't have GDV camera's but there are plenty of other ways to interpret health/wellness/improvements. This forum will be great for this purpose..Perhaps creating a seperate subforum is in order, sticky posts, using the polling features etc.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-01-31T15:30:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-quantlet.19011/post-207631>

Today is the Quantlet's birthday. The first devices were delivered to their owners today right off the assembly line in New York. So to celebrate we are going share a factoid that is linked to the Quantlet that we used in development. The Quantlet use many of the same principles that nature put in a leaf. A leaf is where photosynthesis occurs. So what ideas did we borrow from nature? The Reaction Center of the leaf acts as a solar battery that generates an electrical potential from sunlight. The first step in photosynthesis is the absorption of light by an antenna pigment (chlorophyll). This absorbed energy is then transferred to the reaction center, which becomes electronically excited by the exciton created. The reaction center is responsible for using this electronic excitation to transfer electrons across the photosynthetic membrane these electrons are subsequently used to fix carbon in leafs. In us the pigment we are interested in is hemoglobin that is sitting in water and the RBC's are surrounded by DHA cell membranes to hold this "solar charge". The blood plasma's water is able to absorb a lot of the thermal motion of sunlight. The Quantlet helps that action of blood as a radiator of light energies with the thermoelectric coupler. The reaction center in a leaf is a bit different than a RBC. It functions not despite its thermal motion but by exploiting it. The blood plasma serves that function in our circulatory system and this is why RBC's have no mitochondria, which acts like a "human reaction center" correlate to a leaf. It was believed that electron transfers in nature are strongly coupled through the Coulomb interaction to the surrounding medium in any situation and is thus controlled by thermal motion of the environment. The coupling energy itself has long been considered immune to thermal motion, but this is no known not to be true. This links directly to the double slit experiment made famous in quantum physics. In a leaf this is controlled by stomatal conductance and the angle of inclination of the leaf to the incident rays of the sun. Humans have a different phototropic effect in their blood that is operational in the picture below, and the Quantlet looks to augment to improve performance. The initial and final states of electron transfer, meaning that the states in which the electron is at the donor and at the acceptor proteins, respectively, are only very weakly coupled to each other by an energy (D). Quantum physics requires that the two states must be energetically well matched for the electron to tunnel and transfer to another protein or molecule. This match is achieved sporadically through energy fluctuations induced by thermal motions of the surrounding protein matrix in a leaf and it is accomplished by RBC's floating in the water of blood plasma (93%). This deep science is described by Marcus theory and its generalizations. The amount of nature's science we have put into this device is truly amazing. We are glad you decided to join our tribe of modern quantum MITO-HACKING your performance!!!!!

### WalterNL — 2017-01-31T16:16:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-quantlet.19011/post-207638>

Awesome, will be ordering one very soon.

### Brad K — 2017-01-31T17:34:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-quantlet.19011/post-207658>

Thanks for all your work. I'll be ordering very soon!

# Thread: Optimal in Europe
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-in-europe.19012/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 14
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### WalterNL — 2017-01-31T12:45:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-in-europe.19012/post-207622>

Hello fellow readers,

I read in some other blog about anti-fragility, also read the book with the same title. Becoming more anti-fragile has been my goal, I believe it underlines a lot of things we call health. My personal experience with other diets/lifestyles that focus solely on food and exercise is that while they may improve some biomarkers, increase muscle mass, etc., they also make me more fragile. I was a lot more susceptible to getting ill (like having a cold, headache, skin burn or excessive muscle pain), and the smallest thing could kick me down. I never accepted that part of 'eating more healthy'.

So it was a huge relief that following the 'protocols' have made me much more resilient than I ever thought possible. Getting the basic stuff (more energy, better feeling, etc.) is one thing, but being better able to cope with external stressors is another, since I don't plan to live a sheltered life away from all challenges. Getting sunshine, grounding/magnetism, and good water is what's made the difference. I believe all diets I've tried would've worked much better if I had this groundwork back then.

Perhaps it also helps that the protocols are better substantiated, with a combination of the sciences, old wisdom and human traditions. Getting in touch with nature would've sounded weird to me a decade ago, but at this moment it's so obvious I'm surprised how ignorant I was. Diet is not that interesting to me anymore, except that it's obviously better to have the least processed, local food. I'm not looking for any perfection.

Anyway here's my short road to optimal;

I started my journey to get better after having problems with digestion (bloating, gas, irregular movements, etc.), migraines and low energy. My search led me to paleo and Bulletproof diets which helped but did not suffice. Above all I like simplicity, and while taking many supplements and biohacking can feel liberating for a while, I never see it as a long term strategy. Plus I always felt something was out of whack, something was missing and the usual answer to be more 'strict' starts to get annoying.

Like most people here I can relate the knowledge on the blog to my personal experiences. Several friends and coworkers have gotten sick in seemingly inexplicable ways, but after reading many blogs I could put the pieces together.

I've been implementing large parts of the protocols over the past year. The things I do consistently are mineral water (many great mineral waters available here), red/orange lights in the bedroom (which is on ground floor & near sea level), wearing blue-blocking glasses after sunset, sleep hygiene, and being serious about circadian rhythm. In summer I absolutely need to do CT to sleep properly, but in winter I'm not drawn to it naturally so do it less often, but I sleep in a fairly cold room around 50-55F. I play around with icepacks regularly. During working days I always go outside during lunch, I have to walk a lot in general, about one full hour a day.

Nicotine has helped me push through some rough patches. Too much coffee screwed me up bad a few years back, but nicotine has both stopped dependence and any negative effects I used to have (I was dehydrated like hell while doing 'coffee fasting'). I use supplements like curcumin, iodine, magnesium, B-vitamins, plus maybe astaxanthin, ubiquinol when under stress. Herring, shrimp and mussels are plentiful here. I started to enjoy oysters too, but they're a hassle. I prefer salmon, cod (liver) and sardines for convenience. I also have fish oil caps, mostly sn2-position according to the producer.

Mentally & physically I feel great 95% of the time. I no longer experience the afternoon slump I did for years in college, nor do I ever feel groggy anymore after waking up. Energy levels and mental clarity are simply much better than ten years ago. Another obvious change is that I rarely feel cold anymore. My latest revelation occured while skiing in the French Alps: not feeling cold even with -20C.

My bowel movements are pretty much always type 4 Bristol chart. They come at one or two times: around 7-8 AM and around 3 to 4 PM. Depending if I ate enough. Some days I don't have any bowel movement but there are days I simply don't eat a lot. When I feel like fasting, I fast.

But so far the good news, I don't eat much greens (one cup/day), I eat grains sporadically, usually I only get AM sunlight in weekends, I drink beer about once a month, and sometimes go overboard on the stimulants. I use Wifi at home, and I can tell there's about 20 other Wifi-networks around me. Oh and I wear contacts a few days a week. I've gained about 10kgs since starting to work at an office. The overuse of stimulants/dehydration/stress leads to migraines a once or twice a year, an aura with mild headache (improvement as it was more often and worse).

I've only done a blood test once at 25. It was not comprehensive, but all within normal ranges. Blood pressure was too high, it's within normal range now. Sadly it's not easy to get blood tests here.

I'm still a biohacker, turning into a mitochondriac. I appreciate nature a lot more, and am skeptic of technology even if I still love technological progress. Needless to say I'm constantly looking for ways to improve my environment. I'm living near latitude 52 now, have no plans to move south but who knows what the future holds. Moving to some place outside the city where I can be like the Sphinx is my ultimate goal now. Diet can definitely be improved, but of course it starts with light.. I'll but the Quantlet and a small RO filter for drinking water soon. Getting more sunshine is a challenge, but at least I'm drawn to the sun whenever it pops up here. I also want to visit the sea more often since it's close by.

Summary: 29 yrs old, 52nd parallel north, near North Sea, office work.

Problems fixed:
\-Bowel discomfort/bloating
\-Anxiety
\-Poor sleep
\-Better skin
\-Mental clarity
\-Kidney stones
\-Wrist pain
\-Nausea

Still dealing with:
\-Stuffy nose in certain houses/offices
\-Being overweight (about 10kgs, but least of my worries)
\-Poor habits

Some of my best biohacks:
\-Exo Terra Sun Glo lamps
\-Donat MG mineral water
\-UVEX blue blocking glasses
\-Nicotine gum/lozenge
\-Coffee
\-Astaxanthin & ubiquinol

Thanks for reading, looking forward to join discussions and learn even more.

### JanSz — 2017-01-31T16:25:37+0200

Above all I like simplicity, and while taking many supplements and biohacking can feel liberating for a while, I never see it as a long term strategy. Plus I always felt something was out of whack, something was missing and the usual answer to be more 'strict' starts to get annoying.

Like most people here I can relate the knowledge on the blog to my personal experiences. Several friends and coworkers have gotten sick in seemingly inexplicable ways, but after reading many blogs I could put the pieces together.

I've been implementing large parts of the protocols over the past year. The things I do consistently are mineral water (many great mineral waters available here), red/orange lights in the bedroom (which is on ground floor & near sea level), wearing blue-blocking glasses after sunset, sleep hygiene, and being serious about circadian rhythm. In summer I absolutely need to do CT to sleep properly, but in winter I'm not drawn to it naturally so do it less often, but I sleep in a fairly cold room around 50-55F. I play around with icepacks regularly. During working days I always go outside during lunch, I have to walk a lot in general, about one full hour a day.

Nicotine has helped me push through some rough patches. Too much coffee screwed me up bad a few years back, but nicotine has both stopped dependence and any negative effects I used to have (I was dehydrated like hell while doing 'coffee fasting'). I use supplements like curcumin, iodine, magnesium, B-vitamins, plus maybe astaxanthin, ubiquinol when under stress. Herring, shrimp and mussels are plentiful here. I started to enjoy oysters too, but they're a hassle. I prefer salmon, cod (liver) and sardines for convenience. I also have fish oil caps, mostly sn2-position according to the producer.

Mentally & physically I feel great 95% of the time. I no longer experience the afternoon slump I did for years in college, nor do I ever feel groggy anymore after waking up. Energy levels and mental clarity are simply much better than ten years ago. Another obvious change is that I rarely feel cold anymore. My latest revelation occured while skiing in the French Alps: not feeling cold even with -20C.

My bowel movements are pretty much always type 4 Bristol chart. They come at one or two times: around 7-8 AM and around 3 to 4 PM. Depending if I ate enough. Some days I don't have any bowel movement but there are days I simply don't eat a lot. When I feel like fasting, I fast.

But so far the good news, I don't eat much greens (one cup/day), I eat grains sporadically, usually I only get AM sunlight in weekends, I drink beer about once a month, and sometimes go overboard on the stimulants. I use Wifi at home, and I can tell there's about 20 other Wifi-networks around me. Oh and I wear contacts a few days a week. I've gained about 10kgs since starting to work at an office. The overuse of stimulants/dehydration/stress leads to migraines a once or twice a year, an aura with mild headache (improvement as it was more often and worse).

I've only done a blood test once at 25. It was not comprehensive, but all within normal ranges. Blood pressure was too high, it's within normal range now. Sadly it's not easy to get blood tests here.

I'm still a biohacker, turning into a mitochondriac. I appreciate nature a lot more, and am skeptic of technology even if I still love technological progress. Needless to say I'm constantly looking for ways to improve my environment. I'm living near latitude 52 now, have no plans to move south but who knows what the future holds. Moving to some place outside the city where I can be like the Sphinx is my ultimate goal now. Diet can definitely be improved, but of course it starts with light.. I'll but the Quantlet and a small RO filter for drinking water soon. Getting more sunshine is a challenge, but at least I'm drawn to the sun whenever it pops up here. I also want to visit the sea more often since it's close by.

Summary: 29 yrs old, 52nd parallel north, near North Sea, office work.

Problems fixed:
\-Bowel discomfort/bloating
\-Anxiety
\-Poor sleep
\-Better skin
\-Mental clarity
\-Kidney stones
\-Wrist pain
\-Nausea

Still dealing with:
\-Stuffy nose in certain houses/offices
\-Being overweight (about 10kgs, but least of my worries)
\-Poor habits

Some of my best biohacks:
\-Exo Terra Sun Glo lamps
\-Donat MG mineral water
\-UVEX blue blocking glasses
\-Nicotine gum/lozenge
\-Coffee
\-Astaxanthin & ubiquinol

Thanks for reading, looking forward to join discussions and learn even more.\[/quote\]
migranes are often corelated with very low progesterone

when you walk, try to be barefoot as much as possible

do not use cod liver oil or any other fish oils
if you are able get fatty acids analysis and use its results to regulate your seafood intake

Not sure if you need RO if you have good spring wate available

29 yrs old, 52nd parallel north, near North Sea, office work
you could use Red Sperti
15min/day would help you in maintaining good vit A & D
just start slow, do not burn skin
[https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B001ID8P78/ref=oh\_aui\_search\_detailpage?ie=UTF8&psc=1](https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B001ID8P78/ref=oh_aui_search_detailpage?ie=UTF8&psc=1)

It is good idea to not to be over weighted.
Eat 1x/day, end eating 4-6 hrs before bed time.
You may want to drink only water 2 days/week

..

\-Stuffy nose in certain houses/offices----->>think mold, new carpets (real bad)

### WalterNL — 2017-05-01T11:54:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-in-europe.19012/post-213146>

> [Sun-sybarite said:](/goto/post?id=213144)
>
> @[WalterNL](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19189/) I have never heard of anti-fragility, will have to research that. This was a nice post. I agree with what you said about keeping it simple. All those diets making you take so many supplements and then making you feel bad about not being strict enough - I'm glad to be past that too, hate feeling bad about myself, thats just not right!
>
> Can you share anything specific you did to make the afternoon slump go away? I rely a lot on black tea(caffeine) for that and sometimes I feel groggy after waking up too.
>
> ''Mitochondriac' - interesting word!

The most important thing for me was to fix my circadian rhythm, so having light at right times of day, darkness at night. Things really started to improve once I got sunshine and grounding at least in the weekend. Diet wasn't so important anymore, other than eating fairly healthy and not eating too late. At the office I drink spring water all day long, and for lunch I always go walk outside. I still have coffee and a low dose of nicotine, but that doesn't help me without water.

Exercise did nothing in this regard. Of course it has certain benefits, but nothing with regard to focus & energy while working.

Getting up and sleeping at the right time is not the problem, but being a night owl its easy for me to stray into staying awake later and waking up late as well. I will work on this, think it will end up being a lifelong effort.

Thats true about exercise, in fact I find it more draining than energising these days. Only exception is swimming or any watersports, wonder why...

Drinking spring water vs. Normal water - do you feel a difference?

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carb-timing.19015/post-207677>

Carb timing is a seasonal decision not a diurnal one because of your photopic retina.

### Danco3636 — 2017-02-07T03:20:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carb-timing.19015/post-208094>

> [Leigh Mills said:](/goto/post?id=207831)
>
> Our high carbohydrate vegetables are in winter. I thought I had read that you suggest eating high carb in summer? Is that right Jack?

### Symbiohead — 2017-02-10T20:00:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carb-timing.19015/post-208364>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=207757)
>
> Carb timing is a seasonal decision not a diurnal one because of your photopic retina.

Is there room in there for another element besides seasonality? Ive looked into the relationship of photosynthesis and how all organism receive energy and nutrition from the sun (not just plant). As it turns, radiation from the sun as related to its angle makes so that tree fruits, coconuts and nuts receive its highest biophoton nourishment in the morning from around 6m to 11:30am. As the day progresses and angle of the sun shifts then other organisms receive a frequency more conducive to its nourishments. Thus around 11:30 to 6pm land animals like grass fed beef and all vegetables & grasses receive the most biophoton activity and make the best use of photosynthesis. Toward the evening the residual solar radiation- even if unseen- emits the biophotons that feed roots & anything underground like fish & seafood. For assimilation and absorption of true solar nutrition wouldn't it be beneficial to use this as a good guide toward circadian & solar eating?

# Thread: Rocky's Journal and memories of now..
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rockys-journal-and-memories-of-now.19017/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 87
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Rocky — 2017-02-01T05:40:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rockys-journal-and-memories-of-now.19017/post-207694>

Fascinating to be here writing this now as I joined JK in 2012. The thing is; I like to party (smoke and drink) too much and didn't take this as seriously as I should have - even though I needed this way before my join date.

Rocky is not my real name, not even my nickname. I have no idea why I chose Rocky and not Ignatz. My real name is Wyatt.

I am 64 years old now and have enjoyed relatively good health until the summer of 2013 when I landed in the hospital after a gall bladder attack. I sat in the emergency room of the hospital for over an hour before they admitted me. It was a hot June day and I had a winter coat on because I couldn't get warm. An intern had me get them a urine sample and I peed what looked like coca cola. Long story short, seven no food days later they released me after sufficiently poking, prodding, testing, injecting and all the other annoying things they do in the hospital. Still, I'm glad I was able to get help because I might not have gotten through all that otherwise.

The doctor wanted to remove my gall bladder but I liked to eat meat too much and it didn't seem like a good idea to her dismay. She told me I would be back there in 6 months. I still have my gall bladder and haven't had issues since.

Apparently my gall bladder got sluggish for whatever reason and that was the problem. It took me almost a year to get righted after that little brush with disaster.

So begins my search to not only remedy that but to bring my health up even more. I hope this journal is private to Jack Kruse dot com and is not shared over the internet...but, it's too late now.

### JanSz — 2017-02-01T16:06:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rockys-journal-and-memories-of-now.19017/post-207710>

Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

Hi Rocky;

64yo
galblader
like to party (smoke and drink)
It seems the Big I, or ED - erectile dysfunction has settled in but I'm not ready to give that up if I don't have to.
\-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Sex and partying is highly supported on this board.
Smoking AFAICT degenerated somewhat to the point that they would have you take active ingredients and skip dirt that may hurt health.
There was a time here where @[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) was discussing mushrooms in rather interesting ways.
Problem, if any, with above is that it should be done in a way that does not interfere with Circadian rhythm, does not expose us to undesirable nnEMF and is in synch with our %heteroplasmy.

\-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Go to doctor and have him sign this script (see link):
Then go to LabCorp and have a blood drawn.
Be at the labs door at 6:00 or 6:30AM
In my place doors open at 7AM, you want to be first there.
Some of the tests should be done timely.
For a week or two before blood draw, do not eat unusual fats or foods, have your normal average diet.
May want to do blood draw in mid week (so you would have your regular sleeping pattern and do not go there straight from the party).
Be fasting 12hrs, at 6AM drink 12oz clean water
script is here:
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-4#post-199717](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-4#post-199717)
Print the text, top line of text real close to top edge of paper
at docs office ask to have it xeroxed over doctor's prescription pad, fill in your data and doctor sign it.
Final product should look like in the picture, in the link.
2-3 weeks latter go to doctor's office ask for complete copies of test results.
Talk with doctor, latter on post here his ideas.
Print (complete) test results asking printer to create PDF file.
Attach PDF file to you next post.
\========================================

In the mean time get this tests:
You should be able to get them all by your self.

[https://www.spectracell.com/order/](https://www.spectracell.com/order/)
Micronutrient Test (MNT)
CardioMetabolic Risk
\----------

[https://dutchtest.com/product/dutch-complete-2/](https://dutchtest.com/product/dutch-complete-2/)
DUTCH Complete
\-----------------------------------------------

When you have all done (not before)
attach all test results and post it here.
Good idea to post some more of your health history.
Are you missing any body parts?

Post height, weight, waist size or pants size, body fat%
\========================================================
In the mean time:
follow Circadian Rhythm
get sun exposure or via Red Sperti get
Vitamin D (25-Hydroxyvitamin D)(250nmol/L=100.16ng/mL)
observe skin breaking time, do not get burn.

Stay away from fish or krill oils.
\========================================================
/

### JanSz — 2017-02-01T16:34:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rockys-journal-and-memories-of-now.19017/post-207712>

Be careful of hair and prostate products.
Most of them are 5aR inhibitors.
They do not help with prostate, may help some with hairs in exchange for erections.
Often just stopping use is all that is need.
Small number of "lucky" guys get "post-finasteride syndrome"
...

### Rocky — 2017-02-02T04:47:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rockys-journal-and-memories-of-now.19017/post-207763>

Thanks for all the great responses!

Total testosterone is fairly high at 898 mg/dl from last years blood work (348-1197)
Free testosterone is 16.2 pg/mL (6.6-18.1)
So I'm not sure HRT is going to help.

I stopped cigarettes and have two to three glasses of wine a week now since 12/31/16

I'd get out in the sun more but work all day, if it's out on the weekend I am always in with the tankini - lol

I live in Oakland, CA and do technical support and sales

Typically eat breakfast and lunch, no dinner
**Breakfast:**
4 egg whites and two whole eggs
Usually one bottled fresh oyster or can of sardines
3 ounces of plain whole milk kefir
grated cheese
Piece of bacon
Sauteed brocolli, onions, etc.
Ounce of dark chocolate

**Lunch**
3/4 lb of grass fed ground chuck
Marina sauce
Kale and red onion
2 ounces of peanuts (just cause I like them)

I drink water from a Berkey water filter with flouride filters
I do wear blueblockers all the time
I work in a office so there is plenty of fluorescent and nnEMF's

My mom is 93 and over the last couple years has had a couple strokes
She also has some dementia, atralfib, knee and hip joint replacements, high blood pressure.

I don't know a lot about her mom's health history, except I remember her being overweight. They lived 200 miles away from us.

I haven't had a lot of health issues. Tendency toward sweets and weight gain so the ketogenic diet works well for me. Have gained and lost weight/fat over my life several times.

I'm 6'3" 225lbs currently, 25% body fat, 38 inch waist. I have been as low as 185lbs and as high as 270 lbs in my adult life. I had a hip resurfacing in 2007. When I was a kid I had the mumps and shingles.

I workout doing strength training at the gym a couple times a week.
Try to walk a few times a week as well.
I do not take any medications of any kind.
Cialis doesn't work anymore.

Been listening to podcasts
Reading Epi Paleo Rx

I try and get 8 hours a night and am usually in bed 8:30 to 9:00 and up at 5:00 am

### JanSz — 2017-02-02T16:04:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rockys-journal-and-memories-of-now.19017/post-207776>

\[qu ote="Rocky, post: 207763, member: 1411"\]Thanks for all the great responses!

Total testosterone is fairly high at 898 mg/dl from last years blood work (348-1197)
Free testosterone is 16.2 pg/mL (6.6-18.1)
So I'm not sure HRT is going to help.
It depends what you mean by HRT.
Good testosterone level is a good start in regard to erections, but there is more
PSA
prolactin
estradiol
estrone
progesterone
cortisolAM/PM
FreeT3
DHT, dihydrotestosterone
adrenaline
all above is part of HRT (in my mind, but not many others)

I stopped cigarettes and have two to three glasses of wine a week now since 12/31/16
stopping cigarettes is good. But may want to try nicotine, see if/how it affects you.

I'd get out in the sun more but work all day, if it's out on the weekend I am always in with the tankini - lol
We are light creatures. Light is #1.
If you cant be on sun, try to recreate best indoor light you can.
Check this (improve on it).
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-4#post-201738](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-4#post-201738)

I live in Oakland, CA and do technical support and sales

Typically eat breakfast and lunch, no dinner
**Breakfast:**
4 egg whites and two whole eggs
Usually one bottled fresh oyster or can of sardines
3 ounces of plain whole milk kefir
grated cheese
Piece of bacon
Sauteed brocolli, onions, etc.
Ounce of dark chocolate

**Lunch**
3/4 lb of grass fed ground chuck
Marina sauce
Kale and red onion
2 ounces of peanuts (just cause I like them)

Go on one meal a day, 5 days a week
End eating 4-5PM

I drink water from a Berkey water filter with flouride filters
I do wear blueblockers all the time
We need all light frequencies at the right time.
I work in a office so there is plenty of fluorescent and nnEMF's

My mom is 93 and over the last couple years has had a couple strokes
She also has some dementia, atralfib, knee and hip joint replacements, high blood pressure.
Take her to your home. Install lots of good lights indoor. Hard carpets.
Have her loose weight, raise vit D (that will help with blood pressure).
Give her Boluoke (earth worms), for afib.

I don't know a lot about her mom's health history, except I remember her being overweight. They lived 200 miles away from us.

I haven't had a lot of health issues. Tendency toward sweets and weight gain so the ketogenic diet works well for me. Have gained and lost weight/fat over my life several times.

I'm 6'3" 225lbs currently, 25% body fat, 38 inch waist. I have been as low as 185lbs and as high as 270 lbs in my adult life. I had a hip resurfacing in 2007. When I was a kid I had the mumps and shingles.
170# looks like a good weight for you, unless you have lots of muscles. 34" waist. 10% body fat
[http://imageshack.com/a/img923/8990/1lgPIx.jpg](http://imageshack.com/a/img923/8990/1lgPIx.jpg)
I workout doing strength training at the gym a couple times a week.
Look at it as a time wasted to get sunlight. Exercise outdoor.
Try to walk a few times a week as well.
I do not take any medications of any kind.
Cialis doesn't work anymore.
Keep it using daily (5mg), it helps in blood flow.

Been listening to podcasts
Reading Epi Paleo Rx

I try and get 8 hours a night and am usually in bed 8:30 to 9:00 and up at 5:00 am\[/quote\]

Putting my weekly fast back into effect after being ill with this crazy cold or flu I caught three weeks ago and it felt really good. With all this, trying to find direction in an otherwise lost life. Waking up at 64 with regret is no fun, so I am doing everything I can run faster and jump higher so to speak. The path to overcoming emotional disconnect and associated pain is not so clear right now.

Thought the Magnetico was supposed to charge the body which means better function, better blood flow? So far as I know my heart is good, for that matter circulation is good. I can do HIIT without a problem and climb hills without a problem.

I've thought about the Quantlet. Trying to determine the best place to spend money instead of shotgunning like I usually do.

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=208278)
>
> I guess you can't go wrong with a magnetico. Have you thought of getting a quantlet?
>
> I would be concerned about your ED ....... means less blood flow to the pelvic area. Is your heart okay?
>
> really pay attention to how you feel in Florida ..... maybe keep a diary?

### JanSz — 2017-02-09T15:45:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rockys-journal-and-memories-of-now.19017/post-208306>

One thing about magnetico.
It requires separate room.
That is room where you only sleep.
When you get off the bed you should be about 5-6feet away from magnetico.
If magnetico is next to the wall, the room on the other side of the wall is also affected.
\---------------
If you plan on having a guest in room with magnetico, you likely want large enough for botho of you.

/
You like the magnetic field on top of magnetico.
You avoid magnetic field from the side of magnetico.
/

### Rocky — 2017-02-10T06:01:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rockys-journal-and-memories-of-now.19017/post-208351>

That is good to know JanSz - I don't have big rooms, in my place and especially the bedroom because I have a desk in there. May have to rearrange things! I sleep on a foam mattress, I may need to get a different one to use Magnetico.

Thank you for your reply!

### caroline — 2017-05-01T03:27:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rockys-journal-and-memories-of-now.19017/post-213135>

### Rocky — 2017-05-01T03:49:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rockys-journal-and-memories-of-now.19017/post-213138>

How sweet are you! I haven't been around much, but did come yesterday to read a little.

Making changes. Found I was eating too much fat. Have quite a bit of insulin resistance. Eating more fish, trying to get more sun, taking cold showers every morning. Taking the suggestions JanSz gave me to heart. Paying a lot of attention to light and avoiding the mismatch therein. As always read Doc on his Facebook posts. I notice more energy and mental clarity so something is changing. Wish I had more time to be on here, but working toward that.

How are you dear Caroline?

### caroline — 2017-05-01T04:57:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rockys-journal-and-memories-of-now.19017/post-213139>

Hi Rocky .......sounds like you are doing great - and listening to your intuition.

I am good - thanks for asking Rocky. We are looking to make a move to a more optimal place ........now that we know better!

As Jack has said ...We have to get off the bench and onto the field!

It is so easy to get bogged down and stuck in our comfort zone.

We need to get in the game and experience everything life throws at us.

Exciting times for all of us!!!

### Rocky — 2017-05-02T04:33:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rockys-journal-and-memories-of-now.19017/post-213191>

Do you know where your optimal place is Carol; or - where you are looking? The comfort zone is quite normal but it doesn't take you very far does it.

I love it where I live, but it is a little crazy around these parts.

Want to be a beach bum myself, we'll see..

Ps: been buying farm raised oysters and they are fairly big. I used to wash them down with water, but now - too big. Is there a preferred way to eat these. I am not crazy about chewing them up - lol

### caroline — 2017-05-02T06:03:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rockys-journal-and-memories-of-now.19017/post-213200>

yes - we do know where we want to go .........down the coast 3-4 hours. We would be able to afford to live within walking distance of the ocean and lots of bush around too.

The trouble is - selling this place for a good price and then finding something else smaller and cheaper....

Beach bum is very preferable!

I suppose just cut the oyster in half and swallow?

### Rocky — 2017-05-02T15:13:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rockys-journal-and-memories-of-now.19017/post-213213>

Carol,

Right, I have to cut them in fifths to get them down. Do people really like and even crave eating these things? I've heard people like them, but I have no idea why.

Here's to living out your beach bum dream!

### caroline — 2017-05-03T01:56:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rockys-journal-and-memories-of-now.19017/post-213260>

I have no idea why people love oysters either .....EXCEPT - in a restaurant in Orlando - I had them lightly grilled with a bit of butter and parmesan cheese. I was very, very happy to eat loads of those!

Yes - here's to being a beach bum .... our health depends on it! good luck to all of us to living an optimal life and doing what comes naturally!

the three legged stool in spades.......

Tell us more about your progress.......

### Rocky — 2017-05-04T03:08:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rockys-journal-and-memories-of-now.19017/post-213331>

I'm glad to hear someone else is not a fan of oysters, but I know they are good for me, so I will press on eating them raw.

Difficult when you are working to stay with your plan at times. The other night I had to go to a business dinner and didn't eat. I told them I wouldn't eat they insisted I go and then asked me why I wasn't eating and if I wanted some of theirs. I'll be happy to have more control over my life someday.

Wish I had something brilliant to say, but the bottom line principles of here is what I am using at the moment. No fancy testing or fancy hacks, other than time restricted feeding, lots of fish, sunlight, water, and as much magnetism as the day will allow.

Make sure you go to the area for a long vacation before you move or buy. You might not like it.

I will cycling in some seasonal fruit soon, sort of looking forward to that.

### caroline — 2017-05-04T03:51:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rockys-journal-and-memories-of-now.19017/post-213335>

Hi Rocky - could you have made a call before your business dinner and ordered a steak or seafood?

It is tricky to know if you will love somewhere - but whats not to love ....ocean, bush, and a lot less people and light pollution. It is only 3-4 hours south of us so basically what we have and know already - only much better.

We really are looking for a home base so we can take off with our camper trailer ......

### Rocky — 2017-05-04T05:42:29+0300

### Rocky — 2017-05-05T16:18:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rockys-journal-and-memories-of-now.19017/post-213446>

I know, probably malware keeping the 'grass is greener on the other side program' in check. The vision is there, only the action needs to happen. Happy Saturday Caroline!

### caroline — 2017-05-06T02:44:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rockys-journal-and-memories-of-now.19017/post-213476>

It is not easy to pick up and move.....we are finding we lose focus really easily! Please keep me on track! I am sitting in our huge backyard with our wonderful view and thinking .... why do we want to leave here again?????

We have a double lot that sits on the side of a hill....having to mow and trim and manicure that sucker is good enough reason to move!

### caroline — 2017-05-06T02:47:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rockys-journal-and-memories-of-now.19017/post-213477>

### Rocky — 2017-05-07T17:41:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rockys-journal-and-memories-of-now.19017/post-213596>

Your place sounds amazing, except the lawn maintenance.

Unless you are living in a negative, hostile or otherwise unholy situation...why on earth would you want to move. All that cleaning and packing of the last 20 or 30 years...yikes. Still, if the end result is at least equal to or better and there are things you will gain from it and need in your life to make it sing, why not.

I believe it's Happy Monday almost!

### caroline — 2017-05-08T03:24:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rockys-journal-and-memories-of-now.19017/post-213635>

I do think - as good as this location sounds......we live about four blocks from the railway line going into Sydney. I think it is having an impact on us. There seems to be more and more freight moved by rail and we are hearing this in the very early morning. We also have an old antenna on this place. It is not used but I think it may still attract all kinds of signals.

Yes - cleaning and packing will be a horror. Tony ran his business from here for 30 years.....you can't even imagine!

Rocky - are you close to the ocean? What is on your wish list?

Make your life sing ....I love that!

Happy almost Tuesday!

### Rocky — 2017-05-08T04:05:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rockys-journal-and-memories-of-now.19017/post-213637>

I am not too far away from the ocean, but traffic sucks getting there. I do get to the bay in Alameda which is a good alternative. I am located at the red flag. My thinking is toward leaving here because it would be good strategic move financially. I am by myself and will miss the area and the friends I have here, but I think the time draws nigh. You better start having some yard sale Caroline and get ready for judgement day! Have a gooday mate!

Thanks Doctor Jack!

It was a great weekend for sun and I soaked it up at home, doing HIIT, and at the beach today. Not burnt, kinda brownish for a Scotch Irish Caucasian. I also made some sun water in glass jars. Had eggs, sardines, shrimp and beef with a little vegetable for garnish. I think Vitamin D is on the way up! It's nice to be an amateur mitochondriac!

### Rocky — 2017-07-17T04:58:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rockys-journal-and-memories-of-now.19017/post-219126>

I have a pity party going on here sorry to say.

I was doing good and making changes and getting results. Yesterday I was hiking in the hills lost my footing and went down the hill uncontrollably, grabbing a tree limb with my left hand then bouncing off a log and then onto a rock. I'm sore all over but the worse part is I separated my left AC joint in my shoulder and the clavicle is sticking up a half inch or more.

There is a lot of back and forth about whether to have surgery with a grade III AC sprain. Wondered if anyone has thoughts or experienced.

### caroline — 2017-07-17T06:47:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rockys-journal-and-memories-of-now.19017/post-219131>

I am so sorry to hear that Rocky ....maybe you can "ask Jack"

Can a separated joint be made to pop back in?

My partner had rotator cuff surgery - we rented an ice/compression machine and recovery has gone very well. His Osteo figures he is 6 weeks ahead of others who had surgery at the same time.

I assume you are in a decent amount of pain? Keep up the seafood and the sunshine and good water.

Rocky - I am so proud of you for implementing so many of Jack's protocols. It sounds to me like you are flourishing - except for this blip in this road of life.

### Rocky — 2017-07-17T16:10:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rockys-journal-and-memories-of-now.19017/post-219157>

Thanks Caroline..

I have a grade III AC separation. Operations for this are debatable, and many ortho's say don't do it. It seems physical therapy is best and letting tissue scar over, and then you're left with a weird bump on your shoulder. Unfortunately you can't pop it back in.

### drezy — 2017-07-17T16:30:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rockys-journal-and-memories-of-now.19017/post-219159>

Ouch. I've got bad news for you... I think this is going to effect any future you had in modeling. [Kiniki](https://www.kiniki.com/pages/mens) is going to have to stay with their current line-up of 25 year old guys.

### Rocky — 2017-07-31T04:52:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rockys-journal-and-memories-of-now.19017/post-220487>

Love any input on this. I made the very difficult decision to have surgery on the Grade V Ac separation. It entails wearing an immobilizer sling for 4 weeks and being very careful. The procedure is called "Ac joint reconstruction with graft", and it is done arthroscopically.

I am really dreading going through this and don't to lose everything I've been working for.

After the first few days walking or stationary bike is permitted. I live alone so getting nutrition is going to be a chore for me.

I have been eating an appropriate protein diet with leaner meats, and fish and a few vegetables because I still need to lose more fat. With the reduced activity level I am wondering if I should keep my protein level the same (about 158 grams) as when I was fairly active or is it ok to reduce? All the while getting sunlight and water as much as possible.

Thanks in advance for any comments!

### caroline — 2017-07-31T05:10:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rockys-journal-and-memories-of-now.19017/post-220489>

Hi Rocky! my partner had shoulder/rotator cuff surgery 4 months ago. He is doing extraordinarily well. We rented an ice/compression machine for one month post surgery. His shoulder and arm were immobilised for 7 weeks. He was off pain meds in 3 days and has never looked back.

He would sit on our deck in the sunshine with the ice machine going.....30 minutes on and then 1 hour off. Also lots and lots of laying on the grass at sunrise.

I fed him lots of seafood and we used collagen a lot too.

Listen to your intuition. Eat if you are hungry and drink lots of good water. Eat the sun!

How is your pain level Rocky?

### Rocky — 2017-07-31T05:26:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rockys-journal-and-memories-of-now.19017/post-220492>

Caroline,

Wow, 7 weeks is huge - and how is his recovery with PT? In my case you are not to take the immobilizer off until the end of the 4 week period. I swear I don't know how I'm going to do that or any of it.

I wish I had someone to feed me seafood and put the ice machine on me! They are too difficult to set up for one person to do on their own, and there is a good chance you can be injured if you are not paying attention from a frostbite like condition.

The pain is pretty much gone unless I do something wrong which makes me question doing the surgery. It is a Grade V which tend to get or need operated on.

It's very difficult to get clear answers on this unless you study the heck out of it. Tried to post a picture but it's not happening..

### Rocky — 2017-07-31T05:31:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rockys-journal-and-memories-of-now.19017/post-220493>

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded.
