I was able to attach a photo...

I forgot to mention the ER told me it was a Grade II, when I went to see the Ortho he took one look and said it is a Grade V. All the ligaments are torn and the clavicle is pushing into the muscle above.

### caroline — 2017-07-31T06:16:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rockys-journal-and-memories-of-now.19017/post-220495>

Rocky - there must be something on youtube? I don't think you would get frostbite with an ice machine ....have you seen one?

Tony had to be fitted for the sleeve and it is very padded - the ice water flows thru the sleeve....and it is on a timer.

Tony's recovery is going really well- but he has worked very hard at his rehab. HIs arm and shoulder were completely immobilised for the 7 weeks. He couldn't drive \[standard\] for almost 3 months..

You are on your own I think? It would be so hard. Cutting your meat, putting on sox and shoes etc. etc.

Tony decided on surgery because the tear was on the outside edge of being repairable. The surgery is amazing - only one little mark left now.

### Rocky — 2017-07-31T20:07:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rockys-journal-and-memories-of-now.19017/post-220545>

Hi Caroline,

I listened to the the whole presentation, for the quick uptake go to 4:43 minutes of this video.

Glad Tony is doing good in his recovery!

### Jack Kruse — 2023-04-16T22:47:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rockys-journal-and-memories-of-now.19017/post-320574>

[https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1647669683720527872](https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1647669683720527872)

### Rocky — 2024-12-05T23:22:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rockys-journal-and-memories-of-now.19017/post-343351>

It's taken a long time to get here. Found Jack in 2012, became a member, something resonated. I was entrenched in making money, working long hours in a wire filled, neon lit, stressed out, blue lit office. Fotunately I lived through it.

Dr. Jack and Caroline told me years ago to workout outside and this week I broke free of going to the gym for 90% of my workouts. I open the sliding glass door in the morning and get busy after coffee. Some of the workout is done using resistance bands by tying a rope aroun the wall as seen in this photo.

At 72, it feels like I have a new lease on life...

I face East so there is pleny of Sun coming through in the morning on most days.

So in the end, shout out to Uncle Jack whose tireless efforts saved a wretch like me.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Neuroplasticity, a little meditation and the 3 legged stool
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/neuroplasticity-a-little-meditation-and-the-3-legged-stool.19019/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### bionaut — 2017-02-01T18:03:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/neuroplasticity-a-little-meditation-and-the-3-legged-stool.19019/post-207721>

In the blog "how does the Leptin Rx work" Jack mentions Dr Merzenich a pioneer in the neuroplasticity field. I read his book soft wired and began a regiment using their online neuroplasticity software at brainhq.com. Below is one of Merzenich's ted talks for your enjoyment.

In parallel to this I had been eating copious sardines and shellfish broth/coconut oil/turmeric/pqq+ColdROWater with 1T magnets on the container+ sun+grounding. During this period I also practiced 2 meditation techniques consistently and juggling 3 ball cascade.
1)Samatha(2years)- mindfulness using a candle as a focal point
2)Loving Kindness Meditation(30 days) - Practicing forgiveness in my own life to clear out any emotional hangups.

I really wish I had been able to do a brain scan before and after. I more than halfway through this and I literally feel like I have a new brain. In total I am around 30hrs of meditative time and 64hrs of screen time in a 66 day period. 40 of those 66 days was able to get Fl sun.

Some of the improvements I have seen are longer periods of mental silence, increase in recall speed, more detailed visualization of mental images, sharper detailed memories in the last month, better ability to shuffle a sequence of objects/words/tasks in any order. Issues resolving losing my wallet or car keys, having a word on the tip of my tongue but not saying it, stress from eye contact. There are a couple of other interesting effects from meditation such as a sensitivity to blood flow and modulation of that flow that have improved.

Below are my stats from brainhq using their scoring is on a gaussian curve with +xx percentile improvements from a baseline. This is my standing using their analysis on their product currently about 70% of the way through all of their content.
{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

Overall I am pleased with the results, and it is seem easy enough for others to do if they are interested in pushing their minds hard.

Heres a couple other details for the excess screentime. I used the deluminate app in chrome to change the white screen backgrounds to black and I have an orange acrylic plate on my monitor to block the blues indoors. Most of this was done outside with an unplugged laptop in the afternoon. After several weeks of letting the software plot my progress I initiated their "High Achievers" and then their "Auditory Intensive" courses.

### smith5 — 2017-02-02T22:40:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/neuroplasticity-a-little-meditation-and-the-3-legged-stool.19019/post-207803>

That's fun how he's drawing a bit delusional conclusions based on a relatively narrow set of experimental findings (which don't include, for example, Penfield's work) and applied to the same narrow sets of subjects.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-02-03T01:46:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/neuroplasticity-a-little-meditation-and-the-3-legged-stool.19019/post-207823>

Weisel and Hubbel also proved this as did Penfield.

### smith5 — 2017-02-03T22:30:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/neuroplasticity-a-little-meditation-and-the-3-legged-stool.19019/post-207894>

I implied specifically Penfield's interpretation of memory representation in the brain which is related to "learning" and thus would contradict to a part of Merzenich's interpretation of his experimental findings. Anyway, this does not rule out the fact that his conclusions (unrelated to their "practical applications") are ~20% delusional by default, because people in "psychosis" can be hypermnemonic for details (and also because of other stuff).

Im gonna be on the beach every morning catching the sunrise. Is it easy to get good water on the resorts? All I really care about is relaxing in the sun,taking advantage of the crater and hopefully drinking good water.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/time-for-a-qauntum-vacation.19023/post-207747>

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/time-for-a-qauntum-vacation.19023/post-207754>

Damn jack I guess I'm gonna have to wait till march then. I know it will be worth it but it's been a an extra cold sunless wasteland up here lately. More time to work on CT I guees. Any idea of where we'll be staying? How long?

### caroline — 2017-02-02T01:52:39+0200

### JMO — 2017-02-02T03:44:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/time-for-a-qauntum-vacation.19023/post-207761>

### Rubicon — 2017-02-04T21:59:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/time-for-a-qauntum-vacation.19023/post-207951>

> [Sun Disciple said:](/goto/post?id=207746)
>
> Anybody have any tips and tricks for making it qauntum? Im gonna be on the beach every morning catching the sunrise.

The water is good in the Yucatan. It's easy to get RO water for drinking. I believe it's sourced from underground cenotes & fluoride free.

Mexico mandates fluoridation of salt. It's tricky to get non-fluoridated salt there...bring some from Canada if you're going to be cooking.

You might like the Facebook group started by @[lohd2015](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/18338/):
**Quantum Living in the Yucatan** [https://www.facebook.com/groups/1189751757760644/](https://www.facebook.com/groups/1189751757760644/)

### Debntx — 2017-02-02T17:05:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi.19026/post-207781>

Just want to say hello - there is so much for me to learn here but looking to feel healthy again and learn how to mitigate some of the things in my life/work life that I did not know were/are harming me so.

Hopefully soon I will be able to ask intelligent questions since I definitely have no answers.

in my case the demons are fluorescent lighting, lack of sunlight, and stress in my job delivering bad news to people all day - a challenge to be sure - i believe this has lead to a very high cholesterol level and chronic low back pain which i am really wanting to learn how to deal with - i am not doing a good job and i refuse to go back on statins which i did take for a very short time years ago -a very not good experience.

the best just recent first step to combat some of my stress level on the job was the simple step of wearing blue blocking glasses at work - that alone made my stress go down a notch - I did not realize the effect lighting and those bright blue monitors had on me physically - also i go outside and when its warmer (sometimes cooler) i take my shoes off and which does not go unnoticed - people walk by and say "you must be from the country" but mostly they just notice and walk by - i am learning not to care.

Leaving to go do battle now. take care everyone.

### Debntx — 2017-02-02T17:40:15+0200

thank you.
I can definitely feel the difference when I have spent time outside and when I haven't.

Because of what I learned about vitamin D - i stopped supplementing and am now trying to get it naturally - although I am currently using the Sperti Vitamin D lamp - its just temporary til I can be out more.

I understand the need to question all my beliefs and change my mind even when I may not understand it thoroughly - if an idea resonates with me - i have the tendency to go with it but especially when there is much reason to do so - using myself as the guide and changing my course as necessary based on what works for me - I will be soon taking labs - hopefully the good I've been doing will be reflected - if not... well, its a process...

### Lahelada — 2017-02-05T16:38:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/presbyopia-a-light-story-too.19033/post-207998>

Thank you ! I am looking at something here that may suggest that the entry of blue lightin a presbyopic eye is higher than in a normal or myopic eye. I will be back later with thoughts more ordered. My friend is 46 or so , so it is not early onset.

### Lahelada — 2017-02-05T18:08:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/presbyopia-a-light-story-too.19033/post-208003>

.... This process, called accommodation, alters with age: the crystalline lens loses its ability to change curvature when an object is close to the eye ... In addition to this alteration in the ability to accommodate, the crystalline lens also undergoes histological modifications which cause in particular an increase in the absorption and dispersion of visible light in the short wavelength range, i.e. blue light, which gives rise to functional disorders of sensitivity to contrast and sensitivity to glare which appear and/or become accentuated with age. In order to combat the deleterious effects of the poorer quality of transmission of blue light in the aged crystalline lens, yellow filters are recommended. ......

Such filters are in particular described in Wolffsohn et al., _Optometry and Vision Science_, volume 77(2), pages 73-81, (2000).

Would that mean

1\. that exposure to screens even at daytime by the open window must be reduced as the ratio of UV and blue light is skewed in favour of blue? In other words ambiental UV cannot make up for the higher blue transmission to the retina. A black light and/or red light to get the ratio right even though they are polarised light sources? An immediate solution that springs to mind is not using yellow tinted glasses but to use the photographic filters that go directly on the screen so as not to interrupt circadian rhythm .

2\. As absorption rate at midday is higher than nature intended would that mean that in cases of presbyopia an hour or so of yellow glasses at midday would be recommendable if outside?

3\. Has effects for a myopic /presbiopic human trying to lose weight? Apart from "going fishing " at a more permanent level.. The blue light cheesecake is bigger than presumed due to presbyopia .Hah,all over sudden this is about me ,too.

4\. A final contemplation and it is big a reach I think . Presbiopia is a common thing in later life. Could it be a detonator for the following sequence of events ? Heteroplasmy being the factor that goes before obviously : More influx of blue being the reason for the taste preference of older people shifting to sweet ? I remember my grandmother here who suffered from a high degree of presbyopia defending her bread with marmalade quite vociferously. And yes she suffered from "old age sugar" as it was called then. It would explain the argument of "my gran never had an Iphone and still developed diabetes type 2 " etc .
Thinking more about grandma and her development of AD . First came presbyopia/ then the hunger for sweet, first signs of dementia ,psychotic attack and finally being interned in an old peoples home where she tried to eat earth from the flowerpots if left to her own devices. Seeking probiotics ? Nobody knew then. Presuming too much from too little ?

### Cpt.Tired — 2017-10-30T07:12:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/presbyopia-a-light-story-too.19033/post-226923>

Were you able to get a solution to the myopia Lahelada?

### Lahelada — 2017-10-30T12:46:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/presbyopia-a-light-story-too.19033/post-226933>

It was not for me Capt. T . My friend uses blockblockers now and says that vision has not deteriorated since. That is as much as I know.

# Thread: how to unflouridate water?

**Subforum:** Mitochondrial Rx

### Nicolaj Sølvsten — 2017-02-05T07:30:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-unflouridate-water.19037/post-207982>

What do you think is the best way to remove flouride from water? I have no access to spring water.

### JanSz — 2017-02-05T18:56:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-unflouridate-water.19037/post-208010>

> [Nicolaj Sølvsten said:](/goto/post?id=207982)
>
> What do you think is the best way to remove flouride from water? I have no access to spring water.

Improve your chances, use Lugol's.
Limit exposure to other halides.

...

### Dimitri — 2017-02-05T20:18:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-unflouridate-water.19037/post-208018>

> [Nicolaj Sølvsten said:](/goto/post?id=207982)
>
> What do you think is the best way to remove flouride from water? I have no access to spring water.

I use a Berkey water filter with the optional fluoride filter add on. It is rated to remove 95% of fluoride for up to 1000 gallons, but it was only tested for sodium fluoride. Others have reported a 50-80% reduction of total fluoride which includes fluoride's other forms.

No matter what you choose you need to make sure you are filtering out the sodium hydrofluorosilicate and the hydrofluorosilicic acid which are the organic forms of fluoride, harder to remove. Sodium fluoride is just the inorganic form.

### Nicolaj Sølvsten — 2017-02-06T19:12:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-unflouridate-water.19037/post-208064>

Thank you, upon research I found that adding calcium ions to water will reduce effects of sodium fluoride.

[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/11527228](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/11527228)

Thereby I found a biohack that involves mixing eggshells dissolved in vinegar with water which will cause the fluoride to bind to the calcium and precipitate to the bottom.
[http://orbisvitae.com/ubbthreads/ubbthreads.php?ubb=showflat&Number=16595#.WJiqNVUrJhE](http://orbisvitae.com/ubbthreads/ubbthreads.php?ubb=showflat&Number=16595#.WJiqNVUrJhE)

Still on a search for a good water filtration system, found some negative experineces with Berkey
[https://ecofriendlymamausa.com/2015/09/29/berkey-fluoride-filter-test-results/](https://ecofriendlymamausa.com/2015/09/29/berkey-fluoride-filter-test-results/)

[https://www.consciouswater.ca/fluoride-water-filter/](https://www.consciouswater.ca/fluoride-water-filter/)

WHAT LINKS THE SUN/EARTHQUAKES, and HURRICANES? Do Hurricane Maria and the recent Mexican earthquake have a temporal relationship to the recent solor flares (7) that energized the ionosphere? I think so. I also find it interesting that both disturbances are at the same latitudes. The Mexican earthquake appears to have triggered an eruption of Mexico’s Popocatépetl volcano. The butterfly effect of geo-engineering???? People will doubt it but there is so much we don't know about wave interactions it is premature to say it is impossible. But the science is present if we look for it and connect the dots. Few will. They will chuckle. I am interesting in learnign how nature works at its core. I think we should begin to ask, "What is the common thread between geoengineering, EQs, volcanic eruptions, extreme weather, and solar activity?" These phenomena might even connects the pieces to anomalies at similar latitudes on Earth. I think it is waves that are energized but the mechanisms are the key and we are not advanced enough to know how it happens but my bet it is through a change in electrcial tension within the ionsophere that changes the stresses on the plates. My friend Chris follwed through on these ideas and I think its worth considering his ideas here: "You nailed it, Doc! In fact, that wave phenomena is in the Low Frequency (ELF,ULF,VLF) range. They occur as Alfven waves propagate along wave-guides in our ionosphere. These Alfven waves occur as longitudinal and subsequent transverse waves as radiative cascade takes place. Magnetic field strength is proportional to alfven wave propagation. Alfven wave heating in our Thermosphere creates Global warming, just as it does in the sun's corona. Of course, the creme de la creme is that these alfven waves(as a longitudinal Birkeland current filament between Earth and the Sun, provide the means of "spooky action at a distance". And any artificial perturbation of our ionosphere via modulation of alfven waves will cause instantaneous corresponding activity on our sun. The key to anomalies occurring at similar latitudes is that Earth has three electrojets of alfven wave propagation, one at each pole and one along our equatorial plane. Perturbations occur along all three wave-guides by ionospheric heaters strategically placed to do so. When they're energizing the equatorial electrojet, anomalies occur nearer the equator." ### Debntx — 2017-02-06T03:59:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-journal-debra.19042/post-208036>

History

Mother -mental illness
Sister - Breast cancer died 9/16
second child - dx autistic
first child - large cell bone tumor tibia (had cryosurgery which was new at the time and fully recovered)

birth - 8yrs: lot of moving around, some outdoor life -

early childhood 8 to 12yr - outdoor life, access to small lake house in the summer, swimming, fishing, climbing trees, locked outside during good weather and when not attending school, bike riding in the neighborhood etc...

age 12yr-15yr - major changes to indoor environment, listening to radio, watching a lot of TV, bout of bulimia

I have about 6 amalgams and i am not too keen on messing with them - I did have an old root canal tooth removed (2005)-it totally splintered - procedure to remove took over 4hrs - just think of all that mercury and blue light exposure - after that had lots of symptoms that I did not realize probably related - most of them mental / emotional -panic attacks, shaking, tightness in my chest

edit - yeah i was told i had MS but nothing came of it -

Current issues:
high cholesterol 551 (per blood CarterBloodCare 1-29-17)
my cholesterol level when I was 27 was 231 - so it's over double what it was at that time.
weight issue - I weigh a little over 180lbs and i am 5'4''
work stress
low back pain
tinnitus (no dx- just aware of "noise")

Things I do: mountain valley spring water green glass bottle, vital choice canned wild salmon, brothery broth, selective vitamin/mineral supplements, Epsom salt baths on occasion, Dry body brushing routine, rebounding, a stretch/move routine, Pocket Sauna for red light therapy using rubylux NIR bulbs, earthing sheet/throw but not as much now- until i can understand where i am at with my EMF situation in the home. Sperti Vitamin D lamp 5min 3x week,Uvex glasses (computer work and home and before bed if necessary) -Iris tech software for eye protection- I have a magnetico which i think can make a difference - probably takes time and is very subtle. minor kettlebell routine - just not doing any strength exercise at this time.
spend time in the am drinking my coffee and grounding and getting the early am sun when its not cloudy or too cold (i'm just not there yet)

dminder - great tool that can be used to track the sun and UV strength at my zipcode

per 23andme - J1C3(D) -

supplements i take am - LEF Super K, LEF Selenium, Nature's Way Kelp Capsule (stopped), Now Vitamin A, Seeking Health Methy B12 and Methy Folate, Nordic Naturals Fish Oil (started in the last week-now stopped), Thorne K2 additionally

supplements i take midday: Seeking Health B- complex , NAC edit just learned what i should be taking with it

things i want to look into:
will look into getting a filter for shower, hacking my home light environment although i have removed all LED lights and use incandescent lights -ordered some beeswax candles today - and I want to install kill switches for my bedrooms when i can

Will be receiving my Miami inflatable spa Wednesday - not sure if will use cold or hot probably a little of both after i learn to care for it

I did purchase a Trifield EMF meter-rec'd today - edit: return meter - i want to actually hire someone and not even do it myself

One good thing about today:
getting more sun (edited)

currently what i have to eat on an average day during the work week:
Breakfast: start my day 8am w/bulletproof coffee w/1 tbsp raw cacao, 1 egg yolk (down from 3), 1 tbsp kerry gold butter , 1 Tbsp MCT (brain octain)
snack:
Lunch: 12pm'ish either a thermos of broth and a paleo valley beef stick
Dinner: can of vital choice salmon and a justins dark chocolate peanut butter cup
snack:
Dinner changes to leftovers sometimes: part of ribeye or leftover takeout pizza pie or a bowl of homemade soup - also love pork skins

Most everything I do in the area of self care is just so I can continue to be able to work - what i would like to see happen and going for is where I am not spending so much time in mitigation/mitogation mode to take the time to really live and not just survive but also thrive but based on my zip code - not sure that is possible - hopefully my next labs will be helpful

my last lab work i had done 10-12-16 BUN 10 mg/dl
Creatine, Serum 0.74 mg/dl

next will be doing LEF female panel

### Debntx — 2017-03-12T17:39:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-journal-debra.19042/post-210191>

not taking labs at this time - what's taking a lot of time is just implementing the basics - light, water, magnetism

now my co-workers will definitely think I'm wack (not really but they do ask me why i do some things ) - i am at the point i could say some things but it would be pearls before swine and i am not at the articulation phase of my healing - i need to go farther where what I'm doing is paying off more noticeably so that's what's getting noticed not the "behaviors" - ie taking my shoes off outside, experimentation with lighting in my cube, water posters/sun poster, wearing my jacket inside -always wearing long sleeves inside - wearing blue blockers inside etc...- fixing to add a water fountain (if i can get away with it)

i continue to turn off my room at night - have succeeded in convincing my youngest to go along with this as well (i know i could mandate but i didn't) - they are very resistant as they "need" their fan, sleep music and access to their light - i run a cord from the bathroom to accommodate - yet, it's a start. They are currently using my Red Sperti - that was a real accomplishment to get them to agree to start it. I probably should relent and give vitamin D supplement instead of using the sperti primarily for them - their value was 27 ng/ml

Just recently learned that the vitamin A issue and the brain is more than just taking the vitamin - especially is/was important for autistics - funnily, my youngest thinks being autistic is special and that they are fine just like they are and to a point I agree - but i say, "be a healthier autistic - what's wrong with that"- i am not saying something is "wrong" with you - but apparently that is the message they are receiving - I am working on myself in this matter.

waiting for paperwork to return to analog electric meter - interesting conversation i had - Mike with electric provider emphasized he/they didn't need my permission to change the meter when they did (from analog to smart) - they own the equipment, they could do it at any time - what can you say after that  except thank you for sending the paperwork-yep

i wake up mostly on my own - hear the birds chirping and think well it must be such and such time - and I'm not far off - I feel rested and that's a good feeling.

experiencing a resurgence of some noise in my ears - tinnitus?

i am not doing CT at this time... After shower yes i put it to colder for a short period of time - i believe CT will really be a big help to me - hard to fit in with everything else I am responsible for.... waah waah

working on building my fortitude with coming days (5G) - how do you do that? amongst other things - just keep on trucking...

edit: another thing i have noticed - the amount of water i can drink is getting higher - meaning i am drinking more- i seem to need/want it more - i am having to buy way more! good thing i think

### Debntx — 2017-03-26T18:11:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-journal-debra.19042/post-211131>

okay, since becoming more aware of how I had buried the sun based on listening/reading Jack's work- has not been long - less than a year - actually about 6 months since i started implementing this important part-getting more sun - not a great amount but definitely more - that means more am exposure - on sunny days exposing more of my body to the sun -

about 6 months ago on September 29, 2016 I had an event at work that made me question and wonder about everything: i collapsed at work. Just sitting there doing my computer work/making my calls and suddently i felt my body was giving way but my mind stayed on... i got up and walked out in the aisle and told my closest co-worker "I think I need your help"......then i knew my body was falling so i let go and went with it and guided myself to the floor -i actually had little control but my mind was very aware... i said to myself as i was going down " is this what it feels like to die..." weird i know - what is weirder only one co-worker used their electrons to fan me thinking i was just light-headed and had fainted but this was something else which i will never understand or be able to explain ever- what is weirder about this situation is guess what my next thought was "oh my gosh I left some work open on my computer.... worker - bee complex i suppose. and the person i asked for help they "couldn't" do anything at that moment- they were in the middle of some work and had to close it first!!!! - what does that say about ....anyway... that is the reason I was looking for deeper answers . As it also turns out that is the same day my sister passed from this life - it was easy to think that the reason I collapsed is because my sister was fighting so hard to live and through the web of time reached out to me for last and final time - not rational i know - I still miss her so. She had been diagnosed with breast cancer but I feel she actually died from lack of sunlight, doing things that depleted her battery further and morphine poisoning - and in addition there was a cell tower on the next property over which i believe was the primary initial cause along with being a worker bee who worked long hours at a major defense contract company - under fake lights - but what ordinary person would believe that? She was 55yrs - too young in my book.

when i do go to work I do wear long sleeves since i would under fluorescent lights- i am working on my work environment - placed posters of water, sunrise/sunset and have a red light - no UV though (working on how i can do this discretely) - want to add a small water fountain - wear my blue blockers - i have about 3 different pair for different times of the day

started magnetico classic 11-2016 and upped to super queen 2-2017 - raised head of bed a few inches - weighted blanket - my sleep has improved tremendously - i do dream more but don't remember them and i don't really care because they are strange and emotional ei feeling of sadness, fear , being lost etc... trying to find something, keep something safe then loosing it... anyway more of that

Previously had upped my water but now more discriminating in my water choices -and only drink one in the green glass bottle usa - also love the sparkling versions but i seem to get "puffy" when i drink those

age= 57
weight 180+ -(although i don't really know for sure) - i have recently noticed my clothes are slightly more loose - not a lot just a little - okay i actually just now weighed myself : 177 lbs thumbs up to me!

biggest issues: stress (work), cholesterol, losing my eyebrows, dryish white spots appearing on my skin (forearms, some on my shins of all places-a large one which actually recently tanned over on my forearm), my cubical being on the 3rd floor (not something i can change easily at the moment) and low back discomfort (lessened), tinnitus (off/on), eating Braums ice creams bars at least once or twice near weekends at least 3 at a time - can't do 1 - i have to do 3

my wins through time: more feeling of wellness, feeling stronger physically and mentally, no more panic attacks

it almost seems like now that i'm doing more right I am seeing more stuff that is coming up/out wrong - don't get me wrong - i am a lot better than i was a year or two ago-its been gradual but noticeable small changes - in many ways i feel stronger both physically and mentally but think this is just going to get better with application of principals that i have become more acutely aware from reading JK information

It looks as though i will have to rethink the CT thing - and actually do it - why do I not like getting wet/cold - just saying out loud - i do the warm shower/cool ending thing - was doing face dunks but haven't lately -

see my labs below - since using only sun exposure and some use of sperti during coldest month my vitamin D went from 40ng/ml 46ng/ml - the winter here in TX was very mild - having more cloudy days these days

"Have you read the getting started guides? the Leptin RX and the C/T RX......."
probably the first couple of pages - of each - i need to partition a part of my brain and dedicate it to doing that very thing...

getting sun while listening to various JK podcasts and then red light therapy and getting ready for work - i will have to work it in - while at work cell phones, books, cameras, fitbits have been banned with threat of possible termination - interesting, eh? (Clarification- not banning you having them- these items have to be stowed away from view - not able to be seen)- not on your desk in view, not able to access)

and just recently (yesterday) watched documentary Take back your Power" on A prime - posted by fellow JK member - mind blown!

TotalTestosterone=20ng/dL(3-41)
FreeT=1.4pg/mL(0-4.2)
Progesterone=0.2ng/ml
Estradiol=<5pg/mL
DHEAs=161.5ug/dL(29.4-220.5)
CRP,cardiac=2.93(0-3)

Hgb=11.2(11.1-15.9)----------
MCH low
MCHC low

TSH=1.62

Vit D=46.5(30-100)-------------

\==================================================================

Without much of other testing your
Progesterone=0.2ng/ml
Estradiol=<5pg/mL
both are very low.
You could supplement both, starting with both.
Working on both is important.
Doctors often prefer to start with estradiol and newer get to progesterone,
if you would have no choice,
start with progesterone
It would be better to also test Estrone and Estriol
but
if you have some difficulties with getting those tests
I estimate that your estrone may be ok or close to ok because you have decent DHEAs.

Goals
progesterone,serum=(15-20)ng/mL (so tested in serum)
estradiol,serum=(100-150)pg/mL (so tested in serum)

It may be as simple as
200-300mg Prometrium/before bed (for progesterone)
Estrogen ??

You are going to face choices maintaining your hormonal protocol.
With doctor, without doctor
no tests just going by how you feel
with blood tests ordered by doctor, ordered over internet
with blood spot tests that can be ordered by doc or bought over the counter
with urine tests

some supplementation does not show in serum
some supplementation does not show in saliva

some supplementation is cheaper to buy over internet

mess
mess mess

lets ask some ladies here for their experience and suggestion
\=================================================================

Very likely you have number of other problems, otherwise you would have perfect cholesterol

Having no choice, but it is usually easier to deal with higher than lower cholesterol.

I note low Hgb=11.2(11.1-15.9)
hopefully you love lots of liver (usually liver, onions, butter are delicious)(for me)

done at LabCorp or Mayo Clinic

but
lets worry about that after you are in full swing with your
progesterone/estradiol program

light amount
natural vit D of over 100

My thought process was that AM sun exposure would assist greatly but i need to do some tweaking apparently and definitely get more time behind me in this area - i don't think i should expect that positive change is a fast process - slow is probably more like it

i have a digital thermometer - will use that for now to track basal temp

i had a omega 3/6 test done by vital choice - looking for those results to post - i think i was a level "6" - should be a better ratio maybe now since i do eat more wild salmon - 5 times a week

i am kind of wondering about the cholesterol thing- maybe i am causing it with my bulletproof coffee - has kerry gold butter/mct (c8?) and added egg yolks in recent months (made no difference to total)-part of my day i truly love
although is was on the high side previous to beginning - just not as high as it is now -

although i am insured - i have no primary doctor so will have to order any tests online - not impressed with type of care received or that recommendations were based on sound principles-don't want to be part of the system any longer but keep
because of my youngest still at home. funny, the company i work for boasts a robust insurance plan - well, you know why right? it has to be! a lot of sick people to take care of that work for them - in their artificial lit environments/habitats-and its not changing very soon - i am trying to make it to retirement - 4-8 years away at least - will do what i have to do though in the meantime - just want to be somehow smarter about it

First learn how how the thermometer works.
Keep the position and location always the same.

96.33F or 97.21F are both low.

You want 36.6C=97.88F

If it is lower than that, it is a reason for many problems.
One is weight gain.
Some, many in USA, say that actually 37C=98.6F is the right body temperature.
Anyhow, you are low on temperature.

Vitamin B5 (Pantothenate)
General Information Sheet on Vitamin B5

Deficiency Symptoms:
Pantothenate deficiency symptoms are thought to be uncommon because of widespread
distribution in all foodstuffs. However, human deficiency symptoms may include:
fatigue,
depression,
burning feet,
dermatitis,
burning or pain of arms and legs,
anorexia,
nausea,
indigestion,
irritability,
mental depression,
fainting,
hair loss,
increased heart rate, and
susceptibility to infection

Anything familiar there?

also from that same article saw that using ascorbic acid (i would would vitamin C powder) is possibly b5 sparing (taken from a couple of rat studies) - consider that maybe add a gm or 2 - unless you alrdy use that alrdy?

Keep on reading (mostly on Spectracell website) on:

pantethine
pantetheine
pantothenic acid
pantothenate
Panthenol (also called pantothenol)
vitamin B5

let me know if you figure what works
\===========================

Pantetheine is the cysteamine amide analogue of pantothenic acid (vitamin B5).
The dimer of this compound, **pantethine is more commonly known, and is considered to be a more potent form of vitamin B5 than pantothenic acid.**
Pantetheine is an intermediate in the production of coenzyme A by the body.
[http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pantetheine](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pantetheine)
\-
.
[http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pantothenic\_acid](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pantothenic_acid)
[http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pantethine](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pantethine)

\===============================
**Panthenol**
[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Panthenol](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Panthenol)

**Panthenol** (also called **pantothenol**) is the alcohol analog of **pantothenic acid (vitamin B5)**, and is thus a provitamin of B5.
In organisms it is quickly oxidized to **pantothenic acid**. It is a viscous transparent liquid at room temperature.
Panthenol is used as a moisturizer and to improve wound healing in pharmaceutical and cosmetic products.

Panthenol comes in two enantiomers, D and L. Only D-panthenol (dexpanthenol) is biologically active, however both forms have moisturizing properties. For cosmetic use, panthenol comes either in D form, or as a racemic mixture of D and L (DL-panthenol).
\========================================

Wonder if we could eat this (some meant for cosmetic use):

[https://www.amazon.com/Chemistry-Co...TF8&qid=1492878162&sr=8-10&keywords=panthenol](https://www.amazon.com/Chemistry-Connection-DLPANTH1LB-DL-Panthenol-Lb/dp/B01HSLFHHE/ref=sr_1_10?ie=UTF8&qid=1492878162&sr=8-10&keywords=panthenol)
DL-Panthenol 1 Lb
Price: $27.95

### Debntx — 2017-05-13T19:17:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-journal-debra.19042/post-213992>

from the short time of practicing light water magnetism

some noticeable improvements:

when i sleep - through the night - i do dream - occasionally wake up around 5pm to pee but mostly i stay in that slumber state (hibernation like) all night and wake up feeling refreshed
and then upon waking experience less pain sitting up in bed and then standing - the pain has changed (improved) to an awareness only of mild discomfort versus all out pain that affected my mood - that part is gone! i do my stand up and stretch (power stance) and feel no pain - so win for me!

had to put up blackout curtains since I am waking up earlier about 45min - kinda helped - i think i am becoming more sensitive to light - my guess - though i am getting 7-8 hr sleep nightly - i wake up the same time frame consistently - instead of sleeping in on weekends (i have more gumption - i look forward to getting up every morning!) - have more want to in my life instead of should or have to - i have more waking dreams, plans - a good place to be and getting better

I had also previously experienced tightening in my chest on a regular basis and when i would get home from work - i would try many thing to relax and nothing seemed to work - not sure what the deal was - BUT....
now have moments of relaxation - i sense this with my breath - hard to describe but its like after a good cry that double breath of relaxation - it happens randomly - it even happens at work - i remember this feeling from youth and glad i can feel this again...

when sun bathing i do so on my patio- on a towel - i noticed when i "tested" for grounding that i didn't get the good signal or green light - i started adding moisture and of course realized the effect was still there - but now i just pour water all my towel and where my feet and hands i drench the area with water - i feel or think i soak up more electrons that way. When i started adding water to where my hands rest - i promise you I felt electron movement through my fingertips - sensation was temporary but effect persisted i'm sure and pour water where my back touches so i can get those good electrons going there too. When I make like a sphinx I make sure that my feet and hands are in pools of water - want to be as connected as possible - so now when I am at work and grounding on concrete - i add water (feet) - if they thought i was crazy before - they do now....

My clothes are fitting better although I haven't lost that much weight -

hope to raise my vitamin D from 66ng/ml to at least 80ng/ml - of course, without supplementation - so off I go

### drezy — 2017-05-25T00:44:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-journal-debra.19042/post-214611>

Careful with that assumption (fat = smooth). Look up collagen and fibroblasts. The golden key to any aesthetician would be a way to directly control fibroblasts so they produce more collagen.

baby feet = lots of collagen
old people feet = low collagen

From [https://www.jackkruse.com/hormone-cpc-1-dhea/](https://www.jackkruse.com/hormone-cpc-1-dhea/) :

> Similarly, DHEA can stimulate pro-collagen production and may have beneficial affects on aging skin. All these are very important indicators for peri- and post-menopausal women. Older women can be incredibly sexy when they find their groove, and DHEA is a big part of why Stella found her groove, in my opinion.

Alternatively you could just buy this dumb "french melon" extract and rub it all over your face like Cindy Crawford probably **doesn't.** Wrinkles are an internal issue displayed externally melon or no melon.

I get tired of seeing women get so used by skin scam artists. What's next special extract of \[insert exotic sounding natural plant here\] sponsored by \[insert just a good looking face here\]?

### drezy — 2017-05-25T01:18:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-journal-debra.19042/post-214613>

Actually this is a more important part for most people:

> . **A constant source of high quality marine/animals protein is needed to maintain your redox potential.** The quality of the collagen in your body is directly tied to your redox potential. _When your redox potential is low, protein is scheduled for excessive turnover by the ubiquination system._ This is energy intensive to the cell. If you have torn tendons or meniscus in your body, these are symptoms tied to a poor redox potential. Collagen is the “electric wire” embedded in your intracellular and extracellular water that conducts piezoelectric/flexoelectric signals all over your body.

[https://www.jackkruse.com/redox-rx/](https://www.jackkruse.com/redox-rx/)

working your way to being cold adapted is increasing your redox potential.

### JanSz — 2017-05-25T14:25:31+0300

For number of years (15-20) I was using LEF recommendations on DHEAs levels on bloody tests.
As far as I can tell, they were developed by dr Dzugan.
Currently those recommendations are:
Women 275-400 μg/dL
Men 350-500 μg/dL
Dr Dzugan after leaving LEF still keeps his old recommendations
Men (520-640) μg/dL

As result I was using 400mg for relatively short time but for long time (years) I was using 300mg/day to get to those levels.
Couple of times I would stop suplementation and test my DHEAs latter, it would fall consistently to below 100.
That was working for me until last year when my levels shoot up to +1000 with explanation that they could not measure higher levels.
Also my E1 (estrone) went up over laboratory range.

Currently I am still working on proper DHEAs level but additionally I am using E1 as a gauge.
My DHEA dose now is within 25-50mg/day.

Comments welcome.
@[drezy](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19879/)
@[SatoriHeart](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19303/)

### drezy — 2017-05-27T21:21:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-journal-debra.19042/post-214873>

Leaving copper to the pros.

Your oyster fear may just be due to the fact that you might not have witnessed your mom and dad throwing back oysters at the dinner table. I sure didn't. My dad runs away from seafood, but strangely now loves to have everyone over for lobsters which, uncharacteristically, he'll eat then.

Jack has said a shmillion things about oysters so I'll draw on another source to inspire you out of your [ostraconophobia](https://www.quora.com/What-is-the-fear-of-crustaceans-called). As a food porn addict my favorite books are on food history. The below quote is from professor Felipe Fernandez-Armesto in _[Near A Thousand Tables](https://www.amazon.com/Near-Thousand-Tables-History-Food/dp/0743227409/ref=mt_paperback?_encoding=UTF8&me=)_ and it is a protest and exposition of how we eat and think about oysters. The whole book is as amazing as this excerpt.

I'll also add that he very graciously corresponded with my son to assist with a **6th grade presentation on modern food supply!** Very few at this level take the time to reply for those reasons, trust me we've tried with many others, so I'm a fan of this guy for life. Before you embark on your oyster adventure please read below from the best food historian I've ever read:

> It is no way to eat oysters. You see the fastidious eater fiddling with them in restaurants, coating them with lemon juice strained through muslin napkins, or dosing them with bizarrely flavored vinegars, or sprinkling them with glowing stains of vermilion Tabasco or some other blindingly, chokingly hot sauce. This is deliberate provocation, designed to refresh the bivalves before death, a little mild torture under which you can sometimes feel that you see the victims wriggle or flinch. Then the diner manipulates spoon and scoop, prising and sliding the oyster out of its bed onto a curl of cold silver. The sheen of the creature clashes with the shine of the cutlery as he raises the slick, slippery mollusk to his lips.
>
> Most people like to eat them that way, but it means they forfeit the full, true oyster moment. Unless you discard the utensils, raise the half-shell to your mouth, throw back your head, scrape the creature from its lair with your teeth, taste its briny juice and squelch it slightly against the palate before swallowing it alive, you deprive yourself of a historic experience. For most of history, oyster eaters enjoyed the slightly fetid, tangy smell of the inside of the shell, undoused with the disguising dressing of aromatic acids. This was the way fourth-century poet and traveler Ausonius liked them, in “their sweet juice, mingled with a sensation of the sea.” Or in the words of a modern oyster expert, your aim is to receive “some piercing intuition of the sea, and all its weeds and breezes…. You are eating the sea, that’s it, only the sensation of a gulp of sea water has been wafted out of it by some sorcery.”
>
> For almost uniquely, in the repertoire of modern Western cuisine, the oyster is eaten uncooked and unkilled. It is the nearest thing we have to “natural” food— the only dish which deserves to be called “au naturel” without irony. Of course, when you eat it in a restaurant, its shell has been barbed and unclamped with all the panoply of civilization by a trained professional, wielding appropriate technology, an inviolable ritual and a stylish flourish. Before that, the oyster was reared underwater on a stone tile or wooden trellis, herded in an oyster bed, grown for years under expert eyes and harvested by practiced hands— not plucked from a rock pool as a prize seized from nature. Still, it is the food that unites us with all our ancestors— the dish you consume in what is recognizably the way people have encountered their nourishment since the first emergence of our species.

This trend you mention is global. It does come down to choice for us though. I got extremely disenchanted once I saw the "primal" movement push out tons of prepackaged goods. Now it's happening to the "ketogenic" movement. Avoid pre packaged stuff with more than 1 or 2 ingredients as much as you can.
Your dad may have sensed what he needed. As I proceed down this path I'll have enlightened cravings. They are not low dopamine cravings. They are way different. I've been ketogenic, or damn near, for years but occasionally now I will crave seasonal carbs. The first time I gave in, I stepped on the scale the next morning expecting to see a weight gain. Wrong! I had my biggest day-to-day weight loss in that time.

Get the DHA and sun into you to boost your dopamine levels. At that point you, inevitably, will be way more in tune with better decisions. That's been my experience at least.

### JanSz — 2017-05-28T17:35:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-journal-debra.19042/post-214928>

[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Essential\_fatty\_acid\_interactions#Competitive\_Inhibition](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Essential_fatty_acid_interactions#Competitive_Inhibition)
**Competitive Inhibition**
DGLA and EPA **compete with AA** for access to the cyclooxygenase and lipoxygenase enzymes. So the presence of DGLA and EPA in tissues lowers the output of AA's [eicosanoids](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Eicosanoids). For example, dietary GLA increases tissue DGLA and lowers TXB2.[\[22\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Essential_fatty_acid_interactions#cite_note-Guivernau-22)[\[23\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Essential_fatty_acid_interactions#cite_note-Karlstaad-23) Likewise, EPA inhibits the production of series-2 PG and TX.[\[17\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Essential_fatty_acid_interactions#cite_note-Calder-17) Although DGLA forms no LTs, a DGLA derivative blocks the transformation of AA to LTs.[\[24\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Essential_fatty_acid_interactions#cite_note-Belch-24)

**Counteraction**
Some DGLA and EPA derived eicosanoids **counteract their AA derived counterparts**. For example, DGLA yields PGE1, which powerfully counteracts PGE2.[\[25\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Essential_fatty_acid_interactions#cite_note-Fan-25) EPA yields the antiaggregatory prostacyclin PGI3 [\[26\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Essential_fatty_acid_interactions#cite_note-Fischer-26) It also yields the leuokotriene LTB5 which vitiates the action of the AA-derived LTB4.[\[27\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Essential_fatty_acid_interactions#cite_note-Prescott-27)

Good luck and keep us posted.

### drezy — 2017-05-28T22:46:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-journal-debra.19042/post-214952>

> [SatoriHeart said:](/goto/post?id=214924)
>
> I could care less if my DHA is shooting through the roof.

I do agree with that statement. I'm sure that I even test myself way less (~every 3 years maybe) than anyone here. Comparing my DHA to the US average DHA carries only contextual meaning when I understand the whole context (what if we just got a large influx of Eskimoes or equatorial people). I could very well just be lazy and I'm willing to own that. My most regular tests (given my own conditions & N=1) are:

-   Hand temperature in the AM (blood pressure)
-   Libido (hormone panel)
-   strength with squats and deadlift and BP and HR tracking 5,10,&20 minutes after a workout (stress test)
-   Sleep quality, duration, and energy on waking ()
-   Occasional alcoholic drink and 24 hour perspiration monitoring/stink test after AM caffeine usage (liver strength/metabolism also a dopamine test of sorts)
-   mental focus (dopamine)
-   Wound healing speed

It all sounds maybe too simple, but for me and my situation, fixating on numbers could potentially push me toward believing that I'm being iron clad scientific about the whole process even if any of the above "test" were thrown off.

If "feeling good" came with the wrong "numbers" which would you pick? Who chose those "normal numbers" anyway? Have those "normal" numbers and number ranges ever changed over time and upon review? Are we convinced they will never ever change again and they are set in stone right now?

yes, i think self-talk important - i had realized that almost as soon as I hit post reply - as i reread what i wrote I came back and edited out - this stuff gets sent out in the original though

A gradual approach - "It is the mark of an educated mind to be able to entertain a thought without accepting it." attributed to Aristotle according to google box - and you know who had said it plenty

You know there is so much information here - I put into practice/action as I entertain and therefore more able to know more for myself....

I am reminded of times when I told someone of my ideas/experiments - for example a couple of years ago ( i was/am always trying something - usually something health related )- they said something to the effect (whatever it was won't work) "you believe anything" - I think you can try things (action) without necessarily believing in them just to see if they work for you. One example was I told someone I was going to stop wearing contact lenses and glasses and improve my vision - they thought no way - can't be done - that's crazy - my vision did not improve in the way I thought it would in actual diopters? but my vision quality did improve - I now can drive at night without fear and i have better depth perception and colors are richer etc etc... so yes improvement but not in the way I imagined - this was before awareness of the sun reentered my life (pre-JK) - i am still holding the thought of improvement as I learn more... I don't have to believe any of it - I can just do...

I love reading about others experience here and glad people are trying out for themselves different things - but this whole process seems like such an "alone" experience - this n-1 -i am glad that i can talk about it here

Have a good day!

### drezy — 2017-05-31T18:43:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-journal-debra.19042/post-215180>

I saw that comment. I think if you set up your regular environment (footwear is part of that) you should test it electrically (and EMF) instead of assuming that it's a good setup.

Most supplements are non-sulphated, non water soluble, and don't end up being delivered by efficient D binding protein.

Oral vs sun based D
or
lipoprotein dependency vs D Binding protein delivery

Start from 23 minutes in if you want a running start.

Along with loads more, Jack points out that un-sulfated D (only fat soluble) requires a really great working digestive system. This is the same with every fat soluble vitamin supplement (DEA&K).
[https://www.jackkruse.com/the-sunshine-of-your-life/](https://www.jackkruse.com/the-sunshine-of-your-life/)

ok i think i'm in the clear on vitamin D - all from the sun - no supplement - although i do eat vitalchoice salmon - nothing is mentioned about vitamin (hormone) D - i eat fat in my diet - i did wonder how i could increase vitamin D from 46 to 66 in 3 weeks - pretty cool - but with my vitamin deficiency b5 - i thought I might be messing up skin - i have white pots - seemingly devoid of melanin - very small spot -smaller than an eraser head on a pencil - but they were probably there pre-sun exposure but were revealed when i started getting my am and week end doses -

i did write something about clouds the other day - here is something i was wondering - chemtrails (no conspiracy theorist here) but are chemtrails (the kind that dissipate very slowly but spread first)- can they act how a window acts to block the sun? meaning block certain frequencies of sunlight in a way that could be negative - this vs real clouds

the idea of minerals - transmutation - (in the body - using sun?)-meaning if you lack - the body can substitute or change a mineral you do have to accomplish a goal/process in a cell - i haven't read it exactly like here but somewhere i saw a comment that this may occur - i don't believe it but that's irrelevant- it's an idea that is interesting if it could happen - maybe Jack has written on this already

more reading of course - since i just started this process my brain is still trying to comprehend what I'm even reading - not a criticism of myself - just the way it is

just working on light water magnetism - will eventually get to CT - my water sources are city water - i am working on an idea of spot CT - bowl of ice water and a towel - drench towel and maybe some ice and place in two spots one around my neck and the other recommended place - some artery close to the skin in the groin? shortcut CT? - every time i listen about CT - i think its something that would really benefit me

The simplest questions dive down to the most fundamentals (and the coolest) of science (that aren't usually taught). Elements and molecules absorb certain frequencies of light you can start here: [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fraunhofer\_lines](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fraunhofer_lines) . Even If I spray misted water above your head I would be "blocking" certain frequencies of light.

CT is effectively magnetism. Doc lays out how best to begin CT. CT has helped all 3 of us in our house with unrelated issues.

### drezy — 2017-06-01T04:45:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-journal-debra.19042/post-215224>

Debra

The Achilles heel of my whole family is my mother. She is a 60 year old smoking diabetic with a 90+% heart blockage last tested a few years ago.

Talk about mission impossible! Still my heart is on course to being crushed and I don't want the person who brought me into this world to diminish her potential. There is probably a 99.9% chance that there is nothing I can do to avoid this. Still, @[SatoriHeart](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19303/) has (half jokingly) diagnosed me as a [hypomanic](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/i-think-im-turning-into-a-mitochondriac.19191/#post-214825) I think a combination of you, @[Sue-UK](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/9658/) , and @[Mystic Rose60](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/14425/) may have the ability to reach/support her a bit. I'm on a mission here, it's just that it's impossible. It's a lot to ask and I feel ultimately humbled and quite pathetic to even ask it, but as she is getting more sun her dopamine seems to increase and I may be able to get her to log in here.

As a son, with incredible gains for me and my wife and son, it's actually gotten my mother curious. Still, I think that when I talk to her she hears Charlie Brown's teacher's voice. Family is a tough one to crack, they hear you but don't want to listen.

### Debntx — 2017-06-01T07:17:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-journal-debra.19042/post-215231>

i can relate -

in regards to health recommendations to family members-
whatever I thought i knew was only half the answer - it involved food, supplements, etc.... little did I know only half-truths... i feel that if they were exposed to the things I am learning about today - might have made a huge difference -
I would email information/articles - take them supplements - and let them try them and decide for themselves - but...... you know how that turned out - my methods - very ineffective

People make choices and sometimes not the ones we like.... my methods for sharing information (showing that I cared) just didn't work for them - should I feel bad? Nope! - (even though I did at the time)

my family were never ones to go the doctor for anything really - i always thought that was nuts when I was younger but I see the value in it now...maybe they were smarter than i thought

and my oldest daughter.... when i speak/email/text... oh my... whah whah whah... whah whah.. (read charlie brown parent/teacher voice) - I asked my daughter about an email that I had sent her about some health related topic and according to her she told me that she put that stuff in her "mom" file - one day....

what i hate now is I'm the one who convinced my daughter to take Vitamin D supplements in the first place and now I am telling her to not take them - or encourage her to exercise but then she goes to crossfit and I have the nerve to ask - can you do this outside - it's the little things - hopefully, though she will read up on some of the stuff I've sent and evaluate if what is recommended would work or be good for her - that is the best thing I can ever hope for -

There is probably a 99.9% chance that there is nothing I can do to avoid this. Still, @[SatoriHeart](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19303/) has (half jokingly) diagnosed me as a [hypomanic](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/i-think-im-turning-into-a-mitochondriac.19191/#post-214825) I think a combination of you, @[Sue-UK](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/9658/) , and @[Mystic Rose60](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/14425/) may have the ability to reach/support her a bit. I'm on a mission here, it's just that it's impossible. It's a lot to ask and I feel ultimately humbled and quite pathetic to even ask it, but as she is getting more sun her dopamine seems to increase and I may be able to get her to log in here.
>
> As a son, with incredible gains for me and my wife and son, it's actually gotten my mother curious. Still, I think that when I talk to her she hears Charlie Brown's teacher's voice. Family is a tough one to crack, they hear you but don't want to listen.

My kids used to think I'm completely nuts . FIL died at 88, end stage CHD, vascular dementia and finally a brain tumour. 14 months of complete hell, after 5 years of considerable support to let him live at home. DH and I even had to holiday separately. I watched DH trying to cope ..... the most visible sign was how it affected his spine - when FIL died, gradually he started walking differently, as if a weight had literally been lifted off his shoulders. With my mother it was AD. The last 2 years were hell, I didn't realise how much it was affecting me until she died. DH could see it better than I could. The ripples though both situations were felt by my children and my grandchildren. My brother told me if he got AD he wanted to just be put in a home and the family could forget him. I told him he was delusional if he thought his kids would just leave him to it, but I think his attitude just shows his dopamine levels (and he's the one living in the sun but with sunglasses, suncream, high emf .....). Having gone through such shit looking after one each of DH and my parents, what keeps me focused is that I don't want to put my kids through the same thing. I told the kids that, and I remind myself of it regularly - now my kids fully understand my intention, and why at 60 I will do whatever it takes to avoid doing the same to them in later life, I'm not so completely nuts, they are a bit more curious .... Just got to get to optimal to show them that my (scientifically backed) crazy really works.

### Debntx — 2017-06-03T19:01:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-journal-debra.19042/post-215354>

what is strange to me is currently I am dealing with a vitamin deficiency of B5 per the spectracell MNT - i started taking B5- apparently prior to testing I was blissfully? unaware of this - and now it seems that since supplementing I am experiencing more skin type issues - does it get worse before it gets better?

also vitamin A - I do take - maybe too much - but when I read Jack's dr Facebook page recently - i was pointed to an article about Vitamin A - looking at the article it seems I may take too much yet some of my symptoms seem to be vitamin A deficiency - I am not asking for any to diagnose but if someone could enlighten in general would much be appreciated.

I did look at some pictures that showed what a deficiency may look like- although my issue is not as extreme but similar - weird no? how i can take Vitamin A (fish liver oil)- Now Brand and show these symptoms of deficiency - probably points to some other deficiency - more the reason to stick to foods that supply these nutrient where you possibly can

edit: i do want to add that did have colonoscopy a few years ago (2013? or so) and experienced long term (over 1.5 yrs) of very loose stool - yes i did go back to the doctor that did the procedure and they directed me to my PCP which i didn't have one at the time - anyway 1 1/2 is a long time - finally got it under control with various probiotics - one or two in particular - just guessing here but maybe that led to some other deficiencies that at that time I just wasn't aware of and the thought to test never entered my mind - weirdly did not lose a lot weight - only 20lbs in that time period - I am sure there is a benefit for those that think they need it but part of me would not recommend colonoscopy to anyone - for any reason

I don't think you can supplement yourself to health - but of course, when you know what you need through testing - maybe more possible to assist yourself.... hopefully you don't experience the 180 and do more harm than good - i am just hoping for the best outcome

>
> also vitamin A - I do take - maybe too much - but when I read Jack's dr Facebook page recently - i was pointed to an article about Vitamin A - looking at the article it seems I may take too much yet some of my symptoms seem to be vitamin A deficiency - I am not asking for any to diagnose but if someone could enlighten in general would much be appreciated.
>
> I did look at some pictures that showed what a deficiency may look like- although my issue is not as extreme but similar - weird no? how i can take Vitamin A (fish liver oil)- Now Brand and show these symptoms of deficiency - probably points to some other deficiency - more the reason to stick to foods that supply these nutrient where you possibly can

I suggest that correlation between symptoms and presumed deficiencies is very poor.
Similarly poor is correlation between some of the popular metabolic tests and derived reasons for those deficiencies.
So, when I test using Spectracell and know that my B5 is low
if, eventually I will be able to raise my B5 on my next spectracell test,
I do not expect that I will get baby skin.
That does not stops me from improving as many of my numbers as I am able.
....,

Recently someone posted DUTCH test with surprise,
he was 100% convinced that he had very low testosterone since he got symptoms to support his opinion,
but instead test indicate very high level of that.
Plus few other surprises.
..

### Debntx — 2017-06-13T16:44:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-journal-debra.19042/post-216070>

i seem to draw biting insects to me - since more outside time - mosquitos love me when they can and some other really tiny ones - i don't know what they are - if i were more healthy would they want to suck my blood -is that irrelevant? or is it because they can just "see" me a lot better - any suggestions - what can i do to repel these b's - does blood type matter? O+?

observation - still have not grown back my brows yet - really looking forward to it - i do have my outer third - the part that is missing is the whole bottom all across and just thinner overall - but on a brighter note the hair on my legs is doing great!!!

another observation: i have this bump on my right heel that flares occasionally - then it will go away - well, it was flaring and the one thing i was doing was taking the iron supplement floradix - i stopped taking the floradix and it wasn't too long after that my heel pain went to almost nil -happened before but i did not see the connection at the time - so my clue about iron - even if anemic is "maybe you need something else" -

down to 175lbs - not dramatic but without trying is great - i would rather be healthy - than lose weight for only its sake

dropped some of my supplements for the time being - i actually still take super K, vitamin A, ubiquinol, panthethine, pantothenic acid - occasional mag, selenium - and use nutritional yeast with my cup of broth during the week - with a little vitamin C

looking forward to normalization of blood lipids - balance - will measure in a couple of weeks

i will keep doing what i'm doing - light, water, magnetism - for the time being contrast showers - ending in cold

i also like the idea of keeping my spring water on my magnetico and drinking that but i am thinking of using that water for my ice cubes - i heard or read about it somewhere here?

sleep - much much improved - hopefully labs will show how much better - or give some more good clues

edit 6-16
not sure where i read this but dropping vitamin A - too much may cause bone pain - maybe i'm making my back pain worse by taking? won't hurt to stop taking - if this seems to make a difference will make a note

i have decided that coffee impacted me more than i knew - that one cup - affected my stress levels - levels of pain my body felt - this after 4 days
now the bad - i am not thinking as fast as I did while "on" coffee - maybe my brain was slow before but my perception prevented me from seeing cuz i know i felt faster....  and smarter
don't miss it like I thought I would except for some of the effects but more positives popping up here and there - and if I want I can drink a cup but right now it's not a driving force
also, i am not substituting tea, or soda in its place

does your body store caffeine for use later? slow release?

makes me wonder if i am low dopamine or low something else - or if after stopping coffee - maybe it takes a while to normalize

whatever it is I am still much better than I was 6 months ago

edit: i just want to add that now i know why giving up coffee wasnt as big a deal as it could have been - it's called AM UV light

### JanSz — 2017-08-13T16:11:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-journal-debra.19042/post-221589>

@[Debntx](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19762/)
Hi Debra
I have a good news for you.
I have figured out how to raise mine (and yours)

PANTOTHENATE

on Spectracell test.
Largely due to your help, but I want you to know that it is actually helpful as confirmed by test.
Over last few years I was always deficient, no mater what kind pills I used.
This works:

[https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B00I4S524Y/ref=oh\_aui\_detailpage\_o01\_s01?ie=UTF8&psc=1](https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B00I4S524Y/ref=oh_aui_detailpage_o01_s01?ie=UTF8&psc=1)
Vitamin Pro B5 (Dpanthenol) 100g
Mystic Moments

This works and it is easy to use.
There is another product of similar name, I bought it, it is much more liquid due to variety of additives.
We want to cut additives to a minimum.
The other one may be good as additions to a shampoo or similar,
not to eat. But if nothing else was available, I would eat it too.

I have a skewer, really a piece of wire about 1/8" dia or slightly less, it as long as a fork or spoon.
I dip that wire in container about 1/2" or 3/4" deep,
take it out,
rotate wire along longitudinal axis until there is a clean blob
put it in my hot coffee
let it stand for a while or mix it
it dissolves quickly into coffee

I did that daily then did my next Spectracell test.
Actually I was also eating those (useless pills)
my
PANTOTHENATE
went from 11 to 20
where at 11 it was borderline
now is in good standing

# Thread: CT bad for increasing Muscle Mass
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-bad-for-increasing-muscle-mass.19045/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Fitness
**Posts:** 51
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### cap — 2017-02-07T06:02:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-bad-for-increasing-muscle-mass.19045/post-208098>

This research seems to show CT would be bad for trying to gain Muscle or great for those not trying to gain mass..

Thoughts?

Would it be advised to not do CT or cold showers Peri-workouts if you are trying to gain Muscle?

[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/26174323](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/26174323)

### Jack Kruse — 2017-02-07T15:02:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-bad-for-increasing-muscle-mass.19045/post-208111>

Not true.........Did you see how they did the experiment? Under artificial light therefore their conclusions are very flawed. Why? Muscles have mitochondria and blue penetrates into muscles to affect and destroy them. So does red light to build muscle but indoor light has no red light............and this is the real effect you are seeing.

During sleep this is why blue light and really any light affects the brain and its size and function too.

SLEEPING SHRINKS THE BRAIN....AND THAT'S A GOOD THING
Why? Anything that looses energy gets larger as a result. When things begin to shrink they contain their energy for optimal function. For example, when you sprain your ankle does it get bigger or smaller? Whne you have heart failure does that heart get bigger or smaller? When a star ages doesn't it get larger and progress on to become a red giant before exploding? So it makes complete sense that during sleep the brain become more energy efficnet and this is why it shrinks. People who cannot sleep well tend to have brains that are not energy efficient and this is why their brains get larger. It is also why astronauts eyes get larger when they go in space and have many problems with their retina and optic nerves when they return to space. It seems like biology is just getting a whiff of the thermodynamics of size. Ah, to sleep, perchance … to shrink your neural connections? That's the conclusion of new research that examined subtle changes in the brain during sleep.
The researchers found that sleep provides a time when the brain's synapses — the connections among neurons — shrink back by nearly 20 percent. During this time, the synapses rest and prepare for the next day, when they will grow stronger while receiving new input — that is, learning new things, the researchers said.
Without this reset, known as "synaptic homeostasis," synapses could become overloaded and burned out, like an electrical outlet with too many appliances plugged in to it, the scientists said. [http://www.livescience.com/57740-sleeping-shrinks-brain-synapses.html](http://www.livescience.com/57740-sleeping-shrinks-brain-synapses.html)

### shah78 — 2017-02-07T17:49:40+0200

The more the better.

### shah78 — 2017-02-07T17:54:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-bad-for-increasing-muscle-mass.19045/post-208133>

I sit in the tub for an hour or two, until I drive for my three to seven minute workout at the gym. Then I go back and sit in the tub some more. So I get a three hour "workout" in, with no sweating, exertion, cortisol spike, muscle strain, injury. I call it the Science Fiction workout. I'm sometimes joined in the workout by Kirk, Spock, McCoy and few Klingons.

Fibromyalgia
arthritis
then variety of other ills showed up.
Imy findings here

### WalterNL — 2017-02-09T11:02:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-bad-for-increasing-muscle-mass.19045/post-208291>

> [JanSz said:](/goto/post?id=208135)
>
> Some say that icing after workout prevents muscle growth.
>
> good inflammation
> bad inflammation

Agreed. The article is about CT directly after strength training. Lifting weights elicits an inflammatory response which you need to recover, so doing CT wouldn't help (same thing goes for taking antioxidants after a workout). But if you CT when it doesn't interfere with that response, or simply don't lift weights and only CT it's no problem.

### WalterNL — 2017-02-10T11:21:52+0200

Cold exposure can inhibit mTOR so possibly reduce hypertrophy. Research in this area does not take into account anything of the three legged stool so take it with a grain of salt.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-bad-for-increasing-muscle-mass.19045/post-208916>

Really if you do everything that optimizes good sleep and giving the right stimulus growth will happen.

Doing the things that optimizes sleep would include CT among many other things but dialing in your sleep is going to have the biggest impact over everything in my experience.

And dialing in a good night sleep pretty much Follows along what Jack here says lays out.

Like Barry here has said:
"In order for the adaptation to occur, the stimulus must resonate"

Saw Palmetto berries contain an oil with a variety of fatty acids and phytosterols.
These fatty acids include capric, caprylic, caproic, lauric, palmitic, and oleic acid and their ethyl esters.

I hate typing too, and reading on a computer. Hopefully this post is not superfluous

So if you want Mystric acid....... use Nutmeg not Maca (Peruvian Ginseng). Even Mace is not nearly as high in Myristic Acid, as you noted. I think you understand this but this might help anyone who reads the blog and gets confused.

This link has a table comparing Mace and nutmeg. Mace analysis tested as 8 % Mystiric acid and Nutmeg a whopping 55%
[https://www.researchgate.net/public...acids\_in\_Myristica\_fragrans\_and\_its\_wild\_taxa](https://www.researchgate.net/publication/268343568_Fatty_acids_and_leaf_amino_acids_in_Myristica_fragrans_and_its_wild_taxa)

Maca seems pretty awesome too, not surprising as it grows an amazing location as far as light, water and magnetism go (Peruvian highlands)

With regards to Maca route......I found this little write up from this site [http://www.medicinehunter.com/maca-and-sex](http://www.medicinehunter.com/maca-and-sex)

"A number of holistic and complementary medical doctors in the US, from general practitioners to psychitarists, are using maca with a variety of patients. Menopausal women experience relief of various uncomfortable symptoms of menopause. Both men and women report a significant boost in libido. And a number of men who have suffered from erectile dysfucntion have improved, as a result of taking maca. One New York psychiatrist I know recommends maca to those who take Prozac. That anti-depressant often sends libido plummeting, and he finds that maca brings it back. It takes many years to inculcate a plant medicine into a large number of medical practices, but with maca this process is happening."

Also, Fatty Acid content of Maca root from .....[http://www.inkanat.com/en/maca/maca.pdf](http://www.inkanat.com/en/maca/maca.pdf)
