Ben Greenfield uses it with I think glutamine after working out (working out at that level generates a lot of inflammation) - also, the alternative health community uses it to "retrain" the gut - like your mother's gut flora did at birth - basically, their motto is bug bomb, rebalance the gut flora, and retrain the gut flora so it can recognize a pathogen - if you get crappy flora at birth, you are unable to recognize a pathogen because pathogens are the norm... for your friend, I would skip the colostrum and concentrate on collagen, electrons, red light and cold - have him drink bone broths with lime /lemon juice (vit c=electrons)(bone broths improve collagen which improves electron flow) and since it's summer there, if you're by the beach, have him jump into the ocean and just hang out - preferably in the morning - epsom salts with IR therapy might be a great move also - try to make him as least inflamed as possible - I think colostrum also has beta glucans - which I think mushrooms also have - for immunity improvements - not really your friend's problem -

mixed with little sugar,
covered with warm milk, straight from the cow (ok from a tit).

The one my neighbor uses is the reddish type.

No dancing naked here because adult son just moved back in (gave him a 6-month limit). That Kiniki bathing suit is going to come in handy.

I must be doing something right, _optimally speaking_, because people at work are dropping like flies. Shingles, bronchitis, strep and flu. On the other hand, it leaves me as the sole survivor in my area today so I will be very busy. I'll gladly take this manifestation!

Slimmer is a state of mind . I may or may not see a size 8 again but this post-menopausal woman is happy with herself at this time.

Finding myself thankful for the all of people in my life and stability of my job.

### Sue-UK — 2017-02-18T11:38:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-is-everyone-doing.19089/post-208816>

I'm at a crossroads after losing Mum last month ..... I still feel completely peaceful about that, but it makes it difficult to judge whether changes (positive and negative) that are happening are as a result of having that situation change, or of what I'm trying out. Probably a bit of both, but it makes judging mitohacks difficult. I have pressed the pause button and am treading water until another pathway reveals itself .... I could retire or go back to the business life I had before I drastically cut my hours down as Mum's condition worsened, but I'm pausing and leaving myself open to a few quantum possibilities...

Things that are driving me nuts? Getting drawn into something that interests me but I can't connect the dots ....(currently Niels Finsen's red light therapy for smallpox . What's the mechanism for daylight cause scarring.....) I don't like to think about how much time I've spent trying to work stuff out, but its giving me something to do while I wait for the quantum possibilities.

No dancing naked, but sphinx moon bathing anyone?

### Inger — 2017-02-20T13:57:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-is-everyone-doing.19089/post-208930>

I am dancing naked....
I am doing great! It is the toughest months of winter here but I have not yet been sick... lets see  at work they have the flu now!

I have dry brushed my body almost every morning/day now for many weeks, in front of my reptile light and infrared lamp.
I have to say, it is awesome!!! Very energizing, and my skin sure have become even softer and nicer! It is doing some real good things I feel   so glad I started doing it  I can only recommend it for everyone to try
I have two brushes now and use them both, one in each hand.

Partner and I are reading Osho when I am at his place or he is at mine.... or he reads for me in candle light and I listen
It is a great stuff!
Anyways, whatever happens, I feel that life is just magic and a beautiful gift. Soon spring will arrive

Started to drink seaweed water again as my body somehow craves it now. I have not been eating much seaweed at all lately. I forgot! I think I need iodine! The seaweed water just tastes so awesome to me.

### Lahelada — 2017-02-21T01:48:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-is-everyone-doing.19089/post-208977>

Little things from here. I decided today that I am well enough to get into exercisisng Movenat or variation on teh theme. Enjoyed it so much I did 90 minutes.

I also got quite cocky this summer ,which was easy when you have a blister from sandals and walked barefoot in the city centre, shoes dangling from my arm. I passed hundreds of people. I repeated the exercise several days running and out of teh hundreds maybe 10 noticed... Smombies abound..

I am having fun planning a biohack in Patagonia. I found a place that has no cell reception, no electricity and 25 miles from twon. It is in latitude 40 though so I need to climb quite high to get UV . Ct can be done in the lake in front of the cabin. Still looking for ways to get tests before and after. The doc I thought was onboard really was not, so still looking for someone to prescribe a very unusual (for here) combination of tests.

I made really great friends this Xmas and New Year . lots of fun and enlightening conversation. "2017 started as 2016 finished - on a high C .

Oooh ,edit ,edit I do dance . Every morning on my patio. Clothes are dependent on mood.
Sexier ,hmm I feel sexier. ..

### Samy Colddigger — 2017-03-07T06:00:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-is-everyone-doing.19089/post-209809>

I sure as hell am looking forward to a nice vacation in Yucatan! It has been a mighty cold and dark winter! It wouldn't even be that bad, would it not be for my current job where they have Wifi Boosters and blue lights everywhere. Have been getting outside for sunbathing whenever it is Sunny, even in -20 C weather. I lay in the forest with my huge down Parka and the black interior lining soaks up the sun and keeps me warm enough to stay out for hours. But those sunny days that I get off are few and far between. So it means I have been really grateful for my good sleep set.....maybe as good as it gets for living on the grid without being able to shut the breaker off due to the pipes freezing! I have a 10 G magnetico and we sleep in a dark, cold basement with very low RF readings. Still AC magnetic fields though..... but Having lived in Vancouver last year, getting maybe a little more light but way more EMF, I can safely say I have made progress on this front. However, I have always loved the sun and I am giving all my thoughts these days to living in the place where I can get sun and grow my own food. I am no longer so down with the idea of living and working in a dark/blue light/emfed frozen hell hole buying food that comes from 1000s of kilometers away, starting a family so my kids can go to school and get their mito trashed!. We can get frozen seafood which is good....but I need to make the step up on the LIght Water and Magnetism front! Esp after finding out my Haplotype of k1 (ashkanazi, kurds, etc)..... think I am definatley not yoked for north of 60 degrees! So hopefully going to figure something out where I can work somewhere in the sun a build a life there. Any thoughts? I would be down for Mexico, I have been there before and always feel awesome there. Best I have ever felt really....maybe save for a few hot Summers in the Yukon. It boggles my mind to think how long I have lived with low charge. So this is something I would really like to bio hack......getting my Vit D into optimal range. Just getting charged up for a couple months, not a couple weeks. So thinking of heading back Yucatan in the summer after our little spring break visit.

JUST gotta hack the north until then! Thanks for the tips on the scrub brush.....I do the heat lamp and cold showers but never though about the brush. Another Hack that is awesome is just go for a run outside (very cod weather) with long johns and just the compression shirt on top. Add in some running on snow with just socks on for awesome flow!

Was wondering about the Paternal influence on energy status....or is there much influence at all? I have the k1 mito (I knew I always loved Spain and Portugal) but my paternal haplogroup is Scandinavian.....so im pretty much the worst thing to happen for the those Nordic supremacists.......funny how I studied this issue a lot in history of science and always detested the idea......and growing up with Yukon Natives I always knew that fitness was very much about the environmental influences!

With my life being forever effected by the death of my sister(died at age for Feb 29th on a leap year, 1993), this time of year has always been hard. But I see it in a new light now thanks to Jack. My sister Tesse died from "something" called hemi-encephalepathy. I recently found out my mom used a microwave throughout the pregnancy.!! A rare disease that they wanted her brain for....looking for all the wrong things!?. But I know now that it was simply heteroplasmy. Both me and my brother are perfectly healthy......Tesse was the youngest and experienced different conditions it seems.

Thanks Jack for shedding light on some of the toughest questions I have ever asked.

Sam

### johnsonmd — 2017-04-15T14:56:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-is-everyone-doing.19089/post-212346>

I have been following more and more of Jacks protocol for about 2 years. I got a magnetico about a year ago. I notice this week, that my fingernails are getting really strong and I am feeling better and sleeping better!
Also, in August, I fell off a ladder and broke my tailbone. I remembered the story about the football player who broke his neck and was back on the field in 3 weeks. I decided to do CT in the horse water trough with ice for 40 minutes each morning and then lay out in the sun for 40 minutes in my bikini. 10 days later, my Dr said it didn't look broken! He wanted to know what did I do? I showed him Jacks book.
I eat lots of seafood and veggies and drink my spring water from the local Indian reservation. I pray and meditate... and sing and dance! I watch for the sun and go outside and get sunshine whenever possible!

### Markus56 — 2017-02-17T06:24:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/markus56-journal.19091/post-208766>

I live about 200 miles above San Francisco at 1200 ft. I am outside everyday from 1-6 hours sometimes working in the garden planting or weeding. if the sun is out Im in shorts and maybe no shirt even at 50\*. I often do bates method eye exercises sunning and palming outside while grounded. my home is on avg 50\*. I turn off all power in my apt while I sleep.
I use blueblockers at night when in the community kitchen( I live in community)or if on the computer with red LEDs for light. I often wear blue blockers when shopping day or night.
I think the tests look pretty good.
had labs done 2/2/2017

### Markus56 — 2017-02-17T22:44:41+0200

### JanSz — 2017-02-17T23:41:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/markus56-journal.19091/post-208798>

SHBG - sex hormone binding globulin
Protein that absorbs some steroid hormones.
It does that for storage and transport.
Hormone that is attached to binding protein is not-active.
You likely have some excess of SHBG.
I am guessing that because you have very good Total Testosterone but free testosterone is lowish.
Most of reasons why SHBG is too big is not known (to me).
Most popularly discussed,
SHBG may raise when one have excess of estrogens.

The way you describe your daily activities, you spend lots of time on the sun with lots of skin exposed.
Make sure that you do not use sunscreens, eat sulfur containing food (for vit D), and carotene containing food (for vit A).
Sunscreen may be one reason why your vit D looks low.
There are other reasons, that I do not really understand, but dr @[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) mentioned their existence.

You have a lots of "good" stuff.
HDL CHOLESTEROL 138
Someone who knows what to look for could likely figure something out of it.

You have done those labs as routine health maintenance or something feels not right?

//

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/light-as-medicine-great-article.19093/post-208789>

[https://wisconsinacademy.org/magazine/light-medicine](https://wisconsinacademy.org/magazine/light-medicine)
The article references Tilna Karu from the Russian Academy of Laser Sciences and here is a movie called: Mitochondrial Mechanisms of Photobiomodulation:

### Sue-UK — 2017-02-18T17:20:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/light-as-medicine-great-article.19093/post-208840>

Interesting article.  It ties in with a book I've been reading, a 1901 translation of Niels Finsen's book phototherapy, which is papers from the 1890s. About using what he called the "chemical rays", (blue, violet and especially UV) for the treatment of lupus vulgaris skin lesions, and the need to avoid those rays for the treatment of smallpox. If the chemical rays were completely excluded and red light used before the smallpox spots became "suppurated" the patients recovered without scars. Often if caught early enough the suppuration didn't occur and "It is noted that during six to eight weeks the skin remains covered with hyperaemic or pigmented spots; at the end of this time, however, they finally disappear." I've been driving myself nuts trying to work out the mechanism, but also wondering, apart from the fillers, what does a smallpox vaccine do the melanin system and the ability to do the human equivalent of photosynthesis?

### Sue-UK — 2022-11-08T18:18:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/light-as-medicine-great-article.19093/post-317401>

>

If the chemical rays were completely excluded and red light used before the smallpox spots became "suppurated" the patients recovered without scars. Often if caught early enough the suppuration didn't occur and "It is noted that during six to eight weeks the skin remains covered with hyperaemic or pigmented spots; at the end of this time, however, they finally disappear." I've been driving myself nuts trying to work out the mechanism, but also wondering, apart from the fillers, what does a smallpox vaccine do the melanin system and the ability to do the human equivalent of photosynthesis?

A possible clue comes from this paper [https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3180186/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3180186/)
**Hydrogen peroxide fuels aging, inflammation, cancer metabolism and metastasis

"In normal wound healing, the production of hydrogen peroxide is shut off."

"Similarly, during scar formation, keloid fibroblasts produce hydrogen peroxide, undergo aerobic glycolysis and secrete lactate. Thus, myofibroblasts also have the capacity to undergo the Warburg Effect (i.e., aerobic glycolysis), a process that was previously thought to be confined to cancer cells."

"**

# Thread: Radioactivity?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/radioactivity.19095/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2017-02-18T16:05:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/radioactivity.19095/post-208826>

The ceramic is likely made with radioactive elements from China. Ordinary ceramics often contain elevated levels of naturally occurring radionuclides, e.g., K-40 and the various members of the uranium 238/235 series and thorium decay series. The use of uranium in ceramic glazes ceased during World War II and didn’t resume until 1959.In 1987, NCRP Report 95 indicated that no manufacturers were using uranium-glaze in dinnerware.
Ceramics can be particularly radioactive if some compound of uranium (e.g., uranium oxide, sodium urinate) has been used to impart color (e.g., orange-red, green, yellow, black) to the glaze. It is widely known that uranium was used in the glaze of orange-red Fiesta dinnerware, but uranium glazes have also been used other types of ceramics: wall and floor tiles, pottery, laboratory ceramics, etc. The glaze can serve two functions: it provides color, and it seals the ceramic.
Although the uranium in the glaze emits gamma rays, alpha particles, and beta particles, the gamma and alpha emissions are weak. The beta particles are the easiest to detect, and they are also responsible for the bulk of the radiation exposure to those handling ceramics that employ a uranium glaze.

NCRP Report 95 reported the following measurements for dinnerware employing uranium glazes: 0.2 to 20 mrad per hour on contact as measured using film badges like we use in neurosurgery for Xray exposure.

Measurements of the radioactivity of ceramics that are reported in mR/hr are difficult to interpret unless it is known whether or not the detector was responding to beta particles. The exposure rate, in mR/hr, is defined for x-rays or gamma rays. It is not defined for betas. If a measurement is reported in mR/hr, the detector should not be responding to beta particles. CITES:Harry McMaster.Earthenware Dishes and Glaze Therefor. U.S. Patent No. 1,890,297, December 6, 1932 .

### Scompy — 2017-02-18T19:02:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/radioactivity.19095/post-208849>

Wow, that's a great chunk of information! It makes me very curious about a few items in my home now. I think it's time I picked up a meter just to see.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-02-18T22:22:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/radioactivity.19095/post-208862>

This is why you should never buy plates from China.

HE started his run with them off too to take full effect of the UV stimulus higher up on the mountain. This is the kind of thing a mitochondriac would do consistently. I applaud Max for leading his EU partners in how to combat Vitamin D winter. Even touch sensation, though for more subtle reasons, relies on electromagnetic vibrations and interactions. skiing - the sunlight reflecting from the snow causes the Albedo effect

# Thread: Storing Water for Long Term Emergency Storage
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/storing-water-for-long-term-emergency-storage.19101/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread

══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Melanie Procter — 2017-02-19T11:52:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/storing-water-for-long-term-emergency-storage.19101/post-208889>

Hi,

I listened to Jack's podcast in which he was interviewed by Luke Story today. I was searching for information on long term water storage. He mentions storing water underground in the dark and how to bring life back into the water. I highly recommend this podcast.

I wanted to ask if anyone knows if water should be treated for long term storage. I know that if you buy the PVC buckets, they recommend treating the water with chlorine. I thought it would be better to clean the buckets with ozonated water, and then store it. I am not sure for huge amounts if glass would be possible. I am thinking of storing water for my mom in Florida before the hurricane season and here at my resort in Phuket in case of emergency. Does anyone have any ideas on the optimal way to store large amounts of water. Again, most websites are recommending chlorine treatment I just think all the masterminds on this website would have innovative, great solutions.

Thanks

Mel

### Jack Kruse — 2017-02-19T15:58:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/storing-water-for-long-term-emergency-storage.19101/post-208894>

> underground resevoir is the best way but it will cost!!! Use basalt rocks if you do!!!

# Thread: Grounding Article that got me thinking....
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-article-that-got-me-thinking.19107/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### JoeT — 2017-02-20T14:55:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-article-that-got-me-thinking.19107/post-208938>

[https://www.facebook.com/drjackkruse/posts/1152037424860610](https://www.facebook.com/drjackkruse/posts/1152037424860610)

We all know that due to the dirty electric antennae effect, grounding products can do a lot more harm than good, per Michael Neurent.

However, given that many of us are probably doing our grounding in suburban backyards, that also begs the question as to whether we could still be doing more harm than good, even outdoors. For instance, per my Cornett, which likely reads low (per Neurent), the best readings I can achieve in my backyard are about .5 Mg.

That is generally considered a "safe" level for indoors. However, I'm not so sure that is a "safe" level to ground yourself to? I wonder if the cons outweigh the pros even in this situation?

### JanSz — 2017-02-20T15:21:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-article-that-got-me-thinking.19107/post-208939>

Is Grounding Good for You?

Definite maybe.

/
Lets get a list of think that are good regardless of anything that anybody may throw at it.

.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-02-20T17:25:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-article-that-got-me-thinking.19107/post-208952>

[http://livethefuel.com/051-mitochondria-biohacking-jack-kruse/](http://livethefuel.com/051-mitochondria-biohacking-jack-kruse/)

### JoeT — 2017-02-22T20:46:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-article-that-got-me-thinking.19107/post-209066>

So, here is what I've found thus far: obviously being deep in nature is the most optimal. But for those of us who can't be during the work week, grounding in an area with .1 or .2 mg is probably safe. The first problem here is that most of us likely don't have access to a meter that is precise enough. (i.e: Cornett is only sensitive down to about .5 mg).

So, my plan is to rent a meter from Michael and determine if my backyard is more than .1 or .2 mg. If it is, then it is no bueno and I will need use Michael's meter to determine if there is a local spot that is suitable .

### Alper — 2017-02-24T16:57:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-article-that-got-me-thinking.19107/post-209232>

Just thought about grounding in the winter (in high latitude without catching a cold, which I caught last time I tried to ground at 4°C, wet underground ): Huging a tree should have the same effect as grounding with your feet, shouldn´t it?

### JoeT — 2017-02-26T00:12:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-article-that-got-me-thinking.19107/post-209288>

yes that works, however, probably not as well because the balls of your feet are the optimal contact point. But it's surely better than not grounding at all.

### JoeT — 2017-02-27T22:02:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-article-that-got-me-thinking.19107/post-209403>

Another great article on potential hazards while grounding, with a "geekish" paper attached.

In addition, I see that gold member "Health2015" posted in the "ask jack" section (which I don't have access to) about feeling "wired" after grounding. Grounding should have the precise opposite effect. There are some serious red flags there, and may indicate her grounding environment is leading to absorption of more nnEMF than native electrons.

[http://www.electricsense.com/7130/grounding-health-emf-protection/](http://www.electricsense.com/7130/grounding-health-emf-protection/)

### JoeT — 2017-02-28T00:26:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-article-that-got-me-thinking.19107/post-209410>

And one more thing just to finish this Rant.....I'm kind of pissed at Mercola for putting his name on grounding sheets. Grounding is obviously more complex than meets the eye, and depends heavily on context and environment. It appears he did not do is due diligence, and his credibility is damaged in my eyes.

Organic or not
ask him where the chicken feed was stored before.
If it went thru being stored in silo it is deprived of butyric acid (C4:0)

Our food of animal origins most of the time (or 100% of the time) comes thru feeding the animals a food that goes thru storage in silo.

Human population see decreased gut flora.
Our gut flora feeds on butyric acid.
Butyric acid messes up stuff stored in silo.
Silo are managed by minimizing butyric acid.

///
That is why even natural food tends to last long (without preservatives), thanks to the absence of butyric acid.

Have a look at this blog. This guy knocks it out of the park analysing the study and you will see what is wrong with it The days of studies getting published only if they are free of bias and balanced in reporting are over if they ever existed. If you are really interested ina study for your own purposes and not just to read you need to read the complete paper and if it is really important get the underlying data.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eggs-are-a-cheap-solid-food-that-has-dha-too.19108/post-269602>

Egg sandwich r

> [DLO said:](/goto/post?id=209293)
>
> Does anyone know if quail and duck eggs are less reactive than chicken eggs? My allergens go nuts over egg white and egg yolk.... Duck eggs seem to be more keto friendly but aside from heart rate testing prepost any other ideas?

I personally really like dove/pigeon eggs. You need about three for every one normal size chicken egg. If you keep chickens you cant go away for a holiday or something without somebody looking after them, but pigeons/doves look after themselve

The French go crazy over dove eggs, and pigeon meat is £150 for a meal at posh French dinners. I can do one of my specialties is a French style dove delicacy

I prefer to keep pigeons than chickens because they look after themselves.

A pole dovecote is an excellent idea, you can lower it every day to take the eggs out and maybe a squab or two, (baby dove) then raise I back up to keep hem safe from predators.

They feed themselves with grubs and worms Ect ect. And you can take a lot of eggs from just a few families of pigeons.

I have one pole dove vote but would like a few more. Also homing pigeons is a great hobby you can take one with you and send a message home on its leg, the kids love it it's so exciting

### Apteryx — 2022-03-02T01:50:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eggs-are-a-cheap-solid-food-that-has-dha-too.19108/post-310663>

Just my humble suggestion: prior to entertaining eggs as too primary of a source of certain minerals (specifically metal cations) ... may be worthwhile to give some deep consideration to the matter of the exact subtleties of egg composition

Not the least of reasons, phosvitin. Powerful chelator.

You can do a {Control + F} search to catch all mentions. The matter will quickly gain razor sharp clarity

[https://www.mdpi.com/books/pdfdownload/book/502](https://www.mdpi.com/books/pdfdownload/book/502)

Egg Consumption and Human Health Special Issue, ed. Maria Luz Fernandez, University of Connecticut USA / Editorial Office MDPI AG St. Alban-Anlage 66 Basel, Switzerland

Some interesting things arise: _"it acts as an important melanogenesis inhibitor to control excessive melanin synthesis in the melanocytes of animal and human skin \[21\]. It was suggested that egg-yolk phosvitin has the potential to be used as a natural bioactive compound as a hyper-pigmentation inhibitor for human skin \[...\]"_

Hope this piques your curiosity, if you are in a position for this to be of relevance to you (i. e. entertaining reliance on eggs as a significant nutritional source)

Apteryx.

# Thread: Biohacking Shingles
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/biohacking-shingles.19115/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Benedick Howard — 2017-02-21T05:36:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/biohacking-shingles.19115/post-208991>

About 14 months ago I went to the doctor for a resent outbreak of a mostly dormant long standing issue with asthma. I was put on Symbicort 80 2x daily.
I have been doing Ashtanga yoga for decades and was prepping to shift some of that time into running.
Last year I got back into running for the first time in decades 3x weekly average. I took it slow.
After 14 months I was up to 8 miles on Saturdays and 2 -3 mile runs during the week.
I slowly increased my speed averaging 12 minute miles and high 9s best.

Nothing hurt and I could breath again!
But during the year my left eye vision got blurrier. Some days noticeable worse than others. I thought that it was due to a 2 year old percussive injury when an overstretched bungi cord unleashed sending it directly to the open eyeball. It knocked me out for a moment and I collapsed to the ground in pain.
Medical examination showed no damage.

So I got through last year's running, felt so relieved to be running again and then in December (a few months after coming across Dr Jack's work) I started taking DHA. I had been doing coconut oils for years because of it feeding the cell membranes, but I was unaware that DHA formed the mitochondria membrane. I have been eating really healthy, living off grid, very small nnEMF, great sunlight and clean air. Hey its Hawaii!

I began eating more seafood.

I noticed energy surges, brain clarity surges and felt stronger, although my run times were worsening, I just did not have the energy.

THEN BAM.

Three weeks ago in Parsvakonasana a standing side twist I reached fuller in to the pose than usual. I did not think about it until that night the pain began in between my ribs and over the following days exploded into shingles with twitching muscles and bolts of pain shooting up the ribs towards the spine.

But the shingles was not yet diagnosed. So I went back to my usual routines of MMS (sodium chlorite mixed with citric acid) and microcurrent stimulation.

Gassing the chlorine directly to the tissues was astounding how quickly the pain left. But each day it spread more and the pain just got worse. Using up to 6 caps 500 gm extra strength tylenol.
Taking the MMS internally 10 drops every hour or two helped a lot.

If I had known more about shingles pain earlier I would have done more MMS application, I was not adapting fast enough to the situation. I think it was containing, but the pain was so much I was having awful troubles just getting out of bed, sleeping etc.

About day 4 (they are all a blurr) I went to the doctors thinking I was low on potassium. They diagnosed me with Shingles. I could not believe it. After a second opinion and day later I got going with 1000mg Valacylovir 3 x daily. I have been taking potassium chloride about 500mg daily, B12, B complex, l-lysine, hours and hours of sunlight, homemade fermented veges, bananas, salads, chicken, ahi, potatoes, greens, kambosha, eggs, lots of butter, goats cheese, coconut and good virgin olive oil, no sugars, some carbs, no coffee.

At day 4 with the anti viral. The swelling has gone down immensely, very miminal seepage. I drained all the puss sacks 2 days ago. The doctor also prescribed 10 mg 3x Prednisone. I took it once that first night. No pain. BUT migraines, dryness and tortured dreams and such, as the research I could do on the internet (WIFI hurts the fascia around the ribs).

After I started on the Valacyclovair I stopped taking MMS internally, just gassing the sores with MMS. By then I was in a ritual of dressing the sores, gassing, some minimal exercise and LOTS of the early margining sunlight.

I took "Clear" magnesium citrate (day3) and it made relaxation way faster and easier to get comfortable in bed or hammock during the day.

The tissue under and around the sores are very sensitive. Wind sensitive, people energy sensitive, cold sensitive and WIFI sensitive. I don't have wifi on unless I am connected to the internet to read Dr Jack's latest posts!

At the end of the Valacyclovir the sores had stopped oozing. Never needed the inhaler with the antiviral.

But today, 3 days later, one or two spots in the sores started to minimally ooze, so I went back to the doctor for another course in Valacyclovir 1gm 3x daily for 7 days, 50 mg tremadol hcl for backup when the tylenol does not work.

This is why astronauts have massive viral titers spike when they are up in the ISS.......The virus is sensing the environment it i sin is sub optimal so it wants to escape to a new host genome.

### Benedick Howard — 2017-03-01T04:52:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/biohacking-shingles.19115/post-209459>

Thanks for the support, I can feel your support and it sure makes a huge difference energetically.

Update ::
Fairing better and about done with the 2nd course of Valcyclovir.

I have a "better day" then crash the next day. But making headway.

Mostly not taking the Tremadol because it makes the asthma return. They seem related somehow.

The sores are immensely healed on the front ribs and just about cleared on the back (which the MMS gassing cleared up easier bc there was way less), and the MMS gassing saved it from spreading further and after an application you can see the skin clear a little each time. 1-2 application daily and internally too.

Pain a problem still, yesterday I discovered that gentle long yoga stretches really help regain some normalcy from my tormented bent over posture! So keep moving. Lower back hurting slightly now from an old injury.

Played ukulele and sung for the first time which I am so grateful for, its been a month and I had to miss a gig last Sunday

I am convinced that my right wrist it sickened from holding the cell phone. The discomfort extend up the arm to the funny bone, then up to the armpit. It was very uncomfortable days 4 - 20 ish.

I am using the phone (when I use it) on a selfy stick for maybe 10 minute sessions and mostly I leave it in airplane mode and connect when the phone is away from my body.

Discovered some new microcurrent application strategies for the hyper sensitive areas that knocks the pain down in minutes. Thankfully.

I am grateful for the learning, the discoveries and being forced to take time out to re-evaluate my priorities, maybe you can relate!

### Mommy3times — 2017-02-21T05:56:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-northern-louisiana-optimal-like-the-southern-gulf.19116/post-208993>

My husband received an offer to move to the shreveport/Bosier City area and I don't want to assume that just because Jack lives near the gulf and its optimal, that this area would be optimal as well. Its definitely not an option to stay in Kalispell, MT and any feedback would be greatly appreciated. Im concerned about:

-   High humidity- have re-occuring sinus issues and several small thyroid nodules. Grew up in Des Moines, IA and 2 yrs n MT. For the 1st time in my life, I can finally breath deeply, due to:
    -   Eliminated diary and gluten.

    -   Icing entire neck b4 bed

    -   Switching to ice cold CT in my nose with a grossman water-pick, eliminated the smell of infection. IR sauna. magnetico
    -   Seafood is fairly priced from Alaska and often is on sale.
-   All my CT is done on the ski mountain (thin long top/ bottom) for 5 hours each weekend (10-38 degrees) & no jacket everywhere. Ice baths had made my husband wonder if Im going crazy and he's upset that I eliminated wifi and havent provided double-blind studies on EMF.. so I try to stay in under the radar.

-   Air-Conditioning, most people dont have it and its the only excuse I have to open the windows at night.

-   No elevation
-   Unknown about what the deep south really holds and what new symptoms will manifest.

My health is ok, but I have 35 lbs to loose and was unsuccessful at Keto for 9 months (eliminated hypoglecimic) and Leptin Reset for 3 mos- bloating finally reduced timed with my sunglo and Nicotine gum. My son is underweight (gaining 5 lbs on Leptin Reset and fat).

Any info is great .. Im feeling a bit overwhelmed at the depth of options for future hacks, I appreciate all the efforts and posts that have been made to help change lives on this forum!

> [Mommy3times said:](/goto/post?id=208993)
>
> My health is ok, but I have 35 lbs to loose

Think fat.
Think insulin.
Certain low insulin level.
When you are above that level (you are likely eating or ate recently)
Whatever energy from that food you have not used it is stored as a body fat.
When your insulin is below that level, you ate some time ago.
If body needs energy it will take it from your body fat.

If you are determined to loose weight, you have two choices.
1\. stop eating, until you have reached your goal weight. Just drink plenty of water.

2.Divide the 24hr in the day into 2 parts.
8hrs during which you may eat
16hrs during which you do not eat
You can drink clean unadulterated water, spring water, any time.

> [Elena Zvon said:](/goto/post?id=254278)
>
> So is it better to have a smartphone but often switched to Airplane mode, with twiglight and blueblockers or and old model like Nokia? should i check for the SAR, or all old phones might be emmiting more EMF than mordern smartphones? I tried to google it at least in my language anf find only information of safer or less safe smartphones. Another opinion I heard ht you get less emf from modern non-smartphones with buttons than old models..sorry. feel confused and I feel i need to addres it asap, along with a terrible recent habbit of reading on the smartphone too much, which does me harm, no matter how useful is th einfo i read.My 7 y old daughter is never on the tablet, and does not know how to use the phone. I screwed my health \_lyme and advanced sarcoma, i can not do it to my daughter..I am so sick that I can not get her to school, so with the help of my mum we do homeschooling that saves her from emf and desire to have a phone.

Elena, its better to have a smartphone in airplane with twilight and blue blockers at night. My cell is pretty much always in airplane now unless i need to make a call or expecting an urgent call. I also have a hack that i do when i am expecting a call by turning off cellular on my iPhone. when you turn cellular off you will still get reception and calls go through but your phone is not constantly emitting radiation pinging cell towers every 15 seconds or so while in standby. This hack is verified by others and me too with 2 meters. when cellular is off you get calls but no texts or emails. I just turn it on several times a day to check them. thats all. The safest use of your cell when in standby. I also used to read tons on iPad and phone and still do but i mostly load an article or whatever and turn it to airplane (check also that bluetooth and wifi are also off). this way you can read on your phone without radiation. hope this helps. Natalia

### Sujoe123 — 2017-02-22T19:13:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/molybdenum.19122/post-209060>

I take one teaspoon of MSM twice per day. I am CBS upregulated and BHMT upregulated.... both waste sulfur products up to 10 times the normal rate each... from what I have read.... Is molybdenum needed to use the sulfate in MSM?? I know that molybdenum changes the toxic sulfite to sulfate. I take 500 micrograms of molybdenum a.m. and p.m. I also supplement NAC, creatine, glutamine and glycine, zinc, selenium and phosphytidal serine. I guess I should put this under success stories because now my hair does not fall out by the hundreds!! I have listened and read Dr. Stephanie Seneff and she does not mention taking extra molybdenum with MSM. Any thoughts from you cellular bio majors? I also take some extra copper because molybdenum can decrease copper.

### Sujoe123 — 2017-02-22T19:44:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/molybdenum.19122/post-209063>

Even doing all these things with grounding and sunlight... I can only keep my malonylaldehyde test clear of cellular damage for about three-4 hour sections of the day following these supplements with PPQ, CoQ10, VitC, VitE, R-Lipoic and astaxanthin, and DHA, and EPA. Also, sleep on the super Magnetico. Also large total dose of intermittent Folinic, methly folate, and methyl B12 with P5P and R5P and B2 due to other homozygous SNPs in B12 and folic acid pathways. I stay away from added folic acid to breadstuffs grains and cereals because these synthetic folics just take up the receptor sites for the methylated forms that I need. This is so difficult to manage with slow and fast SNPs in fighting each other creating roadblocks in some places and dumping valuable nutrients to waste in the sulfur glutathione pathway, also the positive and negative feedback considerations!!!! My health is considerably better than years ago but this has been a tremendous expensive struggle to learn this much.... especially with the memory damage that all this has caused. Even though I am a nurse practitioner of anesthesia I had to study about 7 times longer than my classmates and my retention is poor. Thank God for multiple choice test questions!! My doctor put me on disability for memory and physical deterioration about 6 years ago and I have read like crazy to understand what happened and why it got so much worse. With the 14-hour days in the operating room my health just crashed!!! Thank you Dr. Jack for helping me understand part of the equation why this happened! EMF HELL HOLE!!! No sunlight or grounding!

Hi, I have one copy of the CBS 699 , COMT V158, COMT h62, and two copies of the BHMT 08, VDR taq, MAO A, and the MTRR.

My sulfur metabolism issues seem to be more severe than most.

I take about 1000 to 1500 mcg of Molybdenum and 20 mg of magnanese before every meal containing sulfur rich foods.

I am also compound heterozygous with a copy of each of the 677 and the 1298. 2ooomg methyl folate and methyl B12

I have a question for you, are you homozygous or heterozygous CBS and which mutation of the BHMT are you? BHMT 01, or BHMT 02, or BHMT 04 , BHMT 08?

The reason that I am asking is because if you have sulfur metabolism issue, is it wise to be taking so many sulfur containing supplements? I agree that some are more necessary than others such as CoQ10, but depending on the severity of your mutations, couldn't you be causing a build up in ammonia and other toxic chemicals?

I am not judging, just trying to learn more about approaches to these issues. Dr Doug Wallace mentioned that the mitochondrial DNA can affect the expression of the Nuclear DNA, so my goal is just that.

And I agree,...........thanks Dr Kruse for your valuable wisdom that you have made available for everyone, especially for those on this site!!!!
